En: Madam, I assure you we're real police officers, the lieutenant repeats dutifully.
Zh: “夫人，我向你保证，我们是真警察，”警督尽职尽责地重复到。

En: Kim, tell her we're real policemen!
Zh: “金，告诉她我们是真警察！”

En: Ma'am, we're looking for Billie Méjean's apartment. We have important news to deliver.
Zh: “夫人，我们正在寻找比莉·梅让的公寓。我们有重要消息要传达。”

En: No reply. You can hear faint sweeping inside; a gust of wind blows through your clothes.
Zh: 没有回应。你可以听见里面微弱的扫地声；一阵风吹过你的衣衫。

En: This could be a way into the apartment building the smoking man vanished into.
Zh: 这可能是进入公寓大楼的一条路，抽烟的男人就是从那里消失的。

En: Backyard door? There must be another entrance to the east.
Zh: 后院的门？东边肯定还有一个入口。

En: Backyard door? With the smoker? You already were there. Didn't work.
Zh: 后院的门？那边是不是有个抽烟的人？你已经去过那边了。没用。

En: There is no reply. Just faint sweeping sounds inside.
Zh: 没有回应。你可以听见里面微弱的扫地声。

En: Am I?
Zh: 我有吗？

En: Obviously. Look at that vague yet threatening stuff spewing forth from your mouth...
Zh: 很明显。看看从你嘴里喷涌而出的那些模糊而又吓人的东西……

En: You already tried and all you got was *hurt*. Better not to try anymore. Then you won't get HURT.
Zh: 你已经试过了，不过唯一得到的只有*伤害*。最好不要再试了。那样你就不会受伤。

En: A sturdy metal door guards the southwest entrance to the apartment building. It's locked.
Zh: 一扇坚固的金属门守卫着公寓大楼西南方的入口。它是锁着的。

En: Knock.
Zh: 敲门。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The door rattles against your knuckles, but there's no response.
Zh: 随着你指关节的敲击，大门发出嘎吱嘎吱的响声，不过没有人回应。

En: Knock again.
Zh: 再敲一次。

En: The door rattles again, but this time you hear an elderly woman's voice calling out from inside...
Zh: 门再次发出嘎吱嘎吱的响声，不过这一次你听见里面传来一个老妇人的声音……

En: Convince her that you're a real policeman.
Zh: 说服她你是个真正的警察。

En: Kim is here.
Zh: 金在这里。

En: Found your badge.
Zh: 找到你的警徽。

En: Kim confirmed you are officers.
Zh: 金确认你是警官。

En: Billie Méjean's apartment.
Zh: 比莉·梅让的公寓。

En: Dresscode: RCM official.
Zh: 着装要求：RCM警官。

En: You can't think of anything convincing. Time for some police brutality!
Zh: 你想不出什么让人信服的事情。是时候来点警察暴力了！

En: I'm gonna light this place up! (Roll up your sleeves.)
Zh: “我要把这个地方点燃！”（卷起你的袖子。）

En: You're well versed in the kind of threatening legalese that implies criminal liability but in fact has no meaning whatsoever.
Zh: 你很精通这类意味着刑事责任的威胁性法律术语，不过实际上没有任何意义。

En: This is the police -- open the door.
Zh: “我是警察——把门打开。”

En: Hold on, who am I speaking to?
Zh: “等一下，我在跟谁说话？”

En:
The police?! Everyone knows the police don't come around *here*.“ The hallways echoes with her cackle.	她的笑声在走廊里回荡：“警察？！所有人都知道警察不会来*这里*的。”
0x010000570001DAA8	Dialogue Text	PIER / APARTMENTS PIER DOOR	Cleaning Lady	It doesn't matter who I am. Now go on, get out of here!"
Zh: “我是谁无所谓。现在，赶快离开这里！”

En: But I'm not joking.
Zh: “但是我没有开玩笑。”

En: Hey, are you there? I've checked the backyard, but couldn't get in that way.
Zh: “喂，你在吗？我检查过后院了，不过那边进不去。”

En: Good. We've had enough problems with bums and drunks and thieves loitering in the hallway... You have no business here.
Zh: “很好。我们已经受够那些在走廊里闲晃的流浪汉、醉鬼和小偷了……这里没你什么事。”

En: No, I already told you, I won't be responsible for any more strangers getting into the building...
Zh: 那个声音回复到：“不行，我已经告诉过你了，我不会再让任何陌生人进入这栋大楼了……”

En: I need to talk to a young man in this building.
Zh: “我需要跟这里的一个年轻男性聊几句。”

En: Go check the backyard door, maybe someone there will... She trails off, leaving the sentence unfinished.
Zh: “去后院门看看，也许那边有人可以……”她的声音越来越小，话没有说完。

En: You don't scare me, punk! I may be an old woman but I can still take the likes of you!
Zh: “你吓不到我的，小阿飞！我也许是个老女人，不过我还是能对付你这种人的！”

En: Ma'am, if you don't open this door, I'm gonna introduce you to a lateral vascular neck restraint -- and it's going to *hurt*.
Zh: “夫人，如果你不打开这扇门，我就要对你施行锁喉的手段了——而且，那样一定会*很疼*的。”

En: No, you're not.
Zh: “不，你不会的。”

En: Open up or I'm gonna breach this motherfucker! (Kick the door.)
Zh: “把门打开，不然我就砸了这个狗屁玩意！”（踹门。）

En: From within comes the faint sound of a broom sweeping across the concrete floor.
Zh: 里面传来扫帚清扫混凝土底板的微弱声响。

En: Ma'am, your non-compliance is hindering a police matter. I may be forced to refer you for potential prosecution.
Zh: “夫人，你不服从的行为是在妨碍警方办案。我可能会被迫把你列为潜在的诉讼对象。”

En: I know my rights! And don't you 'ma'am' me, boy! the voice snaps back.
Zh: “我知道自己的权利！还有，不要再叫我‘夫人’了，小子！”那个声音突然又响了起来。

En: Insufficiently deferential!
Zh: 不够恭敬！

En: Miss, the lieutenant jumps in, "would it help if we offered to show you our badges?"
Zh: “女士，”警督插了进来，“如果我们把警徽拿给你看的话，会不会有帮助呢？”

En: But the sweeping on the other side has stopped.
Zh: 另一边扫地的声音停下来了。

En: Hold your horses... You hear the click of a night latch, before the lady on the other side gets caught in a coughing spasm.
Zh: “不要着急……”你听见门闩发出的咔哒声，另一边的女士咳嗽个不停。

En: I don't care about your stinkin' badge. Just come in, she manages to say with a wheeze.
Zh: “我不在乎你的什么鬼警徽。进来吧，”她气喘吁吁地说到。

En: Okay, miss, would it help if I showed you my badge?
Zh: “好吧，女士，如果我把警徽拿给你看的话，会不会有帮助呢？”

En: (Lie.) "I've got my badge right here, if you want to see it."
Zh: （撒谎。）“我的警徽就在这里，如果你想看的话。”

En: Please, let me inside. It's cold out here.
Zh: “请让我进去。外面很冷。”

En: Ah! It's like a carrot to the stick, sort of.
Zh: 啊！有点像*胡萝卜加大棒*。

En: Stop banging on the door! I'm not letting any more strangers inside.
Zh: “别再敲门了！我不会放陌生人进来的。”

En: Really? You're a miner?
Zh: “真的吗？你是矿工？”

En: Don't you have a real home?
Zh: “你没有真正的家吗？”

En: It's not the nicest place, but I guess it'll have to do.
Zh: “它不是最好的地方，不过我猜必须这样吧。”

En: Yes. I keep hoping a shaft will collapse on me, but somehow it never happens.
Zh: “是的。我一直希望会有个竖井倒下来砸在我身上，但是不知道为什么，这种事从没发生过。”

En: Does anyone, in a city like this? she replies wistfully, looking around.
Zh: “在这种城市里，会有这样的人吗？”她看了看周围，盼望地回复着。

En: It doesn't *have to* do anything at all. Nothing does. She looks at the paint dripping down the wall. "Like me. Right now, I'm doing nothing at all."
Zh: “没什么*必要*的事。完全没有。”她看着墙上滴落的油漆。“就像我。现在我就什么也没做。”

En: If there's pain about any particular home she's lost, she's buried it deep, fortified herself against it.
Zh: 如果说她曾经因为失去某个特别的家而痛苦，那一定被她隐藏地很深，把自己保护起来不去想它。

En: Cool. I have other questions. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。我还有其他问题。”（总结。）

En: Shoot, piggy. It's what you do, isn't?
Zh: “说吧，猪头。你就是干这个的，不是吗？”

En: To hell with the art part, man. It's all about the high anyway.
Zh: 让艺术见鬼去吧，老兄。反正也全都是关于*嗨*的。

En: I have an opinion on this -- wanna hear it? (Proceed.)
Zh: “对此我有个想法——想听听吗？”（继续。）

En: Yeah?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Actually I don't have an opinion. I lied.
Zh: “其实我没什么想法。我撒谎了。”

En: Lying is cool, I guess. She absent-mindedly paints a red streak on her left cheek.
Zh: “我猜，撒谎其实挺酷的。”她心不在焉地在左脸上抹了一道红色的条纹。

En: This is the Cindy that misters 'Piss' and 'Fuck' said had the armoured gloves. Play it cool now...
Zh: ‘尿兄’和‘干弟’说盔甲手套就在这个辛迪手里。现在冷静一些……

En: Something about skulls, gloves... what was it? Maybe it'll come to you later.
Zh: 关于骷髅头，手套的某种东西……到底是什么呢？也许之后你会想起来。

En: A brush -- an artist? The red splatter is... urban expressionism?
Zh: 一支刷子——艺术家吗？红色的斑点是……城市表现主义？

En: The woman on the boat does not notice her staring.
Zh: 船上的女人没有注意到她的目光。

En: She means the opposite.
Zh: 她的意思正好相反。

En: You've *lessened* her desire to deface the building.
Zh: 你*减少*了她破坏这栋建筑的欲望。

En: I don't condone the idea of using physical force on her. She's a *girl*.
Zh: 我赦免你对她使用武力的想法。她只是个*女孩*。

En: The wannabe-SKULLS are dear to her. The armour less so.
Zh: 骷髅头崇拜者对于她来说很珍贵。盔甲就不一样了。

En: She doesn't want to sound surprised at anything you say.
Zh: 你说什么，她都不想表现出很惊讶的样子。

En: And there's a little girl wearing the gloves there?
Zh: “那里有个戴手套的小女孩？”

En: Got it.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: Piggy, I have no idea why I just told you that... She looks at you, a little sad suddenly. "You look like a shaggy dog left in the rain for too long. I have a weakness for animals."
Zh: “猪猡，我不知道我为什么要告诉你这个……”她看着你，突然有些悲伤。“你看起来像只毛发杂乱的狗——被留在雨里太久太久。我的弱点就是动物。”

En: I have a weakness for animals. It's the animal-thing again. Damn it. She looks at you, a little sad suddenly.
Zh: 她看着你，突然有些悲伤。“我的弱点就是动物。又是动物之类的事就对了。该死。”

En: No-no. That's all the snitching Cindy the SKULL does for today. Actually, I don't even know why I told you what I just told you...
Zh: “不不不。今天骷髅头辛迪的告密行动就到此为此了。事实上，我甚至都不知道自己为什么要跟你说这些……”

En: Or a weakness for police officers, miss. He makes a note in his notebook. "Thank you either way."
Zh: “或者你的弱点是警察，小姐。”他在笔记本里记了点什么。“不管怎么说，还是要感谢你。”

En: She wants it to be something true -- and *total*.
Zh: 她希望它是某种真实的东西——还要*完整*。

En: Better not to.
Zh: 最好不要。

En: She'd never admit to it anyway.
Zh: 反正她永远不会承认的。

En: In this vision of hers, you are mutually opportunistic organisms. Growing like mould on the streets.
Zh: 在她看来，你是互助投机主义生物体。就像霉菌一样在街道上生长。

En: What is it with these SKULLS and *cooperation*? They have to be the nicest violent street gang in Revachol West.
Zh: 这些骷髅头还有*合作*是怎么回事？他们大概是瑞瓦肖西部最好的街头暴力帮派了。

En: She's probably not a good example of the gang either. *Or* maybe these SKULLS are more of an art movement than a gang.
Zh: 她可能也不是帮派分子的好榜样。*或者*，也许这些骷髅头更像是一种艺术活动，而不是黑帮。

En: Got it. Enough of that then.
Zh: “明白。暂时先这么多吧。”

En: Thank you for passing on the message -- now run along, piggy.
Zh: “感谢你来传递信息——现在赶紧走吧，猪猡。”

En: She did it herself. Pumped it out. She's proud of it, too.
Zh: 她自己做的。把它抽出来的。而且她对此感到很骄傲。

En: She really did it. She's proud of it, too.
Zh: 真是她做的。而且她对此感到很骄傲。

En: She *does* have eyes that seem to be smeared with coal.
Zh: 她*的确*拥有一双似乎沾染了煤灰的眼睛。

En: The inspiration will come to her once *hell* is set loose on the streets. It's too calm right now.
Zh: 等到*地狱*降临到这些街道的时候，她就会有灵感了。现在还有些太平静。

En: If you're *really* interested then I'm sorry, she whispers, "but I don't do anti-radiation drugs anymore so I don't know where to get them."
Zh: 她被自己的笑话逗笑了，接着，她低语到：“如果你*真的*很感兴趣，那我只能说抱歉，我已经不再吃抗辐射药物了，所以我也不知道能去哪里找。”

En: Where did you get these then?
Zh: “那你是从哪里弄到的？”

En: From a guy on Boogie Street. Porta Rosa. Go there after midnight and you can get all kinds of funny things. Veterans of the People's Pile selling their stash.
Zh: “从布吉街的一个家伙那里。波勒塔·罗莎。午夜过后到那里去，你就能弄到各种各样有意思的东西。人民反应堆的老战士在那里卖他们的私藏品。”

En: She immediately regrets telling you -- but it's too late.
Zh: 她立即就后悔告诉你了——不过为时已晚。

En: Damn me and my affinity to farm animals, she's probably thinking. She hopes the information is useless.
Zh: 真他妈该死，我对家畜真的毫无抵抗力，她也许正这样想到。她希望这条信息派不上用场。

En: Man, that's lame...
Zh: 拜托，这也太逊了……

En: No-no. It's great.
Zh: 不不。它很棒。

En: But that would mean he *doesn't* say every pathetic thing that pops in his head.
Zh: 但是那就意味着，他*没有*把脑袋里面蹦出来的每一件乏味的事情都说出来。

En: She only gave it to you because she doesn't see you as competition.
Zh: 她把东西给你，只是因为她不觉得你是一个竞争对手。

En: The right idea is not coming to her. It's excruciating.
Zh: 她想不出正确的概念。这很折磨人。

En: Damn, it was too strong.
Zh: 该死，它太强了。

En: Do you have any idea what happened to the hanged man's armour?
Zh: “你知不知道那个被害人的盔甲去哪了？”

En: Wait -- don't you wanna know how I knew to ask you?
Zh: “等等——你不想知道我为什么知道要来找你吗？”

En: Not particularly. Her eyes dart across the horizon. "I wanna keep this as brief as possible, you see. I want to return to painting the world *red*."
Zh: “不怎么想。”她的目光掠过地平线。“你看，我想尽量简短一些。这样就能继续*染红*这个世界。”

En: Piss and Fuck told me.
Zh: “尿兄和干弟告诉我的。”

En: Okay, so tell me what you know then.
Zh: “好吧，那就把你知道的都告诉我。”

En: I think you didn't hear me. Why would I care?
Zh: “我想你没听见。我为什么要在乎？”

En: Fine. I don't care about those wannabe-SKULLS -- and I don't care about your armour.
Zh: “好吧。我不在乎那些骷髅头崇拜者——而且我也不在乎你的盔甲。”

En: Ugh, alright, piggy. I'll give you this one. I saw a little girl in the fishing village running around with military-grade hand-wear. Looked cute as hell.
Zh: “好吧，猪猡。我就告诉你一条消息。我看见渔村里有个小孩子戴着军事级的手套在到处游荡。看起来真是可爱的要死。”

En: *Grand* art. Art DeLuxe. The artsy-est, the most ground breaking, the...
Zh: “*伟大的*艺术。高级艺术。最有艺术气息，最具开创性……”

En: Let me stop you right there, piggy, she interjects, raising her hand. "You have no idea, do you? No idea -- no brush. Fuck off."
Zh: “我要打断你一下，猪猡，”她举起手，插了进来。“你不知道，是吗？没想法——就没刷子。滚开。”

En: You'll see. You will *all* see and tremble.
Zh: “你会看见的。你们*全都*会看见，会颤抖。”

En: Yeah... Not gonna hold my breath, piggy. You look like you'd suck. At... everything really, she quickly adds.
Zh: “是啊……我不会抱多大期待，猪猡。你一看就是要搞砸的样子。真的……所有的事，”她飞快地点点头。

En: Cool. No brush then. Not a problem.
Zh: “好啊。那就不要刷子。没什么大不了。”

En: You're about to cry internally, aren't you piggy? She utters a disapproving grunt. "Pathetic. Just give up, art isn't for you."
Zh: “你在心里哭呢，是不是，猪猡？”她发出不赞同的嘟囔声。“真可悲。还是放弃吧，艺术不适合你。”

En: (Attempt to establish contact.) "Hey, sister. Let's talk *politics* for a minute."
Zh: (试图建立联系)“嘿，姐妹。我们来谈谈*政治*吧。”

En: And what do *you* know about politics? She squints at you suspiciously.
Zh: “那*你*懂什么政治？”她疑惑地眯起眼睛看着你。

En: I'm looking for comrades to help me fire up the Great Mazovian Sausage Grinder. Will you help me?
Zh: “我正在寻找同志帮我开动大马佐夫香肠绞肉机。你愿意帮我吗？”

En: Mañana at the harbour said there's someone organizing an *underground revolutionary cell*.
Zh: “港口的马列拉说有人在组织一个*地下革命小组*。”

En: One of the Union guys said there's someone trying to organize an *underground revolutionary cell*.
Zh: “工会的某个人说，有人想组织一个*地下革命小组*。”

En: I know deep down that you bleed white. You're a communist, Cindy. Admit it.
Zh: “我深知你流着白色的血。你是个康米主义者，辛迪。承认吧。”

En: My nose told me that you're also a communist. We should team up, join forces.
Zh: “我的鼻子告诉我，你也是个康米主义者。我们应该联手，联合起来。”

En: I'm searching for a bunch of *mole people*.
Zh: “我在寻找一群*鼹鼠人*。”

En: An *underground cell*? My, my. Sounds big time...
Zh: “一个*地下小组*？天啊，天啊。听起来大有来头……”

En: Well, well. Sounds like quite the snout you've got there...
Zh: “好啊，好啊。听起来你的鼻子挺灵的……”

En: Pigs and moles, eh? Sounds like something out of a children's fairy story.
Zh: “猪和鼹鼠，嗯？听起来像小孩童话故事里的东西。”

En: The girl erupts in a cackle that seems like it should belong to a much older woman...
Zh: 女孩爆发出一阵咯咯的笑声，似乎应该是属于一个更年长的女人……

En: Oh, that's very nice. The little piggy wants to make sausages. Out of what, his little piggy friends?
Zh: “哦，很好。小猪猡想做香肠。用什么做，他的小猪猡朋友吗？”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "Don't worry, Kim. I would never make you into a sausage."
Zh: (转向金) “别担心，金。我绝不会把你做成香肠的。”

En: That's what we need to talk about. I don't *know* who I'm supposed to be making sausages out of!
Zh: “这才是我们需要讨论的问题。我不*知道*我应该把谁做成香肠！”

En: No, out of the bourgeoisie.
Zh: “不，是用资产阶级做的。”

En: That's right. I'm gonna make juicy sausage links out of the entire 41st Precinct.
Zh: “这就对了。我要将整个第41分局的人做成多汁美味的一节节香肠。”

En: For the briefest instant, the lieutenant's hand hesitates above the notebook he's scribbling in.
Zh: 有那么一瞬间，警督的手在他抄写的笔记本上面犹豫了一下。

En: Wow. There's a proper *radical* piggy.
Zh: “哇，这里有一只真的*激进*的小猪。”

En: In the break room of Precinct 41, Sergeant Mack Torson is just now warming a grey sausage over an electric range. The oil in the pan hisses and pops. Torson is dabbing a spot off his pants when his partner, Satellite-Officer Chester McLaine enters, sniffs the air, and frowns...
Zh: 在41分局的休息室里，警长麦克·托森刚刚在电炉上加热一根灰色的香肠。锅里的油发出嘶嘶的爆响。托森正在擦掉裤子上的油渍的时候，他的搭档随迁警官切斯特·麦克莱恩进来嗅了嗅空气，皱了皱眉头……

En: It was not a concern of mine. The lieutenant blinks once.
Zh: “这不关我的事。”警督眨了一下眼睛。

En: Your olfactory department wants you to know that it accepts no responsibility for wherever this line of interrogation leads you.
Zh: 你的嗅觉部门想让你知道，无论这条审讯路线会把你引向何方，它不承担任何责任。

En: Sure. I know someone who'd love to talk that ideological stuff. You're looking for Steban.
Zh: “当然，我认识个人，他很愿意谈那些意识形态的东西。你要找的是史蒂文。”

En: So the little piggy is a big, bad communist now...
Zh: “所以小猪现在是个大坏蛋康米主义者了……”

En: How sad. This little piggy doesn't know very much, does he?
Zh: “真可怜。这只小猪猡并不知道太多，是不是？”

En: It's not. It's very serious.
Zh: “不是。这很严肃。”

En: I guess it does sound a little childish when you say it like that.
Zh: “我觉得，你这样说听起来确实有些幼稚。”

En: Oh, did I hurt the very serious pig's feelings?
Zh: “哦，我是不是伤了严肃认真的猪猡的感情？”

En: Yes, it really does...
Zh: “是的，确实如此……”

En: Whatever. I know someone who's into some pretty underground stuff. You're looking for Steban.
Zh: “算了。我认识一个人，他喜欢一些很地下的东西。你要找的是史蒂文。”

En: Who's Steban?
Zh: “史蒂文是谁？”

En: A right communist. Who runs a *mega cool* and *very secret* meeting.
Zh: “一个正确的康米主义者。他开了一个*超酷*和*非常秘密*的会议。”

En: Does this 'Steban' happen to go around in a jacket like the one I'm wearing?
Zh: “这个‘史蒂文’不会碰巧喜欢穿一件我现在穿着的这件夹克到处跑吧？”

En: Does this 'Steban' happen to have a jacket like this? (Show her the white jacket.)
Zh: “这个‘史蒂文’是不是刚好有一件这样的夹克？” (给她看那件白色夹克)

En: Does this 'Steban' happen to go around in a white jacket?
Zh: “这个‘史蒂文’不会碰巧喜欢穿件白夹克到处跑吧？”

En: Do you know where to find him?
Zh: “你知道在哪里可以找到他吗？”

En: He might.
Zh: “他可能会。”

En: I might.
Zh: “我可能会。”

En: Will you help me find him?
Zh: “你能帮我找到他吗？”

En: Will you tell me?
Zh: “你会告诉我吗？”

En: She pretends to think about it for a moment. "No."
Zh: 她假装思考了一会儿。“不。”

En: The lieutenant lets slip a sigh that seems to suggest this turn was utterly predictable.
Zh: 警督发出一声叹息，似乎暗示这种发展完全在预料中。

En: Come on, Cindy. From one communist to another, help me out.
Zh: “拜托，辛迪。身为康米主义者要互相帮助，帮帮我吧。”

En: Is it money you want? Here, take some. I've got plenty. (Offer her 5 reál.)
Zh: “你是要钱？来，拿吧。我有很多。”(给她5块钱)

En: I guess I should just give up, then. Nothing *ever* works out for me.
Zh: “看来是时候放弃了。我从来没有成功*过*。”

En: Just tell me what you want me to do!
Zh: “告诉我你想让我做什么！”

En: Keep your money. What I want from you's better than gold...
Zh: “留着你的钱吧。我想从你那里要的东西比金子还贵……”

En: Oh, fine, I'll help. But first, I want something from *you*...
Zh: “哦，好吧，我帮你。但首先，我想从*你*那里要些东西……”

En: God, if he isn't the saddest pig in the world...
Zh: “上帝，他是不是世界上最悲伤的猪猡……”

En: A wicked grin extends across her face.
Zh: 她的脸上露出了邪恶的笑容。

En: She's got you by the balls, chief. And she intends to squeeze them.
Zh: 她抓住了你的弱点，头儿。她还想乘胜追击……

En: Don't look so deflated. Whatever she asks, you keep your chin up!
Zh: 别一副泄气的样子。无论她问什么，你都要抬头挺胸！

En: Incidentally, 'The Saddest Pig in the World' is the title of a popular Gottwaldian children's book, written and illustrated by Moritz Metzger. In '26 it received the prestigious *Kritikerpreis* for youth literature.
Zh: 顺便说一下，《世界上最悲伤的猪》是一本流行戈特伍德童书的题目，插图和作者都是Moritz Metzger。26年，该作品获得了著名的*批评家奖*的青少年文学奖。

En: A laughing skull. Death hilarious. This is gonna be baaaad...
Zh: 一个会笑的骷髅头。死一般的幽默。这会很很很很糟糕……

En: What's that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: Oink for me, piggy. Just once.
Zh: “给我学猪叫，要小猪，就一次。”

En: I'm not oinking for you.
Zh: “我才不给你扮猪叫。”

En: Come on, this is no way to treat your revolutionary brother!
Zh: “拜托，你不该这么对待你的革命兄弟！”

En: (With gusto.) "Oink, oink!"
Zh: (兴高采烈地) “哼！哼！”

En: (Reluctantly.) "Oink, oink."
Zh: (不情愿地。) “哼，哼。”

En: (Through gritted teeth.) "Oink."
Zh: (咬牙切齿地) “哼。”

En: Be a good pig, now. No oink, no goods.
Zh: “现在学会做头好猪猡。不叫就没好处。”

En: Wrong. This is exactly how I treat my little brother.
Zh: “错了。我就是这样对待我的小弟的。”

En: Wow. That was easier than I expected. It's almost like you've been *wanting* to do it this whole time...
Zh: “哇，比我想象的要简单。简直就像你一直*想做*猪一样……”

En: There, that wasn't so bad, was it?
Zh: “好，还不算太坏吧？”

En: Well, well. Seems like we're dealing with one tough pig. I'm impressed...
Zh: “好吧，好吧。看来我们要对付一头强悍的猪猡。我很佩服……”

En: A cool, damp feeling ripples through you. You realise you needn't have rolled over quite so easily.
Zh: 一股清凉、潮湿的感觉在你身上荡漾。你意识到自己没必要这么轻易地屈服。

En: It's not the worst indignity you've suffered the last several days, but it is up there.
Zh: 这不是你在过去几天里遭受过的最严重的侮辱，但还是发生了。

En: Yes, somehow you managed to oink with at least a modicum of dignity.
Zh: 是的，不知怎么，你至少还能有一点尊严地哼一声。

En: The lieutenant, needless to say, is not impressed.
Zh: 警督呢，不用说，对此并不满意。

En: Sounds like you're really serious about meeting Steban. It's touching, sort of.
Zh: “听起来，你真的是很想见史蒂文。还算感人。”

En: Steban's group meets only at night, in an old room in these apartments here. It just so happens you're in luck: Their weekly meeting is *tonight*.
Zh: “史蒂文的小组只在晚上开会，就在这里公寓楼的一个旧房间里。你运气不错：他们这周的会议*今晚*要开。”

En: Poke your snout around sometime after 22.00 and you might just find them.
Zh: “晚上10点后用你的鼻子去打探下消息，你也许就能找到他们。”

En: Wait, how am I supposed to get into the apartments if I don't live there?
Zh: “等等，我不住那里，要怎么进公寓？”

En: Hold on, what else can you tell me about this Steban?
Zh: “等一下，你还能告诉我这个史蒂文的什么？”

En: That's very helpful. Thank you, Cindy.
Zh: “这很有帮助。谢谢你，辛迪。”

En: Hmm, why do I feel like there's a catch...
Zh: “嗯，为什么我觉得有陷阱……”

En: Use your little piggy brain. I'm sure *someone* will let you in...
Zh: “用你的小猪脑吧。我相信一定会*有人*让你进去的……”

En: Just that he's a real communist. Not like the play-acting you've been doing. The rest you'll have to see for yourself.
Zh: “只是，他是个真正的康米主义者。不像你一直在演戏。其余的你就得自己去看了。”

En: Hold on. You didn't think it would be that easy, did you?
Zh: “等一下。你以为不会那么容易吧？”

En: Oh, smart pig, because there *is*...
Zh: “哦，聪明的猪猡，因为*的确*有……”

En: See, Steban's a bit on the paranoid side. He's got all these *mega secret passphrases*, to keep out infiltrators and the like. You can't join the meeting without one.
Zh: “你看，史蒂文有点偏执。他设的那些*超级秘密口令*，以防止卧底和类似的东西进去捣乱。你没有口令就不能参加。”

En: Sure, I do. Steban trusts me.
Zh: “当然，我能进去。史蒂文信任我。”

En: Do you know the passphrase?
Zh: “你知道口令吗？”

En: Hang on, could it have something to do with that strange *inside-talk* the boiadeiro mentioned?
Zh: 等等，会不会和那个牛仔提到的那个奇怪的*内部谈话*有关？

En: But it won't be this one.
Zh: 但不会是这个。

En: Just not me.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: *Ahem*... The lieutenant clears his throat.
Zh: “*咳咳*……”。警督清了清嗓子。

En: Not to interfere in your personal errand, but I wonder whether it might have something to do with that phrase Mañana mentioned overhearing.
Zh: “不是要干涉你的私事，但我想，这是否可能与马列拉无意中听到的那句话有关。”

En: But you're not going to tell me, are you?
Zh: “但你不会告诉我，对吧？”

En: That's fine. I'm pretty sure it's 'Remember Dobreva and Abadanaiz'.
Zh: “那好吧。我很确定是‘为了多布列娃和阿巴达奈斯’。”

En: But you're not going to tell me, are you?
Zh: “但你不会告诉我，对吧？”

En: That's really dumb. You should feel embarrassed even suggesting that.
Zh: “这可真够笨的。你甚至应该为提出这样的建议而感到尴尬。”

En: I was *totally* about to say that.
Zh: “我*正*要这么说。”

En: Oh, right. Good thinking, Kim.
Zh: “哦，对。好想法，金。”

En: Wait, what phrase?
Zh: “等等，哪句话？”

En: You're right. How *foolish* of me. Won't happen again, detective.
Zh: “你说得对。我真*傻*。下次不会了，警探。”

En: Of course you were, detective. Apologies for interrupting. The lieutenant smiles to himself.
Zh: “当然，警探。抱歉打扰了。”警督自嘲地笑了笑。

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: The lieutenant consults his notebook. "'Remember Dobreva and Abadanaiz'."
Zh: 警督查阅他的笔记本。“‘为了多布列娃和阿巴达奈斯’。”

En: Guess this is what happens when two pigs put their heads together... She seems slightly disappointed.
Zh: “想必这就是两头猪猡凑在一起的结果吧……。”她似乎有点失望。

En: No, you weren't. Otherwise you *would have* said it. In truth, it was quite beyond your associative powers.
Zh: 不，你没有。否则你*会*说出来的。事实上，这已经完全超出了你的联想能力。

En: Intra-piggy conflict. Very cool... For a moment her eyes appear to light up.
Zh: “猪猡之间的冲突。很酷……”一瞬间，她的眼睛似乎亮了起来。

En: Now, let's see if you're half as dedicated a communist as you say you are...
Zh: “现在，让我们看看你关于自己是个康米主义者的说辞有多少是真的……”

En: That's enough. Off with you then. She makes a lazy shooing motion with her dangling hand.
Zh: “这就够了。你走吧。”她用悬空的手做了一个懒洋洋的撵人动作。

En: Huh. I guess this little piggy has done his legwork after all... She sounds slightly disappointed.
Zh: “哈，我猜这只小猪猡还是做了调查……”她的声音有些失望。

En: Why would I? That would be so boring.
Zh: “我为什么要这样做呢？那就太无聊了。”

En: Why would I? That would be so boring.
Zh: “我为什么要这样做呢？那就太无聊了。”

En: Now, let's see if you're half as dedicated a communist as you say you are...
Zh: “现在，让我们看看你关于自己是个康米主义者的说辞有多少是真的……”

En: That's enough. Off with you, then. She makes a lazy shooing motion with her dangling hand.
Zh: “这就够了。你走吧。”她用悬空的手做了一个懒洋洋的撵人动作。

En: Good fucking god, Mack. What is that smell?
Zh: “妈的，麦克。那是什么味道？”

En: What, like you've never reheated leftovers in the break room? He skewers the greasy sausage with a small fork and removes it to a limp paper plate.
Zh: “怎么，说得像你从来没有在休息室里热过剩菜一样？”他用小叉子把油腻腻的香肠串起来，然后把它移到一个软绵绵的纸盘上。

En: Hey, if you wanna dress that up, I think there's still that old bottle of mustard logged in evidence... He's already laughing at his own joke-in-progress.
Zh: “嘿，如果你想装点一下，我想起那瓶被记录为证物的旧芥末还在……”他已经被自己正在讲的笑话逗乐了。

En: What, you mean the one Vic yoinked off...
Zh: “什么，你是说维克甩掉的……”

En: ... that hobo in front of the Frittte? Hahaha!
Zh: “……那个在弗利多面前的流浪汉？哈哈哈……”

En: Keep listening.
Zh: 继续听。

En: (Turn to Cindy.) "Okay, then. Will you help me find the communists?"
Zh: （转向辛迪）“好吧，那么你能帮我找到那群康米主义者吗？”

En: Now you got to ask yourself, Mack. Are you desperate enough for *hobo mustard*?
Zh: “现在你得问问自己，麦克。你真的很想很想吃*流浪汉芥末*吗？”

En: Torson chews thoughtfully at his sausage for some time. "Hey, Chester, speaking of... do you think Harry ever solved his money situation, or do you think he's gone full hobocop?"
Zh: 托尔森若有所思地咀嚼着他的香肠好一阵子。“嘿，切斯特，说到……你觉得哈里有没有解决他的财务问题，还是说他已经完全是个流浪警探了？”

En: Torson chews thoughtfully at his sausage for some time. "Hey, Chester, I've been wondering... do you think anybody's ever gone full hobocop?"
Zh: 托尔森若有所思地咀嚼着他的香肠好一阵子。“嘿，切斯特，我一直在想……你觉得有人试过全程做流浪警探吗？”

En: You mean, like, is he out there right now, sleeping in a storm drain, chasin' down rats for leads?
Zh: “你是说，比如，他现在是不是在外面晃悠，睡在雨水沟里，追着老鼠找线索？”

En: Like, *really* hobocopped? Nah, that stuff's just an urban myth, Headless FALN Rider-level stuff...
Zh: “就像，*真正*的流浪警探？不，那只是个都市神话，无头FALN骑士级别的东西……”

En: Yeah, I could see it. He's always been weird about money stuff.
Zh: “是啊，我看的出来。他对金钱的态度总是很奇怪。”

En: He chuckles. "Doesn't mean Harry isn't givin' it a good try, though."
Zh: 他笑着说。“但这并不意味着哈里不会好好尝试……”

En: Okay, Cindy. Does that mean you'll help me find the communists?
Zh: “好吧，辛迪。这是不是说你会帮我找到那群康米主义者？”

En: It's your lucky day! I'm looking to acquire some original art by the *crème de la crème*.
Zh: “今天是你的幸运日！我想收购一些*名家手笔*的原创艺术品。”

En: I'm looking to acquire some original art. Do you have anything?
Zh: “我正在收购一些原创的艺术品。你有什么作品吗？”

En: What's that? The piggy's looking to get into the art market? Her eyes light up. "Watch out or you'll be auctioned off and face the chop!" She draws a line across her throat with a thumb.
Zh: “那是什么？猪猡想进入艺术市场？”她眼睛一亮。“小心点，不然你会被拍卖掉，然后被宰！”她用拇指在喉咙上画了一条线。

En: The lieutenant straightens his back, waiting to see where this is going.
Zh: 警督挺直了背，等着看事态的发展。

En: The *happening*, it's happening!!
Zh: 是*即兴艺术表演*，要发来了！！

En: Are we witnessing a performance, perhaps? Play along.
Zh: 也许我们是在看一场演出？那就演下去吧。

En: Relax, it's all part of her *image*.
Zh: 放松，这都是她*形象*的一部分。

En: No one speaks to a police officer in that tone. Show the little missy here her place.
Zh: 没人会用这种语气跟警察说话的。让这个小屁孩明白她的斤两。

En: She stares at you, fearlessly, waiting for a response.
Zh: 她无所畏惧地盯着你，等待回应。

En: (Step back.) "Is this a... happening?"
Zh: (退后一步) “这是在……什么即兴艺术表演吗？”

En: I'm not a pig. I am *The Law*. Show me what you're selling or I will shake you down.
Zh: “我不是猪猡。我就是*法律*。让我看看你在卖什么，否则我就教训你。”

En: Perfect, this is all very *on-brand* for you. Love that little persona you've created.
Zh: “完美，这一切都很*符合*你的范儿。很喜欢你创造的那个小人物形象。”

En: (Pretend you're a pig stuck in a cage.) "Help, let me out. I don't want to DIE!"
Zh: (假装你是一头困在笼子里的猪) “救命啊，放我出去。我不想死！”

En: This 'little persona' will grind you into a sausage if you don't stop. I'm not a sellout.
Zh: “如果你不停止的话，这个‘小人物’会把你磨成香肠的。我不是商品。”

En: Don't come at me with that. You've got nuthin' on me. Not a pork scratching.
Zh: “别用这套来对付我。你对我没辙。不过是猪肉挠痒痒。”

En: I don't do 'happenings', I like to leave a *mark*. She looks at you as if she's ready to wound you, then stares at the red paint running down the wall.
Zh: “我不做即兴艺术，我喜欢留下*痕迹*。”她看着你，好像准备要弄伤你，然后盯着墙上流下的红漆。

En: Whoa! Calm it with that shit, cop. I don't have any art you can buy. Geez.
Zh: “哇哦！冷静点吧，条子。我没有你想买的艺术品。天哪。”

En: No, we will not shake anyone down today, the lieutenant clears up, his voice calm and measured.
Zh: “不，我们今天不会勒索任何人。”警督清了清嗓子，声音平静而慎重。

En: Words are one thing, but he's not letting you follow through with it.
Zh: 说的是一回事，但他不会让你贯彻到底的。

En: Fine. Whatever. I'll let it slide, but only because I'm interested in your art.
Zh: “好吧，随便你。我就不追究了，不过这只是因为我对你的艺术感兴趣。”

En: Too bad, pork sausage, I don't sell my art. The whole point of graffito being on the street is so *everyone* can see it. No charge -- expression for all.
Zh: “太糟糕了，猪猡香肠，我不卖我的作品。涂鸦在大街上的意义就是让*所有人*都能看到。不收费——为了所有人而作。”

En: The whole point of graffito being on the street is so *everyone* can see it. No charge -- expression for all.
Zh: “涂鸦在大街上的意义就是让*所有人*都能看到。不收费——为了所有人而作。”

En: There has to be a way to convince her... Everyone has a price.
Zh: 一定有办法说服她…… 每个人都有一个价位。

En: Forget this *public* art. She must have something private and esoteric. Something that tormented her until she brought it into existence.
Zh: 忘掉*公共*艺术吧。她一定有一些私密而深奥的东西。一些折磨她的东西，直到她将它摆到台面变成现实。

En: But how does she make a living? How does she keep a roof over her head? It must be hard.
Zh: 但她是如何谋生的？她是如何解决温饱的？一定很辛苦。

En: I bet it's all about the scene for her. Tell her you can introduce her to some artsy jet-setters in Saint-Batiste. Maybe do some lines together in the powder room.
Zh: 我猜她一定是为了自己的戏份。告诉她，你可以把她介绍给圣巴蒂斯特的一些大腕。也许可以在化妆室里一起说几句话。

En: I'm after something a little more challenging. I have an Actual Art Degree, after all.
Zh: “我想找些更有挑战性的工作。毕竟我有一个正经的艺术学位。”

En: Don't you have anything personal? Show me your most conceptual *chef-d'oeuvre*.
Zh: “你有没有个人点的作品？给我看看你最概念化的*名作*吧。”

En: If all your art is free, how do you make a living?
Zh: “如果你的艺术都是免费的，你怎么谋生？”

En: If you sell me a piece, I can introduce you to some people. Free wine, celebrities, some sniff...
Zh: “如果你卖给我一件作品，我可以给你介绍一些人。免费的酒，名人，一些记……”

En: Alright, quit playing, tell me the price. I can make you rich and successful.
Zh: “好了，不玩了，告诉我价格。我可以让你发财，让你成功。”

En: I thought you were a pig? She squashes her nose with a thumb so it resembles a snout.
Zh: “我还以为你是猪呢？”她用大拇指捏住鼻子模仿猪鼻的样子。

En: I don't do 'personal', if that's what you sickos are after. No navel-gazing pieces about what it means to be 'me'.
Zh: “我不做‘私作’，如果这就是你们这些变态想要的话。我也没有什么关于成为‘我’意味着什么的自恋狂文章。”

En: You call this a living? She gestures to her surroundings. "Look around you, hog-man, we're all bleeding out on the slab."
Zh: “你把这叫做谋生？”她指了指周围的环境。“看看你周围，猪猡，我们都在地上流血等死呢。”

En: If I wanted to whore myself out for some *Nasenbonbon*, I'd go to Porta Rosa. Or better yet, I'd take you with me and pimp you out to some friends of mine.
Zh: “如果我想为一些*甜头*而去出卖自己的肉体，我会去玻尔塔·罗萨。或者更好的是，我会带着你一起去，找我的朋友帮你拉皮条。”

En: I don't have a price. If I wanted to whore myself out, I'd go to Porta Rosa. Or better yet, I'd take you with me and pimp you out to some friends of mine.
Zh: “我没有什么价位。如果我想出卖自己的肉体，我会去玻尔塔·罗萨。或者更好的是，我会带着你一起去，找我的朋友帮你拉皮条。”

En: They'd be delighted to get their hands on a squealing pink thing like you, she hisses. "Cut off your ears, make some pig soup out of them. Now sod off! You've got nothing to offer that would make me change my mind."
Zh: “有你这么一个吱吱叫的粉红色的玩意儿一起玩，他们会很高兴的”她嘶声说道。“割掉你的耳朵做猪肉汤。现在给我滚！我是不会改变主意的。”

En: No one cares, I know I don't. So sod off, pig! You've got nothing to change my mind.
Zh: “没有人在乎，我知道我不在乎。所以，滚吧，猪猡！我是不会改变主意的。”

En: This place might be rotten, but at least it's hers.
Zh: 这个地方可能很烂，但至少是她的地盘。

En: No, I'm an Art Cop. I chase down works of lazy derivation in the heated battle for original aesthetics.
Zh: “不，我是艺术警察。我在激烈的原创美学之战中追捕那些懒惰的衍生作品。”

En: Yes, I'm a pig, but only if we decide to deconstruct what it *means* to be a pig.
Zh: “是的，我是一头猪，但前提是我们决定解构做一头猪的*意义*何在。”

En: Both police work and art criticism require analytical talent and informed judgement calls.
Zh: “警察工作和艺术批评都需要分析才能和明智的判断力。”

En: But you feel at home here, right? Besides, there's nowhere else to go.
Zh: “但你在这里感觉很自在，对吗？再说，你也没有别的地方可去。”

En: One day something will come along and pull you out of the muck.
Zh: “总有一天，会有什么东西来把你从泥潭里拉出来。”

En: I'm sorry to hear that. I can only imagine the horrors you've seen.
Zh: “听到这个消息我很难过。我只能想象你所看到的恐怖。”

En: Ooh, little piggy knows the big words. What a waste of a perfectly good saucisson, stuffing it full of artspeak.
Zh: “噢，小猪猡懂大道理。真是浪费了好好的香肠，塞满了书呆子的脑袋。”

En: Sorry, even with your fancy degree, you've got nothing to offer me.
Zh: “对不起，即使你有花哨的学历，你也没有什么可以给我的。”

En: A strong gust of wind knocks into her. Then she shouts: "So what, pig? You gonna mock me now? I don't need this."
Zh: 一阵强风吹向她。然后她喊道：“那又怎样，猪猡？你现在要嘲笑我吗？我不需要。”

En: What, like your feeble business offer? Are you going to be the turning point in my shitty life story? A little fairy from above?
Zh: “什么，就像你那微不足道的商业提案？你要成为我糟糕人生故事的转折点吗？天上来的小仙女？”

En: 'I can only imagine the horrors,' she mocks you in a squeaky voice. "I'm not your Channel 8 charity case. Sod off! No one here needs saving."
Zh: “‘我只能想象你所看到的恐怖’，”她用尖细的声音嘲笑你。“我不是你们第八频道的慈善案例。滚吧！这里没有人需要拯救。”

En: Nearby, a hoarse bark barrels through an alleyway that sits between two monstrous squats. No one walks down there anymore, but the nostrils of those who pass by in wet weather still detect the sickly scent of gasohol and burnt hair.
Zh: 附近，一条位于两栋畸形的违建建筑之间的一条小巷内，一阵嘶哑的狗吠声穿过。现在没有人再到那里去了，但在潮湿的天气里，经过这里的人的鼻孔仍然能闻到汽油醇和烧焦的头发的恶心气味。

En: Let me in. Let me hear what you have to say.
Zh: “让我进去吧。让我听听你有什么话要说。”

En: (Don't push her.) "Okay, okay. I'll back off."
Zh: (别逼她) “好，好。我退下了。”

En: Fuck outta here, she bursts out laughing. "This 'let's get through this together' type of stuff doesn't work on me. Especially if it's coming from a pig!"
Zh: “滚开。”她笑出声来。“这种‘让我们一起度过难关’的鸡汤对我没用。尤其是从一头猪猡的嘴里说出来的时候！”

En: Good call, piggo. She smiles to herself, satisfied.
Zh: “说得好，猪哥。”她自顾自地笑着，很满意。

En: Convince her to show you her art.
Zh: 说服她给你看她的艺术作品。

En: Actual Art Degree.
Zh: 正经艺术学位。

En: Art Cop known.
Zh: 已知艺术警察。

En: Hail the mother-wall!
Zh: 母墙万岁！

En: Wompty-Dompty Dom.
Zh: 三教九流中心。

En: Struggling artist.
Zh: 挣扎的艺术家。

En: Snappy pitch.
Zh: 简短的提议。

En: Dresscode: Pseudo-SKULL.
Zh: 着装要求：伪骷髅头。

En: Sure, she's all draped up in ironic detachment, but everyone's gotta eat.
Zh: 当然，她是用讽刺的超然将自己包裹的严严实实，但每个人都得吃饭。

En: I do have something to offer in exchange for your art. Money.
Zh: “我确实有东西可以用来交换你的艺术作品。钱。”

En: Great, the little foray into the art world continues, murmurs the lieutenant, looking up.
Zh: “太好了，进军艺术界的小尝试还在继续。”警督抬头喃喃自语。

En: Blah-blah. I already told you, I'm not for sale, piggo. She looks almost disappointed by your lousy offer.
Zh: “巴拉巴拉。我已经告诉过你了，我不卖，猪哥。” 她看起来几乎对你糟糕的提议感到失望。

En: Blah-blah. I'm not for sale, piggo. She looks almost disappointed by your lousy offer.
Zh: “巴拉巴拉，我不卖，猪哥。”她看起来几乎对你糟糕的提议感到失望。

En: Turn it around -- it's the seller who wields the power in this relationship.
Zh: 扭转过来——在这段关系中，掌握权力的是卖方。

En: Really? You're just going to let go of a chance to milk some stupid piggo rolling in cash?
Zh: “真的吗？你打算放弃一个可以榨取一些在钱里打滚的蠢猪的机会？”

En: I don't care what you sell me and I don't care what you do with your money. It's your choice.
Zh: “我不在乎你卖给我什么，我也不在乎你用你的钱做什么。这是你自己的选择。”

En: Uh-oh, you're manipulating me! She sounds almost fascinated by this discovery. "I do like a chance to rip off some stupid piggos. And I need a pack of cigarettes. And some paint."
Zh: “呃哦，你在操纵我！”她听起来几乎被这个发现迷住了。“我确实喜欢有机会骗一些蠢猪的钱。而且我要一包香烟。还有一些颜料。”

En: Okay, whatever, piggy. Time to leech off each other like the parasites we are. Let me see what I've got... She reaches into her heavy coat and pulls out a small piece of plastic coated thick with emulsion.
Zh: “好吧，随便你，猪猡。是时候像寄生虫一样互相吸血了。我看看有什么……”她把手伸进厚厚的大衣里，拿出一小块涂了厚厚颜料的塑料板。

En: Even the lieutenant leans in closer to inspect the work.
Zh: 连警督也凑近来观看了。

En: Here. Happy? The plastic looks sad and damaged, as if it's been ripped out of some bigger piece. A glitzy barnacle of colours covers its surface.
Zh: “给你。开心吗？”这块塑料看起来很悲伤和破烂不堪，好像是从某个更大的碎片上撕下来的。斑斓的色彩像藤壶一样盖住表面。

En: Interesting. You can immediately see multiple ways to interpret this piece. What is the *experience* that the artist is trying to create? What *bodies* and *spaces* are they seeking to explore?
Zh: 有趣。你马上可以想出了多种方式来解释这个作品。艺术家想要创造的*体验*是什么？他们想要探索的*形体*和*空间*是什么?

En: It looks like a palette. Nothing more than a board for mixing paint.
Zh: 它看起来像个调色板。无非是一块混合颜料的板子。

En: Remember, this is a *buyer's* market. You're the one who's in charge of pricing.
Zh: 记住，这是一个*买方的*市场。你才是负责定价的人。

En: Come on! Isn't there any other way I can prove myself worthy?
Zh: “快说啊！难道我就没有别的办法来证明自己的价值吗？”

En: She's locked up tight. What does she want? Maybe it's as simple as asking her. But whine about it so she feels superior.
Zh: 她被紧紧锁住了。她到底想要什么？也许问她就可以了。但是发发牢骚，可以让她觉得自己很优越。

En: Squeal. Then maybe we can talk business.
Zh: “尖叫一声。然后我们再谈正事。”

En: (Turn to the lieutenant.) "Kim, you do it."
Zh: (转向警督) “金，你来吧。”

En: (Squeal, but only a little.) "Reeeh."
Zh: (尖叫，但只有一点点) “噫。”

En: (Squeal a moderate amount.) "Reeehk, reeehk!"
Zh: (适量地尖叫)“噫，噫！”

En: (Squeal ignominiously.) "*Reeehk!* *Reeehk!* *Reeehk!*"
The lieutenant remains perfectly still except to give you a devastating case of side-eye.
Zh: 警督除了无可救药地瞥了你一眼之外，完全不动。

En: Not in a million years.
Zh: 再过一百万年都不会。

En: Not him -- you. I want *you* to squeal for me, pork chop.
Zh: “不是他——是你。我要*你*为我尖叫，猪猡。”

En: Come on! You really need to bring home the bacon. She chuckles.
Zh: “拜托！你真的要这东西吗？”她笑着说。

En: Do I need to come over there and pull on your curly tail? Squeal already! She grins with power.
Zh: “要我过去拉着你的猪尾巴吗？这就叫了！”她笑得很大声。

En: Good little piglet, she says with words coated in butter.
Zh: “乖小猪。”她说话的口气像是涂满了黄油。

En: No way. Forget it. (Chicken out.)
Zh: “不可能。算了吧。”(退缩)

En: Awww, c'mon! I was just starting to have some fun.
Zh: “啊，拜托！我才刚开始觉得有点意思了。”

En: Humiliation wasn't enough for her? How about you offer her something -- like a job?
Zh: 羞辱对她来说还不够？不如你给她一些东西——比如一份工作？

En: What does she need? You don't have much to give her. Hmm, you could try offering her a job.
Zh: 她需要什么？你给她的东西不多。嗯，你可以试着给她一份工作。

En: How would you like a job? I can get you one. A real job with a wage!
Zh: “你想要一份工作吗？我可以给你一份。一份真正的有工资的工作！”

En: Fuck-a-doodle-doo! She stifles an outburst of laughter. "And what job would that be? Cutting you into rashers?"
Zh: “去你妈的吧！”她忍不住笑出声来。“那会是什么工作呢？把你切成薄片？”

En: I want to see your painting again.
Zh: “我想再看看你的画。”

En: Sure, piggo. She reaches into her heavy coat and pulls out a small piece of plastic coated thick with emulsion.
Zh: “当然，猪猡。”她把手伸进厚厚的大衣里，拿出一小块涂了厚厚颜料的塑料板。

En: Show it to me. (Study it closer.)
Zh: “给我看看。”(仔细研究)

En: Would you describe this as one of your signature pieces?
Zh: “你会不会说这是你的标志性作品之一？”

En: Let's talk about the price again.
Zh: “我们再来谈谈价格吧。”

En: Let's come back to this later.
Zh: “我们之后再来谈这个。”

En: She juts out her hand, letting you examine the piece. Three red splotches have been shot at a diagonal across the surface. Tinier spots of various colours fan out from the same point of origin.
Zh: 她伸出手，让你审视这件作品。表面上有三个红色斑点呈对角线状。不同颜色的小点从同一个原点呈扇形散开。

En: Well, piggo, what do you make of it? She sounds almost mocking, waiting for your reaction.
Zh: “好了，猪猡，你怎么看？ ”她听起来几乎是在嘲笑，等待你的反应。

En: There's tension in these strokes of genius, a desire to step into a dialogue with the viewer, to provoke them. A meditation on... *Sein und Nichtsein*?
Zh: 这些天才的笔触中充满了张力，一种与观众对话的欲望，激发观众的渴望。反思……*生存还是毁灭*？

En: As said before, it's a palette. Nothing to see here.
Zh: 我之前就说过，这是个调色板。没什么好看的。

En: It looks like a palette. Nothing more than a board for mixing paint.
Zh: 它看起来像个调色板。无非是一块混合颜料的板子。

En: The angle of the paint drops reveals that they were fired at the canvas with the force of a hand cannon.
Zh: 从颜料滴落的角度可以看出，它们是以手炮的力量向画布发射的。

En: It's one of those ink-blot tests. An invitation to connect with the *divine*.
Zh: 这就是某种墨迹测试。一个与*神灵*联系的邀请。

En: You're exploring the liminal space between being and unbeing. A cunning juxtaposition.
Zh: “你在探索存在与不存在之间的边缘空间。巧妙的并置。”

En: It's just a mixing palette.
Zh: “这只是块调色板。”

En: Someone got killed here. A five millimetre calibre, point-blank range.
Zh: “有人在这里被杀了。五毫米口径的子弹，近距离射击。”

En: I don't really have an opinion.
Zh: “我真的没有什么意见。”

En: Yeah, whatever, Art Cop. A wide smile crosses her face. "Spaces, bodies, shit. Hit me with that lingo!"
Zh: “是啊，随便了，艺术警察。”她脸上露出灿烂的笑容。“空间，形体，狗屎。用这些行话来对我说教吧！”

En: Yeah, whatever. A wide smile crosses her face. "Spaces, bodies, shit. Hit me with that lingo!"
Zh: “是啊，随便了。”她脸上露出灿烂的笑容。“空间，形体，狗屎。用这些行话来对我说教吧！”

En: Damn. She's almost ready to abandon the entire endeavour, but then changes her mind. "Well, it's also *art*. It's a conceptual illusion. Both the tool and the outcome."
Zh: “该死。”她几乎准备放弃这一切，但随后又改变了主意。“嗯，这也是*艺术*。这是一种概念上的幻觉。无论是工具还是结果。”

En: Tool? Maybe she's onto something after all. Athletes are simultaneously tools and artists -- bodies chiselled to a singular purpose.
Zh: 工具？也许她终究还是有所发现。运动员既是工具，也是艺术家——他们的身体为了一个非凡的目的雕琢而成。

En: Yes, my artistic integrity. It's impossible to tell whether she's being serious or not.
Zh: “是的，我的艺术完整性。” 无法判断她是认真的还是不认真的。

En: Of course you don't. Why do I even bother? Just cough up the money!
Zh: “你当然没有。我为什么要费心呢？只要把钱掏出来就行了！”

En: She is. Despite the demeanour, playing this game brings no joy to her. She cares about art and her private relationship to it.
Zh: 她是认真的。尽管举止得体，但玩这个游戏并没有给她带来乐趣。她在乎的是艺术和她与艺术的私人关系。

En: *Signature*? Oh yeah, pigster. I sneezed while holding a wet brush. Paint flecked onto the canvas, and voila. You don't get more signature than that.
Zh: “*标志性*？哦，对了，猪头。我拿着湿刷子打了个喷嚏。颜料飞溅到画布上，然后就有了。没有比这更标志性的了。”

En: So you painted this with a sneeze? Truly remarkable... a clear sign of a *genius*!
Zh: “所以你打个喷嚏就画了这个？真是了不起……这是‘天才’的明显标志！”

En: That's disgusting.
Zh: “太恶心了。”

En: An exquisite commentary on the precious serendipity of the creative process.
Zh: “对创作过程中珍贵的偶然性的精妙评论。”

En: Interesting. What I really want to know is -- what do you call this new style?
Zh: “有意思。我真正想知道的是——你如何称呼这种新风格？”

En: (Tickle the longest of your nasal hairs.)
Zh: (挠挠你最长的鼻毛。)

En: That's what I said, piglet.
Zh: “我就是这么说的，猪猡。”

En: Says you. She looks you up and down.
Zh: “你说的。”她上下打量着你。

En: If you say so. I like to think that my trigeminal nerve painted it.
Zh: “如果你这么说的话。我喜欢认为是我的三叉神经画的。”

En: Art-ishoo.
Zh: “问题艺术。”

En: How much do you want for it?
Zh: “你想要多少钱？”

En: What about it? She tilts her head, waiting for your offer.
Zh: “怎么样？”她仰着头，等待你的报价。

En: Sixty seven thousand reál. And your head on a silver tray.
Zh: “六万七千雷亚尔。还有你的脑袋砍下来放在银盘里呈过来。”

En: Remember, this isn't about the art or the artist -- this needless purchase implies your *excessive wealth*. Art comes across as more tasteful than simply hanging cash on the wall.
Zh: 记住，这与艺术或艺术家无关——这种不必要的购买意味着你拥有“过多的财富”。艺术给人的印象比简单地把钞票挂在墙上更有品位。

En: I can part with 50 reál.
Zh: “我可以给你50雷亚尔。”

En: I'll pay 25 reál.
Zh: “我出25雷亚尔。”

En: How about 10 reál?
Zh: “10雷亚尔怎么样？”

En: I have more questions about the painting.
Zh: “我对这作品还有更多的疑问。”

En: Suit yourself. She tucks away the painting and closes her coat. You hear waves breaking on the nearby shore.
Zh: “随你便吧。”她把画收起来，合上外套。你听到附近海岸上的海浪声。

En: Not bad! I guess I'll take it. She holds back her excitement as she gives you the painting.
Zh: “还不错啊！那就成交吧。”她忍住兴奋，把画给你。

En: Alright. Can't complain about that. You've got yourself a deal. She gives you the painting.
Zh: “好吧。这没什么好抱怨的。就这么定了。”她把画给了你。

En: That it? Alright. It all helps. She gives you the painting.
Zh: “就这些？好吧。总归有点用。”她把画了给你。

En: Ask away.
Zh: “问吧。”

En: She may antagonize you but she's still a struggling artist with no prospects. She *needs* as much money as you can give.
Zh: 她可能会激怒你，但她仍然是一个没有前途的，苦苦挣扎的艺术家。你能给她多少钱，她就需要多少钱。

En: Lowball her. The wretched are used to rolling with the punches.
Zh: 给她开低价。悲哀的人都习惯了随波逐流。

En: Is that all she thinks it takes to be an artist? Outperform her with her own style. Make it *yours*.
Zh: 她以为做艺术家很简单吗？用其人之道还治其人之身。把她的风格变成*你的*。

En: Splendid! Your first physical asset. But no time to stop -- money grows old unless it keeps moving. You need to find a *buyer*, preferably someone who will pay a high price for such an *exclusive* piece from a local artist.
Zh: 太好了！你的第一个物质资产。但没有时间停滞不前——钱不流动就会变老。你需要找到一个*买家*，最好是愿意为当地艺术家的这种*独家*作品支付高价的人。

En: The Whirling-in-Rags just lost one of its staff.
Zh: “褴褛飞旋刚跑了一个员工。”

En: (Point at Joyce.) "*She* could employ you. She works for the people who own the harbour."
Zh: （指着乔伊斯） “*她*可以雇用你。她为海港的所有者工作。”

En: I know the leader of the Union. Bet they need an extra hand around the docks.
Zh: “我认识工会的领导。我打赌码头那边需要帮手。”

En: You could work behind the counter at the local Frittte? Seems easy enough.
Zh: “你可以在当地弗利多的柜台后面工作？似乎很容易。”

En: The guy who owns the pawnshop could probably find you some odd jobs to do.
Zh: “当铺的老板大概能给你找些零活做。”

En: You could work for me! Be a police officer.
Zh: “你可以为我工作！当个警察。”

En: She hisses at the boat rocking gently on the coastal waves. "She'd flay my skin. Wear me like a mink the first chance she got." A mad grin spreads across her face. "Unless I got to her first."
Zh: 她对着在随波轻轻摇晃的船嘶吼着。“她会剥了我的皮。一有机会就把我干掉当貂皮大衣穿。”她的脸上洋溢着疯狂的笑容。“除非我先干掉她。”

En: Oh really? Her eyes flash like stolen coins under moonlight. "Good to know. That makes even easier pickings."
Zh: “哦，真的吗？”她的眼睛在月光下像偷来的硬币一样闪烁。“挺好的。那就更容易被闯空门了。”

En: Oh, I *know* they need an extra hand. Especially after that cargo went mysteriously missing last week.
Zh: “哦，我*知道*他们需要一个帮手。尤其是上周那批货神秘失踪之后。”

En: You think he'd allow me back into his store? No chance. Not unless he wanted to lose his stock all of a sudden.
Zh: “你认为他会让我回到他的店里吗？不可能的。除非他想一下子存货全不见。”

En: I think you mean *boring*. She scowls. "Can't anyway. Technically, I'm banned. Not that it's ever stopped me getting my cigs."
Zh: “我想你的意思是*无聊*。” 她皱起眉头。“反正不能。技术上讲，我被禁止进入。尽管根本阻止不了我从那边顺到烟抽。”

En: We have a policy that means we cannot hire any SKULL members onto the force.
Zh: “我们有一项政策，意味着我们不能雇佣任何骷髅帮成员入队。”

En: Me -- a brickman? I haven't got the snout necessary to snuffle around your filthy yard. She spits out her tongue.
Zh: “我——一个砌砖的？我还没那么灵敏的鼻子在你那脏兮兮的院子里嗅来嗅去。” 她吐了吐舌头。

En: Is that all you've got, piggy? Looks like the job market has failed me *once again*. Wind howls between the nearby apartment blocks. "No job, no art. What a pity."
Zh: “你就这点本事吗，猪猡？看来就业市场*又一次*让我失望了。”附近的公寓楼之间风声呼啸。“没有工作，没有作品。真可惜。”

En: Do you know something about that? asks the lieutenant. His question goes ignored.
Zh: “你知道些什么吗？”警督问。他的问题被忽略了。

En: Try as you might, you just can't fathom what a young rebel dressed like a granny would want from you. She certainly isn't a flowers and chocolate kinda girl.
Zh: 尽管你很努力，但你就是无法理解一个穿着像老奶奶一样的叛逆年轻人想从你这里得到什么。她肯定不是一个想要鲜花和巧克力的那种女孩。

En: Cindy picks the dirt out of her fingernails as you stand there, quiet and clueless. Without looking up, she says: "Ask something or piss off, swine."
Zh: 辛迪抠着指甲缝里的泥，而你站在那里，沉默不语，毫无头绪。她没有抬头，就说：“有话就问，不然就滚蛋，猪猡。”

En: It's a sign! Better pay up or you'll anger the terrible beast from... what did you say? She knows -- she just wants to ham it up. "The *threshold*. Careful now, I heard it has a soft spot for smoked pork."
Zh: “这是预兆！最好付钱，否则你会激怒那只可怕的野兽，从……你说什么？”她知道——她只是想装腔作势。“*门槛*。小心点，我听说它对熏猪肉情有独钟。”

En: Hmph, her improv skills could do with some work. That was a *pathetic* performance. Are we supposed to be scared? NONE OF US ARE SCARED!
Zh: 哼，她的即兴表演技巧还可以再练练。演的太*垃圾*了。我们应该害怕吗？我们谁都不害怕！

En: What's that? The piggy's looking to get into the art market with a snappy business pitch? Her eyes light up. "Watch out or you'll be auctioned off and face the chop!" She draws a line across her throat with a thumb.
Zh: “那是什么？猪猡想用抢手的商业广告进入艺术市场？” 她眼睛一亮。“小心点，不然你会被拍卖掉，面临被宰杀的命运！”她用拇指在喉咙上划了一下。

En: Sure, any time. Us *shit* artists have to help one another. She looks down at the wall and frowns. "Besides, I was out of fuel oil anyway."
Zh: “当然，随时可以。我们*狗屁*艺术家必须互帮互助。”她低头看着墙壁，皱紧眉头。“再说，反正我也没燃油了。”

En: I'm sorry, but I'm going to have to ask you again: Give me the brush, please.
Zh: “很抱歉，我必须再要求你一次：请把刷子给我。”

En: You know what you've got in that fuel canister you scavenged from your Kineema? Red-dyed heavy fuel oil. Time to get to *proper* work, artiste.
Zh: 你知道能从锐影的燃油罐里抽出什么来吗？红色的重燃油。是时候做点*正经*事了，艺术家。

En: Oops, my apologies. I guess I was trying too hard...
Zh: 哎呀，抱歉。我想我是努力过头了……

En: You're a police officer and a *grown up*. Why are you trying to impress her? Maybe try a different approach?
Zh: 你是一名警官，也是一个*成年人*。你为什么要给她留下什么好印象？也许可以换个别的方法？

En: You're a police officer and a *grown up*. Why are you trying to impress her? Maybe try a different approach? Like climbing up there...
Zh: 你是一名警官，也是一个*成年人*。你为什么要打动她？也许可以换个别的方法？比如爬上去……

En: So you won't talk about the murder -- but maybe you can tell us something about the murder victim's missing armour?
Zh: “所以你是不会谈论凶杀案的事了——不过也许你可以跟说说凶杀案被害人不见的盔甲？”

En: So you won't talk about the murder -- but maybe you can tell me something about the murder victim's missing armour?
Zh: “所以你是不会谈论凶杀案的事了——不过也许你可以跟说说凶杀案被害人不见的盔甲？”

En: What do I care about some fucking tin egg shells?
Zh: “我他妈为什么要在乎那些该死的薄鸡蛋壳啊？”

En: C'mon Cindy, just help me out here.
Zh: “拜托，辛迪，帮帮我吧。”

En: You might find armour useful if the streets flow red once more.
Zh: “如果街道再一次被染红的话，你也许就会觉得盔甲有用了。”

En: Ugh, alright, sad piggy. I'll give you this one -- I saw a little girl in the fishing village running around with military-grade hand-wear. Looked cute as hell.
Zh: “好吧，可怜的猪猡。我就告诉你吧——我看见渔村里有个小孩子戴着军事级的手套在到处游荡。看起来真是可爱的要死。”

En: Could've been her. Small kid with giant white armoured hands. If you've seen one of them you've seen them all.
Zh: “也许是她。一个戴着白色盔甲手套的小孩。如果你见过其中一个，那就算全见过了。”

En: Could that girl have been Little Lily? It's not a bad place to start.
Zh: 那个女孩会不会就是小莉莉？这是个不错的起点。

En: I know a little girl in the village -- Little Lily.
Zh: “我知道村子里那个小女孩——小莉莉。”

En: Isn't armour... art? Art for the, uhm, body?
Zh: “难道盔甲不是……艺术吗？呃，身体的艺术？”

En: If you haven't been there, the village is a shithole down the coast from the main plaza. She waves her hand in a general westward direction. "Have a good time."
Zh: “如果你还没去过，那个村子就在广场附近的海岸边，那里是个鬼地方。”她朝着西边挥了挥手。“玩得开心点啊。”

En: There weren't any other small girls in the village.
Zh: 村子里没有其他小女孩了。

En: She squints her eyes...
Zh: 她眯起眼睛……

En: Her lips move. "On fire?" she whispers to herself.
Zh: 她的嘴唇蠕动着。“着火？”她自言自语到。

En: I met your friend Steban.
Zh: “我见过你的朋友史蒂文。”

En: I watched you go in, pig. You aren't telling me anything new.
Zh: “我看着你进去的，猪猡。你没有告诉我任何新的东西。”

En: So did you two fancy each other?
Zh: “那你们俩是不是互相看上了？”

En: I don't know how to put it, but I think we discovered something extra-physical together.
Zh: “我不知道该怎么说，但我想我们在一起发现了一些物理外的东西。”

En: Well, we got into a little *physical altercation*, but I think he got over it.
Zh: “嗯，我们发生了一点*肢体冲突*，但我想他已经恢复过来了。”

En: He's a good comrade. Very advanced, ideologically speaking.
Zh: “他是个好同志。从思想上讲，很先进。”

En: Yeah, he's alright. (Shrug.)
Zh: “是啊，他还不错。”(耸耸肩)

En: At first I thought that was just Uli putting me on, but then I saw Steb's cheek. You gave him a right love tap. She seems to approve.
Zh: “起初我以为那只是Uli在骗我，但后来我看到了史蒂文的脸颊。你正面给了他一下爱的轻触。”她似乎很赞同。

En: Heh. He was cooler before he started palling about with pigs...
Zh: “呵。在他开始和猪猡打交道之前还更酷一点……”

En: God, you even talk the same now. Soon you'll be proofreading each other's homework...
Zh: “上帝，你现在连说话都一个模子出来的。很快你们就会互相校对作业了……”

En: Stop, pig. You're gonna make me heave...
Zh: “别说了，猪猡。你让我起鸡皮疙瘩了……”

En: Anyway, I told him you'd never go in for the posters. Shame. I had some really good ones all planned out.
Zh: “总之，我告诉他，你永远不会赞成海报的点子。真可惜。我都准备好一些很好的海报设计了。”

En: Anyway, at least you convinced him to put up the posters. Good to see the old king covered in proper slogans. I saw one old bird nearly faint coming out of the Frittte.
Zh: “不管怎么说，至少你说服他把海报贴出来了。很高兴看到老国王浑身都是正确的口号。我看见一只老鸟从弗利多出来时差点晕倒。”

En: That's fine. You don't need her to tell you, because you *already know it*.
Zh: 那就好。你不需要她告诉你，因为你*已经知道了*。

En: You should listen to your partner every once in a while. He's not so dim as you are.
Zh: “你应该偶尔听听你的伙伴的意见。他不像你这么笨。”

En: Nice vest, officer. Taking art classes now?
Zh: “马甲不错，长官。在上艺术课吗？”

En: Hello again, officers. Have you come to admire my mural?
Zh: “又见面了，警官们。你们是来欣赏我的壁画的吗？”

En: You seem pretty cool. Who are you?
Zh: “你看起来挺酷的。你是谁？”

En: You keep looking off to the side -- what are you looking at?
Zh: “你一直往旁边看——你在看什么？”

En: What are you doing to the wall?
Zh: “你对这面墙做了什么？”

En: Do you know anything about the recent murder?
Zh: “你知道最近凶杀案的事吗？”

En: I'm Cindy the fucking SKULL -- what else do you want to know? Date of birth? Blood type? The last time I was tested for Hep C?
Zh: “我是骷髅头辛迪——你还想知道什么？生日？血型？我最后一次测试丙型肝炎的时间？”

En: I ain't no snitch, pigstein. She wrinkles her nose. "Go forth and forage in someone else's shit. No shortage of squealers in these parts."
Zh: 她皱了皱鼻子。“我不是告密者，死条子。去找别人的麻烦吧。这个地方可不缺告密者。”

En: Let's start with your blood type and go from there.
Zh: “我们从你的血型开始，然后继续。”

En: We just want to know your name, little lady. No need to get defensive.
Zh: “我们只想知道你的名字，小淑女。没必要这么防备。”

En: I just want to know your name, little lady. No need to get defensive.
Zh: “我只想知道你的名字，小淑女。没必要这么防备。”

En: Just answer some questions okay?
Zh: “直接回答问题好吗？”

En: Will you look at that! He's not just a pig, he's also a dog. Ain't got no bones for you, doggie. Despite the sass, she puts the brush aside and turns to you.
Zh: “你看看那个！他不仅仅是一头猪，他还是一条狗。我这里可没有骨头给你，小狗狗。”尽管说话很无礼，她还是把刷子放在一边，转向了你。

En: And here I was trying to be polite. Just can't win with you pigs. Despite the sass, she puts the brush aside.
Zh: “我是想礼貌一点。就是拧不过你们这些条子。”尽管说话很无礼，她还是把刷子放在一边。

En: Ask away, pig man, but I don't promise to answer. Despite the sass, she puts the brush aside.
Zh: “问吧，死条子，我不保证会回答。”尽管说话很无礼，她还是把刷子放在一边。

En: Don't use that tone with me. I am the law.
Zh: “别用这种口气跟我说话。我就是法律。”

En: Sod off, pig man. Art is above the law. Despite the sass, she puts the brush aside.
Zh: “滚开，死条子。艺术是高于法律的。”尽管说话很无礼，她还是把刷子放在一边。

En: Go where? Accosting a minor?
Zh: “怎么继续？跟一个未成年人搭话吗？”

En: Listen to your partner, pig man. Keep your grubby hooves off little old ladies. Despite the attitude, she puts the brush aside.
Zh: “听听你的搭档话，死条子。继续把你的脏蹄子搭在那些小老太太身上吧。”尽管态度恶劣，她还是把刷子放在一边。

En: She turns her head to face the coast and nods disdainfully toward the woman performing maintenance on the boat docked next to the pier.
Zh: 她转过脸，面向海岸，轻蔑地朝着那边的女人点了点头，对方正在维修停靠在码头上的帆船。

En: She turns her head to face the coast and nods disdainfully toward Joyce, performing maintenance on her boat.
Zh: 她转过脸，面向海岸，轻蔑地朝着乔伊斯点了点头，她正在维修停靠在码头上的帆船。

En: She nods disdainfully toward the woman performing maintenance on the boat docked next to the pier.
Zh: 她轻蔑地朝着那边的女人点了点头，对方正在维修停靠在码头上的帆船。

En: She hisses: "That Ozonne whore. *Someone's* gotta keep an eye on her."
Zh: 她嘘了一声。“那个欧佐纳尔的婊子。*有人*应该盯着她一点。”

En: Can't you tell? I'm painting a beautiful mural. An aero-graffito visible from low orbit...
Zh: “你看不出来吗？我正在画一幅美丽的壁画呢。能从低轨道看见的飞空涂鸦。”

En: Pissf****t and Fuck the World send their best.
Zh: “尿兄和干弟向你问好。”

En: Catch you later, Cindy. [Leave.]
Zh: “回见，辛迪。”[离开。]

En: I haven't really started it yet. I'm waiting for the right words. She studies the wall, suddenly pensive.
Zh: 她研究着墙壁，突然陷入沉思。“其实我还没开始呢，我正在等一个好句子。”

En: I don't believe it. I've never known those boys to have manners.
Zh: “我不信。我从来不知道那群小子懂什么礼貌。”

En: I'm sorry for invading your personal space like this, but I'm going to take that paintbrush from you, kiddo.
Zh: “很抱歉这样侵入你的私人空间，不过我还是要拿走你的漆刷，小娃儿。”

En: I've got you cornered! Hand over that brush!
Zh: “我把你逼入绝境了！把刷子交出来！”

En: They seem to hold you in high esteem.
Zh: “他们好像很尊重你。”

En: I think they're afraid of you.
Zh: “我觉得他们很怕你。”

En: Maybe it's just SKULL solidarity.
Zh: “也许只是骷髅头让你们团结一致吧。”

En: They'll never be SKULLS. But... She softens. "But their hearts are in the right place."
Zh: “他们永远不会成为骷髅头的。不过……”她的态度温和了许多。“不过他们的心是好的。”

En:
You mean Joyce?'	“你是说乔伊斯？”
0x0100005800001EAC	Dialogue Text	PIER / CINDY	You	The Wild Pines rep?"
Zh: “野松公司的代表？”

En: Who is she?
Zh: “她是谁？”

En: Who is she?
Zh: “她是谁？”

En:
You mean Joyce?'	“你是说乔伊斯？”
0x0100005800001ED1	Dialogue Text	PIER / CINDY	You	That's the Wild Pines rep, right?"
Zh: “那是野松公司的代表，对吧？”

En: On a first-name basis with her, are we? Piggy's moving up in the world.
Zh: “跟她之间的关系都亲密到能直呼其名了，是吗？条子也在往上爬呢。”

En: Yes. We should go talk to her. He nods in her direction. "She's a professional negotiator, though. I have the feeling she will be very cooperative -- while telling us nothing."
Zh: “是的。我们应该跟她聊聊。”他朝她的方向点了点头。“不过，她是一个职业交涉人。我感觉她会非常配合——然而什么都不告诉我们。”

En: Probably the Wild Pines rep. We should talk to her. He nods in her direction. "She's a professional negotiator, though. I have the feeling she will be very cooperative -- while telling us nothing."
Zh: “也许是野松公司的代表。我们应该跟她聊聊。”他朝她的方向点了点头。“不过，她是一个职业交涉人。我感觉她会非常配合——然而什么都不会告诉我们。”

En: Hold on. What's Ozonne?
Zh: “等一下。什么是欧佐纳尔？”

En: Why all the negativity?
Zh: “为什么这么消极？”

En: Yeah, I don't like her either.
Zh: “是啊，我也不喜欢她。”

En: Ozonne is an archipelago two days' travel away from Revachol. Its moneyed residents, used to posh restaurants and upscale boutiques, rarely have reason to visit Martinaise.
Zh: 欧佐纳尔是一个群岛，距离瑞瓦肖有两天的行程。那里的居民比较富有，习惯了奢侈餐厅和高档精品店，几乎没有来马丁内斯的理由。

En: Have you got a crush on her? Aching for an opportunity to defend her honour?
Zh: “你看上她了吗？这么渴望捍卫她尊严的机会？”

En: Pig's not half as dumb as he looks. She gives you a stately nod of approval.
Zh: 她郑重地朝你点点头，表示赞同。“条子也没有看起来那么蠢嘛。”

En: It's where they grow whores like her, and their whore men.
Zh: “那里就是她那种婊子生长的地方，还有那些婊子的渣男。”

En: You should take the lead, ask her unexpected questions -- you know, do your thing. Don't be afraid to get a bit *wacky*. Throwing her off is our best bet.
Zh: “你应该主动引导，问她一些意料之外的问题——你知道的，按照你那套。就算是*古怪*一点也别害怕。最好的办法就是把她弄得摸不清头脑。”

En: Good idea, piggies. Run along now, fuck her shit up good. Impound that boat while you're at it. I'd like to watch her *swim* back to Ozonne.
Zh: “好主意，条子。现在赶紧走吧，去好好找找她的麻烦。没收她那艘船。我想看她*游回*欧佐纳尔。”

En: Pig's not half as dumb as he looks. She gives you a stately nod of approval.
Zh: 她郑重地朝你点点头，表示赞同。“条子也没有看起来那么蠢嘛。”

En: Yeah, I don't like her either.
Zh: “是啊，我也不喜欢她。”

En: Why all the negativity?
Zh: “为什么这么消极？”

En: Have you got a crush on her? Aching for an opportunity to defend her honour?
Zh: “你看上她了吗？这么渴望捍卫她尊严的机会？”

En: On a first-name basis with her, are we? Piggy's moving up in the world.
Zh: “跟她之间的关系都亲密到能直呼其名了，是吗？条子也在往上爬呢。”

En: It's where they grow whores like that one over there, and their whore men.
Zh: “那里就是她那种婊子生长的地方，还有那些婊子的渣男。”

En: Hold on. What's Ozonne?
Zh: “等一下。什么是欧佐纳尔？”

En: Madame Professional Negotiator in the flesh -- and the flimsy linen. She narrows her eyes. "Something morbid about old ladies trying to look young."
Zh: “职业交涉人本尊——还有脆弱的亚麻。”她眯起眼睛。“老女人总想让自己看起来年轻点，有点病态。”

En: Isn't it part of your job to know all the illustrious personages 'round this shithole? She's the Wild Pines representative. Madame Professional Negotiator in the flesh -- and the flimsy linen. Her eyes narrow to slits.
Zh: “了解这附近所有的名人不就是你工作的一部分吗？她是野松公司的代表。职业交涉人本尊——还有脆弱的亚麻。”

En: That's rich coming from a young girl dressed up like a granny.
Zh: 这就是一个穿的像老太太的年轻女孩理解的富有。

En: Aren't you a young lady trying to look old?
Zh: “你难道不是个希望自己看起来比较老的年轻女士吗？”

En: Oh, but that's the way it should be. The natural order of things.
Zh: “哦，不过本来就应该这样啊。这是事物的自然规律。”

En: Something morbid about old ladies trying to look young.
Zh: 她的眼睛眯成了一条缝。“老女人总想让自己看起来年轻点，有点病态。”

En: I made up the sending their best part. But I did talk to them.
Zh: “问候他们那段是我编的。不过我确实跟他们聊过了。”

En: Figures. All of you are liars. They're such sweet boys, though, aren't they? My sweet, sweet wannabe-SKULLS. They'll never be accepted. But their hearts are in the right place.
Zh: “你们都是骗子。不过他们的确是群很贴心的小伙子，不是吗？我那甜蜜，又可爱的骷髅头崇拜者。他们永远不会被接纳的。不过他们的心是好的。”

En: SKULLS are silly. What are you even trying to achieve?
Zh: “骷髅头太傻了。你到底想达到什么目的？”

En: Fat chance. But you *can* still do your part to revitalize the neighbourhood.
Zh: “希望渺茫。不过你还是*可以*尽自己的一份力量，来振兴附近这一带。”

En: SKULLS are cool. Can *I* be a SKULL?
Zh: “骷髅头太酷了。*我*可以当骷髅头吗？”

En: Have you noticed the *quiet?* Every so often, you might hear a gunshot pierce the air somewhere in Jamrock. But in Martinaise? No gunshots, no sirens. The people are languishing in boredom and complacency.
Zh: “难道你没有注意到这么*安静*吗？偶尔，你可能会听见加姆洛克的方向传来划破天空的枪声。不过在马丁内斯？没有枪响，没有警笛。大家都在厌倦和自满中煎熬。”

En: She throws you a conspiratorial glance, then presses her finger to her lips and squints up at the sky -- as though straining to hear something in the distance.
Zh: 她神秘兮兮地撇了你一眼，然后把手指压在嘴唇上，眯起眼睛仰望天空——就好像在竭尽全力去聆听远方的什么东西。

En: This place is a sepulcher. We'll paint it red. We bring the raucous -- you bring the sirens.
Zh: “这地方是个坟墓。我们会把它刷成红色。我们带来了喧嚣——你带来了警笛。”

En: Have you noticed the *quiet?* Every so often, you might hear a gunshot pierce the air somewhere in Jamrock. But in Martinaise? No gunshots, no sirens. The people are languishing in boredom and complacency.
Zh: “难道你没有注意到这么*安静*吗？偶尔，你可能会听见加姆洛克的方向传来划破天空的枪声。不过在马丁内斯？没有枪响，没有警笛。大家都在厌倦和自满中煎熬。”

En: She throws you a conspiratorial glance, then presses her finger to her lips and squints up at the sky -- as though straining to hear something in the distance.
Zh: 她神秘兮兮地撇了你一眼，然后把手指压在嘴唇上，眯起眼睛仰望天空——就好像在竭尽全力去聆听远方的什么东西。

En: This place is a sepulcher. We'll paint it red. We bring the raucous -- you bring the sirens.
Zh: “这地方是个坟墓。我们会把它刷成红色。我们带来了喧嚣——你带来了警笛。”

En: Okay then. How's that?
Zh: “那好吧。为什么会这样？”

En: But I really want to be a SKULL.
Zh: “不过我真的很想当个骷髅头。”

En: The bemusement in her voice doesn't fully mask genuine tenderness.
Zh: 她声音里的困惑并没有完全掩饰那份真诚的亲切。

En: Ooh, the piggies have learned how to saunter up staircases. I didn't think you could do that with hooves. But -- here you are.
Zh: “哦哟，现在猪猡都学会爬楼梯了呢。我本以为以你的小蹄子根本做不到呢不过——你已经在这里了。”

En: Why are you so committed to defacing the building?
Zh: “你为什么决心要把这栋建筑弄脏呢？”

En: And I need an aircraft carrier. So what?
Zh: “那我还需要一艘航空母舰呢。那又怎样？”

En: Ooh, police brutality. That's the good stuff!
Zh: “哎哟，警察暴行。那倒是挺不错的！”

En: Actually -- there is a shortage of people who talk to us in a normal, calm, informative manner.
Zh: “事实上——很少有人能够用一种普通、平静而有效地方式跟我们交谈。”

En: We weren't put on this earth to make your life pleasant, fuck-o.
Zh: “我们生来可不是让你过上舒心日子的，混球。”

En: She's making a valiant effort to come off as a nihilist, but she's really just an angry little commie. No wonder she was talking about the streets flowing red -- that's what happened during the Revolution.
Zh: 她努力想要给人留下虚无主义者的印象，其实真的不过是一个愤怒的小康米党派人士。难怪她会说什么街头流动的红色——那是大革命期间发生的事情。

En: So tell me, what's it like being a SKULL *and* a communist?
Zh: “告诉我，当一个骷髅头*和*一个康米主义者，是什么样的感觉？”

En: So you don't know what to write?
Zh: “所以你不知道该写什么？”

En: I'm gonna have to stop you. This is hooliganism.
Zh: “我要制止你。这是一种流氓行为。”

En: Thanks. I'm sure the inspiration will come to me now that I have an official RCM stamp of approval.
Zh: “谢了。我很肯定，有了RCM的官方认证许可之后，灵感很快就会来了。”

En: What are you gonna do, throw me off the balcony? I'm unstoppable.
Zh: “你打算怎么做，把我从阳台上扔下去吗？没有人能够阻挡我。”

En: What are you gonna do, climb up here? I'm unstoppable.
Zh: “你打算怎么做，爬上来吗？没有人能够阻挡我。”

En: Let's be realistic. What are you going to do, throw her off the balcony?
Zh: “我们面对现实吧。你打算怎么做，把她从阳台上扔下去吗？”

En: Let's be realistic. What are you going to do, climb up there?
Zh: “我们面对现实吧。你打算怎么做，爬上去吗？”

En: You heard your partner, piggy. I'm unstoppable.
Zh: “听见你搭档说的话了吗，猪猡。没有人能够阻挡我。”

En: I love public art! Don't mind us, keep doing what you're doing.
Zh: “我喜欢公共艺术！不用在意我们，你继续忙你的吧。”

En: I like public art. Keep doing what you're doing, don't mind me.
Zh: “我喜欢公共艺术。你继续忙你的吧，不用在意我。”

En: This place is severely lacking in havoc. Not even the occasional trash can fire to break up the tedium.
Zh: “偶尔的小打小闹已经无法让这座城市紧张了，这座城市缺乏一场真正的浩劫。”

En: I thought I'd mix it up, you know, summon the forces of crime and social chaos -- with a wall-sized invitation.
Zh: “我想我会把它混在一起，你知道的，召唤犯罪和社会混乱的力量——用整整一面墙那么大的一份邀请函。”

En: Have you ever tried your hand at graffito? When faced with a blank wall, most people write unimaginative stuff like *Pigs Go Home* and *Mona was here*.
Zh: “你有没有试过涂鸦？当你面对一面空墙的时候，大部分人会写缺乏想象力的东西，比如*条子滚回去*还有*莫娜到此一游*。

En: We rarely see pigs 'round here, though, just Union cads -- and my name's not Mona, so...
Zh: “不过我们这里很少会看见条子，只有工会的那些无赖——还有，我的名字不叫莫娜，所以……”

En: (Get closer and stare her down.) "Cindy, give me the goddamned brush."
Zh: （靠近一些，盯着她看。）“辛迪，把那把该死的刷子给我。”

En: How come you're letting this baby rat run circles around you? End this *now*.
Zh: 你怎么能让这只小老鼠超过你呢？*现在*就结束这一切。

En: Fine, take it, I'm all out of fuel oil anyway. She drops the paintbrush at your feet.
Zh: “好吧，拿去吧，反正我也没燃油了。”她把刷子丢在你的脚边。

En: Fuel? He looks at the 'paint' dripping down the wall, then back at her, struck by a sudden realization.
Zh: “燃油？”他看着从墙上滴落的‘油漆’，然后又看着她，突然想起了什么。

En: Fuel? He looks at the empty bucket, then back at her, struck by a sudden realization.
Zh: “燃油？”他看了一眼空桶，然后又看着她，突然想起了什么。

En: Fuel?
Zh: “燃油？”

En: That wasn't paint. It was heavy fuel oil, marked red for use by government vehicles.
Zh: “那个不是油漆。是重燃油，以红色为标志，是政府車辆用的。”

En: Yeah, fuel. What'd you think I was using, aquarelles?
Zh: “是啊，燃油。你以为我会用什么，水彩吗？”

En: Watch your back, ungulate. You've got eyes on you.
Zh: “小心你的背后，猪猡。有人监视你呢。”

En: (Stand your ground with a look of weary determination.) "Cindy, I'm going to stand here till you hand over that paintbrush."
Zh: （带着一脸疲惫的决心，坚定自己的立场。）“辛迪，我会一直站在这里，直到你把刷子交出来。”

En: Have you got any kids? Because you sound like the world's saddest dad right now.
Zh: 她怀疑地看着你。“你有小孩吗？因为你现在听起来就像全世界最悲伤的父亲。”

En: But, fine, take the brush. I'm all out of fuel oil anyway. She drops the paintbrush at your feet.
Zh: “不过，好吧，刷子拿去吧。反正我也没有燃油了。”她把刷子丢在你的脚边。

En: That isn't a fresh paint smell coming from the wall. She's using heavy fuel oil.
Zh: 墙上可不是新鲜的油漆味。她用的是重燃油。

En: Hatred? Disgust? It's difficult to tell which of the two is more present in her girlish features.
Zh: 怨恨？反感？很难说少女的脸庞上哪种情绪表现地更多一些。

En: Red-dyed heavy fuel oil is only used in government vehicles. Or at least that's the idea.
Zh: 红色重燃油只会使用在政府車辆上。或者至少是这样的。

En: Is that heavy fuel oil?
Zh: “那是重燃油吗？”

En: Is that government fuel oil?
Zh: “那是政府燃油吗？”

En: What did you think I was using, aquarelles? This shit is pumped out of government vehicles.
Zh: “不然你以为我在用什么，水彩吗？这狗屁玩意是从政府車辆里面抽出来的。”

En: What did you think I was using, aquarelles? Sucked it out of a cop's fuel tank myself -- back in Jamrock.
Zh: “不然你以为我在用什么，水彩吗？这狗屁玩意是我自己从警察車里的油箱抽出来的——在加姆洛克。”

En: Fumes are bad for you.
Zh: “废气对你来说是有害的。”

En: This has to be illegal.
Zh: “这一定是非法的吧。”

En: That's some pretty clever cultural commentary.
Zh: “这些文化评论还真是挺聪明的。”

En: You'd better hand it over.
Zh: “你最好把它交出来。”

En: That's some clever cultural commentary.
Zh: “这些文化评论还真是挺聪明的。”

En: Or what? You'll push me off this ledge and pry the bucket from my dead hands?
Zh: “不然怎样？你要把我从平台上推下去，然后撬开我这个死人的手，把桶拿走？”

En: Or what? You'll shoot me down from this ledge and pry the bucket from my dead hands?
Zh: “不然怎样？你要开枪把我从平台上打下来，然后撬开我这个死人的手，把桶拿走？”

En: That smell coming from her paint bucket -- it's not paint, it's heavy fuel oil.
Zh: 她的油漆桶里散发的味道——不是油漆，是重燃油。

En: Something to think about next time you're driving around in your pretty little piggy-carriage.
Zh: “下次开着你那辆漂亮的猪猡車到处转悠的时候，最好好好考虑一下。”

En: Cite me the statute that says you can't paint murals with fuel oil, and I promise I'll cease and desist.
Zh: “引用一条不能用燃油画壁画的法律规定，我保证会马上停手。”

En: You ain't seen nothing yet, piggy-boo.
Zh: “好戏还在后头呢，猪头。”

En: Something to think about next time you're driving around in your pretty little piggy carriage.
Zh: “下次开着你那辆漂亮的猪猡車到处转悠的时候，最好好好考虑一下。”

En: Fumes are bad for you, okay.
Zh: “废气对你来说是有害的，好吗。”

En: You ain't seen nothing yet, piggy-boo.
Zh: “好戏还在后头呢，猪头。”

En: What'd you think I was using, aquarelles?
Zh: “你以为我会用什么，水彩吗？”

En: The government uses such a nice red colour to mark its heavy fuel oil. I simply couldn't resist.
Zh: “政府用了这么漂亮的红色来染重燃油。让我无法抗拒。”

En: Red-dyed heavy fuel oil intended for exclusive use in government vehicles, to be precise, he says, studying the contents of Cindy's bucket.
Zh: 他研究着辛迪桶里的内容。“准确的说，是染红的重燃油，专门用于政府車辆的。”

En: That fuel oil was marked red for use by government vehicles only. He looks at the 'paint' dripping down the wall with a frown.
Zh: 他看着墙上滴落的“油漆”，皱起眉头。“那个红色燃油，是仅供政府車辆使用的。”

En: That fuel oil was marked red for use by government vehicles only. He looks at the empty bucket.
Zh: 他看着空桶。“那个红色燃油，是仅供政府車辆使用的。”

En: Oh well. He shrugs. "Nothing we can do about it now."
Zh: “好吧。”他耸耸肩。“现在我们什么也做不了。”

En: Can't I just use paint?
Zh: 我不能用油漆吗？

En: Right on. No way I'm settling for paint if even children are painting with fuel oil.
Zh: 对啊。如果就连小孩都用燃油画画的话，那我绝对不能勉强使用油漆啊。

En: Are you kidding me? Fuel oil is so much cooler. No way you're disfiguring that beautiful wall with something as pedestrian as ordinary *paint*!
Zh: 你在跟我开玩笑吗？燃油更酷一些吧。你绝不可以用那些普通又缺乏想象力的*油漆*来玷污这么美丽的一堵墙。

En: That beautiful wall deserves nothing less.
Zh: 那堵墙值得更好的。

En: Hey, Kim. Do you think you could lend me some of your fuel oil?
Zh: “喂，金。你的燃油能借我一点吗？”

En: Kim, my friend. Would you be willing to sacrifice some of your fuel oil for art?
Zh: “金，我的朋友。你愿意为艺术牺牲一些自己的燃油吗？”

En: My fuel oil is for my Kineema. Use your own fuel if you are unable to contain your artistic impulses, but please, leave my Kineema out of it.
Zh: “我的燃油是给锐影用的。如果你无法抑制住艺术创作的冲动，那就用你自己的燃油，不过，麻烦你，别把我的锐影扯进去。”

En: She sighs. "You got me, pigshit. I don't make for a very good SKULL. I like animals too much. Simians and... pigs."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“你难倒我了，猪猡。我不是非常擅长骷髅头。我太喜欢动物了。猿猴啊，还有……猪。”

En: Hello again, officers. Have you come to admire my mural?
Zh: “又见面了，警官们。你们是来欣赏我的壁画的吗？”

En: Her voice sounds different when she speaks this candidly. Older somehow.
Zh: 说话这么坦率的时候，她的声音听起来有些不同，不知道为什么，好像比较苍老。

En: She shrugs. "But I'm still gonna write some damned good graffito on this wall."
Zh: 她耸耸肩。“不过我还是要在这面墙上写下一些牛逼的涂鸦。”

En: Attempt to explain your artistic intentions.
Zh: 尝试解释你的艺术动机。

En: You know Cindy's secretly a communist.
Zh: 你知道辛迪是个秘密的康米党员

En: You're aware of Cindy's living conditions.
Zh: 你知道辛迪的生活条件。

En: You know the girl's high of choice.
Zh: 你知道这个女孩很挑。

En: Dresscode: Pseudo-SKULL.
Zh: 着装要求：伪骷髅头。

En: They do say the painter always paints his own portrait.
Zh: 他们的确说过，画家总是会给自己画肖像画。

En: You don't have the skills to execute something like this in practice, but, oh boy, the idea's going to blow her fucking mind!
Zh: 你的技术不足以切实执行你的想法，不过，天啦，这个想法一定会打动她的！

En: I'm going to paint a self-portrait, but sort of, you know, *from the inside*, to show people what it's like.
Zh: “我要画一幅自画像，不过有点，你知道的，*发自内心*的那种，让别人看看它是什么样的。”

En: I'm going to create a highly detailed skeleton of an ancient bird that went extinct a hundred million years ago.
Zh: “我要创作一幅非常细致的远古鸟类骨骼画，它在一亿年前就灭绝了。”

En: And then I'm going to paint it red using heavy fuel oil. And then I'm going to fucking light it on fire.
Zh: “然后我会用重燃油把它涂成红色。再他妈的用火把它点燃。”

En: It's the phoenix, get it? What do you say?
Zh: “它是凤凰，明白吗？你怎么觉得？”

En: She hunches her shoulders and throws you a grim look. "I say, sod off. You don't have the technical skills to do that."
Zh: 她耸耸肩，恶狠狠地瞪了你一眼。“我会说，滚蛋。你可没那种专业技术。”

En: I like your idea. Should've thought of it myself. I don't need this kind of competition in my neighbourhood.
Zh: “我喜欢你的主意。我应该自己想出来的。我的周围不需要这种竞争。”

En: That's pretty fucked up, even for you, piggo.
Zh: “那还真是糟糕透顶，甚至对你来说也是一样，猪猡。”

En: What if I add some interpretive dance?
Zh: “如果我再加一点形意舞呢？”

En: Stop belittling me, Cindy!
Zh: “别小看我，辛迪！”

En: Thanks, Cindy. (Pick up the brush.)
Zh: “谢了，辛迪。”（捡起刷子。）

En: Okay, I won't do that. Please just give me some paint and a brush. I need to do some art.
Zh: “好吧，我不会的。请给我一些油漆和一把刷子。我需要搞点艺术。”

En: Cindy, I'm going to need your paintbrush.
Zh: “辛迪，我需要你的刷子。”

En: Cindy, I need some paint. And your brush too.
Zh: “辛迪，我需要一些油漆。还有你的刷子。”

En: For art. It's for art, okay?
Zh: “为了艺术。这是为了艺术，好吗？”

En: Well, if it's for *art*... But... Her eyes narrow to slits. "What kind of art are we talking about?"
Zh: “呃，如果是为了*艺术*的话……不过……”她的眼睛眯成了一条缝。“我们说的是什么样的艺术？”

En: What for?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: The apocalypse is coming. I need to warn people.
Zh: “末日即将降临。我需要警告人类。”

En: For my motor carriage. We're going undercover.
Zh: “给我的汽車用。我们要去卧底。”

En: I'm doing renovation. It's boring, but necessary.
Zh: “我在翻修。很无聊，不过很有必要。”

En: Everything is sad and shit and we need art to make it okay. Just give me the brush.
Zh: “一切都太悲伤，太糟糕，我们需要艺术让它变得好起来。把刷子给我。”

En: To crush a man's dreams like that... I hope you're happy.
Zh: “就这样毁掉一个男人的梦想……希望你能开心。”

En: To be honest, I haven't really thought of anything yet... But I'm sure I will.
Zh: “老实说，我还没什么想法呢……不过我肯定会有的。”

En: What do you think I'm trying to paint here -- a mural for a better tomorrow? Why do you really need it?
Zh: “你觉得我想在这里画什么——描绘美好明天的壁画？你为什么需要它呢？”

En: I ain't helping no pigs fool honest, upstanding citizens. I'm not an *antisocial element*.
Zh: “我才不会帮猪猡愚弄老实又正直的市民呢。我不是*反社会元素*。”

En: But boring. You're not using my lovely brush to spread boredom.
Zh: “但是很无聊。我这把可爱的刷子可不是用来给你传播无聊的。”

En: Oh, but I'm all out of fuel oil.
Zh: “哦，不过我已经没有燃油了。”

En: You're a real sad sack, you know that? Go ahead, then. She drops the paintbrush at your feet. "Art it up. Just try not to hurt yourself. And *no self portraits*."
Zh: “你真是个可怜的讨厌鬼，你知道吗？那就去吧，”她把刷子丢在你的脚边，“搞你的艺术去吧。尽量别弄伤自己就好。还有，不要*自画像*。”

En: You're a real sad sack, you know that? Go ahead, then. She drops her paintbrush into the puddle below. "Art it up. Just try not to hurt yourself. And *no self portraits*."
Zh: “你真是个可怜的讨厌鬼，你知道吗？那就去吧，”她把刷子丢进了下面的水坑，“搞你的艺术去吧。尽量别弄伤自己就好。还有，不要*自画像*。”

En: Stop quivering like jello, then you won't get fucked.
Zh: “别像个果冻抖个不停的，这样你就不会被搞得焦头烂额了。”

En: She looks you up and down. "I think everyone would rather you didn't."
Zh: 她上下打量着你。“我想大家都觉得你还是不要做的好。”

En: There, there, piggy, she says in mock compassion. "I guess art just isn't really *you*. Cause you suck. In life. And in everything."
Zh: “好了，好了，猪猡。”她假装同情地说到。“我猜艺术真的不适合*你*。因为你烂透了。其他所有事也一样。”

En: Then you have time to find your own *fucking* brush.
Zh: “那你就有时间去给自己找一支*该死*的刷子了。”

En: Sounds like you're just about to live out your self pity, not make a statement. I can't have shit art on my conscience.
Zh: “听起来你只想活出自己的自哀自怜，而不是发表声明。我的良心不允许出现垃圾艺术。”

En: But I thought you'd like my idea!
Zh: “我还以为你不是喜欢我的主意吗！”

En: What's your problem?
Zh: “你有什么问题？”

En: I'm sorry, was the image offensive? Did you survive an avian attack as a child?
Zh: “很抱歉，那个意象冒犯到你了吗？你小时候被鸟类攻击过吗？”

En: But, Cindy, art is bigger than either of us.
Zh: “不过，辛迪，艺术比我们俩都重要。”

En: That's not fair!
Zh: “这不公平！”

En: Then get your brush from fucking *art*.
Zh: “那就拿着你的刷子去搞*艺术*吧。”

En: Such is the bloody law of the bloody streets, you wankstain.
Zh: “这就是该死的街头法则，你这个恶心玩意。”

En: I didn't realize you were so petty and insecure.
Zh: “我没想到你居然这么小气，还这么没安全感。”

En: Insult me all you want. You're still an art cop sans art materials.
Zh: “随你怎么侮辱我吧。你还是个没有艺术天分的艺术警探。”

En: Didn't we all?
Zh: “我们不都是这样吗？”

En: You know what you've got in that fuel canister you scavenged from your Kineema? Red-dyed heavy fuel oil. Paint and a brush -- you're ready to do this.
Zh: 你知道能从锐影的燃油罐里抽出什么来吗？红色的重燃油。颜料和刷子——你已经准备好了。

En: You know what you should be able to find in your government-issued vehicle? Red-dyed heavy fuel oil.
Zh: 你知道能从政府派发的汽車里找到什么吗？红色的重燃油。

En: Perfect, she isn't interested, the lieutenant murmurs to himself, "all the more reason to move on from this little side task."
Zh: “很好，她不感兴趣，”警督自言自语道，“更有理由从这个小小的支线任务中走出来了。”

En: Your partner here gets it. Anything else I can *not* do for you, gentlemen? She gives you both a mischievous smile.
Zh: “你的搭档懂了。先生们，还有什么我*不想*为你们效劳的事吗？”她给了你们俩一个调皮的微笑。

En: Told you! Told you these artist-types repel wealth.
Zh: 早就告诉你了! 我说过这些艺术型的人排斥财富。

En: You feel the biggest sneeze of your life itching its way to the centre of your face...
Zh: 你感觉到你这辈子最大的喷嚏发痒着往脸部中心进发……

En: Uh, what are you doing...? She looks disgusted, her individual facial features racing to be the most dramatic expression of her disapproval.
Zh: “呃，你在做什么……？” 她一脸嫌弃的样子，面部特征表现出了最强烈的不满。

En: I'm an artist too. (Your nostrils flare involuntarily.)
Zh: “我也是个艺术家。”(你的鼻孔不自觉地张开)

En: Nothing. Nothing! Khm... (Stop the sneeze while you still can.)
Zh: “没什么。没什么！咳……”(趁你还没打喷嚏的时候止住)

En: You tilt your head back, close your eyes, and get ready to snap it all forward...
Zh: 你把头往后仰，闭上眼睛，准备把喷嚏全部往前扣……

En: The coat gets flung over her head as she braces for impact.
Zh: 当她准备承受冲击时，外套甩到了她的头上。

En: But the rest doesn't come. You exhale softly -- no spittle, no shout. It's over. Just like that.
Zh: 但剩下的喷嚏并没有来。你轻轻地呼出一口气——没有唾沫，没有喊叫。就这样结束了。

En: Perhaps not *everyone* is an artist after all.
Zh: 也许毕竟不是*每个人*都能做艺术家。

En: Was that it? She looks relieved. "Stop playing with your snout and let's get on with this so you can go back to your stinky sty. You still want this painting or what?"
Zh: “就这样？”她看上去松了一口气。“别玩你的鼻子了，我们继续，这样你就可以回你的臭猪圈去了。你还想不想要这幅画了？”

En: AHHH--
Zh: “啊啊啊——”

En: The pre-sneeze hype is unreal.
Zh: 喷嚏前的炒作是不真实的。

En: The mucous gripping the sides of your nostrils is cheering. All scents and odours seem to have left the world; your nasal chambers have become wind tunnels of *very* ticklish particles.
Zh: 鼻孔两边粘附的粘液在欢呼。所有的气息和味道似乎都离开了这个世界；你的鼻腔已经变成了充满*非常*易痒颗粒的风洞。

En: The lieutenant furrows his brow at another one of your eccentricisms.
Zh: 警督为你的又一个怪癖皱眉。

En: The lieutenant is desperately searching for a handkerchief.
Zh: 警督正在拼命地寻找手帕。

En: The lieutenant is desperately searching for another handkerchief.
Zh: 警督正在拼命地寻找另一块手帕。

En: I see... blood-clot coins placed as offering to a pulsing threshold. But what does it *mean*?
Zh: “我明白了……凝血块硬币作为祭品放在脉动的门槛上。但这*意味*着什么？”

En: His words are hiding a tinge of excitement -- he's not so detached from your pursuits after all.
Zh: 他的话中隐藏着一丝兴奋——毕竟他也不是完全漠不关心你对财富的追逐。

En: What about Joyce? She's wealthy.
Zh: 那乔伊斯呢？她很有钱。

En: This place isn't exactly bursting with rich people.
Zh: 这个地方并不是到处都是有钱人。

En: I could sell it to Garte, hang it up in the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: 我可以把它卖给加尔特，把它挂在褴褛飞旋里。

En: Evrart can buy it on behalf of the Union.
Zh: 艾弗拉特可以代表工会买下它。

En: The Mega Rich Light-Bending Guy seems blindingly rich.
Zh: 扭曲光线的超级富豪看来富得让人瞎眼。

En: And become a small-time art peddler? No, think bigger.
Zh: 然后做个不起眼的艺术品贩子？不，想得更大胆些。

En: Hmm, doing business with communist dictatorships only works if you've got long-term plans in place... which you don't.
Zh: 嗯，和康米主义独裁分子做生意只有在有长期计划的情况下才行得通……而你没有。

En: Good call. He's definitely the type who might be interested in art. Let's go hustle him and score us some extreme *moolah*.
Zh: 好主意。他绝对是那种对艺术感兴趣的人。让我们去会会他，给我们赚一大笔*票子*。

En: Nah, she'd probably send the entire Wild Pines legal team after you if she knew you were trading goods around their harbour. She's ready and poised for a *hostile takeover*.
Zh: 不，如果她知道你在他们的港口做交易，可能会派整个野松公司的法律团队去找你。她已经准备好进行*恶意收购*了。

En: That high-net-worth individual -- wasn't he an investor? Let's go convince him to buy some *exclusive* local art.
Zh: 那个高净值人士……他不是投资者吗？我们去说服他买一些*独家*本地艺术品吧。

En: That blindingly rich guy seems the type, the one you met in a container? Let's go hustle him and score us some extreme *moolah*.
Zh: 那个富得让人眼瞎的家伙似乎就是你在集装箱里遇到的那个人？我们去说服他买一些*独家*本地艺术品吧。

En: That blindingly rich guy is the type. Sure, he said he doesn't carry much money on him, but he must have something else to trade. Let's hustle!
Zh: 那个富得让人眼瞎的家伙就是这种类型。当然，他说他身上没带多少钱，但他一定有别的东西可以交易。我们赶紧行动吧！

En: There must be *someone* around here with heavy pockets and an interest in the local art. Tell you what, have a wander round and I'll let you know if I hear any wealth.
Zh: 这附近一定有人口袋里有很多钱，而且对当地的艺术感兴趣。这样吧，你去转转，如果我听到财富之类的，我会告诉你。

En: That blindingly rich guy is certainly the type. He's already handed over 100 reál. Let's hustle him for even more *moolah*.
Zh: 那个富得眼瞎的家伙肯定就是这种类型。他已经给了你100雷亚尔了，我们再去找他要更多的钱吧。

En: Wait, you can *hear* wealth?
Zh: 等等，你能*听到了*财富的声音？

En: Yes, if you know what to listen for. That's what happens when you've been in the game for as long as I have. Your senses get *tuned in* to more opportune frequencies.
Zh: 是的，如果你知道该听什么的话。你在这行待久了就会这样。你的感官会被*调谐*到更合适的频率。

En: I got some vibes from the harbour. Let's explore that area again. Who knows what we might find hidden among all those shipping containers.
Zh: 我从港口那边感到一些共鸣。我们再去探索一下那片区域吧。谁知道我们会在那些集装箱中找到什么呢？

En: Okay, where's a good place to start looking?
Zh: 好吧，从哪入手比较好?

En: I got some vibes from that container you moved with the crane in the harbour. Let's see what's inside. There isn't a door yet that Savvy's VIP status couldn't open.
Zh: 我从你在港口用起重机移动的集装箱中感到一些共鸣。让我们看看里面有什么，没有一扇门是鬼鬼的VIP身份打不开的。

En: I got some vibes from that person inside the container you unlocked in the harbour. Why didn't you speak to them before? Let's head over there right now and fix that.
Zh: 我从你在港口打开的集装箱里的那个人感到一些共鸣。你之前为什么不跟他说说呢？我们现在就过去解决这个问题吧。

En: We haven't got into the harbour yet. Let's worm our way in and scope it out. Some of the best things in life come inside shipping containers.
Zh: 我们还没有进去过港口。让我们先溜进去看看。生命中一些最美好的东西就在集装箱里。

En: Good call. Sure, he said he doesn't carry much money on him, but he must have something else to trade. Let's hustle!
Zh: 好主意 当然，他说他身上没带多少钱，但他一定有别的东西可以交易。去赚一票吧！

En: Good call. He certainly seems the type. Let's go hustle him and score us some extreme *moolah*.
Zh: 好主意。他绝对是那种人。让我们去会会他，给我们赚一大笔*票子*。

En: Good call. He's already handed over 100 reál. Let's hustle him for even more *moolah*.
Zh: 好主意。他已经给你100雷亚尔了。让我们再从他身上捞多点*票子*吧。

En: Anything else you want? I'm in a good mood -- but it won't last long.
Zh: “你还想要什么？我心情很好——但不会持续很久。”
