En: Look at us, coming to an agreement. Who'd have thought? A breeze blows strands of hair across her forehead as she leans into the balustrade.
Zh: “看看我们，居然达成协议了。谁想得到呢？”当她斜靠在栏杆上时，一阵微风吹起她额前的发丝。

En: (Whip out your finger pistols.) "It's your lucky day! I'm looking to acquire some original art by the *crème de la crème*."
Zh: （掏出你的手指手枪。）“今天算你走运！我正好在找原创艺术品里*精品中的精品*。”

En: The streets will not flow red with anything. Who are you?
Zh: “街道不会被任何东西染红。你是谁？”

En: What's your name, miss? You seem to be engaged in an illegal activity.
Zh: “你叫什么名字，小姐？你好像在从事非法活动。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Hep. Yeah, when were you last tested?
Zh: “是啊，你最后一次做测试是什么时候？”

En: Had a battery of tests just last week. I'm practically a patchwork of interesting critters. Kind of like a man o' war. Despite the attitude, she puts the brush aside.
Zh: “上周做了一连串的测试。我几乎就是个有趣生物的混杂物。有点像一艘战舰。”尽管态度恶劣，她还是把刷子放在一边。

En: She's grown frustrated with her work and welcomes the opportunity to challenge authority in other ways.
Zh: 她对自己的工作越来越沮丧，很欢迎以其他方式挑战权威的机会。

En: That's right. We've evolved.
Zh: “没错。我们已经进化了。”

En: I don't have time for this. [Leave.]
Zh: “我没时间搭理这个。”[离开。]

En: We cops don't *like* closed doors or unreachable perches. Or people having high ground on us.
Zh: “我们警察*不喜欢*关着的门或者够不到的地方。或者是那些站得比我们高的人。”

En: You're in trouble now, little lady!
Zh: “你现在有麻烦了，小姐！”

En: Yes, Uncle Maynard. *Huge* trouble. She crosses her arms. "You're going to take out your metal detector and your collection of bottle caps and shrapnel."
Zh: “是啊，梅纳德叔叔。*大*麻烦。”她抱起双臂。“你打算拿出你的金属探测器，还有收藏的那些瓶盖和弹片吗？”

En: Yeah, I can see that. Cool mutations. She crosses her arms.
Zh: “是啊，我能看出来。很酷的突变。”她抱起双臂。

En: Yeah, you got me now. The dynamic between us has completely changed. She crosses her arms.
Zh: “是啊，现在你抓到我了。我们之间的动态关系完全变了。”她抱起双臂。

En: The streets will flow red once more. A great torrent, rushing down Rue d'Esperance. You wait and see. The girl flicks paint from her brush and onto the wall below.\n
Zh: “街道会再一次布满鲜红。一股巨大的激流，将会在埃斯佩兰斯路侵泄而下。你等着瞧吧。”女孩用刷子沾满油漆，然后涂画在下面的墙壁上。

En: The streets will flow red once more. A great torrent, rushing down Rue d'Esperance. You wait and see. The girl stares at the sailboat by the pier.
Zh: “街道会再一次布满鲜红。一股巨大的激流，将会在埃斯佩兰斯路侵泄而下。你等着瞧吧。”女孩凝视着码头旁边的帆船。

En: The streets will flow red once more. A great torrent, rushing down Rue d'Esperance. You wait and see. The girl flicks paint from her brush and onto the wall below.\n
Zh: “街道会再一次布满鲜红。一股巨大的激流，将会在埃斯佩兰斯路侵泄而下。你等着瞧吧。”女孩用刷子沾满油漆，然后涂画在下面的墙壁上。

En: The streets will flow red once more. A great torrent, rushing down Rue d'Esperance. You wait and see. The girl stares at the sailboat by the pier.
Zh: “街道会再一次布满鲜红。一股巨大的激流，将会在埃斯佩兰斯路侵泄而下。你等着瞧吧。”女孩凝视着码头旁边的帆船。

En: Is that bed in the coal room yours?
Zh: “锅炉室里那张床是你的吗？”

En: Ooh. Not only have you found my address, you've discovered my biggest secret: I'm a coal miner.
Zh: “哎哟，不仅找到了我的地址，还发现了我最大的秘密：我是个煤矿工。”

En: I found some drugs in the coal room. Yours?
Zh: “我在锅炉室里发现了一些毒品。是你的吗？”

En: They just might be. Rough stuff. If you take the ride, you'd be wise to buckle your little pig-belly up.
Zh: “也许是吧。挺糙的东西。如果你想搭上顺风車，最好在你的大肥肚子上扣紧安全带。”

En: Her neurons must be on fire, the heat creating waves and ripples in all she sees -- above all, in her painting.
Zh: 她的神经细胞一定是着火了，热浪翻腾，让她看到的一切都泛起涟漪——首先，就是她的壁画。

En: Does it make you see everything all... art-like?
Zh: “它是不是会让你看到的一切变得……更加艺术？”

En: I like bumpy rides. Know where I can get more?
Zh: “我喜欢颠簸的旅途。知道去哪能找到更多吗？”

En: I'm gonna have to take you in, kid. For possession of drugs.
Zh: “我要拘捕你，孩子。因为持有毒品。”

En: You really think I need to be high to see the world as art? Poor unimaginative piggy. How I wouldn't want to have *your* peepers.
Zh: “你真觉得我嗑嗨了才能把世界看作是艺术吗？你个头可怜又缺乏想象力的猪头。我还真是很不想被*你*窥探呢。”

En: We apologize, but we're currently *not buying the entrapment you're selling*. Please call again later. Thank you. She smiles at her own joke.
Zh: “很抱歉，不过我们现在不会中你*钓鱼执法*的诡计。你还是之后再试试吧，谢谢啊。”

En: In which case, they are not mine, and I have several witnesses who will corroborate my statement.
Zh: “在这种情况下，它们不是我的，而且有好几个目击证人可以证实我的陈述。”

En: This battle is not going to be worth your while. Let little miss sass here be.
Zh: 这场战斗不值得你浪费时间。还是别搭理这位爱顶嘴的小姑娘吧。

En: Hey, up here, piggo!
Zh: “喂，看上面，猪猡！”

En: Look at me when I'm talking to you.
Zh: “我在跟你说话呢，看着我。”

En: Yeah, you better keep walking.
Zh: “是啊，你最好继续走吧。”

En: Sail away.
Zh: “杨帆远航。”

En: She looks at you, expectantly. It is as if you have said nothing -- because you have not. No begging words have left your mouth...
Zh: 她期待地看着你。就好像你什么也没有——因为你就是没有。你嘴里从没说过祈求的话语……

En: Because it was manufactured in Revachol East by a company called Cor-de-Leite, and it's hull is '19 paces long.
Zh: “因为它是由瑞瓦肖东部一家名叫乳白的公司生产的，而且它的船体有19步长。”

En: She makes a point of being unromantic about it.
Zh: 她主张平淡地处理这个问题。

En: A smirk passes her face. "I think I'll stick with the factory name. But thank you."
Zh: 她的脸上掠过一丝假笑。“我想还是继续保留出厂名吧。不过还是谢谢你的建议。”

En: She's not actually considering it. It's sarcasm.
Zh: 其实她并没有考虑。这是讽刺。

En: The Wayfarer Act specifically denies the RCM the authority to demand anyone's operator license. It's a *little known fact* among us law officials.
Zh: “*警察*里很少有人知道，旅人法案明令禁止RCM当局查看任何人的驾驶员许可证。”

En: 33A -- this old *proletarian* haunt here. She waves at the ruin looming overhead. "As I said, *plenty* of people drive boats. Of all social strata."
Zh: “33A——这个老*无产阶级分子*还在这里萦绕。”她朝着头顶若隐若现的废墟挥挥手。“就像我说的，*很多*人都在开船。各种社会阶层都有。”

En: A... billion?
Zh: 十……亿？

En: With your help -- hopefully -- says her warm tone.
Zh: 在你的帮助下——希望如此——她的口气很亲切。

En: No way. She's more. She can make decisions.
Zh: 不可能的。她的身份远不止这么简单。她可以做决定。

En: Seems like they have no idea about business.
Zh: “看来他们对生意一窍不通。”

En: Everyone *knows* you can't trust artists. They're nearly as bad as *clowns*.
Zh: 所有人都*知道*不能相信艺术家。他们基本上就跟*小丑*一样坏。

En: Unbelievable, sire. Even the *arlecchinos* didn't work.
Zh: 难以置信，大人。就连*滑稽角色*都不起作用。

En: Unbelievable, sire. Even the *performance* artists didn't work.
Zh: 难以置信，大人。就连*表演*艺术家都不起作用。

En: Of course. I sympathize. But I'm afraid I simply can't share anything more until I've seen that badge...
Zh: “当然。我为此表示同情。不过恐怕在看到警徽之前，我无法再跟你分享更多信息了……”

En: She's a professional *negotiator*. She should be open to some sort of mutually beneficial arrangement..
Zh: 她是个专业的*交涉人*。她应该会接受某种互惠互利的约定……

En: I assure you it is no small matter for me either. We *all* share the responsibility for disarming this situation -- I hope you have a badge for me as soon as possible.
Zh: “我向你保证，这对我来说也不是一件小事。我们*全都*要承担解除这种形势的责任——希望你能尽快把自己的警徽找出来。”

En: Like what?
Zh: “比如说？”

En: I'll be frank with you. If I'm going to break protocol, I need to be able to justify it to my superiors. They're going to want to see something *tangible*.
Zh: “我老实跟你说吧。如果要我打破协议，我必须要能跟上级证明这么做的正当性。他们想要看到某些非常*具体*的东西。”

En: Like what?
Zh: “比如说？”

En: The Union is conducting drug trade out of the harbour. It's an open secret in Martinaise. Surely it must not come as a *complete* surprise to the RCM either. Perhaps it's time to look into it?
Zh: “工会在港口外面进行贩毒活动。这在马丁内斯是一个公开的秘密。这对RCM来说，肯定也不是个*完全*的意外。也许是时候调查一下了？”

En: This case is what I will be *known* by!
Zh: “这起案件会让我*名扬天下*的！”

En: *Or* you can find your badge, which, honestly, seems like a lost cause.
Zh: “*或者*你可以找到你的警徽，老实说，这似乎是个注定的败局。”

En: Shake it off.
Zh: 摆脱它。

En: Ashamed?
Zh: 羞愧？

En: I'm not ashamed!
Zh: 我不羞愧！

En: Yes. You're too ashamed to ask this person for *money*, too scared to belittle yourself in her eyes -- those half-precious stones of Eau-de-Nil.
Zh: 是的。你过于羞愧，不敢找这个人要*钱*，你太害怕，不愿在她眼前贬低自己——那双略显珍贵的深绿色宝石。

En: Oh, but you are: too ashamed to ask this person for *money*, too scared to belittle yourself in her eyes -- those half-precious stones of Eau-de-Nil.
Zh: 但你就是：过于羞愧，不敢找这个人要*钱*，你太害怕，不愿在她眼前贬低自己——那双珍贵的深绿色宝石。

En: It's a she, by the way.
Zh: “顺便说一句，是个女人。”

En: Clever. Few suspect a woman in this sort of skulduggery. Good move on their part.
Zh: “很聪明。在这类诡计之中，很少有人会怀疑到一个女人。他们这一步走得很好。”

En: As the shipments approach Revachol? Hmm... somehow you suspect something more *sinister*.
Zh: 货物接近瑞瓦肖的时候？嗯……不知道为什么，你怀疑的是一些更加*罪恶*的事情。

En: *Qualified pleasure craft operator*... so charming. Where's the damn licence?
Zh: *合格的游艇驾驶员*……多么诱人。那个该死的许可证在哪？

En: His medical condition makes him a little *grumpy*, ma'am. I hope that's not a problem.
Zh: “他的身体状况让他变得有些*性情乖戾*，夫人。希望没给你惹麻烦。”

En: Oh no -- not a problem at all.
Zh: “哦，没有——完全没问题。”

En: It truly isn't. She controls herself very well.
Zh: 其实真不是。她自我控制地非常好。

En: I just like the name for some reason.
Zh: “出于某种原因，我就是很喜欢这个名字。”

En: I really do, the lieutenant thinks -- is she thrown off, yet? He's looking at the woman, assessing her...
Zh: 我的确很喜欢，不过到目前为止，她的注意力被完全分散了吗？”，警督看着女人，仔细揣测着……

En: Her nonchalance *might* be related to something called the Wayfarer Act -- a law that says she doesn't *need* a licence. Sly fox... you're not aggressive enough to harass her further on this.
Zh: 她的冷漠*也许*跟一个叫旅人法案的东西有关——法律规定她不*需要*许可证。狡猾的狐狸……你还不够咄咄逼人，没办法在这件事上进一步骚扰她。

En: *Not* cool.
Zh: “一点也*不*酷。”

En: Keep in mind, this is a negotiator Mr. Claire has worked with before, and who was more than fair with him and the Union.
Zh: “记住，这是跟克莱尔先生之前合作过的一名交涉人，对他和整个工会的态度已经远超公正。”

En: Not *hard communist* enough for me.
Zh: “对我来说，还不够*硬核康米主义*。”

En: What exactly do they mean by it, though? What's the demand?
Zh: “不过，他们这么说到底是什么意思？他们有什么要求？”

En: He can't be that bad.
Zh: “他没有那么坏吧。”

En: Uhm... yes.
I can't hear you, darling... speak up, please.
Zh: “我听不见你说的话，亲爱的……请大声一点。”

En: Yes. After they clear the terminal we lose track. The actual production is taking place at various sites in and around Jamrock Quarter. North of here.
Zh: “是的。在他们清理了中转站之后，我们就失去了踪迹。实际的生产发生在加姆洛克区内部和周围的多个地点。就在这里的北部。”

En: Let me get this straight -- the materials come from Samara to Revachol *through* the terminal?
Zh: “我就直说了吧——从萨马拉运往瑞瓦肖的材料会*通过*中转站*吗？”

En: Well -- at least this solves *one* mystery.
Zh: “呃——至少这就解决了*一个*谜题。”

En: What is that lieutenant?
Zh: “是什么呢，警督？”

En: Why I had to call East Motor Tract -- and *beg* them to open the drawbridge for me. I'd wondered since I first drove *in*. On my motor carriage.
Zh: “我为什么必须打给东高速公路——*请求*他们为我放下吊桥。自从我第一次开車*进来*的时候，就一直感到好奇。”

En: I am sorry for the inconvenience, Lieutenant Kitsuragi. But we need them trapped here. This is a unique opportunity. I'm sure you understand.
Zh: “抱歉给你带来了不便，曷城警督。不过我们需要把他们困在这里。这是一个绝无仅有的机会。我肯定你能理解的。”

En: It may come to nothing, or it may just blow the case wide open. I suspect the traffic jam won't disperse for a few more days. You should have the time you need.
Zh: “可能什么也找不到，或者可能会让案子大白于天下。我至少还可以让吊桥再抬起个几天。这些时间对你来说应该足够了。”

En: I need 25 reál.
Zh: “我需要25雷亚尔。”

En: That's a good sum. Not too small, not fantastically large like a billion. She reaches into her raincoat and pulls out a zip bag made of see-through plastic.
Zh: “这是个很好的数额。不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个透明塑料制成的拉链袋。

En: That's a good sum -- not too small, not fantastically large. She reaches into her raincoat and pulls out a zip bag -- in it you see paper notes arranged like black gills.
Zh: “这是个很好的数额——不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个拉链袋——你看见里面装着像黑色鱼腮一样整齐排列的纸钞。

En: She removes a few notes and hands them to you. The paper is cold and oily to the touch.
Zh: 她取出一些纸钞递给你。它们摸起来冰冷又油腻。

En: There's a trace of irony in his voice. Mischief even. This is a tactic.
Zh: 她取出一些纸钞递给你。它们摸起来冰冷又油腻。

En: It's hard to get a read on her precise disposition, but she *appears* helpful.
Zh: 很难觉察到她的准确倾向，不过她*似乎*是乐于助人的。

En: She's having a good time arguing against the law. Too good, perhaps?
Zh: 争辩法律让她感到很开心。也许有点太开心了？

En: She doesn't like it. Probably a bad memory. Mean girls at school or some ancient lover...
Zh: 她不喜欢它。也许有一段不好的记忆。学校里的刻薄女孩，或者是某个旧爱……

En: Because it *stings*. In an ancient and beautiful way.
Zh: 因为它会*刺痛*。以一种古老而又美丽的方式。

En: Ludicrous, even, it's meant.
Zh: 甚至，意味着荒唐。

En: This checks out.
Zh: 这一点检查完毕了。

En: Don't tell her you've met Evrart *right away*. There's time.
Zh: 别*立刻*告诉她你见过艾弗拉特。还多得是时间。

En: You don't have to tell her you met Evrart *right away*. There's time.
Zh: 你不用*立刻*告诉她你见过艾弗拉特。还多得是时间。

En: ... they're profiting from this trade.
Zh: ……他们从这类交易中获利。

En: Uncovering Union secrets could also give you an upper hand when dealing with them.
Zh: 发现工会的秘密能让你在对付他们的时候占据上风。

En: You'll be indebted to her, in a way. But one step ahead of the Union in another...
Zh: 在某种程度上来讲，你欠了她一个人情。不过领先工会一步……

En: How about you share your information on the lynching -- now that you've seen his badge.
Zh: “不如跟我们分享一下你掌握的私刑信息——既然你已经见过他的警徽了。”

En: The goal posts have moved, lieutenant. In the absence of the badge I have informed my employer there will be a probe.
Zh: “门柱已经移动了，警督。在没有警徽的情况下，我已经通知过公司说会有人展开调查。”

En: I cannot rescind that promise. She smiles apologetically. "To my knowledge, the drivers are still at the roundabout. I will tell you *everything* I know -- when you've finished with them."
Zh: “我不能撤回那个承诺。”她满怀歉意的笑了。“据我所知，司机们在环岛。我会把知道的*一切*都告诉你——等你跟他们聊完之后。”

En: How about you share your information on the lynching -- now that you've seen my badge.
Zh: “不如跟我们分享一下你掌握的私刑信息——既然你已经见过他的警徽了。”

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: I can't see why it *wouldn't* be okay. Now, what can I do for you?
Zh: “我不明白为什么这样*还是不行*。现在，我能为你做什么吗？”

En: This one's a slippery eel.
Zh: 这家伙确实是条狡猾的鳝鱼。

En: This was your plan all along.
Zh: “原来一直以来这都是你的计划。”

En: Fair, I guess. The probe will be done soon.
Zh: “挺公平的，我猜。调查很快就会完成。”

En: She shakes her head vigorously. "My plan is to share information. The only way to do that *now* is by telling my employers you've kept your end. Which I hope you will, because let me tell you: we are in *dire* waters."
Zh: 她用力摇摇头。“我的计划是分享信息。为了达到这个目的，*现在*唯一的办法就是告诉我的雇主你会履行诺言。我也希望如此，因为我可以告诉你：我们正处于*水深火热*之中。”

En: I am glad to hear that. Because let me tell you: we are in *dire* waters. The sooner the probe is finished the sooner I can share *crucial* information with you.
Zh: “很高兴听到这个消息。因为我可以告诉你：我们正处于*水深火热*之中。调查越快结束，我就能越早跟你分享那些*关键*信息。”

En: The sooner the probe is finished the sooner I can share *crucial* information with you. She takes a sip of tea. "Now, is there anything I can do for you in the meanwhile? Tea, perhaps?"
Zh: “调查越快结束，我就能越早跟你分享那些*关键*信息。”她抿了一口茶水。“在此期间，我还有什么能帮到你的吗？也许，来点茶水？”

En: Meaning: the information she has will raise the stakes in this game.
Zh: 意思是：她拥有能让这场游戏赌注升高的信息。

En: How bad can the info she has be?
Zh: 她的信息能有多糟糕？

En: She takes a sip of tea. "Now, is there anything I can do for you in the meanwhile? Tea, perhaps?"
Zh: 她抿了一口茶水。“在此期间，我还有什么能帮到你的吗？也许，来点茶水？”

En: All around you, rain keeps falling down. On the wooden boards she's standing on, and on the water around you...
Zh: 在你周围，雨点不停落下。落在她脚下的木板，落在你周围的水面……

En: One of the tall buildings overlooking the roundabout, most likely. That would give them a read on the entire quarter.
Zh: 最可能是俯瞰整个环岛的其中一栋高层建筑。这能让他们了解整个地区的状况。

En: She means: you know her closer now, in some way you know her soul a bit.
Zh: 她的意思是：你现在更了解她了，在某种程度上，你对她灵魂的了解又多了一分。

En: Let's be honest, you have no idea what that meant -- interisolary microwave compression? Moving on...
Zh: 老实说，你不知道那是什么意思——跨洲微波压缩？继续吧……

En: Better not to frighten you, ma'am.
Zh: “最好别吓着你，夫人。”

En: Frighten me? She smiles. "I'm not frightened easily, but I understand -- some things are best for the police to keep to themselves."
Zh: “吓到我？”她笑了。“我没那么容易吓到，不过我能理解——有些事还是警察自己知道的好。”

En: The words *blood bath* sound cold in her mouth. They taste of iron and strawberries.
Zh: *大屠杀*这个词从她嘴里说出来冷冰冰的。尝起来有一股钢铁和草莓的味道。

En: Yes. Exactly.
Zh: “是的，没错。”

En: Precisomundo. Class war time baby.
Zh: “完全正确。阶级战争的时间到了，宝贝。”

En: And this is my colleague from Precinct 41, Detective Du Bois. He waits for you to show your badge.
Zh: 他指向你。“这是我41分局的同事，杜博阿警探。”他等着你拿出警徽。

En: I don't seem to have my badge, actually...
Zh: “其实，我好像没有警徽……”

En: I don't seem to have my badge, actually...
Zh: “其实，我好像没有警徽……”

En: And this is my colleague from Precinct 41. He waits for you to show your badge.
Zh: 他指向你。“这是我41分局的同事，杜博阿警探。”他等着你拿出警徽。

En: *Oh*. And what happened to it, detective? She returns the lieutenant's badge.
Zh: “*哦*。那它出什么事了，警探？”她交还了警督的警徽。

En: The lieutenant breathes a sigh of relief. "Good. We won't be needing to go to my Kineema, then."
Zh: 警督放松地呼出一口气。“很好。那我们就不用回锐影那边去了。”

En: Then what is this positively destitute *jetty* doing here? She puts her foot down on the rotten planks beneath. "And what about that fleet of skiffs?"
Zh: “那这个明显很寒碜的*码头*在这里做什么呢？”她把脚放在下方腐烂的木板上。“还有，那个小艇船队是怎么回事？”

En: This unmistakably *proletarian* fleet of skiffs. She waves at the nearby fishing boats. "As I said, *plenty* of people drive boats. Of all social strata."
Zh: “这明显就是*无产阶级*的小艇船队。”她朝着附近的渔船挥挥手。“我也说过，*很多*人会开船。来自各个社会阶层。”

En: A fleet? Three shabby fishing boats is hardly a *fleet*.
Zh: 船队？三支寒酸的小渔船很难算得上是*船队*吧。

En: Didn't Titus mention that Ruby ran a *complex operation* out of her lorry? Could it be connected? Only one way to find out -- investigate!
Zh: 提图斯不是提过露比在开货車之外，还在运作*复杂的业务*吗？会不会有所关联？只有一个办法可以知道——调查！

En: I heard something about a *complex operation* earlier. This adds up.
Zh: “我之前就听说过*复杂的业务*。这些都加起来了。”

En: She nods. "I'm glad to hear, officer. I'm sure you'll find out just what that means, soon enough."
Zh: 她点点头。“听你这么说，我很高兴，警官。我肯定你会弄明白它的含义，很快。”

En: My badge. Yes. Of course. My badge that I have on me.
Zh: “我的警徽。是的。当然了。我的警徽就在我身上。”

En: He looks at you expectantly. Then it dawns on him...
Zh: 他期待地看着你。然后他明白了……

En: He looks at you expectantly. Then it dawns on him...
Zh: 他期待地看着你。然后他明白了……

En: My badge. Yes. Of course. My badge that I have on me.
Zh: “我的警徽。是的。当然了。我的警徽就在我身上。”

En: Evrart Claire is a man of the *utmost* integrity. If you can say one thing about him, it's that he always puts the interests of the workers first.
Zh: “艾弗拉特是一个*极度*正直的人。如果只能说一件跟他有关的事，那就是他总是把工人的利益放在第一位。”

En: Remember those bold, new exotic revenue streams Evrart said he was going to fund his harbour with? You should go back and ask him about that.
Zh: 还记得艾弗拉特说要用大胆的、外来的新收益流来为港口提供资金吗？你应该回去问问他这一点。

En: Remember those bold, new exotic revenue streams Evrart said he was going to fund his harbour with? You should go back and ask him about that.
Zh: 还记得艾弗拉特说要用大胆的、外来的新收益流来为港口提供资金吗？你应该回去问问他这一点。

En: Be very careful, what information you're gonna share with her.
Zh: 跟她分享信息的时候，一定要非常小心。

En: Joyce L. Messier. I represent the board of Wild Pines -- the owners of the harbour. You must be from the RCM... She steps closer and holds out her hand over the railing.
Zh: “乔伊斯·L·梅西耶。我是野松公司董事会的代表——港口的所有者。你一定是RCM的人吧……”她走近一步，越过栏杆把手伸了出来。

En: Shake her hand.
Zh: 跟她握手。

En: Don't shake her hand.
Zh: 不跟她握手。

En: Joyce L? What does the 'L' stand for?
Zh: “乔伊斯·L？这个‘L’代表什么？”

En: Leyton, my maiden name. Her bony hand dangles from the sleeve of her oversized raincoat.
Zh: “莱顿，我的婚前姓。”她那只瘦骨嶙峋的手从宽大雨衣的袖子里伸了出来。

En: I'm glad to see you here. Her grip is tight and cold. She seems relieved.
Zh: “很高兴在这里见到你。”她的手劲很大，手心冰凉。似乎很放松的样子。

En: I'm glad to see you here. Her grip is tight and cold.
Zh: “很高兴在这里见到你。”她的手劲很大，手心冰凉。

En: I'm afraid my partner doesn't shake hands. Please don't take offence.
Zh: “恐怕我的搭档是不跟人握手的。请不要生气。”

En: I'm glad to see you here. The woman and the lieutenant exchange a brief handshake.
Zh: “很高兴在这里见到你。”女人和警督简短地握了一下手。

En: None taken, officer. They exchange a brief handshake. "Either way, I'm glad you're both here now."
Zh: “不会的，警官。”他们简短地握了一下手。“不管怎么说，很高兴现在能在这里见到你们。”

En: Like steel. There is strength there. If she wanted, she could sink her nails deep into your skin.
Zh: 就像钢铁，很有力量。如果她想的话，她甚至可以把指甲深深插进你的皮肤里。

En: I'm glad to see you here. She withdraws her small hand and picks up her cup.
Zh: “很高兴在这里见到你。”她收回了手，拿起了她的杯子。

En: She is unfazed by your rudeness, probably chocking it up to local custom.
Zh: 她没有因为你的无礼而苦恼，可能是把这个当成了本地的一种习俗。

En: I was dispatched to handle a strike, not a lynching. Anything I can do to assist the RCM in this matter, I will, gladly.
Zh: “我被派来是处理罢工的，不是私刑。在这件事情上，如果我能协助到RCM，我会乐意效劳。”

En: Thank you. That's all for now. [Leave.]
Zh: “谢谢。暂时先到此为止吧。”[离开。]

En: Of course, detective -- take care.
Zh: “当然了，警探——保重。”

En: You're back. Good. She looks at the ripples on the water's surface. "What can I help you with -- not an umbrella, I hope. I don't need one myself, you see..." She pats her wet raincoat.
Zh: “你回来了，很好。”她看着水面上泛起的波纹。“我能帮你什么吗——希望不是来要雨伞的。我自己用不着，你看……”她轻轻拍了拍潮湿的雨衣。

En: Let's talk about the lynching.
Zh: “我们说说私刑的事吧。”

En: Hey, about my missing badge again...
Zh: “再聊下我那枚遗失的警徽吧……”

En: What can you tell me about this lynching?
Zh: “关于私刑的事，你都知道些什么？”

En: You seem smart, I need someone to give me a low-down on this... *reality* we're in.
Zh: “你看起来很聪明的样子，我需要有人给我讲解一下实情……关于我们现在身处的这个*现实*。”

En: You seem rich. Can I have some money?
Zh: “你好像是个有钱人。能给我一点钱吗？”

En: Let's talk some more about this *boat* that you're on.
Zh: “我们再说说你的这条*船*吧。”

En: You're on a boat.
Zh: “你在一条船上。”

En: You're on a boat.
Zh: “你在一条船上。”

En: Joyce L. Messier. I represent the board of Wild Pines -- the owners of the harbour. You gentlemen must be from the RCM... She steps closer and holds out her hand over the railing.
Zh: “乔伊斯·L·梅西耶。我是野松公司董事会的代表——港口的所有者。先生们一定是RCM的人吧……”她靠近了一下，越过栏杆把手伸了出来。

En: Tell me more about the strike at the harbour.
Zh: “跟我再说说港口罢工的事。”

En: What can you tell me about this strike?
Zh: “关于罢工的事，你都知道些什么？”

En: What gave us away?
Zh: “是什么东西泄露了我们的身份？”

En: What gave me away?
Zh: “是什么出卖了我？”

En: Nothing, honestly. I've said it to every drunk in town and you're the first one who's responded.
Zh: “老实说，没什么。我跟城里所有醉鬼都这么说过，不过你是第一个有反应的人。”

En: And when did you first arrive?
Zh: “你第一次来这里是什么时候？”

En: I arrived three weeks ago... She thinks. "Yes, in the middle of February -- the bay was still partially frozen then. I prefer to do these things on-site. Like the RCM."
Zh: “我三周之前到的……”她想了想。“是的，在2月中旬——那个时候海湾有些部分还没解冻呢。我倾向于现场办公。就跟RCM一样。”

En: Why, yes I am... She looks at the deck under her feet. Green and white sails flutter overhead.
Zh: “怎么了，是这样没错……”她看着脚下的甲板。绿白相间的船帆在头顶飞舞。

En: But the strike began in *December*. He looks at his notes.
Zh: 他看着笔迹。“但是罢工是从*12月份*开始的。”

En: I wasn't the original negotiator here. I took over after Mr. Gaumont hit a wall with Mr. Claire, the Union boss. Mr. Claire refused to speak with Gaumont, despite *concessions* he'd granted the Union in prior negotiations.
Zh: “最初的交涉人不是我。戈蒙先生在克莱尔先生，也就是工会领袖那里碰壁之后，我才接手的。克莱尔先生拒绝跟戈蒙先生交流，虽然他从之前的谈判中赢得了*让步*。”

En: Good afternoon, officer, I'm Joyce. She extends her hand in greeting.
Zh: “下午好，警官，我是乔伊斯。”她伸出手来问候你。

En: What exactly is the RCM? I remember hearing it from somewhere.
Zh: “RCM到底是什么？我记得好像在哪里听过。”

En: RCM -- what is that? I'm a little *foggy* here.
Zh: “RCM——那是什么？我这里有点*模糊*。”

En: Uhm... She seems confused. "I meant *you* -- the Revachol Citizens Militia. The police."
Zh: “嗯……”她似乎有些困惑。“我是指*你*——瑞瓦肖公民武装。警察。”

En: She hasn't actually said it to every drunk in town. It was spoken in jest.
Zh: 她并没有真的对城里的每个醉汉这么说过。这只是个玩笑。

En: I believe the official title is Senior Labour Negotiator. In practice I'm a grocery clerk. I relay the Union's demands to Wild Pines, and return with Wild Pines' counter-offer...
Zh: “我确信官方头衔应该叫高级劳工交涉人。其实我就是个杂货店跑腿的。我把工会的需求传达给野松公司，然后把野松公司的回应传达回去……”

En: How are these talks going?
Zh: “那这些对话进行的怎么样？”

En: And how are the talks going?
Zh: “对话进行的怎么样了？”

En: They're *not*. That's the problem. The Union stopped all negotiations a week ago. After that awful lynching took place.
Zh: “并*没有*对话。这才是问题。一周以前，工会就中止了所有谈判。就在那场可怕的私刑发生之后。”

En: You mean Jean-Luc, my Race Mentor? He's a great teacher, a man of *virtù*.
Zh: “你是说让·卢克，我的种族理论导师？他是个很棒的老师，一个很有*美德*的男人。”

En: Tell us more about this *behemoth*.
Zh: “再跟我多说说这头*巨兽*。”

En: Tell me more about this *behemoth*.
Zh: “再跟我多说说这头*巨兽*。”

En: Oh, you mean Measurehead?
Zh: “哦，你是说测颅先生？”

En: Yes -- Jean-Luc *Measurehead*. She leans back and rolls her eyes.
Zh: “是的——让·卢克，*测颅先生*。”她身体后仰，翻了个白眼。

En: Oh, so you've *met* him. She takes a sip of tea. "Has he beguiled you with his inane theories?"
Zh: “哦，看来你*见过*他了。”她抿了一口茶水。“他有没有用他那套疯狂的理论哄骗你呢？”

En: What can I say? The Union employs a giant covered in tattoos. She pauses. "A racist giant. I guess that's part of their *big tent* organization now."
Zh: “我还能说什么？工会雇用了一个纹身巨人。”她停了下来。“一个种族主义巨人。我猜现在已经成为他们*大帐篷政策*的一部分了。”

En: How were the talks going before the lynching? (Proceed.)
Zh: “私刑之前对话进行的怎么样了？”（继续。）

En: Let's say I was not making the kind of progress I'd hoped for when I first arrived...
Zh: “这么说吧，我并没有取得刚来这里之前希望取得的那种进展……”

En: How long have you been here?
Zh: “你来这里多久了？”

En: This isn't the first time the Union has gone on strike?
Zh: “这不是工会第一次罢工了吗？”

En: Heavens, no. There have been *two* prior strikes. Both times the Union won significant concessions -- including overtime pay *and* a medical plan. This time their demands are more... I guess you could say *aggressive*.
Zh: “天啦，不是。之前已经发生过*两次*罢工了。每次工会都赢得了重大的让步——包括加班工资*和*医疗保险。这一次他们要求的更多……我猜可以用*激进*来形容。”

En: My colleague has his peculiarities. Let's not go into this particular one just yet.
Zh: “我的同事有他自己的一些怪癖。我们还是先别提这个的好。”

En: Of course. To each their own.
Zh: “当然。各有不同。”

En: What do you mean *beguiled*?
Zh: “你说*哄骗*是什么意思？”

En: *He* didn't do anything. It was the indisputable truth of his theories!
Zh: “*他*什么也没做。这就是他那套理论无可争辩的事实。”

En: Yes. They're *wunderbar*.
Zh: “是的。实在是太*奇妙*了。”

En: They're *nonsense*, a load of tripe. She tosses back her hair. "But we were on the subject of the talks I believe."
Zh: “那都是些*胡话*，废话连篇。”她把头发朝后一甩。“不过，我觉得我们之前已经聊过这个了。”

En: Charmed, enchanted, put under his spell.
Zh: “被哄骗了，着魔了，被他的咒语迷住了。”

En: Funny, I could think of a few ways to dispute them. She pauses. "But we digress."
Zh: “有意思，我可以想出一些方法来反驳它们。”她停了一下。“不过，我们好像跑题了。”

En: What happened then?
Zh: “发生什么事了？”

En: The information I'm to share with you includes sensitive trade secrets. For the sake of my employer, I have to ask for your names and badge numbers.
Zh: “我跟你分享的信息包含敏感的商业秘密。为了我的雇主的利益，我需要你的名字和警徽号码。”

En: The information I'm to share with you includes sensitive trade secrets. For the sake of my employer, I have to ask for your name and badge number.
Zh: “我跟你分享的信息包含敏感的商业秘密。为了我的雇主的利益，我需要你的名字和警徽号码。”

En: Of course, ma'am. We should have introduced ourselves... The lieutenant hands her a piece of blue plastic. "I'm Lieutenant Kitsuragi, from Precinct 57..."
Zh: “当然可以，女士。我们应该先自我介绍一下……”警督递给她一个蓝色的塑料。“我是曷城警督，来自57分局……”

En: And this is my colleague from Precinct 41. He points to you. "I'm afraid he doesn't have his badge at the moment. I hope mine will suffice."
Zh: 他指向你：“这位是我的同事，来自41分局。恐怕他的警徽现在不在身上。希望我的就足够了。”

En: More curious by the minute... She returns the lieutenant's badge turns to you. "Why is that, detective?"
Zh: “我越来越好奇了……”她把警督的警徽还了回去，然后转向你。“为什么会怎么样，警探？”

En: How curious. She returns the lieutenant's badge and turns to you. "Why is that, detective?"
Zh: “我有些好奇。”她把警督的警徽还了回去，然后转向你。“为什么会这样，警探？”

En: And this is my colleague from Precinct 41, Detective Du Bois. He points to you. "I'm afraid Harry doesn't have his badge at the moment. I hope mine will suffice."
Zh: 他指向你：“这位是我的同事，杜博阿警探，来自41分局。恐怕哈里的警徽现在不在身上。希望我的就足够了。”

En: *Oh*. And what happened to yours, detective? She returns the lieutenant's badge and turns to you.
Zh: “*哦*。那你的警徽怎么了，警探？”她把警督的警徽还了回去，然后转向你。

En: Why should I blame it on an artist?
Zh: 我为什么要责怪艺术家呢？

En: Some artist types stole my badge. It was a mistake to trust them.
Zh: “有些艺术家偷走了我的警徽。相信他们是一个错误。”

En: Some damn *clown* stole it from me. It's a long story.
Zh: “某个该死的*小丑*偷走了我的警徽。这故事就说来话长了。”

En: You mean someone *stole* your badge? Her brow is slightly arched.
Zh: “你是说有人*偷走*了你的警徽？”她的眉毛微微拱起。

En: Not a lot of people on boats, are there?
Zh: “乘船的人可不多，是吧？”

En: Does she have a name?
Zh: “她有名字吗？”

En: Do you have a licence for this boat?
Zh: “你有这艘船的执照吗？”

En: I think I have a handle on the boat thing. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我想我已经掌握了船的情况。”（总结。）

En: Of course there are. She looks around. "We are on an archipelago. How else are you supposed to get around?"
Zh: “当然还是有的。”她看了看周围。“我们在一个群岛上。不然还能怎么四处走动呢？”

En: Wait, we're on an *archipelago*? (Look around.)
Zh: “等等，我们在一个*群岛*上？”（环顾四周。）

En: Uhm, Caillou? I thought we were in Revachol.
Zh: “呃，卡尤？我觉得我们在瑞瓦肖。”

En: Wait, I thought Le Calliou was one big island?
Zh: “等等，我以为卡尤是一座大岛？”

En: We are. And the city of Revachol is on the island of Le Caillou.
Zh: “你说的没错。瑞瓦肖这座城市就在卡尤岛上。”

En: Technically, the neighbouring Ozonne and Face-à-La-Mer island groups are archipelagos, while Le Caillou, by contrast, is a single, fertile land mass, the fourth largest island in the world. It is not an archipelago.
Zh: 从严格意义上讲，临近的欧佐纳尔和法斯拉美是群岛，相比之下，卡尤则是一片独立的沃土，也是世界上第四大的岛屿。它不是一座群岛。

En: Okay. She gives you a crooked smile. "If you want to get technical. The point is we're *all* on islands here. And sail is still the most expedient way to get from one island to another. Especially when you're in a hurry to resolve a *strike*."
Zh: “好吧。”她朝你露出了一个不自然的笑容。“如果你想专业一点的话。重点在于我们*所有人*都生活在岛上。航行仍然是在岛屿之间往来最便利的手段。特别是你急着要解决一起*罢工*事件的时候。”

En: Still. I haven't seen anyone else sail a boat around here.
Zh: “即便如此。我还是没在这里见过其他人开船。”

En: I haven't seen anyone else drive a souped-up Coupris Kineema motor carriage either.
Zh: “我也没见过其他人驾驶一辆马力增强版的库普瑞斯锐影汽車。”

En: Actually, the lieutenant becomes defensive, "that motor carriage has been specially issued to serve as a patrol and *pursuit* vehicle."
Zh: “事实上，”警督摆出了防御姿态，“那辆汽車是专为用来巡逻和*追逐*車辆的。”

En: It's for crossing long distances in the Greater Revachol Industrial Harbour. It's not a toy.
Zh: “它是用来在瑞瓦肖大工业港进行远距离穿梭的，它不是玩具。”

En: Neither is that. The woman nods toward the sloop. "A toy, I mean. It's a machine for crossing long distances in the bay of Revachol. Between the city and the islands."
Zh: “那个也不是。”女人朝着帆船点了点头。“我的意思是，一个玩具。它是一台在瑞瓦肖海湾进行长距离穿梭的机器——在城市和岛屿之间。”

En: Neither is this. The woman pats the cabin hardtop. "A toy, I mean. It's a machine for crossing long distances in the bay of Revachol. Between the city and the islands."
Zh: “这个也不是。”女人拍了拍船舱顶盖。“我的意思是，一个玩具。它是一台在瑞瓦肖海湾进行长距离穿梭的机器——在城市和岛屿之间。”

En: That's not mine. It belongs to my partner, Lieutenant Kitsuragi.
Zh: “那不是我的。它属于我的搭档，曷城警督。”

En: She's referring to Lieutenant Kitsuragi's motor carriage.
Zh: 她指的是曷城警督的汽車。

En: No matter *whose* it is. A motor carriage is just a tool. You need it to do your job -- as I do mine. It's just a means of getting where you need to go.
Zh: “不管它是*谁的*。汽車只是一种工具。你的工作需要它——我也一样。它只是一种送你前往所需地点的手段。”

En: You lost me there -- I have no idea what a Coupris Kineema is.
Zh: “你把我弄糊涂了——我不知道库普瑞斯锐影是什么。”

En: Pardon me, it's not especially relevant.
Zh: “请原谅，这并没有太大的关联。”

En: There it is again -- the name of that infernal machine! I woke up to that sound, you know?
Zh: “又来了——那台地狱战車的名字！我是被它的声音吵醒的，你知道吗？”

En: Hmm, I suppose that one *does* have a rather loud electrical engine. She does not quite understand what you meant. "A specific drone. In any case...."
Zh: “嗯，我猜那个*的确*拥有个特别响亮的电气引擎。”她并没有完全明白你的意思。“一只特殊的雄蜂。总之……”

En: I drove my motor carriage into the sea, you know. It sank.
Zh: “你知道吗，我把汽車开进大海里了。它沉下去了。”

En: Let it be.
Zh: 顺其自然吧。

En: In any case... She takes a sip from her thermal cup.
Zh: “总之……”她从保温杯里喝了一口。

En: What a surprise, she says, not the least bit surprised. "That is one disadvantage of a motor carriage relative to a sloop like this one." The sloop bobs gently in the water.
Zh: “真令人惊讶，”她话是这么说的，但是样子一点也不惊讶。“这就是汽車相对于这种单桅帆船的一个缺点。”帆船在水中轻轻地上下漂浮。

En: What do you mean -- archipelago?
Zh: “你说的群岛——是什么意思？”

En: Understood. About this boat of yours... (Back off.)
Zh: “明白了。关于你的这艘船……”（返回。）

En: Of course. The situation is precarious.
Zh: “当然。现状很不稳定。”

En: Good, she takes a sip of her thermal cup.
Zh: “很好，”她从保温杯里喝了一口。

En: I *mean* we are on Le Caillou, are we not? She raises her brow.
Zh: “我的*意思*是我们在卡尤岛上，难道不是吗？”她扬起眉头。

En: Yes, we are. She raises her brow. "We are on Le Caillou."
Zh: “是的，没错。”她扬起眉头。“我们就在卡尤岛上。”

En: She nods attentively.
Zh: 她认真地点点头。

En: There is a pinch of *defensiveness* in her voice, but it is playful.
Zh: 她的声音里有一些*防御性*的意味，不过是开玩笑的。

En: Officer, I assure you I'm a highly qualified pleasure craft operator.
Zh: “警官，我向你保证，我是个非常合格的游艇驾驶员。”

En: The crowns of her teeth are porcelain, white as the boat's hull as she smiles.
Zh: 她的牙冠是烤瓷的，笑的时候跟帆船的船体一样洁白。

En: I just renewed its safety inspection last month, officers. It is completely seaworthy. In fact, it's taken part in not one, but *two* Insulindic regattas. Even finished once.
Zh: “我上个月才更新了安全检验，警官们。它完全适于航行。事实上，它参与了不止一次，而是*两次*伊苏林迪赛艇会。甚至还完成了一次。”

En: I just renewed its safety inspection last month, officer. It is completely seaworthy. In fact, it's taken part in not one, but *two* Insulindic regattas, and finished once.
Zh: “我上个月才重新做了安全检验，警官。它完全适于航行。事实上，它参与了不止一次，而是*两次*伊苏林迪赛艇会，还完成了一次。”

En: Can I see your licence?
Zh: “我能看看你的执照吗？”

En: That's good enough for me.
Zh: “这些信息对我来说已经足够了。”

En: Actually, you don't.
Zh: “其实，你不用这么做。”

En: Still *need* to see a license, ma'am.
Zh: “我还是*需要*看看你的执照，女士。”

En: I'm pretty sure I do. I police.
Zh: “我很确定我需要。我是警察。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: It is a little known fact among those who *police* that the Wayfarer Act specifically denies the RCM the authority to demand anyone's operator license.
Zh: “*警察*里很少有人知道，旅人法案明令禁止RCM当局查看任何人的驾驶员许可证。”

En: Wait, exactly how little known is this fact?
Zh: “等等，到底有多不为人知呢？”

En: I was just being polite -- even schoolchildren know the Wayfarer Act.
Zh: “我这只是一种客气的说法——就连小学生都知道旅人法案。”

En: What happened the other time?
Zh: “那另外一次是怎么回事？”

En: The other time I *would* have finished the race were it not for an urgent work matter, much like the one I'm on now.
Zh: “那一次我*本来*可以完成比赛的，只是临时来了个紧急工作，跟现在这次很类似。”

En: Makes sense, I guess. I mean, look at me!
Zh: “我觉得挺有道理的。我是说，看我这幅德行！”

En: So we *can't* pull over random civilians and demand their papers? That's weak.
Zh: “所以我们*不能*随意拦下市民，让他们出示证件吗？这也弱了吧。”

En: Unless it's their *passport*, no. Do you want to ask for the lady's passport?
Zh: “除非要求的是他们的*护照*，否则就不行。你想看这位女士的护照吗？”

En: No need to resort to self pity, detective. You can still ask for her passport.
Zh: “没必要自哀自怜，警探。你还能要求查看她的护照。”

En: Why wouldn't it be? This is Revachol. Wayfarer act and all. *Anyone* can drive a boat.
Zh: “为什么不呢？这里可是瑞瓦肖。旅人法案还有其他什么之类的。*任何人*都可以开船的。”

En: More like the Anarchy Act in my opinion.
Zh: “在我看来，更像是混乱法案。”

En: No need to politicize this. You may still ask for the lady's *passport*.
Zh: “没必要搞得这么政治化。你还能要求查看这位女士的*护照*。”

En: You need to save face if you want her respect. DEMAND that passport!
Zh: 如果你想要赢得她的尊重，就得保全自己的面子。要求检查她的护照！

En: Yes. I'm going to need to see your passport.
Zh: “是的。请出示你的护照。”

En: No need.
Zh: “没必要。”

En: The Wayfarer Act is one of the few pieces of legislation governing human interaction in the Revachol Zone of Control, along with the Emergency Trade and Aliments Acts.
Zh: 旅人法案是瑞瓦肖控制区少数几个控制人际互动的法律之一，除此之外还有紧急贸易法案和食品法案。

En: It's one of the foundational laws in Revachol, designed to limit the authority of local institutions. Part of it prohibits the RCM from requesting most documents and licenses without permission from a Coalition representative...
Zh: 它是瑞瓦肖的基本法之一，旨在限制本地机构的权利。里面的部分内容禁止RCM在没有联盟代表许可的情况下，查看大部分的文件和执照……

En: The what now?
Zh: 什么鬼？

En: *Yawn*
Zh: *打哈欠*

En: The one time this busted up cabbage has something useful for your *job* you just want to stare at some boat. Fine. Knock yourself out.
Zh: 这一次，这个破破烂烂的玩意总算对你的*工作*有所帮助了，然而你却只想盯着一堆破船看。好吧。那就别客气。

En: It's a law that keeps Revachol what it is -- in limbo.
Zh: 它是一个让瑞瓦肖保持现状的法律——保持在不稳定状态。

En: The boat? No. *It* is called Cor-de-Leite '19 -- because that's the type of sloop it is.
Zh: “船吗？没有。*它*被称为考迪莱勒 '19——因为它就是这种单桅帆船。”

En: Of course. She searches the pockets of her raincoat, producing a bundle of documents in a sealed plastic bag.
Zh: “当然可以。”她在雨衣口袋里翻找着，然后拿出一堆密封在塑料袋里的文件。

En: She nods politely. "Of course. Any more questions about the boat, sir?"
Zh: 她礼貌地点点头。“当然可以。关于这艘船还有什么问题吗，先生？”

En: The graciousness of her response makes you feel glad you didn't hassle her about her papers for no reason.
Zh: 她这种亲切的回应让你感觉到开心，你不是无缘无故用证件的问题麻烦她的。

En: Look at her passport.
Zh: 看看她的护照。

En: Everything appears to be in order. (Give the documents back.)
Zh: “一切似乎都很正常。”（把文件还回去。）

En: The woman is silent, smiling while the waves lap against the hull. In your hand, under the plastic -- a light blue passport saying *REPUBLIC OF VESPER*. The coat of arms has a lion and an ostrich, sejant.
Zh: 海浪拍打着船身，女人沉默的微笑着。在你的手中，塑料袋下面——是一本写着*维斯珀共和国*的浅蓝色护照。纹章上有一头狮子，一只鸵鸟，正笔直地坐着。

En: Look at the photo page.
Zh: 查看相片页。

En: The woman -- born in the year 3 in the Commune of Revachol -- smiles at you, face adorned with frown lines and crow's feet. The photo is recent. The name reads Rejoyce Leyton-Messier.
Zh: 这个女人——出生于瑞瓦肖公社3年——正朝着你微笑，脸上有一些皱纹和鱼尾纹。这张相片是近期的。上面的名字叫雷乔伊斯·莱顿·梅西耶。

En: She nods and returns the plastic bag to her coat.
Zh: 她点点头，把塑料袋放回自己的口袋。

En: You're from the Republic of Vesper.
Zh: “你来自维斯珀共和国。”

En: You're from Vesper?
Zh: “你是维斯珀来的？”

En: My husband is. I have dual citizenship. A Vespertine is handier when travelling.
Zh: “我丈夫是。我有双重国籍。维斯珀人在旅行的时候要方便很多。”

En: The Republic of Vesper, formerly the Republic of Vesper-Messina, was once a dual kingdom. An amicable parting of ways produced two constitutional monarchies. Vesper, the richer of the two, is an Occidental nation known for its proximity to the pale and its exotic financial derivatives.
Zh: 维斯珀共和国，前维斯珀-梅西纳共和国，曾经是一个二元王国。和平的分裂方式产生了两种君主立宪制度。维斯珀，两者中较为富有的一方，现在是一个奥西登国家，以接近灰域和独特的金融衍生品而闻名。

En: Anything else?
Zh: 还有别的吗？

En: It is a stable country, and yet there has always been something *ominous* about its perfect stability, its place at the edge of the Mundi isola, overlooking the Ubi Sunt? isles fracturing into the Great Unrest of the pale.
Zh: 那是一个稳定的国家，而这种完美的稳定性总是存在着某种*不祥的*感觉，它位于蒙迪洲边缘，远眺尤比萨特洲——在动荡不安的灰域中支离破碎。

En: How about... Dolores.
Zh: “那……德洛莉丝怎么样呢。”

En: How do you like it? (Conclude.)
Zh: “你觉得怎么样？”（总结。）

En: The word *it* feels strange. Such a beautiful boat deserves a proper name...
Zh: 这个词*本身*感觉就很奇怪。如此一艘美丽的帆船应该得到一个更合适的名字……

En: A breeze ripples through the sails and tugs at your hair. Below, the sleek, fishlike shape of the hull parts the water. Beneath that -- a resounding darkness. You're reminded of something, or some*one*...
Zh: 一阵轻风让船帆泛起涟漪，牵扯着你的头发。在下面，鱼身形状的光滑船体泡在水里。在那之下，是一片彻头彻尾的黑暗。你想起了什么事，或者是*什么人*……

En: Why *Dolores*?
Zh: “为什么是*德洛莉丝*？”

En: I don't know... feels pretty.
Zh: “我不知道……就是感觉很美。”

En: Hmm. Well, it means nothing to me. I think I'll stick with the factory name. But thank you for the suggestion.
Zh: “嗯。对我来说它没有任何意义。我想还是继续保留出厂名吧。不过还是谢谢你的建议。”

En: My sloop? I like it a lot. Her lips curl into a wry smile. "It's the *eel's hips*, baby."
Zh: “我的单桅帆船？我很喜欢它。”她的嘴唇挤出一丝苦笑。“它是*鳗尾*。”

En: I'm enjoying this part of the interview -- it has *so* little to do with the murder we're investigating.
Zh: “我很喜欢这部分走访——跟我们正在调查的凶杀案*特别*没有关系。”

En: Technically speaking, you *don't*.
Zh: “从技术层面上讲，你*不可以*。”

En: Surely you're aware that under the Wayfarer Act the RCM doesn't have the authority to demand anyone's operator license. Freedom of movement and all that.
Zh: “你一定知道：根据旅人法案，RCM无权要求查看任何人的驾驶许可证。活动自由，等等等等。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: Then certainly you must know that under the Wayfarer Act the RCM doesn't have the authority to demand anyone's operator license. Freedom of movement and all that.
Zh: “那你肯定知道：根据旅人法案，RCM无权要求查看任何人的驾驶许可证。活动自由，等等等等。”

En: I'm pretty sure I do. I police.
Zh: “我很确定我需要。我是警察。”

En: You may still ask for my passport. She strikes a reconciliatory tone...
Zh: “你还是可以要求检查我的护照。”她的口气缓和了许多……

En: Makes sense, I guess. I mean, look at me.
Zh: “我想，应该言之有理。我是说，你看看我！”

En: So I can't arbitrarily pull people over and ask for their papers?
Zh: “所以我不能肆意拦下别人，要求检查他们的证件了？”

En: More like Anarchy Act in my opinion.
Zh: “在我看来，更像是混乱法案。”

En: You may still ask to see my passport, she says, trying to strike a conciliatory tone. "*That* I'm obliged to share with you."
Zh: “你还是可以要求检查我的护照。”她尽量让自己的口气缓和一些。”*那个*我是必须拿给你看的。”

En: Laced with just a touch of pity.
Zh: 带着一丝怜悯。

En: Let's talk about the *economics* of this boat you're on. (Go in for the kill.)
Zh: “我们聊聊你这艘船的*经济状况*吧。”（单刀直入。）

En: Oh my, the *E-word*. She crosses her arms. "You mean to say that it's a symbol of *conspicuous consumption*? That I'm a member of the ruling class."
Zh: “天啦，*经济*这个词都跑出来了。”她抱起双臂。“你是想说它是*炫耀性消费*的一种符号？我是统治阶级的一个成员。”

En: No. You're siphoning off honest Revacholian tax revenue to shifty-eyed Vespertine financiers.
Zh: “不对。你吸走了老实的瑞瓦肖人民的税收，输入到贼眉鼠眼的维斯珀金融家那里去了。”

En: No -- it's a symbol of our subjugation to *foreign* capital. The bad kind of capital.
Zh: “不是——它是我们屈服于*外国*资本的一种符号。不好的那种资本。”

En: Forget about it. I was out of line.
Zh: “忘了吧。我违规了。”

En: Detective, may I remind you that Mrs. Messier is a *professional negotiator*? He doesn't look like he thinks you'll best her in single combat.
Zh: “警探，我需要提醒你一下，这位梅西耶女士是一名*职业交涉人*？”从他的表情可以看出，他觉得你无法在单打独斗中赢过她。

En: You *need* to make this lady admit she's only riding around on this boat because she's rich.
Zh: 你*需要*让这位女士承认：她能乘着这艘船四处游荡只是因为她有钱。

En: Uhm... why?
Zh: 呃……为什么？

En: Yes, I do!
Zh: 是的，没错！

En: Why what? Stop thinking. Take her down.
Zh: 为什么？别再想了。挫挫她的锐气。

En: It'll come off like I'm envious. And I'm not.
Zh: 这样搞得好像我很羡慕一样。但是我没有。

En: Why am I even thinking about this? I have police work to do.
Zh: 我为什么要这么想？我还有警察的本职工作要做。

En: Actually, yes. I need to nail her for this.
Zh: 其实呢，是这样没错。我要让她为这件事付出代价。

En: Before you do -- it would be pertinent to ask other questions. Gather more info on this *boat* of hers...
Zh: 在你这么做之前——不妨先问问其他问题。收集更多有关她这艘*船*的信息……

En: You have the guns and the ammo! Take this *klassenfeind* down!
Zh: 你有枪，还有子弹！干掉这个*阶级敌人*！

En: Before you do, it might be pertinent to ask a few more questions... learn whatever you can about this *boat* of hers.
Zh: 在你这么做之前，也许再多问点其他问题更好一些……尽可能地了解有关她这艘*船*的信息。

En: Charge! You've got the guns and the ammo.
Zh: 上啊！你有枪，还有子弹。

En: You're *not*? Okay then, just keep on admiring the boat then, unburdened by envy.
Zh: 你*没有*？那好吧，那就继续欣赏这艘船吧，卸下妒忌的重担。

En: Have it your way.
Zh: 随你便。

En: Think nothing of it, officer. She runs her hand along the fibreglass cabin. "What's a little *class conflict* between friends?"
Zh: “别放在心上，警官。”她用手拂过玻璃纤维船舱。“朋友之间的小*阶级冲突*算什么呢？”

En: Fire back -- quick!
Zh: 反击——快！

En: Know about Revolution.
Zh: 了解大革命。

En: It's the eel's heels.
Zh: 它是鳗鱼跟。

En: Revacholian Nationhood agrees!
Zh: 瑞瓦肖民族性表示同意！

En: Fine. Anyway... (Change the subject.)
Zh: “好吧。无所谓……”（换个话题。）

En: Yes -- anyway. She adjusts her hair. The permanent wave in her auburn locks radiates victory.
Zh: “是的——无所谓。”她理了理头发。她赤褐色头的卷发辐射出胜利的光芒。

En: Your synapses flash!
Zh: 你的神经突触在发射信号！

En: Uhm...

That is good to hear, madam. He opens his notebook. "My colleague will take the lead on this interview. I should let you know that he's recovering from an *unusual* medical episode -- *very* unusual -- but I can assure you of his ultimate competency."
Zh: “那太好了，女士。”他翻开笔记本。“我的同事将主导这次询问。我必须提醒你，他正在从一次不寻常的*发病*中康复——*非常*不寻常——不过我仍能向你为他的基本能力作担保。”

En: It happens quicker than a shooting star -- but did the lieutenant just *wink* at you?
Zh: 这一切发生的比流星还要快——不过，警督刚才是不是朝你*眨眼*了？

En: There's a trace of irony in his voice. Mischief even. The lieutenant is hatching some scheme.
Zh: 他的声音里显示出一丝讽刺的痕迹。甚至有些恶作剧的意味。警督正在酝酿某种阴谋。

En: He's trying to signal something. He *wants* you to play up your condition.
Zh: 他在尝试给你发信号。他*希望*你能夸大自己的病情。

En: There doesn't seem to be much space left with this 19-pace sloop docked here.\n
Zh: “这艘19步的单桅帆船停在这里，似乎都没剩下多少空间了。”

En: There doesn't seem to be much space left with this giant pleasure craft docked in the way.
Zh: “有了这艘巨大的游艇停靠在码头上，这里似乎都没剩下多少空间了。”

En: Uhm...
This house was built *before* the Revolution.
Zh: “这栋房子是在大革命*之前*建造的。”

En: Funny, I didn't see any other eel's heels docking there.
Zh: “有意思，我没在看见有别的鳗鱼跟停靠在这里。”

En: Funny, I don't see any other *eel's heels* around.
Zh: “有意思，我没在附近看见别的*鳗鱼跟*。”

En: You mean *eel's hips* -- but point taken. I am a bourgeois woman and this is my fast, light, interminably bourgeois boat.
Zh: “你的意思是*鳗尾*——不过我明白你的意思。我是个资产阶级女性，这是我的快速、轻巧、漫无止境的资产阶级帆船。”

En: There is no shame in her voice -- a touch of sadness, yes -- but no shame.
Zh: 她的声音里没有一丝羞愧——有一点悲伤，没错——但是没有羞愧。

En: Yup. That's the stuff.
Zh: 没错。这就对了。

En: Good thing everyone else has tiny skiffs.
Zh: “还好其他人都有小船。”

En: It *is* Antecentennial. She looks across the water, trying to catch a glimpse of the looming building. \n
Zh: “它*的确*是世纪前的。”她望着对岸，试着瞥见那栋若隐若现的建筑。

En: Okay. It *is* Antecentennial. She looks at the building looming overhead.
Zh: “好吧。它*的确*是世纪前的。”她看着头顶那若隐若现的建筑。

En: I assure you, they drove quite the hard bargain for this space -- but you're right.
Zh: “我向你保证，他们为这片地方拼命地讨价还价——不过你说得对。”

En: I am a bourgeois woman and this is my long, incredibly lightweight, interminably bourgeois boat.
Zh: “我是个资产阶级女性，这是我那艘长长的、极其轻巧的、漫无止境的资产阶级帆船。”

En: I assure you, they drove quite a hard bargain for this space -- but you're right.
Zh: “我向你保证，他们为这片地方拼命地讨价还价——不过你说得对。”

En: I am a bourgeois woman and this is my fast, lightweight, interminably bourgeois boat.
Zh: “我是个资产阶级女性，这是我的快速、轻巧、漫无止境的资产阶级帆船。”

En: Any moment.
Zh: “随时可能发生。”

En: Nothing happens. For quite a while.
Zh: 什么也没发生。持续了很长一段时间。

En: Something is about to happen. (Point to your head.) "In here."
Zh: “有事就要发生了。”（指着你的脑袋。）“在这里面。”

En: Okay. She takes a sip.
Zh: “好吧。”她抿了一口水。

En: She takes a sip.
Zh: 她抿了一口水。

En: Wave your hand next to your head. (It will help!)
Zh: 在你的脑袋旁边摆摆手。（能有帮助！）

En: Your hand tires from making the motion.
Zh: 这个动作让你的手有点累。

En: My... she inspects the motion. "It's as if there is *a tiny spinner* on the side of his head, and he's *reeling* thoughts out."
Zh: “天……”她查看着你的动作。“好像他的脑袋旁边有一个*小纺纱机*，他想把里面的想法*抽*出来。”

En: I told you his methods are... unconventional.
Zh: “我告诉过你吧，他的方法有些……不寻常。”

En: A thought is going to come out of my head and into my mouth -- and I will say it.
Zh: “有个想法会从我的脑袋里跑出来，钻到我的嘴巴里——然后我就会把它说出来。”

En: Of course.
Zh: “当然。”

En: It's gonna happen.
Zh: “会发生的。”

En: Of course.
Zh: “当然。”

En: It doesn't.
Zh: 并没有。

En: Keep waving your hand, slowly.
Zh: 继续慢慢挥舞你的手。

En: Except he isn't.
Zh: “只不过，他没有。”

En: The lieutenant is satisfied with where this is going.
Zh: 警督对事情的进展很满意。

En: Keep doing it.
Zh: 继续这样做。

En: This isn't gonna happen.
Zh: “不太可能想出来了。”

En: They will come from my head -- the arguments.
Zh: “它们是从我的脑袋里冒出来的——那些争论。”

En: Nothing is going to come. (Give up.)
Zh: “什么也没有。”（放弃。）

En: As they do.
Zh: “确实如此。”

En: The thoughts happen *inside* his brain, the lieutenant explains.
Zh: “想法是发生在他的脑袋*里面，”警督解释到。

En: I can see your hand is getting tired now -- there is no need to continue. No one is keeping score.
Zh: “我能看出你的手有点累了——不用再继续了。没人在计分的。”

En: No worries. She takes another sip. "We can just go back to discussing my fast, large, interminably middle class boat."
Zh: “别担心。”她又抿了一口水。“我们可以继续讨论我那艘快速、大个、漫无止境的资产阶级帆船。”

En: I swear to god I'm usually sharp. I don't know what happened. Why am I not *killing it*?!
Zh: “我向老天发誓，我一般都很给力的。我不知道今天是怎么了。我为什么没有*大杀四方*呢？”

En: Fuck this, I'm outta here. [Leave.]
Zh: “见鬼去吧，我走了。”[离开。]

En: What were we talking about anyway?
Zh: “话说，我们到底在说什么？”

En: You're marbles on glass, dear. Don't worry.
Zh: “你的智力没问题，亲爱的。别担心。”

En: Oh no... Her eyes turn large and round...
Zh: “哦，不……”她的眼睛睁得又大又圆……

En: It was *quite* a while ago, but I think... she looks around. "Yes. We were discussing my fast, large, ineluctably middle class boat."
Zh: “那是*挺早*之前的事了，不过我觉得……”她看了看周围。“是的。我们正在讨论我这艘快速、大个、漫无止境的资产阶级帆船。”

En: Lieutenant, I truly don't understand what is happening here.
Zh: “警督，我真的不太明白这到底是怎么回事。”

En: Officer, I am *so* sorry for the whole pier situation.
Zh: “警官，我*真心*对整个码头的状况表示很抱歉。”

En: No problem, let's resume normal, professional relations.
Zh: “没关系，我们恢复普通、专业的关系就好。”

En: Ma'am. You humiliated me.
Zh: “女士。你在羞辱我。”

En: Her concern is sincere, her eyes round and affectionate.
Zh: 她的担忧是真诚的——眼睛圆睁，里面充满关切的神情。

En: What situation? I don't remember any situation.
Zh: “什么什么情况？我不记得什么情况？”

En: What situation?
Zh: 什么情况？

En: Of course. She bows slightly. "You have my full cooperation -- and the cooperation of the Wild Pines Group."
Zh: “当然。”她微微弯下腰。“我会全力合作的——野松公司也是一样。”

En: Of course. You have my full cooperation -- and the cooperation of the Wild Pines Group.
Zh: “当然。我会全力合作的——野松公司也是一样。”

En: Officer, I don't mean to be rude but are you sure your recent medical episode wasn't a *stroke*?
Zh: “警官，我不想这么无力，不过你确定自己最近遇到的医学插曲不是*中风*吗？”

En: You're being handled. Don't stand for it.
Zh: 你被操纵了。不要忍受这一点。

En: How about we concentrate on the dishonourable thing that happened, huh?
Zh: “不如我们集中在已经发生的不光彩的事情上吧，怎么样？”

En: Huh, what do you mean?
Zh: “呃，你是什么意思？”

En: Actually, I *might* have suffered a stroke. I'm not sure.
Zh: “其实，我*也许*真是中风了。我不确定。”

En: You stood there for the longest time! And it looked like you were having trouble forming a sentence.
Zh: “你在那儿站了很长时间。看起来你像是很难组织语言的样子。”

En: Oh, dear, that *is* serious. If this is in any way dangerous for your health, we can always resume our conversation later.
Zh: “天啦，那还*真是*挺严重的。如果这会让你的健康产生危险，我们可以之后再继续这次谈话。”

En: Don't worry. He'll be *fine*. It's all part of his unusual medical condition. He opens his notebook. "Continue, please. He has questions."
Zh: “别担心。他会*没事*的。这只是他反常身体状况的一部分。”他翻开笔记本。“请继续吧。他还有一些问题。”

En: I don't *think* it was a stroke.
Zh: “我*不觉得*这是中风。”

En: My mother had a stroke. She exhibited similar problems -- although not *quite* like yours.
Zh: “我母亲得过中风。她表现出了类似的问题——不过，跟你的*不太*一样。”

En: Of course. She slightly bows her head. "Please accept my apologies. What can I help you with?"
Zh: “当然。”她微微低下头。“请接收我的道歉。我能有什么帮你的吗？”

En: If you have doubts about your brain's blood supply, well... that's what we call a serious matter.
Zh: “如果你对大脑的血液供应有疑问……那就是我们所说的严重问题。”

En: A stroke could partially explain the *memory trouble* you've been experiencing.
Zh: “中风也许可以部分解释你遇到的*记忆问题*。”

En: Don't worry. He'll be *fine*. It's all related to his unusual medical episode. He opens his notebook. "Continue, please. He has questions."
Zh: “别担心。他会*没事*的。这都跟他那个不寻常的医学插曲有关。”他翻开笔记本。“请继续吧。他还有一些问题。”

En: Fuck it... Let's get back to work.
Zh: “去他的吧……我们继续工作。”

En: I called my station's lazareth and they told me to 'drink less.'
Zh: “我给我们分局的检疫所打过电话，他们告诉我‘少喝一点’。”

En: She quietly nods. "You have my full cooperation."
Zh: 她默默地点点头。“我会全力合作的。”

En: Drinking less sounds like a great start! She nods approvingly, but without condescension. "Go on -- you have my full cooperation."
Zh: “少喝点确实是个很好的开始！”她赞许地点点头，但是态度并不傲慢。“继续吧——我会全力合作的。”

En: No one lives forever, baby.
Zh: “没人能永远活下去，宝贝。”

En: Don't worry. It'll get better when you die.
Zh: 别担心。等你死了之后，它就会变好了。

En: Such a tough guy. She gives you a crooked smile. "You have my full cooperation, tough guy -- and that of the Wild Pines Group."
Zh: “真是个硬汉。”她朝你露出一个不自然的笑容。“我会全力合作的，硬汉——野松公司也一样。”

En: Everything. She smiles. "Right up to -- but not including -- *trade secrets*."
Zh: “所有事。”她笑了。“全部——但不包括——*商业秘密*。”

En: Wait -- what if I want to hear about *trade secrets*?
Zh: “等等——如果我想听这些*商业秘密*呢？”

En: What is your role in this, precisely?
Zh: “你在这里面到底扮演了一个什么样的角色？”

En: I have other questions.
Zh: “我还有别的问题。”

En: First you'd have to repeal the Emergencies Act of Trade and Aliments that gives me the right to silence. It's quite the octopus.
Zh: “首先，你需要废除紧急贸易法案和食品法案，因为它们赋予了我保持沉默的权利。它是一只触角伸的很长的章鱼。”

En: How do I repeal it then?
Zh: “那该怎么废除它呢？”

En: Why, by throwing off half a century of foreign domination under the Coalition! Unfortunately for you, the Coalition also leases you the right to police West Revachol...
Zh: “为什么这么问，当然是推翻半个世纪以来联盟控制的外国统治势力了！不过不幸的是，联盟还租给了你们管辖瑞瓦肖西部的权利……”

En: The octopus that straddles Revachol. The *Emergencies Act* is the cornerstone of post-war Revachol, inseparable from the world it created.
Zh: 触手遍及整个瑞瓦肖的章鱼。*紧急法案*构成了战后瑞瓦肖的基石，与它创造出的这个世界是密不可分的。

En: An octopus? I will *slay* it!
Zh: “一只章鱼？我会把它*杀掉*！”

En: Good luck -- It's only kept in place by the vested interests of half the civilized world, including your own.
Zh: “祝你好运——它是由半个文明世界的特权阶级支持的，当然也包括你们自己的。”

En: She nods: "I hope I can be of help."
Zh: 她点点头：“希望我能帮到你。”

En: What the ma'am means is that the Emergencies Act and the RCM both get their authority from the Coalition Government.
Zh: “这位女士的意思是，紧急法案和RCM的权利都是联盟政府赋予的。”

En: Wouldn't want to disturb an octopus. Better let it be.
Zh: “惹恼章鱼也不是好玩的。最好还是随它去吧。”

En: I am glad you see it that way. To repeal the act would mean *repealing* the Coalition government. The one that leases you the right to police West Revachol...
Zh: “很高兴你能这么想。废除法案可能意味着*废除*联盟政府——那个租给你们管辖瑞瓦肖西部权利的组织……”

En: You'd be shooting yourself in the foot, in other words.
Zh: 换句话说，你这是搬起石头砸自己的脚。

En: I'm afraid the Emergencies Act was put in place by the same Coalition government that leases the RCM the right to police West Revachol.
Zh: “恐怕颁布紧急法案的联盟政府同样租给了RCM管辖瑞瓦肖东部的权利。”

En: Did someone say *shooting yourself in the foot*?
Zh: 刚才有人说*搬起石头砸自己的脚*？

En: You would be surprised at the amount of damage I can inflict upon myself.
Zh: “如果你知道我对自己造成了多大的伤害，一定会很吃惊的。”

En: Leave it alone.
Zh: 别管它。

En: But -- I am derailing us. You wanted to know about the strike.
Zh: “但是——我跑题了。你想了解罢工的事。”

En: I imagine a man like you can take quite the *lashing*. She sizes you up. "But I believe we were talking about the *strike*."
Zh: “我能想象到，像你这样的男人应该能承受不小的*冲击*。”她打量着你。“不过我相信我们要说的是*罢工*的事。”

En: Tell me about Wild Pines. What do you do?
Zh: “跟我说说野松公司。你是做什么的？”

En: What do you think of Evrart?
Zh: “你觉得艾弗拉特怎么样？”

En: Tell me about this Union boss -- Mr. Claire.
Zh: “跟我说说这个工会领袖——克莱尔先生。”

En: What are their demands?
Zh: “他们有什么需求？”

En: That's all I need here. Let's change the topic.
Zh: “我想知道的就这么多。我们换个话题吧。”

En: Of course. She passes the thermal cup between her gloved hands. "How else can I help?"
Zh: “当然。”她拿着保温杯在戴着手套的手里来回递送。“还有什么能帮你的？”

En: Mr. Claire told him to, how did he put it? She pauses to compose herself...
Zh: “克莱尔先生告诉他，他怎么说的？”她停下来，稳定自己的情绪……

En: 'Fuck off, midget.' Gaumont is short of stature, you see.
Zh: “‘滚你妈的蛋，小矮子。’戈蒙个子比较矮，你懂的。”

En: Hmh. Okay then.
Zh: “嗯。那好吧。”

En: There are leaflets everywhere, and banners... what did they say again? Oh, yes...
Zh: “到处都是传单，还有横幅……他们说什么来着？哦，对了……”

En: I like it.
Zh: “我喜欢。”

En: Most of them probably don't know what that means.
Zh: “大部分人可能都不知道那是什么意思。”

En: I don't know what to think about that.
Zh: “我不知道该怎么想。”

En: An opening gambit, surely.
Zh: 当然了，这是个开场白。

En: Most of the workers probably don't know what that means.
Zh: 大部分工人可能都不知道那是什么意思。

En: Then you might also like their other slogan: 'DEMAND DEMOCRACY!'
Zh: “那你可能也会喜欢他们的标语：‘我们要民主’！”

En: Fortunately they explained it. Every time the Wild Pines Group makes a decision -- about *anything* -- it needs the signature of *each* of the 2,200 workers in its Martinaise terminal.
Zh: “幸好他们解释了一下。每次野松公司做决定的时候——关于*任何事情*的决定——都需要马丁内斯中转站2,200名工人的签名，*每一个人*的签名。”

En: In its defence, another said *DEMAND DEMOCRACY*. Pretty tame stuff compared to *EVERY WORKER -- A MEMBER OF THE BOARD*.
Zh: “按照它的辩解，另一种说法是*要求民主*。相比*所有工人——都是董事会的一员*来说，已经很平淡了。”

En: Personally, I agree -- It lacks the *pizzazzo* of *EVERY WORKER -- A MEMBER OF THE BOARD*.
Zh: “就个人而言，我同意——它缺少*所有工人都是董事会的一员*那种*激情*。”

En: Oh, yeah. That's a good one.
Zh: “是啊，那个确实不错。”

En: Nope. That's weak.
Zh: “没啊，那个太弱了。”

En: What does that even mean?
Zh: “那到底是什么意思啊？”

En: It's quite simple, you see. Every time the Wild Pines Group makes a decision -- about, what? About *anything* really -- it needs the signature of *each* of the 2,200 workers in its Martinaise terminal.
Zh: “你看，其实很简单。每当野松公司做决定的时候——关于，什么呢？其实基本上是*任何事情*的时候——都需要马丁内斯中转站2,200名工人的签名，*每一个人*的签名。”

En: Just so you understand, this is but one of *twenty-two* terminals owned by Wild Pines. Essentially -- not only are they *kings* of the company, they are also kings of the 72,000 employees of Wild Pines Group.
Zh: “你要明白，野松公司不仅仅拥有一个，而是*22*个中转站。从本质上讲——他们不仅仅要成为公司的*国王*，还想成为野松公司72,000雇员的国王。”

En: Personally, I don't think it has the same *pizzazzo* as *EVERY WORKER -- a MEMBER OF THE BOARD*.
Zh: “就个人而言，我觉得它缺少*所有工人都是董事会的一员*那种*激情*。”

En: Over-aggressive commie posturing.
Zh: “过于激进的康米派言论。”

En: Sounds like an opening gambit. (Proceed.)
Zh: “听起来像是个开场白。”（继续。）

En: What are you going to do? (Proceed.)
Zh: “你打算怎么做？”（继续。）

En: Forget it. I wanted to ask something else about the strike.
Zh: “忘了吧。我想问些关于罢工的其他问题。”

En: So I hoped. She nods. "But this type of opening is usually followed by a more reasonable offer. I have yet to hear one, just the same nonsensical slogan repeated over and over again..."
Zh: “我也希望如此。”她点点头。“不过这种类型的开场白通常都会跟着一个更加合理的要求。我目前还没听见过，只是同一个荒谬的口号，喊了一遍又一遍……”

En: I'm not sure. Naturally I assumed that was just their opening position, a hard-nosed tactic with a side of mockery. But there's been no follow up, just the same nonsensical slogan repeated over and over again...
Zh: “我不确定。我习惯性地以为这只是他们最初的立场，用了一种带有嘲弄意味的精明策略。不过后续什么都没了，只是同一个荒谬的口号，喊了一遍又一遍……”

En: She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。

En: The king is dead. Long live the workers!
Zh: “国王已死！工人当立！”

En: That's pretty funny, I have to admit.
Zh: “我必须承认，这还挺有意思的。”

En: The workers can't be kings. The king is king.
Zh: “工人不可能成为国王的。国王就是国王。”

En: That may well be. It's not up to me to decide who is king. But as negotiations go -- it's not a swell start.
Zh: “可能就像你说的那样吧。谁是国王轮不到我来决定。不过随着谈判的推进——这并不是一个愉快的开始。”

En: They are having a blast -- but how can they *afford* it? After four months my assumption was they would prefer a more *practical* solution.
Zh: “他们玩的很开心——但是他们怎么能*负担*的起呢？按照我的设想，4个月之后他们会倾向于一种更加*实际*的解决方案。”

En: What *we* do -- I'm afraid I don't speak for Wild Pines as a whole. It's a giant undertaking.
Zh: “应该是*我们*——恐怕我不能代表整个野松公司。它是一个巨型企业。”

En: There was a touch of discomfort there. She wants to merely *represent*.
Zh: 这里透露出一丝不安的情绪。她只是想做好*代表*而已。

En: So what do *they* do?
Zh: “那好吧，你们是*做什么*的？”

En: Fair enough. Tell me something else.
Zh: “有道理。再跟我说说别的吧。”

En: She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。

En: The Pines' *core competency* is logistics -- container shipping, freight, that sort of thing. She points to the small dots on the horizon. "See those airships there, blinking? Those are the shipping side of things..."
Zh: “野松公司的*核心竞争力*是物流——集装箱航运，货运，等等等等。”她指向水平线上的小点。“看见那边那些飞艇了吗，闪光的那些？那些就是跟海运有关的东西……”

En: Excuse me, the lieutenant looks up from his notes, "From whom did you *hear* about this lynching?"
Zh: “打扰一下，”警督抬起埋在笔记本里的头，“你是从谁那里*听说*私刑的事的？”

En: I first heard it from the *boia* at the gates,” her reply comes quick, “the one who's very name advertises his aversion to work. I think he said it was *Call Me Mañana*.
Zh: 她回复地很快：“最开始是在大门边的*暴徒*那里听到的——那个人的名字很好地宣传了他讨厌工作的态度。我记得他好像说了句*叫我马列拉*。”

En: No, wait. The chief negotiator heard it from a man at the gates? Surely the Wild Pines Group has more effective channels of information. After all, 2,200 people are on strike -- this is a big deal for them.
Zh: 不对，等等。首席交涉人从大门附近的一个男人那里听到的消息？野松公司肯定还有更有效的渠道来获取信息。毕竟，2,200人的罢工——这对他们来说可不是件小事。

En: ...then to the east, toward the harbour: "And that is the terminal. Another subdivision deals with energy -- oil and gas exploration. Offshore platforms."
Zh: ……然后转向东边，朝着港口的方向：“那里就是中转站。做能源生意的一个分部——负责石油和天然气勘探。海上平台。”

En: The Wild Pines Group is one of the original Revacholian *indotribes* -- companies awarded royal monopolies by the king (the suzerain) himself, centuries ago. The king is long gone, but several of the indotribes remain.
Zh: 野松公司的前身是瑞瓦肖*寡头*——几个世纪以前，由国王（封建主）本人授权的皇家垄断企业。国王早已不在，不过有些寡头还是留了下来。

En: Who are the other indotribes?
Zh: 其他寡头是谁？

En: How much money does Wild Pines have?
Zh: “野松公司有多少钱？”

En: Saint-Batiste, LUM, an unknown entity known as Brightest Star...
Zh: 圣巴蒂斯特，LUM，还有一个被称为闪耀之星的未知实体……

En: They started as an exploration and cargo fleet conducting trade between the Samaran and Insulindian isolas -- 250 years ago, when Pines' ships explored the South-Semenese and charted Lo Manthang on behalf of the suzerain.
Zh: “他们起初是一家从事勘探以及在萨马拉和伊苏林迪洲之间开展货运贸易往来的企业——那是250年前的事了，那个时候，野松公司代表宗主国探索了南塞美尼地区，还绘制出了罗马丹的地图。”

En: You're in *good* company, it seems.
Zh: “看起来你加入了一个*好公司*。”

En: Why, thank you. She does not register the real meaning of the remark.
Zh: “谢谢你。”她没有注意到言辞里真正的含义。

En: What does such a huge system want with a place like this? (Look around.)
Zh: “这个庞大的体系想在这里寻求些什么呢？”（环顾四周。）

En: She enjoys talking about the company. It would not hurt to inquire more.
Zh: 她很喜欢聊公司的事。再多问一点也无妨。

En: I'm not at liberty to discuss the company balance sheet, but I *can* tell you that last year the company booked more than 20 billion reál in revenue.
Zh: “我不能随意谈论公司的资产负债表，不过我*可以*告诉你，去年公司的收入是200亿雷亚尔。”

En: Got it. Tell me something else.
Zh: “明白了。跟我说说别的吧。”

En: What exactly is a *billion*?
Zh: “*亿*到底是什么？”

En: Wow.
Zh: “哇哦。”

En: That's *it*? I thought Wild Pines was supposed to be big time.
Zh: “就*这些*？我还以为野松公司有多了不起呢。”

En: Numbers like that mean nothing to me.
Zh: “那种数字对我来说毫无意义。”

En: It's a number, officer, a *big* one.
Zh: “那是一个数字，警官，*很大*的一个数字。”

En: What is it made of?
Zh: “它是什么组成的？”

En: It's made of one thousand millions -- a million is made of a thousand thousands.
Zh: “它是由1000个百万组成的——百万是由1000个1000组成的。”

En: Where does Wild Pines *get* all these billions?
Zh: “那野松公司是从哪里*弄到*这些亿的呢？”

En: And to think -- there are years when the group runs a *loss* of billions... She turns her eyes towards the wave rising against the sloop.
Zh: “想想吧——有些年头野松公司还会*损失*数十亿呢……”她把目光转移到拍打着帆船的海浪上。

En: And to think -- there are years when the group books *losses* in the billions... A wave hits the sloop, she grasps the mainstay for balance.
Zh: “想想吧——有些年头野松公司还会*损失*数十亿呢……”一阵海浪拍打在帆船上，她抓住支柱保持平衡。

En: Twenty two billion is a large number, but consider this -- the conglomerate employs 72,000 people. They all need wages... She turns her eyes towards the wave rising against the sloop.
Zh: “22亿是个很大的数字，不过考虑一下——企业雇佣了72,000员工。他们都要拿薪水……”她把目光转移到拍打着帆船的海浪上。

En: Twenty billion is a large number, but the conglomerate employs 72,000 people. They all need to be paid. Then there are capital improvements, interest payments... A wave hits the sloop, she grasps the mainstay for balance.
Zh: “200亿是个很大的数字，不过企业雇佣了72,000员工。他们都需要拿薪水。然后还有改良性资本支出，支付利息……”一阵海浪拍打在帆船上，她抓住繩索保持平衡。

En: Centuries of care, deliberation -- and *madness* -- have gone into this endeavour. Vessels pass through the Great Unrest to re-emerge with apricots in tow. The logic of the system is totalizing... It's taken *everything* from its employees to build it.
Zh: “几个世纪的忧虑，审慎——还有*疯狂*——都投入到这项事业之中。船只穿越大动荡的时代，满载着杏子再度起航。这个系统的逻辑联合成整体……要想把它建造起来，需要所有的雇员付出*一切*。”

En: I was not trying to impress you, I swear. The conglomerate employs 72,000 people, wages cover most of the revenue... A wave hits the sloop, she looks at it indifferently.
Zh: “我发誓，我并不是想打动你。公司雇佣了72,000员工，工资收入占据了绝大部分……”一阵海浪拍打在帆船上，她只是漠然地看着。

En: Yes, past a certain point numbers begin to seem *imaginary*. But they are quite real for the 72,000 employees who depend on Wild Pines for their pay checks...
Zh: “是的，过了某个点之后，数字似乎变得*虚幻*起来。不过对于72,000名依靠野松公司挣薪水的雇员来说，它们相当真实……”

En: She nods, forcefully: "There are no minor cogs in the system. Each terminal must be accounted for, lest the entire system break. Every hiccup in such a system means thousands lose their jobs -- the world over. I'm here to assure that doesn't happen."
Zh: 她用力点点头：“系统里的每个齿轮都很重要。每个中转站都必须完成自己的职责，不然整个系统就会崩溃。在这种系统里，每出现一个小问题就意味着数千人会丢掉他们的工作——全世界都是如此。我来这里就是为了保证这种事不会发生。”

En: A conglomerate the size of Wild Pines is like a shark -- if it stops moving and growing it will die. Then what becomes of those 72,000 families? It is a tremendous responsibility.
Zh: “野松公司这种规模的公司就像一头鲨鱼——如果停止运动和生长，它就会死去。那72,000人的家庭怎么办？这里的责任是巨大的。”

En: A conglomerate the size of the Wild Pines is like a shark -- if it stops moving it will die. Then what becomes of those 72,000 families? It's a tremendous responsibility.
Zh: “像野松公司这种规模的公司就像是一头鲨鱼——如果停止运动和生长，它就会死去。那72,000人的家庭怎么办？这里的责任是巨大的。”

En: Wild Pines employs 72,000 people, all of whom have families that depend on their salaries. It is a tremendous responsibility.
Zh: “野松公司雇佣了72,000名员工，每一个人都有依赖这份薪水的家庭。这里的责任是巨大的。”

En: That's all I needed to know -- about something else...
Zh: “我只需要知道这么多——关于别的问题……”

En: We built this district, she says, calmly.
Zh: “我们建造了这片区域，”她平静地说到。

En: All the best parts of it. Rue de Saint-Ghislaine with its bastions, the plaza's Meteoran mosaic. Even some of the old street lamps have been put back thanks to investments from the WP. She points behind you where the seawall rises.
Zh: “所有最好的部分。圣吉莱纳路和它的棱堡，广场的美特拉地砖。甚至有些旧街灯都是靠野松公司的投资装回去的。”她指向你背后，海堤升起的地方。

En: It probably helps to start out with a royal monopoly.
Zh: “从一个皇家垄断企业起步可能是有帮助的。”

En: You know more than you let on. She takes a sip and looks you over afresh. "Certainly it helped, but most of the original indotribes have failed or been absorbed. To survive Wild Pines had to grow and adapt. No suzerain did that."
Zh: “你知道的比你透露出来的要多。”她抿了一口茶水，又重新打量了你一番。“的确有帮助，不过原来那些寡头大部分都已经破产或是被吞并了。为了生存，野松公司必须不断发展和适应。没有宗主国这样做过。”

En: You mean aside from being the terminal's legal owners? She gives you a little smile. "Who are responsible for moving *eight* percent of the world's cargo?"
Zh: “你是说除了要求成为中转站的合法所有者以外？”她微微笑了笑。“你猜是谁负责运输全世界*8%*的货物呢？”

En: *We* -- there it is. She owns up to it.
Zh: *我们*——看。她承认了。

En: Eight percent of *all* cargo in the world? That's quite the endeavour indeed.
Zh: “世界上*所有*货物的8%？那还真是一项不小的事业。”

En: You don't keep it moving -- the workers do. The company is nothing without them.
Zh: “让它运作的不是你——而是工人。没了他们，公司什么也不是。”

En: Before Martinaise was swallowed by the industrial harbour, even before it was part of Revachol -- long before Terminal B was erected here -- the Pines built it as a resort for its Revacholian employees.
Zh: “在马丁内斯被工业港口吞食之前，甚至是在它成为瑞瓦肖的一部分之前——B中转站建成很久之前——野松公司把这里建成了一个瑞瓦肖雇员的度假村。”

En: A company getaway. For a weekend or a summer holiday. Then came the Revolution, but that's another matter... She takes a sip. "I'm here to make sure the Pines can fulfil their responsibilities to the place they *built*."
Zh: “公司休假。待上一周或者是一个暑假。然后爆发了大革命，不过那又是另外一回事了……”她抿了一口茶水。“我来这里是为了保证野松公司能够履行对这个地方——他们*建起来*的地方——的职责。”

En: She's being sarcastic. Don't take her performance literally.
Zh: 她是在挖苦你。别把她的表现当真了。

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En:
That's not what you really think, right?	“你并不是真的这么想的，对吧？”
0x0100004500007624	Dialogue Text	PIER / JOYCE INTRO	Joyce Messier	Of course not. Evrart is *fantastically* corrupt. I imagine he has a thick, viscous goo where you and I have blood."
Zh: “当然不是。艾弗拉特腐败的让人*难以置信*。我能想象，你和我的身体里流淌的是血，而他的身体里流淌的是浓稠又黏腻的毒液。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: He is the most corrupt individual I have ever seen. And I deal with men like him for a living. If there is anyone more venal, more irredeemably nepotistic -- then it's his twin brother, Edgar.
Zh: “他是我见过的所有人当中最腐败的一个。而且，我是为了谋生计才会跟这种人打交道的。如果说还有谁比他更腐败，更无可救药地喜欢拉帮结派——那就只有他的孪生兄弟，埃德加。”

En: Yes. Edgar looks *exactly* like his brother, except for that lazy eye. He also *talks* exactly like Evrart does. And when one's term as foreman is up, the other takes over.
Zh: “是的。埃德加看起来跟他的兄弟*基本上*一模一样，只不过是个弱视眼。他的*说话*方式跟艾弗拉特也是完全一样。当一个人的主席任期到期时，另外一个就会接替上来。”

En: It's how they circumvent the term limits, you see. With a funny little *switcharoo*. While in office they've embezzled god knows *how* much of their workers' dues.
Zh: “你看，他们就是这样规避任期限制的。用一个有趣的*小转变*。任职期间，天知道他们到底侵吞了*多少*工人的会费。”

En: He's not *that* bad.
Zh: “他也没*那么*坏吧。”

En: Evrart is a hero of the worker's movement.
Zh: “艾弗拉特是工人运动的英雄。”

En: That's Evrart, alright.
Zh: “艾弗拉特就是这样，没错。”

En: Wait, there are *two* of him?
Zh: “等等，有*两个*他吗？”

En: What about the Union itself, outside the brothers Claire?
Zh: “那工会本身呢，如果没有克莱尔兄弟的话？”

En: If you were to prick him with something sharp you could see it oozing out -- a knife, maybe? No -- a rapier!
Zh: 如果你想用什么尖锐的东西去戳他，就能看见那玩意渗出来——也许，用一把刀？不——用长剑！

En: You sound like you're about to take a rapier to him.
Zh: “听起来你想要拿剑去戳他。”

En: Oh heavens no! We get along just fine... Yet now that you mention it, I can't stop imagining that black treacle... Just dribbling down his double chin... She stops and smiles.
Zh: “天啦，当然没有！我们相处地还好……不过既然你提了一嘴，我忍不住去想象那些黑色的糖浆……从他的双下巴里滴落下来……”她停下来，笑了。

En: Is he that bad?
Zh: “他真有那么坏吗？”

En: The Débardeurs' Union was once a perfectly normal institution. Twenty years ago, anyway. It must *not* have been easy to establish under the Emergency Act, but they did it. I can respect that.
Zh: “装卸工会曾经是一个非常普通的组织。不过，那都是20年前的事了。在紧急法案的管辖下，能把它创建起来的确*不太*容易，不过他们还是做到了。我很尊重这一点。”

En: I remember hearing something about this Edgar.
Zh: “我记得好像听说过埃德加的事。”

En: Have you *seen* him?
Zh: “你*见过*他吗？”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: I'd rather you tell me about something else.
Zh: “我宁愿你跟我说点别的。”

En: Of course. She takes a sip from the thermal cup.
Zh: “当然可以。”她从保温杯里喝了一口茶水。

En: Edgar looks *exactly* like his brother, except for that lazy eye. He also *talks* exactly like Evrart does. And when one's term as foreman is up, the other takes over.
Zh: “埃德加看起来跟他的兄弟*基本上*一模一样，只不过是个弱视眼。他的*说话*方式跟艾弗拉特也是完全一样。当一个人的主席任期到期时，另外一个就会接替上来。”

En: Let's take this back to the strike.
Zh: “我们还是说回罢工吧。”

En: A mob, the lieutenant says succinctly. "The Débardeurs are a crime syndicate. Sad as it may be, I suspect we'll be forced to cooperate with them."
Zh: “一个黑帮。”警督直言不讳。“装卸工会是一个犯罪集团。不过可悲的是，我们被迫要跟他们合作。”

En: A mob, the lieutenant says succinctly. "The Débardeurs are a crime syndicate. Sad as it may be, we're forced to cooperate with them."
Zh: “一个黑帮。”警督直言不讳。“装卸工会是一个犯罪集团。不过可悲的是，我们被迫要跟他们合作。”

En: Organized labour at its best, as they say. Then something happened in the local chapter elections. The Brothers Claire came and transformed it into a... how do you say? She hesitates, looking for the right expression.
Zh: 她理了理自己的头发。“他们说，参加工会的工人状态是最好的。然后本地选举的时候，发生了一些事。克莱尔兄弟出现了，把它变成了一个……怎么说呢？”她有些犹豫，正在寻找最合适的表达方式。

En: Refreshingly honest, officer. She nods. "The company has tried appeasing in the past, but I'm afraid our concessions have only emboldened Evrart and his brother." She turns to you:
Zh: “你的诚实真让人感觉神清气爽，警官。”她点点头。“公司过去尝试过安抚，不过恐怕我们的让步只会让艾弗拉特和他的兄弟变得更大胆。”

En: An effective advocate for the rights of local workingmen.
Zh: “一个实实在在拥护本地工人权利的组织。”

En: Basically a socialist mob.
Zh: “基本就是个工人主义黑帮。”

En: I prefer not to have an opinion on these things.
Zh: “我宁愿不对这些事情发表意见。”

En: Why do you think so? Her voice is calm, there is only curiosity there.
Zh: “你为什么会这么想？”她的声音很平静，只是有一些好奇的成分。

En: What she calls *corruption* is simply an aesthetic category.
Zh: 她说的*腐败*只不过是一个美学范畴。

En: Thank you for being candid, she nods. "Sadly, Wild Pines have cooperated with what amounts to a crime syndicate for two decades. However much you feed the wolf -- the wolf always hungers."
Zh: “感谢你的这份坦率，”她点点头。“可悲的是，野松公司已经跟这个这个犯罪集团合作了近二十年来。不管你给狼喂多少肉——它们总是会饿。”

En: A giant leech sucking the life out of Revachol.
Zh: “一个巨大的吸血鬼，在吸取瑞瓦肖的生命力。”

En: Of course, officer.
Zh: “当然了，警官。”

En: Indeed. And a hungry one. She nods. "Sadly, Wild Pines have cooperated with what amounts to a crime syndicate for two decades. However much you feed the leech -- the leech always hungers."
Zh: “的确。还是个不知餍足的家伙。”她点点头。“可悲的是，野松公司已经跟这个这个犯罪集团合作了近二十年来。不管你给狼喂多少肉——它们总是会饿。”

En: Or a mob. She nods. "Sadly, Wild Pines have cooperated with what amounts to a crime syndicate for two decades. However much you feed the wolf -- the wolf always hungers."
Zh: “或者说是一个黑帮。”她点点头。“可悲的是，野松公司已经跟这个这个犯罪集团合作了近二十年来。不管你给狼喂多少肉——它们总是会饿。”

En: Between Evrart the Human Leech and Measurehead, this Union sounds like a *motley crew*. I'm into it. (Wink.)
Zh: “在人形吸血鬼艾弗拉特和测颅先生之间，工会听起来更像是一群*乌合之众*。我很感兴趣。”（眨眨眼。）

En: Sounds like they're All-Revachol. I'm All-Revachol, too. To hell with the rest.
Zh: “听起来他们是彻头彻尾的瑞瓦肖人。我也是一样。其他人就见鬼去吧。”

En: Talking in aesthetic categories is the privilege of the overclass. From what I've seen -- most people are just trying to survive.
Zh: “讨论美学范畴是上层阶级的特权。据我所见——大部分人只是想生存下去。”

En: Well... She looks across the bay, trying to find the words. "I suppose that's *one* way to look at it -- they're an interesting composition, albeit vile. But enough about Evrart -- what else can I do for you?"
Zh: “这个嘛……”她遥望对岸，试着搜索词汇。“我想这是看待问题的*一种*方式——他们是一个有趣的组合，虽然卑劣又无耻。艾弗拉特就说到这里吧——我还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Ha! She lets out a loud cackle. "I suppose you are, officer. *All*-Revachol. But enough about Evrart -- what else can I tell you about?"
Zh: “哈！”她大声笑了起来。“我想你也是，警官。*彻头彻尾*的瑞瓦肖人。不过艾弗拉特的事还是说到这里吧——你还有什么想知道的吗？”

En: Point taken. She falls silent. The crow's feet in the corner of her eye deepen. She looks to the village behind you, then to the sea. "But enough about Evrart -- what else can I do for you?"\n
Zh: “言之有理。”她陷入沉默，眼角的鱼尾纹又加深了几分。她看着你身后的城市，然后望向大海。“不过艾弗拉特的事还是说到这里吧——我还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Point taken. She falls silent. The crow's feet in the corner of her eye deepen. She looks to the city behind you, then to the coast. "But enough about Evrart -- what else can I do for you?"
Zh: “言之有理。”她陷入沉默，眼角的鱼尾纹又加深了几分。她看着你身后的城市，然后望向海岸。“不过艾弗拉特的事还是说到这里吧——我还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: They're good labour men. Can't fault them for a little *corruption*.
Zh: “他们是善良的工人。总不能因为一点*腐败*就挑剔他们吧。”

En: Ha! She throws her hair back. "A real Man of the Left. What else can I tell you about?"
Zh: “哈！”她把头发朝后一甩。“一个真正的左翼分子。你还有什么想知道的吗？”

En: Quite a few things, I'm afraid... She falls silent for a moment. Contemplating something.
Zh: “恐怕还有一些事……”她沉默了一会儿，思索着什么。

En: Worry lines crease her forehead, but it's more than worry. In her eyes, a hint of... is that *guilt*?
Zh: 担忧在她的额头上演变成皱纹，不过这不仅仅是担忧。在她的眼睛里，还有一点……那是*愧疚*吗？

En: Why is this necessary?
Zh: “为什么有这个必要？”

En: My name is Harrier Du Bois, from Precinct 41. I don't have a badge *yet*.
Zh: “我的名字叫哈里尔·杜博阿，来自41分局。我*暂时*还没有警徽。”

En: I'm Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Harrier Du Bois, from Precinct 41. (Give her your badge.)
Zh: “我是双重荣誉警督哈里尔·杜博阿，来自41分局。”（把你的警徽递给她。）

En: I just can't go around telling confidential information to strangers. I've signed a contract.
Zh: “我不能把机密信息随意泄露给陌生人。我签过协议的。”

En: But she *knows* you're police.
Zh: 不过她*知道*你是警察。

En: Then I'll find it somehow. Let's talk about something else until I do.
Zh: “那我就只能想办法找到它了。在那之前，我们先说点别的吧。”

En: Of course. She grabs the mainstay and leans toward you: "But before we do, officers -- let me be perfectly clear..."
Zh: “当然可以。”她抓住支柱，朝你的方向靠了过来。“不过在这之前，警官——让我说清楚一点……”

En: Of course. She grabs the mainstay and leans toward you. "But before we do, officer -- let me be perfectly clear..."
Zh: “当然可以。”她抓住支柱，朝你的方向靠了过来。“不过在这之前，警官——让我说清楚一点……”

En: A pleasure to meet you, Harry -- what happened to your badge?
Zh: “很高兴见到你，哈里——你的警徽怎么了？”

En: Awkwardness washes over the conversation. The woman doesn't like this turn of events.
Zh: 尴尬淹没了这次谈话。女人不喜欢事态的转变。

En: 'Twas stolen by some *artist*, surely. Best to pin the blame on them. Everyone *knows* you can't trust artists.
Zh: 它被某些*艺术家*给偷走了，没错。只要责怪他们就最好不过了。所有人都*知道*，艺术家是不能相信的。

En: The situation is *extremely* volatile. The sooner we can speak about this lynching business, the better for all of us.
Zh: “现在的情况*极度*不稳定。我们越早谈论这个私刑的话题，对于我们所有人来说就越好。”

En: The thing is -- I don't have a badge at this moment.
Zh: “问题是——我现在还没有警徽。”

En: What happened to it, detective?
Zh: “你的警徽怎么了，警探？”

En: Pleased to meet you, lieutenant, she inspects the piece of blue plastic, the faded green pearls of her eyes scanning left to right.
Zh: “很高兴见到你，警督，”她端详着那个蓝色的塑料，浅绿色的眼珠子左右扫视着。

En: Quickly, methodically, like an electric printing machine.
Zh: 快速，有条不紊，就像一台电子打印机。

En: She's memorizing your badge number.
Zh: 她在记忆你的警徽编号。

En: She hands it back to you: "It is a relief to see a double-yefreitor here. The situation is precarious to say the least. It needs a quick solution, or we will have a blood bath on our hands."
Zh: 她把警徽交还给你：“能在这里见到一位双重荣誉警督，真是让人感到安慰。最起码可以说，现在的情况很不稳定。需要一个快速的解决方案，否则我们即将面临一场血浴事件。”

En: They said it was for a *performance*. You know how artists are nowadays.
Zh: “他们说这是一场*表演*。你也知道当今的艺术家是怎么回事。”

En: Remember when I told you I literally cannot remember even the most basic facts about reality?
Zh: “还记得我跟你说过，我其实连现实最基础的事实都想不起来了吗？”

En: Remember when my partner told you I'd recently suffered from an *unusual* medical episode? My lost badge is related to it.
Zh: “还记得我的搭档跟你说过，我最近遭遇了一次*不寻常*的医学插曲吗？那枚遗失的警徽就跟此有关。”

En: I've recently suffered from an *unusual* medical episode. My lost badge is related to it.
Zh: “我最近遭遇了一次*不寻常*的医学插曲。我那枚遗失的警徽就跟它有关。”

En: How can I forget? She tilts her head to the side.
Zh: “我怎么会忘记呢？”她把脑袋歪向一旁。

En: One of those facts is where my badge is. Another is my name.
Zh: “其中一个遗忘的事实就是，我的警徽在哪里。还有就是我的名字。”

En: One of those facts is where my badge is.
Zh: “其中一个遗忘的事实就是，我的警徽在哪里。”

En: I was hoping your *condition* was rhetorical, detective. A questioning technique.
Zh: “我希望你的*状况*只是一种夸张的说法，警探。一种讯问技巧。”

En: It's not.
Zh: “不是的。”

En: I wish it were.
Zh: “我希望它是。”

En: Perhaps it is. But it's also true that I don't remember where my badge is.
Zh: “也许是吧。不过我记不起来警徽在哪也是真的。”

En: You mean someone *stole* your badge? Her brow is slightly arched.
Zh: “你是说有人*偷走*了你的警徽？”她的眉毛微微拱起。

En: I'd rather not get into it, but yes: a bunch of clowns -- and some *arlecchinos* -- stole my badge.
Zh: “我也不太想提，不过是的：一群小丑——还有一些*丑角*——偷走了我的警徽。”

En: I don't, in fact. She shakes her head. "I'm afraid what I need to tell you is wholly unrelated to art. I really need to discuss it, however. With the RCM -- and I need your badge for that."
Zh: “事实上，我不知道。”她摇摇头。“恐怕我要告诉你的事情跟艺术毫无关联。不过，我们真的需要讨论一下，跟RCM——为此，我需要你的警徽。”

En: Some *arlecchinos* you say? She shakes her head. "I'm afraid what I need to tell you is anything but *fun* I really need to discuss it, however. With the RCM -- and I need your badge for that."
Zh: “你说一些*丑角*？”她摇摇头。“恐怕我要告诉你的事情一点也不*有趣*。不过，我们真的需要讨论一下，跟RCM——为此，我需要你的警徽。”

En: I... see... She looks increasingly worried. "So are you saying you *lost* your badge during the course of this *episode*?"
Zh: “我……明白了……”她看起来越来越担心了。“所以你是说，在这个*插曲*过程中，你*遗失*了你的警徽？”

En: It's possible. After a night of heavy drinking I lost all memory of my life and the world.
Zh: “有这个可能。在严重酗酒一晚之后，我失去了有关我的生活和这个世界的一切记忆。”

En: Oh, *dear*... she sighs with confusion, sadness even. "Some kind of encephalopathic amnesia? I don't even know how to respond..."
Zh: “哦，*天啦*……”她叹了口气，带着一些困惑，甚至是悲伤。“某种脑病健忘症吗？我都不知道该怎么回应了……”

En: Oh, *dear*... She sighs with compassion, sadness even. "I suppose this does explain some of the more *curious* turns in our conversation."
Zh: “哦，*天啦*……”她叹了口气，带着一些怜悯，甚至是悲伤。“我猜这就解释了我们的对话中为什么会出现那些比较*古怪*的转折。”

En: I *do* believe you -- naive as that may sound. I simply can't imagine what you'd gain by *faking* such a condition.
Zh: “我*确实*相信你——不管听起来到底有多天真。我简直难以想象，你*假装*出这幅摸样到底能有什么好处。”

En: She must have been suspecting something for a while now.
Zh: 她一定是怀疑了有一阵子了。

En: As I said, ma'am, the lieutenant interjects, "his technique may be *very* unconventional. But he *is* an officer of the RCM."
Zh: “就像我说过的，女士，”警督插了一嘴，“他的技巧可能会*非常*不寻常。不过他*的确*是RCM的一名警官。”

En: I have sympathy for your *highly* unusal condition, sir. And I believe you. But as it stands I cannot share any confidential information until I've seen *both* your badges.
Zh: “我很同情你*非常*不寻常的遭遇，先生。而且我也相信你。不过即便如此，在没有看见*你们俩*的警徽之前，我还是不能跟你透露任何机密信息。”

En: I have sympathy for your *highly* unusual conundrum, sir. And I believe you. But as it stands I cannot share any confidential information until I've seen a badge.
Zh: “我很同情你*非常*不寻常的遭遇，先生。而且我也相信你。不过即便如此，在没有看见警徽之前，我还是不能跟你透露任何机密信息。”

En: I understand the dictates of protocol, ma'am, but... is there no way around it?
Zh: “我能理解协议的规定，女士，不过……就没有别的办法了吗？”

En: I'm afraid not, lieutenant. These are my employer's orders...
Zh: “恐怕没有，警督。这是我的雇主下达的命令……”

En: Hang on, she's a professional *negotiator*. She should be open to some sort of mutually beneficial arrangement.
Zh: 等一下，她是个专业的*交涉人*。她应该会接受某种互惠互利的约定。

En: However, she says, with a touch of regret, "This isn't about my feelings. There's also protocol to consider. I'm afraid I can't say any more until I've seen that badge."
Zh: “不过，”她略带歉意的说到，“这跟我个人的感情无关。还有协议的事需要考虑。恐怕在看到警徽之前，我不能继续说些什么了。”

En: What kind of arrangement would it be?
Zh: 那会是什么样的约定呢？

En: I'm afraid I can't say any more until we've taken care of formal protocol.
Zh: “恐怕在处理好正式协议之前，我不能继续说些什么了，警官。”

En: How do I negotiate my way out of this?
Zh: 我该如何通过谈判来解决这个问题呢？

En: A dirty alliance?
Zh: 一个肮脏的联盟？

En: Sympathy for your amnesia.
Zh: 对你失忆的同情。

En: Pities your boat failure.
Zh: 同情你的船只故障

En: I mean favours for favours. A *dirty* alliance of some sort.
Zh: 我的意思是你帮我我帮你。某种*肮脏*的联盟。

En: Consider this -- perhaps she has been planning it all along? That you convince *yourself* into colluding with her?
Zh: 考虑一下——也许她一直都是这样计划的？你会说服*自己*跟她勾结？

En: Perhaps she even knew beforehand of your affliction? A sudden pinch of paranoia comes over you...
Zh: 也许她事先就了解过你的苦恼？一阵突然起来的妄想向你袭来……

En: Easy. Just offer her a *favour*, like I said -- nice and dirty.
Zh: 简单。只要提出给她*帮个忙*，就像我说过的——美妙又肮脏。

En: She's a negotiator. Just float a favour at her. Insinuate.
Zh: 她是一个交涉人。给她帮个忙。巧妙地暗示一下。

En: *Feelings* will guide the way.
Zh: *感觉*会为你指引方向。

En: Surely there's some other way to demonstrate our law enforcement credentials?
Zh: “当然了，还有别的方法能展示我们的执法资格。”

En: Surely there's some other way to demonstrate my law enforcement credentials.
Zh: “当然了，还有别的方法能展示我的执法资格。”
