En: But, madam, I need to know about this lynching. It's *very* important to me. It's the case I'm solving.
Zh: “不过，女士，我需要了解私刑的事。对我来说这很*重要*。我目前正在调查这个案子。”

En: You have so much else. I have only this. (Spread your hands) "This is the entirety of my existence."
Zh: “你还有拥有很多东西。而我只有这个。”（摊开你的双手）“这是我存在的全部。”

En: She's silent. The sloops sail is flapping in the wind.
Zh: 她沉默了。船帆在海风中翻飞。

En: She's silent. The wind flaps the sail above her.
Zh: 她沉默了。海风吹起她头顶的船帆。

En: This boat, for example. And a home somewhere! I *only* have this case.
Zh: “打个比方，这艘船。还有你在某处的家！而我，*只有*这个案子。”

En: Officer... The carbon fibre hull creaks.
Zh: “警官……”碳纤维船身嘎吱作响。

En: You don't understand. It's not like a side-case for me. This is my *main thing*.
Zh: “你不明白。它对我来说不是什么无关紧要的小事。这就是我*最主要的事情*。”

En: Trust me, officer, I am *fully committed* to resolving this impasse. This isn't some *side gig* for me.
Zh: “相信我，警官，我也是*全心全意地投入*，想要解决这个僵局。对我来说，这也不是什么*临时工作*。”

En: I don't remember *anything* except this lynching. There is only this coast -- and this lynching.
Zh: “除了私刑，我记不得*任何事情*。只有这片海岸——还有这个私刑。”

En: I will be frank with you. She puts down her thermal cup. "If I'm going to break protocol I need to be able to justify it to my superiors. They're going to want to see something very *tangible*."
Zh: “我老实跟你说吧。”她放下了手中的保温杯。“如果要我打破协议，我必须跟上级证明这么做的正当性。他们想要看到某些非常*具体*的东西。”

En: Reports from inside Terminal B suggest it's a hub for the local drug trade. This is an open secret in Martinaise. The Union *controls* the terminal, so it goes to reason...
Zh: “B中转站的报告显示，那里是一个本地毒品交易中心。这是马丁内斯公开的秘密，工会*控制着*中转站，这就是他们的动机……”

En: You're in, but expect her to drive a hard bargain.
Zh: 你成功了，不过她可能会极力讨价还价的。

En: *Or*... She picks the cup back up. "You can recover your badge -- though if I may be blunt with you, it sounds like that may be a lost cause."
Zh: “*或者*……”她又拿起了保温杯。“你可以找回你的警徽——如果我可以直言不讳的话，这听起来可能像是一个注定的败局。”

En: She wants you to help her gain leverage to re-start negotiations with the Union.
Zh: 她想让你帮她搞到与工会重启谈判的筹码。

En: You're doing it! Despite your own best efforts you're still getting in, somehow...
Zh: 你做到了！尽管你已经尽了最大的努力，不过你还是摸着门路了……

En: Detective, the lieutenant closes his notebook. "A word in private before we continue."
Zh: “警探，”警督合上笔记本。“继续之前，能不能先跟你私下聊两句。”

En: Excuse us for a moment, madam. [Leave.]
Zh: “请给我们一点时间，女士。”[离开。]

En: Tell me again about this drug trafficking.
Zh: “再跟我说说这个贩毒活动。”

En: I've changed my mind about the drug trafficking case.
Zh: “关于贩毒案，我改变主意了。”

En: Tell me again about this drug trafficking.
Zh: “再跟我说说这个贩毒活动。”

En: Okay. Tell me about this alleged drug trafficking.
Zh: “好吧。跟我说说这个所谓的贩毒活动。”

En: It's quite straightforward. Someone is using Terminal B to smuggle raw ingredients from the Samaran isola into Revachol with the Union's blessing. Wild Pines has suspected it for years.
Zh: “其实有挺简单的。在工会的支持下，有人利用B中转站从萨马拉洲向瑞瓦肖走私原材料。野松公司几年前就怀疑这一点了。”

En: Wait! She just admitted that the lynching and the strike are connected.
Zh: 等等！她刚刚承认私刑和罢工是有关联的。

En: So the strike is *connected* to the lynching?
Zh: “所以罢工跟私刑是有*关联*的？”

En: Yes, I believe there is a connection -- but that's a subject for *later*. Her eyes narrow.
Zh: 她眯起眼睛。“是的，我相信其中是有联系——不过这个话题*之后*再谈。”

En: She is resolute in this, yet you sense that she *wants* to tell you. You'll have to earn her trust first.
Zh: 她在这一点上很坚定，不过你能感觉到她*想要*告诉你。不过首先，你要赢得她的信任。

En: The money I owe is *so much* less than that.
Zh: “我欠的钱比那个可要*少多了*。”

En: What kind of *ingredients* are we talking about here?
Zh: “我们这里说的是什么类型的*原材料*？”

En: Ingredients for *what*, ma'am?
Zh: “*什么*的原材料，女士？”

En: Meth- and dextro-amphetamine, GBL and various synthetic psychedelics. Honestly, it might be quicker to say what you *can't* make from the stuff.
Zh: “甲基和右旋安非他命，丁内酯，还有各种各样的合成迷幻剂。老实说，告诉你它们*不能*合成哪些药物反而更简短些”

En: And you want us to investigate. (Move on.)
Zh: “所以你希望我们能够调查。”（继续。）

En: Wild Pines seems to be *well apprised* of the local drug trade, ma'am. Do you mean to say the Union also *produces* the product? Sells drugs, I mean?
Zh: “野松公司似乎对本地的贩毒活动很*熟悉*啊，女士。你是想说工会也在*生产*产品吗？我的意思是，贩毒？”

En: Yes. But you won't get anything out of Evrart and the Dockworkers' Union. Still -- she raises her bony finger, "every chain has its weak link."
Zh: “是的。不过你从艾弗拉特和码头工人工会那里是问不出任何东西来的。不过——”她举起瘦骨嶙峋的手指，“每根链条都有薄弱的环节。”

En: The hand-off. The motor lorries at the roundabout.
Zh: 发包。环岛的那些货車。

En: The lorries.
Zh: “那些货車。”

En: The *lorries,* she stresses. "Someone needs to move the ingredients from the harbour into the city. Once they reach Jamrock they're distributed to a network of local manufacturers, well beyond our grasp. But in transit they are *vulnerable*."
Zh: “是*货車*，”她强调到。有人需要把原材料从港口运进城市。一旦东西到达加姆洛克，他们就会分发给本地的制造商网络，这就远远超出了我们的掌控范围。不过在运输途中，它们是很*脆弱*的。”

En: *Precisely*. Someone needs to move the ingredients *from* the harbour into the city. Once they reach Jamrock they're distributed to a network of local manufacturers, well beyond our grasp. But in transit they are *vulnerable*.
Zh: “*没错*。有人需要把原材料*从*港口运进城市。一旦他们到达加姆洛克，他们就会分发给本地的制造商网络，这就远远超出了我们的掌控范围。不过在运输途中，它们是很*脆弱*的。”

En: How convenient that they're stranded like beached whales at that roundabout.
Zh: 他们就像搁浅的鲸鱼一样被困在环岛，还真是便利的不得了呢。

En: Why didn't you come to the RCM earlier?
Zh: “你之前为什么没有找RCM？”

En: It's no coincidence the lorries are stranded there like that, is it?
Zh: “货車那样困在环岛不是个巧合，对吗？”

En: What proof do you have that the Union is involved?
Zh: “你有什么证据能证明工会也参与其中了？”

En: Actually, let's discuss something else for now.
Zh: “其实，我们还是先讨论点别的吧。”

En: Her irises are light green, like the River Esperance in bright daylight, upstream where it's clearer.
Zh: 她的虹膜是浅绿色的，就像明亮日光下的埃斯佩兰斯河，上游比较清澈的地方。

En: I'll be explicit: if you find this driver, I will share company secrets with you.
Zh: “我就直说了吧：如果你找到那个司机，我就把商业机密告诉你。”

En: We *did*, on more than one occasion. Apparently there's some sort of inter-precinct disagreement about whose jurisdiction this area falls under.
Zh: “我们*找过*，还不止一次。很明显，这个区域到底属于谁的管辖范围的问题，在各分局之间有些分歧。”

En: We know the company has launched its own probe into the Union's alleged involvement -- we also know it's come up empty. It's not just the RCM -- *no one's* been able to find any hard evidence.
Zh: “我们知道公司对工会涉嫌参与其中的行为已经开始了自己的调查——我们还知道结果什么也没找到。不仅仅是RCM——*没人*能够找出任何确凿的证据。”

En: Well, she smiles and points toward the roundabout, "here's your chance, officers."
Zh: “这个嘛，”她笑了笑，指向环岛的方向，“你们的机会来了，警官。”

En: No. We asked East Motor Tract to raise the drawbridge. The road company is a partner of one of our subsidiaries. However... She pauses, looking to the sea.
Zh: “不是。我们让东高速公路把吊桥抬起来的。公路公司是我们其中一个子公司的合作伙伴。不过……”她停了下来，看着大海。

En: This is a limited-time opportunity. Once the complaint has been processed by the trade committee they'll have no choice but to lower the drawbridge -- and the operation will continue. She looks north.
Zh: “这是一个有时限的机会。一旦投诉被贸易委员会受理，他们就不得不放下吊桥——然后行动就会继续。”

En: Thousands of litres of raw ingredients will pour onto the streets of Revachol. Not the East, across the river, but the West. The vulnerable, the weary...
Zh: 她看向北方。“几千公升的原材料将会涌现在瑞瓦肖的街道上。不是东边，河流对岸，而是西边。这个脆弱的地方，疲倦的地方……”

En: How do you think they're financing this strike? There are thousands of unpaid dockworkers going strong for the fourth month straight.
Zh: “你觉得他们是怎么为罢工筹措资金的？有几千个没领薪水的码头工人已经连续罢工4个月了。”

En: I've heard they shook down local business owners. Drove a few out of business to finance it.
Zh: “我听说他们在勒索本地的企业老板。还把一些人搞到破产来资助罢工。”

En: With all due respect to these desert cacti, the contents of a few cash registers cannot provide for *two-thousand* men. The local businesses can scarcely provide for themselves.
Zh: “尽管我很尊重那些沙漠仙人掌，不过几台收银机里的那点玩意是不够供养*两千多个*人的。本地商业甚至只能勉强养活自己。”

En: There was a shakedown of local businesses preceding the strike. Many were squeezed to bankruptcy to fund it.
Zh: “在罢工开始之前，本地的企业老板遭到了勒索。很多人被压榨到破产来为罢工提供经济支持。”

En: It's true they drove a number of the local businesses to bankruptcy preparing for this venture. I've talked to a few business owners -- it was quite the shakedown. But the contents of a few cash registers cannot provide for *two thousand* men.
Zh: “为了准备这次的冒险活动，他们把一些本地企业逼到破产是不争的事实。我跟几个企业老板聊过——勒索的很是凶残。不过几台收银机里的那点玩意是不够供养*2000*多个人的。”

En: So you think the strike is being funded with source ingredients for drugs?
Zh: “所以，你觉得罢工的资金来源是毒品原材料？”

En: Precisely. Smuggled out of that very gate at night, most likely. Then loaded onto lorries and driven to Jamrock. You simply need to find *one* driver who'll open up to you.
Zh: “没错。极有可能是半夜从那道大门里走私出去的。然后装上货車，前往加姆洛克。你只需要找到*一个*愿意向你敞开心扉的司机就够了。”

En: It sounds like she tried looking into it herself. But she's clearly not the type your typical lorryman would confide in.
Zh: 听上去她之前已经试过了。不过她显然不是那种典型的货車司机会信赖的人。

En: It sounds like she tried looking into it herself, though she's clearly not the type your typical lorryman would confide in.
Zh: 听上去她自己已经试过了，不过她显然不是那种典型的货車司机会信赖的人。

En: A cold breath rushes in from the bay, making its way down the alleyways and potholed streets -- from Martinaise to Jamrock. In the traffic you hear the clinking of glass vials, concealed beneath tarpaulin. Or is it just your teeth chattering in the cold?
Zh: 海湾袭来一股冷空气，朝着小巷和坑坑洼洼的街道直奔而去——从马丁内斯一路奔向加姆洛克。在来来往往的車辆中，你听见隐藏在防水油布之下的玻璃瓶在叮当直响。或者，只是你的牙齿在寒冷中打颤？

En: (Shiver.) "Fucking misery."
Zh: （颤抖。）“妈的太惨了。”

En: A lot of it, she nods. "It comes in from large Samaran factories. In South Safre, Siigay and Hsin-Yao. The literage they must get from this terminal alone must be *oceanic*."
Zh: “的确，”她点点头。“东西来自萨马拉的大型工厂。位于南萨弗里，希依盖和赫辛欧。他们从这个中转站拿到的东西肯定是*越洋*的。”

En: The lorries are still stuck at the roundabout -- if the police is asking, one of the drivers might admit to it.
Zh: “货車还被困在环岛——如果警察讯问，某个司机也许会承认。”

En: She simply nods.
Zh: 她简单地点点头。

En: We will take this case, probe the drivers, see what it yields. (Accept.)
Zh: “我们会接下这起案件，刺探一下那些司机，看看会有什么结果。”（接受。）

En: I'll look into it, probe the drivers, see what it yields. (Accept.)
Zh: “我会调查的，刺探一下那些司机，看看会有什么结果。”（接受。）

En: We already have some suspicions that one of the drivers was present at the lynching.
Zh: “我们已经怀疑有一个司机也许在私刑现场出现过。”

En: The two *might* even be connected.
Zh: “这两件事甚至*有可能*是相关的。”

En: Or not -- though if you have evidence to the contrary I'm *eager* to hear it.
Zh: “或者没有——不过如果你有相反的证据，我很*愿意*听听。”

En: As eager as I am to *share* it, lieutenant, once the job is done.
Zh: “虽然我也很愿意跟你们*分享*，警督，等你们先把工作做完吧。”

En: There's no way we can take this case. (Refuse.)
Zh: “我们绝对不会接下这个案子的。”（拒绝。）

En: There's no way I can take this case. (Refuse.)
Zh: “我绝对不会接下这个案子的。”（拒绝。）

En: I am very sad to hear that, officer. Her voice is ice cold and laced with worry. "If you change your mind, please let me know. If not, good luck finding your badge -- and your memory."
Zh: “听到这个消息我很难过，警官。”她的声音冷若冰霜，还夹杂着一丝忧虑。“如果你改变主意，请直接告诉我，如果没有，祝你能顺利找到你的警徽——还有你的记忆。”

En: In the meantime, I'll still try to assist you any way I can. This disagreement is peanuts compared to what happens if you fail your main investigation.
Zh: “与此同时，我还是会尽一切可能地协助你。如果你的主要调查失败，那后果跟这点分歧相比，根本微不足道。”

En: It may come to nothing, or it may just blow the case wide open. I can keep the drawbridge up for a few more days at least. You should have the time you need.
Zh: “可能什么也找不到，或者可能会让案子大白于天下。我至少还可以让吊桥再抬起个几天。这些时间对你来说应该足够了。”

En: In the meantime, let me know if there's any other way I may be of assistance. She takes another long sip from her seemingly bottomless thermal cup.
Zh: “与此同时，如果我还能别的方面帮上什么忙，请务必告诉我。”她灌了一大口茶水，似乎把保温杯喝了个见底。

En: A waft of flowers and caramel reaches you where you're standing.
Zh: 一阵鲜花和焦糖的味道飘到了你现在的位置。

En: Until you come up with something at that intersection I'm afraid I have little else to tell you -- but I'll try. Ask.
Zh: “除非你在十字路口那里有什么发现，不然恐怕我没什么可以告诉你的了——不过我会试一下。问吧。”

En: I spoke with the lorrymen at the roundabout.
Zh: “我跟环岛的货車司机聊过了。”

En: On the contrary, officer, she smiles. "There are yet camionneurs you have not talked to. And don't look so surprised. In a time like this it would be strange if Wild Pines *didn't* have eyes on the harbour."
Zh: “正好相反，警官，”她笑了。“还有一些货車司机你没有聊过。别摆出一副惊讶的样子。这种时期，如果野松公司在港口*没有*眼线的话，那才叫奇怪呢。”

En: Word has travelled, yes, but nothing of real substance has surfaced yet, I gather? She smiles, then explains: "Wild Pines has eyes on the intersection -- but not ears."
Zh: “是的，消息传开了，不过真正实质性的问题没有浮出水面，对吧？”她笑了，然后解释到：“野松公司在十字路口有眼线——不过耳朵就没有了。”

En: One of the tall buildings overlooking the roundabout -- it would give them a read on the entire quarter.
Zh: 一栋高大的建筑俯瞰着整个环岛——这能让他们了解整个地区的状况。

En: They must have someone in an overlook position, near the gates...
Zh: 他们在可以俯瞰的位置安排了什么人，就在大门附近……

En: It appears we're being monitored every step we take, colleague. The lieutenant shrugs. "Did we have any other business here?"
Zh: “似乎我们走的每一步都被监视了，同事。”警督耸耸肩。“我们在这里还有其他事情吗？”

En: Yes, my eyes on the harbour have sent word to that effect -- what have you discovered?
Zh: “是的，我在港口的眼线传来的消息大概也是那个意思——你发现什么了吗？”

En: In any case, it's a relief to know someone has looked into it. If I may ask -- will there be an official investigation? I assume you discovered there *is* an operation...
Zh: “无论如何，知道有人正在调查总是个安慰。如果不介意我这么问的话——你们会进行官方调查吗？我猜你已经发现*的确*存在着运营活动……”

En: Wait, where exactly are these *eyes* located?
Zh: “等等，这些*眼线*到底在哪里？”

En: We have discovered enough to conclude -- for now.
Zh: “我们的发现已经足够得出结论——不过是暂时的。”

En: It doesn't really matter -- and I do apologize for the surveillance. Wild Pines can't afford to be blind at a time like this.
Zh: “其实真的不重要——我必须要为监视的事道歉。在这种时期，野松公司承担不起盲目的风险。”

En: Yes -- and? Will you start an official investigation?
Zh: “是的——所以呢？你们会开始官方调查吗？”

En: She's trying to conceal her excitement, but the slight glimmer in her green eyes tells you otherwise.
Zh: 她试图掩饰自己的兴奋，不过那双淡绿色眼睛里的闪光告诉你并非如此。

En: Hold on, you seem anxious to know. Why's that?
Zh: “等等，你似乎急着想知道。为什么？”

En: I've kept my eye on the camionneurs -- even tried to get to know one. It's a *mystery* I'm already invested in: which one of them, if any, could organize such a complex operation?
Zh: “我一直盯着那些货車司机——甚至试过去结交一个。这是我寄托过希望的一个*秘密*：如果有的话，他们中的哪一个可能组织如此复杂的业务？”

En: There will be an investigation, that's all I can say.
Zh: “我只能说，会有调查的。”

En: You sent us on a wild goose chase. There's nothing to investigate.
Zh: “你这是让我们白费时间。根本没什么好调查的。”

En: *If* there is an investigation it will be part of an ongoing operation -- subject to confidentiality. I am sure you understand.
Zh: “*如果*有调查的话，它也会是现行调查的一部分——需要严格保密。你肯定能理解吧。”

En: Of course, detectives, she says, her tone more cautious suddenly. "In any case, you've held up your end of our arrangement. I trust you with the rest. Now it's my turn..."
Zh: “当然了，警探们，”她说到，口气突然变得更加谨慎了。“无论如何，你已经在履行协议中你那部分诺言。剩下来的部分，我相信你也能完成。现在轮到我了……”

En: I wouldn't normally break protocol like this, but the situation demands it. If you don't solve this murder I'm afraid we may have a blood bath on our hands.
Zh: “一般来说，我是不会这样打破协议的，不过这也是形势所迫。如果你不能破获这起凶杀案，恐怕我们将会面临一场血浴的大屠杀。”

En: I don't believe that for one moment, officer. There is a pause, then her stern expression clears. "I'm just going to assume that departmental regulations prevent you from saying anything more..."
Zh: “我一点也不相信你的话，警官。”她停顿了一下，然后她严肃的表情放晴了。“我会假设警局的规章制度不允许你再继续说下去了……”

En: In any case, you've held up your end of our arrangement. Now it's my turn...
Zh: “无论如何，你已经在履行协议中你那部分诺言。现在轮到我了……”

En: Okay. I thank you for sharing that much with me. She exhales, as though a weight has been lifted from her shoulders. "My employer thanks you as well..."
Zh: “好吧，感谢你能跟我分享这么多信息。”她呼出一口气，就像从肩头卸下了一副重担。“我的雇主也很感谢你……”

En: The lieutenant is about to interject. Cut him off. This decision should be yours.
Zh: 警督想要插嘴。打断他。这个决定权在你。

En: Yes -- I should decide.
Zh: 是的——我应该做决定。

En: Let the lieutenant handle it.
Zh: 让警督来处理吧。

En: Exactly -- but what?
Zh: 没错——但是什么呢？

En: The scabs at the gate -- did you put them there?
Zh: “大门附近的工贼——是你安排的吗？”

En: The... *scabs*? She raises her brow. "You mean the huddled masses of Jamrock, come to plead for work where the Union refuses to?"
Zh: “你说……*工贼*？”她扬起眉头。“你是说加姆洛克的芸芸众生，到工会拒绝他们的地方请求工作机会？”

En: Don't let her answer it herself.
Zh: 别让她自问自答。

En: If they were organized by Wild Pines or its affiliates then it would be a company secret. I could not share it with you -- not right now at least...
Zh: “如果他们是野松公司或者其附属公司组织的，那这就是一个公司机密。我可以跟你分享这个秘密——不过至少现在不行……”

En: No, I mean scabs.
Zh: “不，我是说工贼。”

En: Let's call them strikebreakers -- you put them there, right?
Zh: “就叫他们破坏罢工者吧——是你把他们安排过去的，对吗？”

En: You're right, I don't know what got into me. Some of that degenerate dock worker lingo has rubbed off, it seems.
Zh: “你说得对，我不知道自己是怎么了。某些堕落码头工人的术语似乎对我产生了影响。”

En: Let her continue.
Zh: 让她继续。

En: It's implied she's open to discussing this matter with you at a later occasion.
Zh: 这意味着她愿意在之后的场合跟你公开讨论这件事情。

En: Yes. They were organized by Wild Pines. The contractors picked them up from Jamrock and drove them over. This is standard practice. The company reserves a right to man its stations.
Zh: “是的。他们是野松公司组织的。佣兵组织从加姆洛克挑了一些人，然后开車送了过来。这是行规。公司保留派人站岗的权利。”

En: If these *workers* were organized by Wild Pines or its affiliates, then it would be a company secret. I could not share it with you -- not yet, at least.
Zh: “如果这些*工人*是野松公司或者其附属公司组织的，那将会是一个公司机密。我可以跟你分享这个秘密——不过现在还不行。”

En: If these *strikebreakers* were organized by Wild Pines or its affiliates, then it would be a company secret. I could not share it with you -- not yet, at least.
Zh: “如果这些*破坏罢工者*是野松公司或者其附属公司组织的，那将会是一个公司机密。我可以跟你分享这个秘密——不过现在还不行。”

En: Ha! She gives you a crooked smile. "If the workers were organized by Wild Pines then it would be a company secret. I could not share it with you  -- not yet, at least..."
Zh: “哈！”她露出一个不自然的笑容。“如果这些工人是野松公司组织的，那就会是一个公司机密。我可以跟你分享这个秘密——不过现在还不行……”

En: To answer your question -- yes. They were organized by Wild Pines. The contractors picked them up from Jamrock and drove them over. This is standard practice. The company reserves a right to man its stations.
Zh: “你这个问题的答案是——没错。他们是野松公司组织的。佣兵组织从加姆洛克挑了一些人，然后开車送了过来。这是行规。公司保留派人站岗的权利。”

En: Can you tell me about these tattoos again?
Zh: “能再跟我说说这些纹身的事吗？”

En: Now can you tell me about these tattoos? (Show her the photo.)
Zh: “现在能跟我说说这些纹身的事吗？”（把相片拿给她看。）

En: I have a photo of the victim's tattoos. (Show it to her.)
Zh: “我有一张被害人纹身的相片。”（拿给她看。）

En: Do you know something about these tattoos? (Show her the photo.)
Zh: “你认得这些纹身吗？”（把相片拿给她看。）

En: Of course, her back straightens. "Anything to help."
Zh: “当然，”她挺直了后背。“乐意效劳。”

En: That's the man who was killed... She almost takes the photo -- but then stops. "I'm afraid this is a discussion for once we've cleared the lynching question."
Zh: “这就是被杀害的那个男人……”她几乎想伸手拿起那张相片——不过又停住了。“恐怕这个话题，要等到把私刑的问题厘清之后再说。”

En: Better not to tie the forestay to the backstay on this. I hope there is something else I can help you with?
Zh: “最好不要本末倒置。也许我能帮你点别的忙？”

En: Fine, let's return to it later.
Zh: “好吧，我们之后再说。”

En: Why? How is you looking at the photo tied to the lynching?
Zh: “为什么？你是怎么看待这张跟私刑有关的相片的？”

En: So you know something about the tattoos?
Zh: “所以你知道这个纹身了？”

En: She *wants* to answer the question. Protocol keeps her from it.
Zh: 她*想要*回答你的问题。不过协议不允许她这么做。

En: I fear I've told you all I know. She takes the photo. "It's an Oranjese Map of the Waterways -- or some version of it. It depicts the travels this man made while he was still alive."
Zh: “恐怕我知道的已经全告诉你了。”她接过相片。“这是一份奥兰治水路的地图——或者是它的某个版本。它显示了其制作者生前曾经走过的路。”

En: Your name is Rejoyce?
Zh: “你的名字叫雷乔伊斯？”

En: Yes -- but please don't call me that.
Zh: “是的——不过请别这么称呼我。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: *Joyce* is much more efficient.
Zh: “*乔伊斯*更有效率一些。”

En: ...is what you *want* to say. But it isn't that easy, is it?
Zh: ……你*想说*的就是这个。不过并没有那么容易，不是吗？

En: What, why not?
Zh: 什么，为什么不行？

En: YOU SEEM RICH. CAN I HAVE SOME MONEY?
Zh: “你好像是个有钱人。能给我一点钱吗？”

En: Look at that lady. Take a *gander*. Squint your eyes, bub.
Zh: 看着那位女士。*瞥*上一眼。眯起你的眼睛，小子。

En: Why yes! Tucked away under that sturdy green raincoat, almost rustic in its simplicity -- a silk shirt and matching scarf around her gentle throat...
Zh: 当然了！在那件结实的、朴素地几乎有些土气的绿色雨衣下——隐藏着一条丝绸裙子，她那纤弱的喉咙周围，还佩戴着一条与之相配的围巾……

En: These are the kind eyes of the rich-man, that seem to say: everything is possible...
Zh: 这就是有钱人的那种眼神，好像在说：一切皆有可能……

En: You're thinking it, but your lips are not moving.
Zh: 你这么想着，但是嘴唇并没有蠕动。

En: What nice fabrics.
Zh: 多好的面料啊。

En: What lustrous hair.
Zh: 多么有光泽的头发啊。

En: While dull orange pearls hang from her ear lobes, red from cold. Her light green eyes scan you, full of knowledge -- and worry. Wealth and all its possibilities.
Zh: 她的耳垂上挂着暗橙色的珍珠，由于寒冷变得有些发红。她的浅绿色眼睛打量着你，充满智慧——还有忧心。这就是财富，还有它所有的可能性。

En: A well-kempt, yet tastefully short bob of dark hair. Despite the first hints of grey, she's elected to keep it au naturale -- shaped into a permanent wave, late-Forties style...
Zh: 她留着整洁又有品味的深色短波浪头。尽管隐约有了一丝灰色，她还是决定保持这种自然的状态——做了个烫发，40年代晚期的风格……

En: Positively statuesque.
Zh: 轮廓非常优美。

En: A breeze carries with it the faint scent of juniper and oranges.
Zh: 一阵轻风吹来，里面夹杂着杜松和橙子的清香……

En: ...within reason.
Zh: ……合情合理。

En: Yeah, so?
Zh: 是啊，所以呢？

En: Now look at you, you misery-clad simian, barely able to tie your own laces. Your armpits are lakes, a scythe of booze precedes you. Your hair sticks to your forehead and your underwear feels uncomfortable...
Zh: 现在看看你，你这个衣衫褴褛的类人猿，几乎连自己的鞋带都系不好。你的腋窝渗着汗水，浑身上下一股酒精味。头发粘在额头上，内裤也感觉很不舒服……

En: You're poor. Poor as balls. You can't ask this person for money. You're too...
Zh: 你很穷。穷的跟鬼一样。你不能找这个人要钱。你……

En: What is this feeling? I've never felt it before.
Zh: 这是什么感觉？我之前从没有过这种感觉。

En: Shame? You haven't really been in the presence of gentlefolk either, have you? What shame is there to be felt in front of these... dockworkers, cops and hotel clerks. But to *belittle* yourself in these eyes of Eau-de-Nil...
Zh: 羞愧？你从来没有真正在上流人士面前待过，不是吗？站在这些……码头工人，警察还有旅社职员的面前，你没有什么好羞愧的。不过出现这双浅绿色的眼睛面前，你就是在*贬低*自己……

En: As I was saying... Her voice breaks the silence and suddenly you're back again. Nearby, a seagull pecks at a piece of garbage... "If there's *any* way I may be of assistance, please don't hesitate to ask."
Zh: “我刚才说过……”她的声音打破了沉默，突然间你又回过神来。附近，一只海鸥在啄食着一堆垃圾……“如果我有*任何*能帮到你的地方，请不要犹豫，尽管开口。”

En: I'm a god damn working man, I'm not *ashamed* to shake this leech for some dough.
Zh: 我他妈可是个工人，从这个吸血鬼身上捞点油水，我根本没什么好*羞愧*的。

En: As I should be -- for even considering it. This is a woman and I'm a *man*.
Zh: 就算是考虑了一下——我也应该这么做。她是个女人，而我是个*男人*。

En: Yes. I should make my own god damn money by now.
Zh: 是的。现在我应该给自己好好弄点钱花了。

En: Say it: "Can I have some money?"
Zh: 说吧：“能给我点钱吗？”

En: Revacholian Nationhood disapproves.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖民族性不同意。

En: Mazovian Socio-economics approve.
Zh: 马佐夫社会经济学批准了。

En: Indirect Taxation disapproves.
Zh: 间接课税不同意。

En: Kingdom Of Conscience approves.
Zh: 道德王国批准了。

En: Heard of BILLIONS!
Zh: 听说有十亿！

En: Did not tell her about investigation.
Zh: 不要告诉她调查的事

En: Told her about investigation.
Zh: 告诉她调查的事

En: Already paid damages to Garte.
Zh: 已经向加尔特支付了赔偿金。

En: You think your little *communism" protects you from this feeling? No. The more *demeaning* it is to grovel at her feet...
Zh: 你以为你的小*康米主义*能保护你不受到这种感觉的伤害？不会的。在她脚下越卑躬屈膝，就会越*自降身份*……

En: Yes. You should be able to take care of yourself. Not grovel at her beautiful feet, dishonouring yourself and your organization with this request...
Zh: 是的。你应该能够照顾好自己。不要在她美丽的脚下卑躬屈膝，这样的要求会让你和你的组织蒙羞的……

En: Maybe you can ask her for some *stock tips* -- that's a *maybe*. Or a *job*. But you're sure as hell too ashamed to ask for cash buddy.
Zh: 也许你可以找她打听点*股市秘诀*——注意，这是个*可能*。或者是一份*工作*。不过你肯定太羞于开口要钱了，老兄。

En: Oh god, the lieutenant is here too! Do not DISHONOUR the force!
Zh: 天啦，警督也在这里！千万不要给组织丢脸！

En: I am sorry. It just doesn't come out of your mouth. What does is:
Zh: 我很抱歉。它不会就这么从你的嘴里说出来的。它是这样做的：

En: Khnnnnnnnffff...
Zh: “唔唔唔唔唔唔啊啊啊……”

En: Khk...
Zh: “呃呃啊……”

En: Money...
Excuse me, I didn't hear you. Her voice is kind, a little hoarse from the wind.
Zh: “抱歉，我听不见你说什么。”她的声音很亲切，被风吹的有些嘶哑。

En: Excuse me, I didn't hear you. her voice is kind, a little hoarse from the wind. "Did you say *money*?"
Zh: “抱歉，我听不见你说什么。”她的声音很有友善，被风吹的有些嘶哑。“你是说*钱*吗？”

En: Surprisingly eloquent, really. The last one, not the first three -- those are ape-speak.
Zh: 出奇的有说服力，真的。选最后一个，不要前三个——那些是类人猿在说话。

En: I am the scum of the earth.
Zh: “我是地球的渣滓。”

En: My.... She doesn't know what to say: "Why do you say that?"
Zh: “天啦……”她不知道该说什么：“你为什么要这么说？”

En: I'm gunk.
Zh: “我是一滩烂泥。”

En: No, I didn't. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “不，我没有。我们说点别的吧。”

En: Of course. She makes a slight bowing motion.
Zh: “当然可以。”她微微弓了下身子。

En: She understands what you're trying to do -- ask for money. But shame is contagious and she doesn't want to offend you by saying it out loud.
Zh: 她能理解你想做什么——要钱。不过羞愧是会传染的，她不想说出来冒犯到你。

En: That's garbage! You're a police officer.
Zh: “胡说八道！你是一名警官。”

En: No I'm not. I'm gunk.
Zh: “不，我不是的。我是一滩烂泥。”

En: I'm the stuff people have under their fingernails. Let's talk about something else please.
Zh: “我是人们指甲底下的脏东西。拜托，我们说点别的吧。”

En: Shall we talk about something else maybe? Her voice is kind, if anything she sounds a little confused.
Zh: “也许我们可以说点别的？”她的声音很友善，如果说有什么话的，里面还夹杂着一丝困惑。

En: What you meant to say was:
Zh: 你想说的是：

En: What is this? An athletic challenge? Scream it from the top of your lungs immediately, show that asshole!
Zh: 这是干什么？运动挑战吗？马上把它从你的肺部顶端大喊出来，让那个混账好好看看！

En: Voila, you're doing it!
Zh: 瞧，你这不是做到了吗！

En: You seem rich. Can I have some money?
Zh: “你好像是个有钱人。能给我一点钱吗？”

En: Can I have some money, ma'am?
Zh: “能给我一点钱吗，女士？”

En: Help me, I'm poor. I need money to keep living.
Zh: “帮帮我，我很穷。我需要钱来维持生活。”

En: Why yes, I am rich. How much money do you need? Hopefully not too much, I couldn't bring all of it with me.
Zh: “没错，我是很富有。你需要多少钱？希望不用太多，我不能把所有钱都带在身上。”

En: I need a *billion* reál.
Zh: “我需要*十亿*雷亚尔。”

En: I need 10,000 reál.
Zh: “我需要10,000雷亚尔。”

En: I need 60 reál.
Zh: “我需要60雷亚尔。”

En: She's surprisingly nonchalant about this. If anything, your finally asking it has relieved some of the tension.
Zh: 令人惊讶的是，她对此一副若无其事的样子。如果说有什么，你最终开口要钱反而缓解了一些紧张的气氛。

En: She's surprisingly nonchalant about this. Could it be that she somehow knows more about your predicament than she's letting on?
Zh: 令人惊讶的是，她对此一副若无其事的样子。会不会是她对你窘况的了解要比表现出来的多？

En: Of course. She nods, she doesn't even smile. "How much do you need."
Zh: “当然。”她点点头，甚至都没笑。“你需要多少。”

En: Ah yes, of course, I'm sad to hear that. She nods, as if its self explanatory. "How much do you need?"
Zh: “啊，当然可以。很抱歉听到这个消息。”她点点头，似乎这是件一目了然的事情。“你需要多少？”

En: MONEY!!!
Really put your back into it. Yell it from the top of your lungs!
Zh: 真的要全情投入。从你肺部的顶端大声喊出来！

En: I'm sorry, I couldn't hear you, she chuckles to herself. "The wind is a bit strong, could you repeat that?"
Zh: “很抱歉，我听不见你在说什么，”她暗自发笑。“这里的风太大了，能再重复一遍吗？”

En: I NEED MONEY!!!
Zh: “我需要钱！！！”

En: HOW MUCH MONEY?!
Zh: “多少钱？！”

En: I need 30 reál.
Zh: “我需要30雷亚尔。”

En: I need 70 reál.
Zh: “我需要70雷亚尔。”

En: I need 100 reál.
Zh: “我需要100雷亚尔。”

En: I need 130 reál.
Zh: “我需要130雷亚尔。”

En: Excuse me sir, I don't have a billion reál.
Zh: “抱歉，先生，我没有十亿雷亚尔。”

En: Come now. Nobody carries that much cash on their person, particularly in a place like Martinaise... I'd get robbed.
Zh: “拜托。没人会在身上带那么多现金的，特别是在马丁内斯这种地方……我会被抢劫的。”

En: What? I thought you said you had like twenty billion or something.
Zh: “什么？你不是说过你有200亿雷亚尔之类的话吗？”

En: Oops, I must have misheard.
Zh: “哎呀，那一定是我听错了。”

En: That was the annual revenue of the *entire* Wild Pines Group, not my personal fortune...
Zh: “那是*整个*野松公司的年收入，不是我的个人财产……”

En: Try again, dear. A little lower this time.
Zh: “再试一次，亲爱的。这次稍微少一点。”

En: Her tone makes clear that you've violated some kind of rich-person taboo. Wouldn't be the first time.
Zh: 她的语气清楚地显示出一点：你违反了有钱人的某种禁忌。应该不是第一次了。

En: Hi-fi? I like high fidelity disco music too.
Zh: “高保真音响？我也喜欢高保真迪斯科音乐。”

En: Hydrodynamique E40? Sounds fast.
Zh: “流体力学E40？听起来很快的样子。”

En: It is. Fast and silent. She gives you a crooked little smile. "Try again, but lower."
Zh: “没错。又快又安静。”她朝你露出一丝不自然的微笑。“再试一次，不过要少点。”

En: I like high fidelity *anything*, sir. She gives you a fast little smile. "Now try again, but lower."
Zh: “我喜欢*任何*高保真的东西，先生。”她朝你露出一丝笑容。“现在再试一次吧，不过要少点。”

En: I need 1 reál.
Zh: “我需要1雷亚尔。”

En: No, you don't. One reál won't get you two pieces of bath tissue to rub together.
Zh: “不，不行的。1雷亚尔的话，你连可以两张叠放在一起摩擦的厕纸都买不到。”

En: I would like 0.1 cents.
Zh: “我想要0.1分。”

En: So would I -- but woe! There is no such denomination.
Zh: “我也想啊——不过，哎！根本没有这种面额。”

En: Ideas like this one are all part of his rare medical condition, ma'am. Nothing to be worried about.
Zh: “夫人，像这种想法都是他遭遇的罕见身体状况的一部分。没什么好担心的。”

En: That's a good sum. Not too small, not fantastically large like a billion. She reaches into her raincoat and pulls out a zip bag made of see-through plastic.
Zh: “这是个很好的数额。不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个透明塑料制成的拉链袋。

En: That's a reasonable sum -- not too small, not fantastically large. She reaches into her raincoat and pulls out a zip bag -- in it you see paper notes arranged like black gills.
Zh: “这是个合理的数额——不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个拉链袋——你看见里面装着像黑色鱼腮一样整齐排列的纸钞。

En: She removes a few notes and hands them to you. The paper is cold and oily to the touch.
Zh: 她取出几张纸钞递给你。它们摸起来冰冷又油腻。

En: There must be about 200 reál in that zip bag. Maybe a little less. Plus some change at the bottom.
Zh: 那个拉链袋里大约有200雷亚尔。也许更少一些。底部还有一些零钱。

En: Whoa, whoa. Did you see how easy that was? Ask her for more! *Toot-toot* Train to moneytown!
Zh: 哇哦，哇哦。你看见有多简单了吗？再找她多要点！*嘟嘟*，乘上开往金钱小镇的火車！

En: Can I have some more?
Zh: “能再多给我点吗？”

En: Nay, 'twould be dishonourable, and mine honour is my life.
Zh: 不可以，这样太不光彩了，我的荣誉就是我的生命。

En: Can you eat honour? Are you an *honour-vore?* Give me a break...
Zh: 荣誉能吃吗？你是个*以荣誉为食*的物种吗？饶了我吧……

En: Uhm... The woman dips her fingers back into the bag. "I really did bring as little cash as possible, to avoid attention. So here you are, the last of bit of filthy *lucre* I can share."
Zh: “嗯……”女人把手指伸进袋子里。“我真的是尽量少带现金的人，就是为了避免引起注意。给你吧，这是我最后能分享的一点*不义之财*了。”

En: This may seem like *odd* behaviour for a police officer, ma'am, but it's a perfectly common side effect of his... He squints at you.
Zh: “夫人，这种行为对于一名警官来说似乎有些*奇怪*，不过这是一种非常常见的副作用，因为他……”他朝你使了个眼色。

En: ... unusual medical episode.
Zh: “……不寻常的医学插曲。”

En: It's all right, lieutenant. The woman dips her fingers back into the bag. "I really did bring as little cash with me as possible, to avoid attention. So here you are -- the last bit of filthy *lucre* I can share."
Zh: “没事的，警督。”女人把手指伸进袋子里。“我真的是尽量少带现金的人，就是为了避免引起注意。给你吧——这是我最后能分享的一点*不义之财*了。”

En: So, she returns the zip bag to her jacket. "I hope I didn't just *bribe* you, officer. It may not be technically *illegal* under the Emergency Act, but still..."
Zh: “所以，”她把拉链袋放回夹克里。“希望我刚才的行为不算*贿赂*你，警官。根据紧急法案的规定，这种行为从严格意义上讲是*违法*的，不过根据紧急法案的规定……”

En: So, she returns the zip bag to her jacket. "I hope I didn't just *bribe* you, officer. It may not be technically *illegal* under the Emergency Act, but still..."
Zh: “所以，”她把拉链袋放回夹克里。“希望我刚才的行为不算*贿赂*你，警官。根据紧急法案的规定，这种行为从严格意义上讲是*违法*的，不过根据紧急法案的规定……”

En: And even *if* I did I still would not give you ten thousand reál. I would rather buy something nice with it. Like expensive hi-fi or a new *Hydrodynamique E40* sail.
Zh: “而且*就算*我有，我也不会给你10,000雷亚尔的。我宁愿用这些钱买点好东西。比如昂贵的高保真音响，或者一面全新的*流体力学E40*船帆。”

En: She removes a few notes and hands them to you. The paper is cold and oily to the touch.
Zh: 她取出一些纸钞递给你。它们摸起来冰冷又油腻。

En: That's a good sum. Not too small, not fantastically large like a billion. She reaches into her raincoat and pulls out a zip bag made of see-through plastic.
Zh: “这是个很好的数额。不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个透明塑料制成的拉链袋。

En: That's a good sum -- not too small, not fantastically large. She reaches into her raincoat and pulls out a zip bag -- in it you see paper notes arranged like black gills.
Zh: “这是个很好的数额——不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个拉链袋——你看见里面装着像黑色鱼腮一样整齐排列的纸钞。

Zh: 她取出一些纸钞递给你。它们摸起来冰冷又油腻。

Zh: 她取出一些纸钞递给你。它们摸起来冰冷又油腻。

Zh: “这是个很好的数额。不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个透明塑料制成的拉链袋。

Zh: “这是个很好的数额——不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个拉链袋——你看见里面装着像黑色鱼腮一样整齐排列的纸钞。

Zh: 她取出一些纸钞递给你。它们摸起来冰冷又油腻。

En: That's a good sum -- not too small, not fantastically large. She reaches into her raincoat and pulls out a zip bag -- in it you see paper notes arranged like black gills.
Zh: “这是个很好的数额——不是太少，也没有多的难以置信。”她把手伸进雨衣，掏出一个拉链袋——你看见里面装着像黑色鱼腮一样整齐排列的纸钞。

En: You bribed me all right. I'm your little *peone* now.
Zh: “你的确贿赂了我。现在我是你的小*傀儡*了。”

En: I am an *honourable* policeman. One of the few good men in Revachol.
Zh: “我是一名*正直的*警官，像我这种人在瑞瓦肖可不多见。”

En: Certainly not. I am an *honourable* policeman.
Zh: “当然没有。我是一名*正直的*警官。”

En: Funny that your worried about this and not your ruthless exploitation of the entire *human race*.
Zh: “有意思，你居然会担心这个，而不是你们对整个*人类*的无情剥削。”

En: You illustrate the expression by holding an imaginary-- and very, very tiny-- *peone* between your thumb and index finger.
Zh: 为了举例说明，你想象自己的拇指和食指之间捏着一个非常非常小的——*傀儡*。

En: I'm still getting my head around this whole *money* concept. You were just helping me out.
Zh: “我还是搞不清楚这整个*金钱*的概念。你不过是在帮我而已。”

En: You're right, ma'am, that donations are permitted under the Emergency Act, unseemly as it may be, as long as they're properly logged with the precinct...
Zh: “你说得对，夫人，紧急法案是允许接受这种捐款的，看起来似乎有些不得体，不过只要这些钱被分局正确记录在案的话……”

En: Which he most certainly will do. She bows, and then raises a single eyebrow in your direction: "Now, how else can I help the RCM today, besides supplementing its salaries?"
Zh: “而且他很肯定会这么做的。”她鞠了一躬，然后朝你的方向扬起了一边眉头：“今天我还有什么能帮到RCM的吗，除了增补工资之外？”

En: I want to talk about something else now.
Zh: “现在我想聊点别的。”

En: That's heartening to hear. There are too few honourable men in Revachol. She gives you a slight bow. "Now how else can I help the RCM today, besides supplementing its salaries?"
Zh: “听起来很鼓舞人心。瑞瓦肖里正直的人太少勒。”她朝着你略微抬起眉头。“今天我还有什么能帮到RCM的吗，除了增补工资之外？”

En: Au contraire, officer. I also feel queasy about the pittance the Coalition calls your salary. Now is there something else I can do for you, or did you want to chat about *dialectics* some more?
Zh: “相反，警官。我也为联盟支付给你们的微薄薪水感到不安。现在我还有什么能帮到你的吗，或者你想再多聊聊*辩证法*的话题？”

En: It's quite the *phainómenon*, I agree. She bows slightly. "Now how else can I help the RCM today, besides supplementing its salaries?"
Zh: “是挺*神奇*的，我同意。”她微微扬起眉头。“今天我还有什么能帮到RCM的吗，除了增补工资之外？”

En: Of course. She bows slightly. "Now how else can I help the RCM today, besides supplementing its salaries?"
Zh: “当然。”她微微扬起眉头。“今天我还有什么能帮到RCM的吗，除了增补工资之外？”

En: She laughs out loud. It is a beautiful laugh, laced with glee and a certain sharpness. It travels far on the water...
Zh: 她大笑出声。这是个美丽的笑声，装点着欢愉和某种锐利。它顺着水面一路流向很远的地方……

En: She clears her throat. "Now, how else can I help the RCM today, besides supplementing its salaries?"
Zh: 她清了清喉咙。“今天我还有什么能帮到RCM的吗，除了增补工资之外？”

En: What would you like to know? She taps her foot on the deck.
Zh: “你想知道什么？”她的脚拍打在甲板上。

En: The *performative experience* has ended. Good -- what can I help you with?
Zh: “*表述行为体验*已经结束了。很好——我能怎么帮你？”

En: I've got some more questions about *reality*.
Zh: “关于*现实*，我还有更多问题。”

En: More lessons in basic reality? She's positively surprised. "My favourite part of the day! Go ahead, ask me anything."
Zh: “还要更多关于基本现实的课程？”她很惊讶，是好意味上的。“这是我今天最喜欢的一段时光了！来吧，随便问。”

En: Well, technically speaking I'm not *on* it right now... She looks at the sloop sway in the waves.
Zh: “呃，从技术层面上讲，我现在不在它*上面*……”她看着单桅帆船在海浪中摇摆。

En: So I've been dealing with Evrart again...
Zh: “所以我又去跟艾弗拉特见了一面……”

En: By the way, I've talked to Evrart Claire.
Zh: “哦对了，我跟艾弗拉特·克莱尔聊过了。”

En: (Show her your badge.) "I found my badge, by the way."
Zh: （把警徽拿给她看。）“话说，我找到我的警徽了。”

En: Fast, observantly. Like an electronic printer.
Zh: 快速，敏锐。就像一台电子打印机。

En: By love, you did! She inspects the piece of blue plastic, her eyes scanning from left to right.
Zh: “亲爱的，你找到了！”她端详着蓝色塑料，眼睛在上面左右扫视。

En: She hands it back to you. "Pleased to meet you, Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Du Bois. I am glad to see a man of high qualification -- the situation is precarious."
Zh: 她把它交还给你。“很高兴见到你，双重荣誉警督杜博阿。很高兴能见到如此高素质的人——现在的形势风雨飘摇。”

En: She is memorizing your badge number.
Zh: 她在记忆你的警徽编号。

En: Seaweed drips from the badge in your hand. It smells of fish. "What can I help you with, lieutenant-yefreitor?"
Zh: 海藻从你手中的警徽上滴落下来。闻起来一股鱼腥味。“我能怎么帮你，荣誉警督？”

En: Good morning, officer. I'm Joyce. She extends her hand in greeting.
Zh: “早上好，警官。我是乔伊斯。”她伸出手问候你。

En: Good evening, officer, I'm Joyce. She extends her hand in greeting.
Zh: “晚上好，警官。我是乔伊斯。”她伸出手问候你。

En: The people who live there now lead a different *lifestyle* if you know what I mean.
Zh: “住在那里的人现在过着一种不同的*生活方式*，如果你能明白我的意思。”

En: It seems the architect of the operation is missing. An abandoned lorry is near the statue of Filippe, a FALN A-Z *Tempo*.
Zh: “看来行动的策划师不见了。一台废弃的货車停靠在菲利普国王的雕像附近，一台FLANA-Z*飞驰*。”

En: It seems the architect of the operation has gone missing. An abandoned lorry is near the statue.
Zh: “看来行动的策划师不见了。一台废弃的货車停靠在雕像附近。”

En: Ah, good to hear. I wouldn't want to pry into a confidential police matter. But, in the event you'd like to share more... I *would* like to know who it was.
Zh: “啊，很高兴能听你这么说。我不想打探警方的机密。不过，如果你想多分享一些信息……我*会*很愿意知道这个人是谁。”

En: I came upon a few hidden maps illustrating the range of the smuggling network.
Zh: “我发现一些隐藏的地图，上面标出了走私网络的范围。”

En: We turned up quite a few *interesting* things actually... (Share information.)
Zh: “事实上，我们的确找到了一些*有趣*的东西……”（分享信息。）

En: I found research about something called *ULAN* frequencies related to the operation.
Zh: “我发现他们在研究一种名为*ULAN*的频率，可能跟行动有关。”

En: I saw a really advanced radio system with *a lot* of stations saved.
Zh: “我看见一种特别先进的无线电系统，里面储存了*大量*电台。”

En: It's possible there's even a connection to La Puta Madre.
Zh: “甚至可能跟马德雷教父有所关联。”

En: What is La Puta Madre?
Zh: “马德雷教父是谁？”

En: A seriously bad guy.
Zh: “一个真正的坏人。”

En: No, he's not. Our friend here has quite the sense of humour, part of his technique, you see?
Zh: “不，他没有。我们这位朋友很有幽默感，这也是他的一部分技巧，你明白的。”

En: A seriously bad guy. I might even be on his take!
Zh: “一个真正的坏人。我甚至都可能跟他是同一条船上的人！”

En: Nevertheless, he sounds *terribly* dangerous. I urge you to take care, officer. I wouldn't want anything to happen to you.
Zh: “不过，听起来他的确*非常*危险。我强烈要求你注意安全，警官。我可不希望你出什么差错。”

En: She's a fan of vintage movie stars. The inside of the cab was plastered with posters.
Zh: “她是复古电影明星的粉丝。货車車厢里贴了很多海报。”

En: High intensity microwave frequencies? They're used for navigational purposes, to compress the interisolary space. They may use it to communicate with incoming freighters...
Zh: “高强度微波频率？它们是用来导航的，用作压缩跨洲空间。他们可能用它来跟进港的货船取得联系……”

En: It didn't have Speedfreaks FM.
Zh: “里面没有狂飙怪人FM。”

En: The lieutenant's stone-like face remains impassive, yet in his eyes -- a flash of reproach.
Zh: 警督的石头脸依然无动于衷，不过在他的眼睛里——有一丝责备的意味。

En: What is... speedfreaks FM? She looks at you quizzically.
Zh: “那个……狂飙怪人FM是什么？”她疑惑地看着你。

En: It's Lieutenant Kitsuragi's favourite radio station!
Zh: “那是曷城警督最喜欢的无线电台！”

En: A very loud radio station about motor carriages and rock music, I think.
Zh: “一个很吵的无线电台，我想是关于汽車和摇滚乐的。”

En: Yes. *All* ears. That's how she operates.
Zh: 是的。*洗耳恭听*。她就是这么做的。

En: A runaway? Good work, this is a tangible lead towards solving the whole mystery.
Zh: “一个逃亡者？你做得很好，这是一条实实在在的线索，能带领我们解决整个谜团。”

En: That speaks of the vastness and sophistication of the operation. Were you able to examine the radio?
Zh: “这就说明整个行动规模庞大，而且复杂老练。你检查过无线电了吗？”

En: We contain multitudes, do we not? It might not be pertinent to her... *profession*, but who knows? It may help you find her yet.
Zh: “我们很多人都是，不是吗？这可能与她的……*职业*无关，不过谁知道呢？也许能帮你找到她。”

En: Well, he sounds *very* dangerous. I urge you to take care, officer. I wouldn't want anything to happen to you. Not with what's coming...
Zh: “呃，听起来他的确*非常*危险。我强烈要求你注意安全，警官。我可不希望你出什么差错。特别是接下来要发生的事……”

En: I've told you what I've found, now it's your turn. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我已经把我发现的都告诉你了，现在轮到你。”（总结。）

En: I appreciate you placing your trust in me. Thank you again for looking into this matter. You have my gratitude -- and the gratitude of Wild Pines Group...
Zh: “我很感激你能这么信任我。再次感谢你能调查这个事件。我很感激——野松公司也很感激你……”

En: It was the nerve-center for the operation. She could contact a whole fleet of lorries.
Zh: “它是行动的神经中心。她可以联系整个車队的货車。”

En: I... fail to see the relevance of this station, but... she smiles warmly, "I'll defer to your judgement. Was there anything else?"
Zh: “我……没看出跟这个电台有什么关联，不过……”她亲切地笑了，“我尊重你的判断。还有别的吗？”

En: Taking it out of commission will deal a blow to the smuggling network. Excellent work.
Zh: “切断它的服务可能会打击到走私网络。干得漂亮。”

En: Unfortunately the dial knob was missing, the radio itself was inoperative.
Zh: “可惜拨号转盘不见了，无线电本身也无法工作。”

En: She nods. "Sensible for her to disable access to such essential information."
Zh: 她点点头。“她禁止别人访问这类重要信息是很明智的。”

En: A first-rate find -- that should tell you all you need to know about their network, I would imagine.
Zh: “真是个绝佳的发现——我能想象，这样你就能得到关于网络的一切必要信息。”

En: Right. She glances sideways at the lieutenant. "It's really not the time or place to discuss such matters. What else did you find in your investigation?"
Zh: “好吧，”她侧过头瞥了金一眼。“现在的时间，还有这个地方，都不适合讨论这种事情。你在调查过程中还有什么发现？”

En: Now's neither the time nor the place to discuss such matters. What else did your investigation turn up?
Zh: “现在不是讨论这些事情的时间，在这里讨论也不合适。你的调查还发现了什么？”

En: Yes. They're spread all over Jamrock... and beyond. I couldn't even identify the exact end points.
Zh: “是的。他们遍布整个加姆洛克……还超出了这个范围。我甚至无法识别出准确的终点。”

En: It's an octopus whose tentacles reach every corner of the city.
Zh: “它就是一只章鱼，把触手伸向了这座城市的每一个角落。”

En: An ominous vision... She nods solemnly. "At least you understand the gravity of the situation. And the maps should prove very valuable indeed."
Zh: “一个不祥的预兆……”她严肃地点点头。“至少你理解了形势的严重性。而且那些地图确实是非常有价值的。”

En: So this goes *beyond* Jamrock... she looks inland. At least you now understand the gravity of the situation. And those maps should prove very valuable indeed."
Zh: “所以行动已经*超出*加姆洛克的范围了……”她看向内陆。“至少现在你理解了形势的严重性。而且那些地图确实是非常有价值的。”

En: Okay. I've made up my mind about the smuggling investigation... (Proceed.)
Zh: “好吧。关于走私调查的事，我已经决定了……”（继续。）

En: Yes?
Zh: “什么？”

En: The Union won *significant* concessions -- including overtime pay *and* a medical plan. This time their demands are more... I guess you could say *aggressive*.
Zh: “工会赢得了*重大*的让步————包括加班工资*和*医疗保险。这一次他们要求的更多……我猜可以用*激进*来形容。”

En: She continues: "The literage the local producers must get from this terminal alone must be *oceanic*."
Zh: 她继续说到：“本地生产商从这个中转站拿到的东西肯定是*越洋*的。”

En: All this talk about *jurisdictions* is really cramping my style. I just want to get to the bottom of this.
Zh: “这一切关于*管辖区*的话题真的束缚了我的风格。我只想查清事实真相。”

En: That's not really my area. I just want to get to the bottom of this.
Zh: “那真的不是我的领域。我只想查清事实真相。”

En: Well, she smiles and points toward the roundabout, "here's your chance."
Zh: “这个嘛，”她笑了笑，指向环岛的方向，“你的机会来了。”

En: The company has tried looking into this matter before -- to no avail. Perhaps someone with *your* authority and resources might turn over the right stones?
Zh: “公司之前试过调查这件事情——不过徒劳无功。也许某个拥有*你*这种权力和资源的人，也许能找出门道来？”

En: Huh, interesting. I had another question...
Zh: “嗯，有意思。我还有个问题……”

En: About the boat? A wisp of steam rises from her thermal cup.
Zh: “关于船的吗？”她的保温杯里升腾出一股水蒸气。

En: Okay, forget the stupid Wayfarer Act. I had another question... (Back off.)
Zh: “好吧，忘了那个愚蠢的旅人法案吧。我还有别的问题……”（放弃。）

En: About the boat? A wisp of steam rises from her thermal cup.
Zh: “关于船的吗？”她的保温杯里升腾出一股水蒸气。

En: Now they won't even let me into the harbour. There's a 2.20 m racist behemoth blocking the gates.
Zh: “现在他们甚至连港口都不让我进去。有个2.2米高的种族主义巨兽堵住了大门。”

En: What happened to this... Gaumont?
Zh: “这个……戈蒙出什么事了？”

En: *EVERY WORKER -- A MEMBER OF THE BOARD!*
Zh: “*所有工人——都是董事会的一员*！”

En: And *now* people are getting *lynched*, I hear. Behind the Whirling-in-Rags... a disastrous situation if there ever was one.
Zh: “*现在*我听说都有人被*私刑*处置了。就在褴褛飞旋背后……如果说什么叫灾难性形势的话，就是现在。”

En: You know... she puts her foot on the guardwire. "I don't mean to sound cold, but if you want something, you have to give something back. *More* than just guilt."
Zh: “你知道的……”她把脚踩在防护线上。“我不是故意要这么冷淡，但是如果你想要什么，就必须付出些什么。*不仅仅*是内疚。”

En: I could've eaten it for all I know. I don't remember anything. This world, this city. Nothing.
Zh: “据我所知，我本来可以吃掉它的。我什么都不记得了。这个世界，这座城市。全部不记得。”

En: We're in logistics. It's our business to know -- and no. As far as the company knows, the Union does not produce it. They transport the ingredients. For a cut.
Zh: “我们是做物流的。这是我们的业务——而且，我们没有。根据公司得到的信息，工会没有生产。他们只运送原材料。为了赚钱。”

En: Am I going to need bolt cutters for this?
Zh: “这活需要断线钳吗？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: Unlikely, officer. I'm talking about the lorries. Once the ingredients reach Jamrock they're distributed to a network of local manufacturers, well beyond our grasp. But in transit they may be *vulnerable*.
Zh: “不太可能，警官。我说的是物流。一旦原材料抵达加姆洛克，他们就会分发给本地的制造商网络，这就远远超出了我们的掌控范围。不过在运输途中，它们是很*脆弱*的。”

En: Perhaps you've noticed that a number of lorries are tangled in a traffic jam at the roundabout just now? Interview the drivers who are still hanging about. One of them might be waiting for a *crucial* shipment. She gives you a knowing look.
Zh: “也许你刚才已经注意到了，有一堆货車被困在了拥堵的环岛？走访一下那些还在四处晃悠的司机。也许其中有人在等待一批*重要*货物。”她朝你会意地看了一眼。

En: Shit yeah, baby, it's the Party Pipeline! Why would you want to shut that down?
Zh: 哦耶，宝贝，这可是渠道派对！你为什么想要把它关闭呢？

En: Why are you trying to shut down the Party Pipeline?
Zh: “你为什么想要关闭渠道派对呢？”

En: I assure you, officer, these are *not* the sort of parties you'd want to attend.
Zh: “我向你保证，警官，这些可*不是*你想要参加的那种派对。”

En: Excellent. She takes a long sip of tea. "According to my reports there are at least three lorry drivers lingering near the roundabout. Hopefully one of them will know something."
Zh: “太好了。”她喝了一大口茶水。“根据我得到的报告，环岛附近至少徘徊着3个货車司机。希望有人知道些什么。”

En: I suggest you go back and canvas for more suspects.
Zh: “我建议你回去，寻找更多嫌疑人。”

En: The lieutenant clears his throat audibly.
Zh: 警督大声清了清喉咙。

En: Now, per our original agreement, it's time for me to share company secrets with you. There's a change in her demeanour. A caution comes over her. A tension.
Zh: “现在，根据我们最初的协议，轮到我跟你分享商业机密了。”她的举止有了一些变化。她变得谨慎起来。有种紧张感。

En: No matter, detective. We're losing the thread again.
Zh: “没关系，警探。我们又失去线索了。”

En: She has no *idea* what kind of parties you've been to.
Zh: 她不*知道*你参加过什么样的派对。

En: How about the *Sleek Fish*.
Zh: “那*滑鱼*怎么样。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Because it's sleek... and fish-like.
Zh: “因为它很滑……而且样子像鱼。”

En: Cool, but your boat really needs a name.
Zh: “挺酷的，不过你的船真的需要一个名字。”

En: The *Ex-Wife*.
Thank you for the suggestion. I see the word has emotional resonance for you. For me, sadly -- none. So I will stick with the factory name.
Zh: “感谢你的建议。我能看出这个词跟你有情感上的共鸣。可悲的是，对于我来说——完全没有。所以我还是坚持出厂名就好。”

En: Okay. How about Cor-de-Leite '19. Why? She taps on the side of the boat, it makes a hollow sound....
Zh: “好吧。乳白'19怎么样。要问为什么？”她拍了拍帆船的侧面，发出一阵空洞的声响……

En: How about the *Ex-* something.
Zh: “那以*前*打头的怎么样。”

En: The *Ex-* something? The *Ex-* what?
Zh: “以*前*打头？*前*什么呢？”

En: The *Executor*!
Just the *Ex-Something*.
Zh: “就叫*旧日之物*。”

En: Very absurd, she nods. "Thank you for the suggestion. I will stick with the factory name for now -- but thank you."
Zh: “非常荒诞，”她点点头。“感谢你的建议。我暂时还是保留出厂名吧——不过还是谢谢你。”

En: Oh, I *do* like the sound of that. A smirk passes her face. "It sounds so *decisive*, so..."
Zh: “哦，这个*确实*听起来不错。”她的脸上掠过一丝假笑。“听起来特别*坚定*，特别……”

En: After Her Innocence Dolores Dei.
Zh: “以无罪女王德洛莉丝·黛命名。”

En: *Powerful*...
I'm not really *big* on moralist symbols. I think I'll stick with the factory name. But thank you.
Zh: “我并不*热爱*道德的象征。我想我还是保留出厂名吧。不过谢谢你。”

En: Okay, but what *kind* of boat is it?
Zh: “好吧，那这是什么*类型*的船呢。”

En: It's a pleasure craft. A nineteen-pacer. It also happens to be rated for Category 1 racing. Though these days I mainly use it for business...
Zh: “这是一艘19步的游艇。它也恰好被评为了一类赛艇。不过现如今，我主要是用它来忙生意的……”

En: Then what does that say? She points to the plaque on the side of the pier. "Does it say *docking reserved for residents of Rue de Saint-Ghislaine 33A*?"
Zh: “那上面说的是什么？”她指向码头侧面的牌匾。“是不是说*此码头为圣吉莱纳路33A号的居民保留*？”

En: A striking woman leans against the cabin top of her sailing boat, smiling as you approach. Her green raincoat glistens with droplets, a silk scarf is tied around her throat.
Zh: 一位引人注目的女性倚靠在她的帆船舱顶，微笑地看着你慢慢靠近。她那件绿色的雨衣上闪烁着晶莹的雨滴，脖子上系着一条丝巾。

En: Good morning, officers. I'm Joyce. She extends her hand in greeting.
Zh: “早上好，警官。我是乔伊斯。”她伸出手问候你。

En: Good evening, officers, I'm Joyce. She extends her hand in greeting.
Zh: “晚上好，警官。我是乔伊斯。”她伸出手问候你。

En: Good afternoon, officers, I'm Joyce. She extends her hand in greeting.
Zh: “下午好，警官，我是乔伊斯。”她伸出手问候你。

En: I'm not a drunk. I'm a police officer. (Wave around that big old bottle while you say it.)
Zh: “我不是酒鬼。我是一名警官。”（一边说话，一边挥舞着大酒瓶。）

En: I'm not a drunk. I'm a police officer.
Zh: “我不是酒鬼。我是一名警官。”

En: Of course you're not. It was only a joke.
Zh: “你当然不是了。我只是开个玩笑。”

En: How interesting. She turns to you, unfazed. "I wish you a swift recovery. In the meanwhile, you have my *full* cooperation. And the full cooperation of the Wild Pines Group."
Zh: “很有趣。”她泰然自若的转向你。“希望你能早日康复。与此同时，我会跟你*通力*合作的。野松公司也一样。”

En: What is implied here?! That you're a *drunk*?!
Zh: 她在暗示什么？说你是个*醉鬼*吗？！

En: Relax. She meant it in jest.
Zh: 放松。她是开玩笑的。

En: You're back. Good. She takes a sip from her silvery thermal cup. "What can I help you with -- at this late hour?"
Zh: “你回来了。很好。”她从银色的保温杯里抿了一口茶水。“这么晚了——我能帮你什么吗？”

En: You're back. Good. She takes a sip from her silvery thermal cup. "What can I help you with?"
Zh: “你回来了。很好。”她从银色的保温杯里抿了一口茶水。“我能帮你什么吗？”

En: It's night. Don't you ever sleep?
Zh: “已经是晚上了。你都不睡觉的吗？”

En: Answer me a question.
Zh: “回答我一个问题。”

En: Of course. That's what I'm here for.
Zh: “当然可以。我就是为这个来的。”

En: Matter of fact, I don't. She takes another sip.
Zh: “事实上，我不睡。”她又抿了一口水。

En: Why is that?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: (Better not to poke further) "That's good. This way you can answer me some questions."
Zh: （最好不要再打探了）“那很好啊。这样你就能回答我一些问题了。”

En: I have a medical condition of my own -- nothing unusual, though. I'm *old* you see.
Zh: “我也有自己的身体状况——不过没什么不寻常的，你也能看见，我*老*了。”

En: It's a medical thing. It's called... *old*. I'm old you see.
Zh: “这是个医学问题。叫做……*老*。你也能看见，我老了。”

En: Old, huh? I think I have that too.
Zh: “老，是吗？那我觉得我也是。”

En: No you're not. You look young.
Zh: “不，你没有。你看起来很年轻。”

En: Don't worry. It's curable -- with *questions*. Ask me some, it'll help pass this night.
Zh: “别担心。这是可以治愈的——用*问题*治愈。来问我吧，帮我度过这个漫长的夜晚。”

En: Thank you -- I do what I can to remain presentable. Now, ask me some questions. It'll help us pass the night.
Zh: “谢谢你——我尽了最大的努力来保持这幅体面的样子。现在，问我些问题吧。它能帮我们度过这个漫长的夜晚。”

En: Didn't she have *another* medical condition?
Zh: 难道她没有*其他*身体状况吗？

En: Were you not overexposed? Does that not cause insomnia?
Zh: “你没有过度暴露吗？那样不是会导致失眠吗？”

En: Very keen of you, officer. I do also have *saudetic syndrome* from overexposure to the pale. It makes me roost in the night. Like an owl.
Zh: “你很敏锐，警官。由于在灰域中过度暴露，我也患上了*安息综合症*。它会让我在夜间栖息。就像一只猫头鹰。”

En: I almost never sleep anymore... if I do it's for an hour, four tops She suppresses a yawn. "Go ahead -- ask me some questions. They are the *only* cure."
Zh: “我几乎不怎么睡觉了……如果睡的话，那就是每次一小时，最多4小时。”她忍住没打哈欠。“来吧--问我点问题。它们是*唯一*的解药。”

En: Her smile is bitter. There is no cure.
Zh: 她的笑容有些苦涩。根本没有解药。

En: Of course.
Zh: “当然。”

En: No need. Tell me something.
Zh: “不需要。跟我说点什么吧。”

En: I could use a coat like that. The rain is freezing.
Zh: “我需要一件那样的外套。这雨都快冻死我了。”

En: Neither do I. (Pat your similarly wet cloak.)
Zh: “我也不用。”（拍拍你同样湿漉的斗篷。）

En: Sadly I need this one myself. It's hydrophobic, repels water, almost *magically*. The company makes them for offshore platform personnel. Very sturdy.
Zh: “可惜，这个我自己得留着用。它不容易被水沾湿，防水效果近乎*神奇*。公司专门为海上平台员工制作的。非常结实。”

En: She gives the material another pat. "What I *can* do for you is answer some *questions*. Nothing like talking to pass a rainy day."
Zh: 她又拍了拍身上的衣料。“我*能够*为你做的，就是回答一些*问题*。要想度过这样的阴雨天，没什么比聊天来的更好了。”

En: Very rugged, she nods in approval. "Good choice. Now, I suspect you had questions -- nothing like talking to pass a rainy day, am I right?"
Zh: “非常结实，”她点头表示赞同。“明智的选择。现在，我怀疑你还有别的问题吧——要想度过这样的阴雨天，没什么比聊天来的更好了，对吧？”

En: Yes. Keep in mind, this is a negotiator Mr. Claire has worked with before, and who was more than fair with him and the Union.
Zh: “是的。记住，这是跟克莱尔先生之前合作过的一名交涉人，而且对他和整个工会的态度已经远超公正。”

En: Cool.
Zh: “很酷。”

En: Yes, extremely. Keep in mind, this is a negotiator Mr. Claire has worked with before, and who was more than fair with him and the Union.
Zh: “是的，没错。记住，这是跟克莱尔先生之前合作过的一名交涉人，对他和整个工会的态度已经远超公正。”

En: Sounds like usual aggressive posturing.
Zh: 听起来像是寻常的挑衅姿态。

En: And your opinion, detective? If I may ask. I'm a curious and talkative person you see, she explains." Would you say the Débardeurs' Union is..."
Zh: 她转向你：“你的看法呢，警探？如果不介意我这么问的话。你也看见了，我是个好奇又健谈的人，”她解释到。“你会说装卸公会……”

En: One more thing -- you said something *happened* in the elections?
Zh: “还有一件事——你说选举期间*发生过*什么事情？”

En: I'm glad you asked. There was a woman -- the previous forewoman of the Union. She disappeared.
Zh: “很高兴你能这么问。有一个女人——工会前任领袖。她消失了。”

En: Disappeared?
Zh: “消失？”

En: Yes. On the last day of the local chapter elections her daughter phoned in and said she wasn't running anymore -- or coming to work. Ever. End of story.
Zh: “是的。在地方选举的最后一天，她的女儿打来电话，说她没有去参选——也没有去上班。再也没有出现。故事结束。”

En: I heard something strange happened to her...
Zh: “我听说她身上发生了怪事……”

En: Disappeared?
Zh: “消失？”

En: Eerie.
Zh: “诡异。”

En: It happens.
Zh: “常有的事。”

En: Some kind of extortion probably.
Zh: “也许是被勒索了吧。”

En: Downright *haunting* if you ask me. The Wild Pines suspected foul play, but what could they do? It was a Union matter.
Zh: “如果你问我的话，这简直就是*惊悚*。野松公司怀疑有什么不正当行为，不过他们又能怎么做呢？这是工会的内部事务。”

En: Does it? She arches her brow. "The company suspects foul play, but there's nothing they could do, it was a Union matter."
Zh: “是吗？”她扬起眉头。“公司怀疑有什么不正当行为，不过他们又能怎么做呢？这是工会的内部事务。”

En: Sadly, the company records do not even give a name. She's just *forewoman*, in private correspondence: Holly. I don't even know if it's a sur- or given name. And I don't have access to the Unions' files.
Zh: “可悲的是，公司记录里甚至连个名字都没有。她只是个*女工头*，在私人通信中：她叫奥莉。我甚至都不知道这是姓还是名。而且我也无权查阅工会文件。”

En: This forewoman? Her name?
Zh: “这个女工头？她叫什么名字？”

En: This *forewoman* -- her name?
Zh: 这个*女工头*？她叫什么名字？

En: Indeed. The company suspects foul play, but there's nothing they could do, it was a Union matter.
Zh: “的确。公司怀疑有什么不正当行为，不过他们又能怎么做呢？这是工会的内部事务。”

En: The point of the presentation is -- these kinds of things *happen* around the Claires. Watch out when you're dealing with him.
Zh: “问题的重点在于——这类事会*发生*在克莱尔周围。你在跟他打交道的时候，一定要小心。”

En: Thank you for your concern, ma'am. We'll be just fine.
Zh: “感谢你的关心，夫人。我们会没事的。”

En: I'm afraid I can't say any more until we've taken care of formal protocol.
Zh: “恐怕在处理好正式协议之前，我只能说这么多了，警官。”

En: Did you? Wonderful! She inspects the piece of blue plastic, the faded green pearls of her eyes scanning left to right.
Zh: “真的吗？太棒了！”她端详着那个蓝色的塑料，浅绿色的眼珠子左右扫视着。

En: You'll never guess what I found. My badge! (Show her your badge.)
Zh: “你肯定猜不到我找到什么了吧。我的警徽！”（把你的警徽拿给她看。）

En: Not much. Their public resume is relatively good -- as far as private military contractors go. I believe they were once called... Downwell.
Zh: “不太多。他们的公开简历相对还是比较好的——就私营佣兵组织而言。我相信他们曾经有个名字叫……深井。”

En: They boast a long list of clients: Saint-Batiste, Welchman-Lorentz, Eendracht... A warning sign, however -- the operations concerned all take place in third- or fourth-world countries. Guarding facilities, escort missions, and such.
Zh: “他们吹嘘了一长串的客户名单：圣巴蒂斯特，威尔士-洛伦兹，艾德拉科特……不过，这是个警告标志——有关行动全都发生在第三或第四世界国家。保护设施，护送任务，等等等等。”

En: Meaning they're used to operating in war zones.
Zh: “这就意味着他们习惯了在战区行动。”

En: Sounds like maybe the colonel went a little *native* during the course of his duties.
Zh: “听起来这个上校在履行职责的过程中有点太*入乡随俗*了。”

En: I wouldn't go as far as to use that term detective.
Zh: “我暂时还不会使用那种说法，警探。”

En: Yes. All the good conflict corridors: Supramundi, Yeesut, the Semenese islands. Countries that don't have a good record reporting *atrocious* military conduct on their soil.
Zh: “没错。所有发生过严重冲突的地区：苏帕穆迪，伊苏特，塞美尼岛。那些国家的国土上都有过*残暴*军事行为的不良记录。”

En: Okay. Anything else you got on them?
Zh: “好吧。你还有关于他们的其他信息吗？”

En: Okay. Anything else you can tell me?
Zh: “好吧。还有其他事情可以告诉我的吗？”

En: Sadly -- no. Before this happened I had little interest in them. Now that I do -- I don't have the resources. She thinks.
Zh: “很遗憾——没有。在这一切发生之前，我对他们兴趣欠佳。现在我有兴趣——却没有资源。”她想到。

En: It's the *expression* she objects to?
Zh: 她反对的是那个*措辞*吗？

En: What? *Going native*?
Zh: “什么？*入乡随俗*？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: It sounds like you object to the words 'going native' more than him raping and killing people here.
Zh: “听起来你似乎更反对‘入乡随俗’这个词，而不是他在这里强暴和杀人的行为。”

En: She's picked a strange hill to die on. Strike!
Zh: 她这可是送羊入虎口啊。进攻！

En: She's silent.
Zh: 她沉默了。

En: What is the purpose of this, detective?
Zh: “你这么做有什么目的，警探？”

En: This is where you draw the line -- *going native*? Not at hiring a paramilitary death squad?
Zh: “这就是你的底线——*入乡随俗*？而不是雇佣一支准军事级的暗杀小队？”

En: Paramilitary death squad is a bit rich. She remains calm: "But yes -- were it up to me I would have at least *vetted* them more thoroughly."
Zh: “准军事级暗杀小队有点昂贵。”她依然镇定自若：“不过没错——如果由我来处理，我至少会更彻底地*审查*一下他们。”

En: Never mind. Forget it.
Zh: “没关系。忘了吧。”

En: Should have cornered her. Maybe it would have yielded something...
Zh: 应该把她逼入绝境的。说不定能挖出点什么东西来……

En: I have no idea what you're talking about.
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么。”

En: Probably.
Zh: “也许吧。”

En: I'm gonna say no.
Zh: “我要说不。”

En: In my line of work it pays to do your research. I was prepared to deal with the RCM. I did *not* think I would be dealing with a group like *Krenel*.
Zh: “干我这一行的，做研究是很值得的。我已经准备好跟RCM合作了。我*不觉得*自己愿意跟*科奈尔*这样的组织合作。”

En: Ah yes, your condition. The International Collaboration Police maintains a database for these sorts of things. Perhaps you should consult it. It was just a suggestion.
Zh: “啊，是啊，你的状况。国际联合刑警维护着一个专门记录此类信息的数据库。也许你应该跟他们咨询一下。这只是一个建议。”

En: Perhaps the arrangement no longer stands... excuse me, it's not my place to pry. If it does you should take advantage of it. Just a suggestion.
Zh: “也许这种安排无效了……很抱歉，我不该打探这个的。如果可以的话，你应该好好利用。这只是一个建议。”

En: Or he *didn't*. She tilts her head. "This is information passed on to me from some teenagers loitering around the canal. I cannot testify by it. "
Zh: “或者他*没有*。”她歪起脑袋。“这些信息是运河附近闲荡的那些青少年传出来的。我无法为它作证。”

En: Who did the passing on then?
Zh: “那是谁传出来的？”

En: What did these teenagers by the canal say?
Zh: “运河边这些年轻人说了什么？”

En: That the man was killed because he assaulted a local woman. I've asked around a bit -- this seems to be the accepted story around Martinaise.
Zh: “说这个男人被杀是因为他侵犯了一个本地女性。我在周围问过——马丁内斯似乎普遍接受了这种说法。”

En: It's the first time I've heard of this. Where did this assault take place? And when?
Zh: “我第一次听见这个消息。侵犯发生在什么地方？什么时候？”

En: Last Sunday night, at the Whirling-in-Rags -- the hostel by the gates. Supposedly the colonel was drunk, maybe on narcotics too.
Zh: “上周日，在褴褛飞旋——大门附近的那家旅社。恐怕上校喝醉了，也许还嗑了药。”

En: If you mean did I see him alive -- yes. But I did not *know* him.
Zh: “如果你的意思是我有没有见过他的活人——有的。不过我不*了解*他。”

En: You don't know how you know -- it's not written on her face, nor in her voice -- but she had sympathy for this man.
Zh: 你不知道自己是怎么知道的——没写在她脸上，她的声音里也没有透露分毫——不过她很同情这个男人。

En: You liked him.
Zh: “你喜欢他。”

En: His name was...
Zh: “他的名字叫……”

En: *Liked* is a bit strong, she tosses her head. "He... he was the most charismatic among them. He handled all the talking. His departure left a major gap in the group's *communication skills*."
Zh: “*喜欢*有点太过了，”她摇摇头。“他……他是其中最有魅力的那个。谈判的事都是交给他的。他的去世让这个组织的*沟通技巧*留下了很大的空白。”

En: Lely. His service name, a nom de guerre most likely. He wouldn't divulge his full name. Only one of them did -- a bad sign if there ever was one.
Zh: “莱利。这是他在部队的名字，很有可能是假名。他不愿意透露自己的真名。只有一个人说了——如果真有什么的话，这肯定是个不好的征兆。”

En: Tell me about the others first.
Zh: “先跟我说说其他人。”

En: This Lely -- anything else? Nationality? How old would you say he was?
Zh: “关于这个莱利--还有别的吗？国籍？他说自己多大？”

En: One is a man, *Korty* they call him. A nickname as well. The other a woman, Phillis de Paule. Korty is... *the gunner,* I believe. De Paule is a radio operator.
Zh: “有一个男人，他们叫他*科迪*。也是个昵称。另一个是女人，菲莉丝·波勒。我相信，科迪应该是……*炮手*。波勒是电报员。”

En: The lieutenant cuts in: "What would you say was his eye colour -- the deceased's?"
Zh: 警督插了一句：“他的眼睛颜色是什么——就是死者的？”

En: She closes her eyes, trying to picture the man's face... then shakes her head...
Zh: 她闭上眼睛，试着想象男人的脸……然后摇摇头……

En: I can't remember. There's a pang of regret to her voice.
Zh: “我记不清了。”她的声音里有一种悔恨带来的痛苦。

En: That's alright, ma'am. Anything else -- nationality? What would you say was his age?
Zh: “没事的，夫人。还有别的吗——国籍？你觉得他多大年纪？”

En: He was forty. Or fifty. It's hard to say which, he had a combat injury on his lower jaw. It made it difficult to estimate his age, or gauge his facial expressions.
Zh: “他40多岁。或者50多。很难看出来，他的下颌上有战斗留下的疤痕，让人很难估算他的年纪，或者观察他的面部表情。”

En: This matches the dental reconstruction we saw on the body; and Klaasje also mentioned it I believe... he says to you, then turns back to Joyce. "What else? Nationality? Accent?"
Zh: “这跟我们在尸体上看到的牙齿重建信息是吻合的；而且我相信，卡拉洁也提到过……”他对你说到，然后又转回到乔伊斯身上。“还有别的吗？国籍？口音？”

En: Indeed. This matches the dental reconstruction we saw on the body, he says to you, then turns back to Joyce. "What else? Nationality? Accent?"
Zh: “确实。这跟我们在尸体上看到的牙齿重建信息是吻合的，”他对你说到，然后又转回到乔伊斯身上。“还有别的吗？国籍？口音？”

En: What else? Nationality? Accent?
Zh: “还有别的吗？国籍？口音？”

En: The lieutenant was testing her -- asking a small detail first to see if she knew him better than she let on. She passed.
Zh: 警督在试探她——先问她一些小细节，看看她知道的是不是比表现出来的要多。她通过了。

En: He was Occidental I think. Light brown hair, a mix accent. Oranjese, or Messinian maybe? His injury gave him an accent all his own...
Zh: “我想他是奥西登人。浅棕色头发，混合口音。奥兰治人，又或者是梅西纳人？他受的伤让他拥有了一种独到的口音……”

En: In a way it was humanizing. He had to learn to speak *through* it -- through the injury. She nods. "That's all I know, I guess. I only met him once."
Zh: “在某种程度上讲，这赋予了他人性。他必须学会*通过*它说话——通过伤口。”她点点头。“我想，我只知道这些了。我只见过他一次。”

En: What else? Nationality? Accent?
Zh: “还有别的吗？国籍？口音？”

En: And that seems to corroborate the ethnicity we gave. The lieutenant is pedantically happy about it.
Zh: “这似乎跟我们推测的种族是一致的。”金的喜悦有些卖弄的成分。

En: The lieutenant jots something down in his notebook, muttering to himself: "Had him down as Mondial... should change it to Occidental."
Zh: 金在笔记本里记了点东西，自己嘟囔着：“之前记录的是蒙迪人……应该换成奥西登人。”

En: Like a crossword puzzle solver. Or a master at the guessing game Peng.
Zh: 就像一个填字游戏计算器。或者是猜谜游戏大师。

En: I can't. This man was no sailor -- and these are no ports. I can understand geographic fragments, but not their meaning.
Zh: “我不懂。这男人不是个水手——这些也不是港口。我能理解地理上的片段，但是不知道它们的含义。”

En: Could you contact the company? Tell them to call them off.
Zh: “你能联系公司吗？告诉公司把他们调走。”

En: I have. And they *will*. However, these orders take time to reach what is basically a rogue unit out in the field, here. Until they do -- it's all on us.
Zh: “我试过了。而且他们*会的*。不过，这些命令需要时间才能传到一个基本上可以说是流氓的作战小队那里。在这之前——只能靠我们了。”

En: She's being truthful. She is pressing them as hard as she can.
Zh: 她很真诚。她已经尽力给他们施压了。

En: The remaining contractors. Their *tribunal*. It's what they believe.
Zh: “是剩下来的雇佣兵吧。这是他们的*审判*，也是他们所相信的。”

En: This does not come as news to us, but still... He exchanges a glance with you. "To *your* knowledge, where did this assault take place? If you know."
Zh: “这不是什么新闻，不过还是……”他跟你交换了一个眼神。“据*你*所知，这次袭击发生在什么地方？如果你知道的话。”

En: Either way, he's alleged to have sexually assaulted a woman. Sometime later a group of dockworkers got their hands on him...
Zh: “不管怎么说，他被指控性侵犯了一名女性。在那之后，他落到了一群码头工人手里……”

En: (Don't mention it.) "And who was this woman?"
Zh: （别提了。）“那这个女人是谁？”

En: And who was this woman?
Zh: “那这个女人是谁？”

En: That's a good question, officer. I don't have the slightest idea. As I said, it's a rumour -- about a rumour. In any case, it's what the colonel's remaining... colleagues... believe.
Zh: “这是个好问题，警官。我一点也不知道。就像我说过的，这是个传闻——关于传闻的传闻。不管怎么说，上校其余的那些……同事……相信了。”

En: You'll meet her soon enough, you feel.
Zh: 你能感觉到，你很快就见到她的。

En: I know the woman you're talking about. We have it under control.
Zh: “我想我好像知道你说的这个女人。我们已经控制住了。”

En: It'll be all of them. The decision is already made.
Zh: 会是他们所有人。已经决定了。

En: Down a deep, black well.
Zh: 一口又深又黑的水井。

En: Feels like there's a good response here, but you're not coming up with one. Carry on.
Zh: 感觉这里有一个很好的回应，但你想不出。继续吧。

En: Must be hard to force your face to move in a new fashion. If you continue like this, you'll have an aneurysm. Then you'll have to, too.
Zh: 强迫你的脸以新方式移动肯定很难吧。如果继续这样下去，你会得动脉瘤的。然后你也必须这么做。

En: Nothing is inaccessible.
Zh: 没有什么是做不到的。

En: The situation at the gates is a powder keg. She says she wants to avert catastrophe but that's where she draws the line? I call bullshit.
Zh: 港口大门的局势就是个炸药桶。她说她想避免大灾难，但那就是她的底线吗？在我看来都是废话。

En: The muzzle-loaders need to be reloaded after every one, two -- or in your case three -- shots, the automatics every one or two *minutes*.
Zh: 每隔一发或者两发子弹之后，前膛枪就需要重新填装——或者在你的情况下是三发，自动武器每隔一两*分钟*才需要重新填装。

En: Then we're on the same page -- as grim as it may be.
Zh: “那我们就达成共识了——虽然可能有些残酷。”

En: Boy oh boy, is that not good...
Zh: “天啦，一点都不好……”

En: My only hope is that yours provides a single, concrete suspect -- before they indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put... She leans against the wooden planks:\n
Zh: “我唯一的希望就是你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人——在他们不分青红皂白地挑选出自己的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她倚靠在木板上：

En: It's not good, no, a wave crashes against the side of her boat. "My only hope is that you provide a single, concrete suspect before the mercenaries indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put..." She grabs hold of the mainsail:
Zh: “这一点也不好，”一阵海浪拍打在帆船侧面。“我希望你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人，赶在科奈尔任意挑选出他们的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她抓住了主帆：

En: Not a terribly good actor that one. Still...
Zh: “那个人不是个好演员。不过……”

En: Five days. Not more. Maybe sooner.
Zh: 5天。没有再多了。也许更早。

En: Occidental... that fits the statement of another witness, the lieutenant says, pleased.
Zh: “奥西登人……这跟另一个目击证人的陈述是一致的，”警督愉快地说到。

En: Okay, we'll steer clear for the moment.
Zh: “好吧，我们会暂时避开的。”

En: Okay, I'll steer clear for the moment.
Zh: “好吧，我会暂时避开的。”

En: You wouldn't even know where to start.
Zh: 你甚至都不知道该从哪里开始。

En: She sips from her thermos.
Zh: 她从保温杯里喝了一口茶水。

En: She sips from her thermos. She does not look away from you.
Zh: 她从保温杯里喝了一口茶水，目光没有从你身上移开。

En: Good for you. She looks at the coastline, then at you. "Now back to the impending blood bath."
Zh: “对你很好啊。”她看着海岸线，然后看向你。“现在继续讨论即将发生的大屠杀吧。”

En: Don't you want to know where I found my badge? (Put your badge away.)
Zh: “难道你不想知道我是在哪里找到警徽的吗？”（把警徽放在一边。）

En: I think I know where you found it, Officer Du Bois -- in that motor carriage protruding from the ice over there.
Zh: “我想我知道你是在哪里找到的，杜博阿警官——在那边冰面突起的那辆汽車里。”

En: Does this mean I don't have to look into that drug stuff?
Zh: “这是不是意味着我不需要调查毒品的事了？”

En: That's enough for now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “暂时差不多了。”（总结。）

En: You found it the motor carriage you drove into the sea.
Zh: “你是在被你开进大海的那辆汽車里找到的。”

En: Damn right.
Zh: “说的没错。”

En: No. It was a in a good, normal place.
Zh: “不。它在一个又好又普通的地方。”

En: I'm glad we reached an agreement without turning the RCM into your personal investigative unit.
Zh: “很高兴我们能达成协议，而不用把RCM变成你的私人调查小队。”

En: Do not gloat, officer. These are grave matters.
Zh: “不要幸灾乐祸，警官。这是很严肃的事情。”

En: It only lasts for the briefest instance but you catch a look of disappointment in her eye. Anger even.
Zh: 虽然只持续了短暂的几秒，但你还是在她的眼睛里捕捉到一丝失望的情绪。甚至是生气。

En: Uh, did you say *blood bath*?
Zh: “呃，你好像说了*大屠杀*？”

En: You said *contain the situation*?
Zh: “你之前说*控制局势*？”

En: What was that about a... *blood bath*?
Zh: “那这个……*大屠杀*是怎么回事？”

En: Tell me again -- how are the lynching and the strike connected?
Zh: “再跟我说说——死刑和罢工有什么联系？”

En: I've already heard about a connection between the lynching and strike, but I need your testimony.
Zh: “我已经听说私刑和罢工之间是有联系的，但是我需要你的证言。”

En: Tell me again about the connection between the lynching and the strike.
Zh: “再跟我说说私刑和罢工之间的联系。”

En: How are the lynching and the strike connected?
Zh: “私刑和罢工有什么联系？”

En: Yes. I'm afraid this strike may descend into a small scale civil war. With possible consequences for all of Revachol West.
Zh: “是的。恐怕这次罢工会演变成一场小规模的内战。很可能会影响到整个瑞瓦肖西部。”

En: Your response does not inspire great faith in the RCM.
Zh: 你的回应并没有激发她对RCM的极大信任。

En: It doesn't matter. What matters is -- you found it. And not a moment too soon.
Zh: “这不重要。重要的事——你找到它了。时机上也许算不上太早。”

En: That is exactly what it means. What I am about to tell you is more pressing, I assure you.
Zh: “就是那个意思。我可以向你保证，我接下来要说的会更加紧迫。”

En: Since you're sharing, ma'am -- this is also the RCM's worst case scenario.
Zh: “既然你这么坦白，夫人——这也是RCM遇到的最糟糕的情况。”

En: I have an indirect role to play, I'm sad to say. My employer experienced a *momentary lapse of faith* in me. In that moment they elected to deploy a private military contractor. As an *insurance* measure. They called it my 'security detail'.
Zh: “很遗憾，我也会间接地扮演一个角色。我的雇主对我产生过*短暂的信任危机*。在那个时刻，他们选择部署一支私人佣兵队伍。作为一种*保险*措施。他们称之为我的‘安保人员’。”

En: A momentary lapse of faith?
Zh: “短暂的信任危机？”

En: Do you need a security detail?
Zh: “你需要安保人员吗？”

En: They were dispatched after I relayed the Union's initial offer.
Zh: “在我转达工会的初步提议之后，他们就派来了。”

En: *Every worker*...
Zh: “*所有工人*……”

En: *...a member of the board*, she nods. "I tried to convince my employer it was simply an opening gambit, like you suggested -- or a joke even. They did not appreciate the humour."
Zh: “*……都是董事会的一员*，”她点点头。“我尝试说服我的雇主，这不过是一个开场白，你也是这么暗示的——或者甚至是一个玩笑。他们并不欣赏这种幽默。”

En: *...a member of the board*, she nods. "I tried to convince my employer it was simply a piece of rhetoric -- or a joke. They did not appreciate the humour."
Zh: “*……都是董事会的一员*，”她点点头。“我尝试说服我的雇主，这不过是一种豪言壮语——或者是一个玩笑。他们并不欣赏这种幽默。”

En: *Every worker*...
Zh: “*所有工人*……”

En: Absolutely not. These mercenaries are muscle, pure and simple. They are meant to intimidate the Union into surrendering.
Zh: “绝对不需要。这些雇佣兵肌肉发达，十分纯粹。他们的目的就是恐吓工会，让他们投降。”

En: 'Every worker a member of the board'. I tried to convince my employer it was simply a piece of rhetoric and not a serious demand. But the... *stridency* of it seems to have spooked them.
Zh: “*所有工人都是董事会的一员*。我试过说服我的雇主，这不过是一种豪言壮语，不是真正的需求。不过……这种*刺耳*的表达似乎把他们吓到了。”

En: Who are they, exactly?
Zh: “他们到底是谁？”

En: Who are they?
Zh: “他们是谁？”

En: Krenel -- an Oranjese military company. As far as I know three arrived in Martinaise. They report to me sporadically, but they do not answer to me. To be frank, our relationship is deteriorating.
Zh: “科奈尔——奥兰治的一家军事公司。据我所知，有三个人来了马丁内斯。他们偶尔会向我汇报，不过不会听我的话。老实说，我们的关系在恶化。”

En: No, no, no -- we *need* to discuss this *now*. There isn't time to waste. Blood bath, remember?
Zh: “不不不——我们*现在*就*需要*讨论。没时间浪费了。还记得吗，血浴的大屠杀？”

En: They wear ceramic armour, have semi-automatic weapons and years of combat experience. They also have Trauma-and-Stressor Disorder and no idea how to conduct themselves in an urban civilian environment.
Zh: “他们穿着陶瓷盔甲，拥有半自动武器和多年的战斗经验。他们拥有创伤应激障碍，而且无法立身于城市居民环境。”

En: The story is, one of them, the colonel -- I don't know his real name -- sexually assaulted a local woman. While he was drunk and separated from his unit. This allowed some of the more militant Union members to subdue him.
Zh: “据我所知，其中的某一个，上校——我不知道他的真名——性侵了一名当地女性。当时他喝醉了，跟自己的小队分开了。这就给工会的某些激进分子提供了制服他的理由。”

En: So what happened?
Zh: “所以发生了什么事？”

En: He was taken out behind the Whirling-in-Rags and lynched. Last Sunday night.
Zh: “他被带到褴褛飞旋后面，然后私刑处死了。就在上周日晚上。”

En: What then?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: Nothing. Mr. Claire refuses to let me into the harbour. I have not been able to discuss this matter with anyone there. The remaining two Krenel contractors carry out their orders -- for now.
Zh: “什么也没发生。克莱尔先生拒绝我进入港口。我没能跟这里的任何人讨论这件事。剩下的两名科奈尔佣兵在执行他们的命令——暂时而已。”

En: It's a smokescreen. In secret, they are conducting an independent military tribunal into the lynching. Once this *investigation* is concluded, executions will follow.
Zh: “这是一种障眼法。他们打算秘密地组织一场针对私刑的军事审判。一旦*调查*结束，紧接着就是执行。”

En: For now?
Zh: “暂时而已？”

En: What is the nature of this so-called investigation?
Zh: “这个所谓的调查是什么性质的？”

En: Whether to execute one, some, or all of the Union militants.
Zh: “是否要处决某人，某些人，或者所有工会的激进分子。”

En: Too many investigations in Martinaise.
Zh: “马丁内斯有太多调查了。”

En: You've made a mess here.
Zh: “你在这里惹了个大麻烦。”

En: My only hope is that yours provides a single, concrete suspect -- before they indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put... She leans against the wooden planks:\n
Zh: “我唯一的希望就是你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人——在他们不分青红皂白地挑选出自己的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她倚靠在木板上：

En: My only hope is that yours provides a single, concrete suspect -- before they indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put... She grabs hold of the mainsail:
Zh: “我唯一的希望就是你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人——在他们不分青红皂白地挑选出自己的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她握住了主帆：

En: If you don't pin this on someone *good* -- and do it *fast* -- they will identify and execute everyone present at the lynching. This, in turn, will force the Union to respond.
Zh: “如果你不能锁定某个*具体的*嫌疑人——而且动作必须要*快*——他们就会把出现在私刑现场的每一个人找出来，然后全部处决掉。这样一来，就会转而强迫工会做出回应。”

En: The Débardeurs have over two thousand men. It will be a thousand to one.
Zh: “装卸工会拥有2000多名成员。那就是1对1000人。”

En: They would have to. To project strength and power.
Zh: 他们必须这么做。为了表现出权势和力量。

En: Have you ever seen a hornet invade a beehive, detective? She leans back. "It's not pretty."
Zh: “你有没有见过大黄蜂侵袭蜂巢的景象，警督？”她向后靠了靠。“那场面可不太好看。”

En: Have you ever seen a hornet invade a beehive, lieutenant? She leans back. "It's not pretty."
Zh: “你有没有见过大黄蜂侵袭蜂巢的景象，警督？”她向后靠了靠。“那场面可不太好看。”

En: Maybe the investigations can team up? You know, share resources and intelligence.
Zh: “也许可以合作调查？你知道的，共享资源和情报。”

En: It was the *Union* who strung him from that tree. A wave crashes against the side of her boat. "My hope is that you provide a single, concrete suspect before Krenel indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put..." She leans against the wooden planks:\n
Zh: “是*工会*把他吊在那颗树上的。”一阵海浪拍打在帆船侧面。“我希望你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人，赶在科奈尔随意挑选出他们的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她倚靠在木板上：

En: It was the *Union* who strung him from that tree. A wave crashes against the side of her boat. "My hope is that you provide a single, concrete suspect before Krenel indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put..." She grabs hold of the mainsail:
Zh: “是*工会*把他吊在那颗树上的。”一阵海浪拍打在帆船侧面。“我希望你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人，赶在科奈尔随意挑选出他们的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她抓住了主帆：

En: They won't. My only hope is that yours provides a single, concrete suspect -- before they indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put... She leans against the wooden planks:
Zh: “他们不会的。我只希望你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人——赶在科奈尔任意挑选出他们的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她倚靠在木板上：

En: Surely you're joking. These people are killers. My only hope is that yours provides a single, concrete suspect -- before they indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put... She grabs hold of the mainsail:
Zh: “你一定是在开玩笑吧。这些人是杀手。我只希望你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人——赶在科奈尔任意挑选出他们的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她抓住了主帆：

En: Investigation upon investigation, here in Martinaise, racing toward some dark deadline.
Zh: 在马丁内斯里，进行着一项又一项调查，都在朝那个黑暗的最终期限冲刺。

En: The Union has over two thousand men.
Zh: 工会拥有2000多名成员。

En: The numbers are against them -- a thousand to one.
Zh: “那按照人数来算——不就是1000比1。”

En: What can they do against the Union? There seem to be a lot of them.
Zh: “他们能把工会怎么样呢？工会人好像不少啊。”

En: These men work in tandem using semi-and fully automatic firearms. Their armour is virtually impenetrable to muzzle-loaded weapons -- even *yours*. Most Union workers don't have guns at all...
Zh: “他们协力工作，使用的是半自动和全自动武器。前膛枪几乎无法穿透他们的盔甲——甚至*你的*也是一样。大部分工会工人连枪都没有……”

En: The Seraise Giant Hornet, the world's second largest insect, can kill forty honey bees a minute while a group of 30 can decimate an entire hive of 20,000 bees in less than four hours.
Zh: 萨弗里大黄蜂，世界上第二大的昆虫，一分钟可以杀死40只蜜蜂，如果是一群30只的大黄蜂，不到四小时就能屠杀整个蜂巢的20,000只蜜蜂。

En: The muzzle-loaders need to be reloaded after every one or two shots -- the automatics every one or two *minutes*.
Zh: 每隔一发或者两发子弹之后，前膛枪就需要重新填装——自动武器每隔一两*分钟*才需要重新填装。

En: She is silent for a moment, then concludes: "As I said -- a blood bath."
Zh: 她沉默了一会儿，然后总结到：“就像我说过的——这将是一场大屠杀。”

En: Maybe a good bloodletting is just what Martinaise needs?
Zh: 也许马丁内斯就需要一场大屠杀？

En: Wash it nice and clean...
Zh: “清洗地干干净净……”

En: Shudder.
Zh: 颤抖。

En: Isn't this a pretty... bleak scenario you're describing?
Zh: “你描述的难道不是一个非常……黯淡的情境吗？”

En: I think the confrontation is inevitable.
Zh: “我想对峙是不可避免的。”

En: Houses drenched in red, blood gushing down Rue Saint-Cispare... the whole place mopped with a giant red rag! Fresh bullet holes for all the old buildings...
Zh: 房屋被鲜血染红，鲜血顺着圣希斯帕路倾泄而下……整个地方都会被一块巨大的红布擦拭浸染！所有破旧的建筑上将会留下新鲜的弹孔……

En: It will not be a pretty sight.
Zh: “那可不会是什么美好的景象。”

En: Clean, yes. Nothing will grow here for twenty years. Krenel will send in gunships and the Coalition Government will follow suit.
Zh: “干净，没错。20年内，这里的一切都会停滞。科奈尔会被送进武装直升机，联盟政府也会有样学样。”

En: I can't see it happen. Too many things would have to go wrong first.
Zh: “我没看出会发生这种事。首先应该会有很多事情出错的。”

En: Too many things have *already* gone wrong. She looks at you, eyes damp from the wind. "Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Du Bois..."
Zh: “*已经*有很多事情出错了。”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。“双重荣誉警督杜博阿先生……”

En: Too many things have *already* gone wrong. She looks to you, eyes damp from the wind. "Nameless, badge-less detective of the Citizens Militia..."
Zh: “*已经*有很多事情出错了。”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。“既没有名字，也没有警徽的公民武装警探……”

En: Too many things have *already* gone wrong. She looks to you, eyes damp from the wind.
Zh: “*已经*有很多事情出错了。”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。

En: *Many* bleak scenarios have already come true. She looks at you, eyes damp from the wind. "Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Du Bois..."
Zh: “*很多*黯淡的情境已经变成现实。”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。“双重荣誉警督杜博阿先生……”

En: *Many* bleak scenarios have already come true. She looks at you, eyes damp from the wind. "Nameless, badge-less detective of the Citizens Militia..."
Zh: “*很多*黯淡的情境已经变成现实。”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。“既没有名字，也没有警徽的公民武装警探……”

En: *Many* bleak scenarios have already come to pass to lead us here... She looks at you, eyes damp from the wind.
Zh: “*很多*黯淡的情境已经将我们引向了现在的局势……”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。

En: It doesn't have to be like this. She looks at you, eyes damp from the wind. "Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Du Bois..."
Zh: “其实不用搞成这样的。”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。“双重荣誉警督杜博阿先生……”

En: It doesn't have to be like this. She looks at you, eyes damp from the wind. "Nameless, badge-less officer of the Citizens Militia..."
Zh: “其实不用搞成这样的。”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。“既没有名字，也没有警徽的公民武装警探……”

En: It doesn't have to be like this. She looks at you, eyes damp from the wind.
Zh: “其实不用搞成这样的。”她看着你，眼睛因为海风变得有些湿润。

En: All we can do is keep that which has not from following suit. One single, concrete suspect delivered into Civil Court -- and I *may* be able to defuse this situation.
Zh: “我们唯一能做的就是不让事情朝那个方向发展。只要能将一个具体的嫌疑人送上民事法庭——我*也许*能平息这种情况。”

En: All we can do is keep the rest from going the same way. One single, concrete suspect delivered into Civil Court -- and I *may* be able to defuse this situation.
Zh: “我们唯一能做的就是不让其他事情往坏的方向发展。只要能将一个具体的嫌疑人送上民事法庭——我*也许*能平息这种情况。”

En: One single, concrete suspect delivered into Civil Court -- a good one -- and I *may* be able to defuse this situation.
Zh: “只要能将一个具体的嫌疑人送上民事法庭——我*也许*能平息这种情况。”

En: See, Kim. This is that Great Bloodletting I've been telling you about.
Zh: “看见没，金。这就是我一直在跟你说的大屠杀。”

En: See, this is the Great Bloodletting I've been telling everyone about.
Zh: “看见没，这就是我一直在跟所有人说的大屠杀。”

En: I am sorry to confirm it.
Zh: “证实这一点，我很抱歉。”

En: It appears you've found it, yes. And I'm not happy for you.
Zh: “看起来你找到了它了，没错。我并没有为你感到高兴。”

En: All we can do is keep the little that has not from following suit. One single, concrete suspect delivered into Civil Court -- and I *may* be able to defuse this situation.
Zh: “我们唯一能做的就是不让事情朝那个方向发展。只要能将一个具体的嫌疑人送上民事法庭——我*也许*能平息这种情况。”

En: All we can do is keep the rest from following suit. One single, concrete suspect delivered into Civil Court -- and I *may* be able to defuse this situation.
Zh: “我们唯一能做的就是不让其他事情往坏的方向发展。只要能将一个具体的嫌疑人送上民事法庭——我*也许*能平息这种情况。”

En: One single, concrete suspect delivered into Civil Court -- and I *may* be able to defuse this situation.
Zh: “其实不用搞成这样的。只要能将一个具体的嫌疑人送上民事法庭——我*也许*能平息这种情况。”

En: I am sorry to have been the bearer of bad news. If there is anything else I can help you with, please ask.
Zh: “抱歉我带来了坏消息。如果还有什么能帮到你的话，请尽快开口。”

En: She cannot let you leave without having her say.
Zh: 她没把话说完之前，是不会让你走的。

En: Where are the remaining two mercs now?
Zh: “现在其他两个雇佣兵在哪？”

En: This *colonel*, the one who was hanged -- did you know him?
Zh: “这个*上校*，就是吊起来的那个男人——你认识他吗？”

En: I have to say -- this is *not* disco.
Zh: “我不得不说——这一点*也不*迪斯科。”
