En: It is very far from *disco*. A wave crashes against the side of her boat. "My only hope is that you provide a single, concrete suspect before the mercenaries indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put..." She leans against the wooden planks:
Zh: “它离*迪斯科*差了十万八千里。”一阵海浪拍打在帆船侧面。“我希望你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人，赶在科奈尔任意挑选出他们的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她倚靠在木板上：

En: It is very far from *disco*. A wave crashes against the side of her boat. "My only hope is that you provide a single, concrete suspect before the mercenaries indiscriminately pick theirs. Simply put..." She grabs hold of the mainsail:
Zh: “它离*迪斯科*差了十万八千里。”一阵海浪拍打在帆船侧面。“我希望你能提供一个具体的嫌疑人，赶在科奈尔任意挑选出他们的嫌疑人之前。简而言之……”她抓住了主帆：

En: What can you tell me about Krenel?
Zh: “关于科奈尔，你能告诉我些什么信息？”

En: You said the deceased assaulted a woman?
Zh: “你刚才说性侵一名女性？”

En: How much time do we have?
Zh: “我们还有多少时间？”

En: If you still have access to the ICP's database, you could run a better background check than I ever could. It may take some time, though... She thinks.
Zh: “如果你还能访问ICP数据库，你就能做一个比我更好的背景调查。不过，可能需要花费一点时间……”她想到。

En: Do you know a lot about the inner workings of the RCM and the ICP, ma'am?
Zh: “你对RCM和ICP的内部事务了解很多吗，夫人？”

En: They've gone to ground, as it were. I don't recommend seeking them out. She raises a cautionary finger. "For one -- they're likely to be armed to the teeth..."
Zh: “可以说，他们已经转入地下了。我不建议把他们找出来。”她竖起一只骨瘦如柴的手指。“有一点——他们很肯定是全副武装的……”

En: They've gone to ground, as it were. I don't recommend seeking them out. She puts her foot on the guardwire, for balance. "For one -- they're almost certainly armed to the teeth."
Zh: “可以说，他们已经转入地下了。我不建议把他们找出来。”她把脚踩在防护线上，让身体保持平衡。“举例来说——他们肯定是全副武装的。”

En: They don't have the same respect for the Revachol Citizens Militia as I do. To put it bluntly they think you're vigilantes, *ghetto savages*. It will not be a fruitful meeting.
Zh: “他们并不像我一样尊重瑞瓦肖公民武装。不客气地说，他们觉得你是义务警员，*贫民窟里的野蛮人*。如果会面的话，肯定不会取得什么成效。”

En: Vigilantes? You're a professional officer of the only *legitimate* authority in Revachol.
Zh: 义务警员？你是瑞瓦肖唯一*合法*当局的一名专业警员。

En: We still need to know where they are.
Zh: “我们还是需要知道他们在哪里。”

En: I still need to know where they are.
Zh: “我还是需要知道他们在哪里。”

En: Okay, we'll steer clear for the moment.
Zh: “好吧，我们会暂时避开的。”

En: Okay, I'll steer clear for the moment.
Zh: “好吧，我会暂时避开的。”

En: We'll confront them *directly*.
Zh: “我们会直接跟他们对峙。”

En: I'll confront them directly.
Zh: “我会直接跟他们对峙。”

En: You should show those meatheads exactly how *savage* you really are.
Zh: 你应该让那些傻瓜看看，你到底有多*野蛮*。

En: They just need someone to put the *Fear of Revachol* into their hearts.
Zh: “他们只是需要有人让他们把*瑞瓦肖的恐惧*记在心里。”

En: Vigilantes? The RCM gets its authority from the Coalition. Where does theirs come from?
Zh: “义务警员？RCM的权利是联盟赋予的。他们的权利是从哪里来的？”

En: You're likely to run into them eventually. When that happens, I'll be in a better position to mediate if I don't appear *involved*.
Zh: “你最后很有可能会碰见他们的。在那种情况下，如果我摆出没有*参与其中*的姿态，也许能更好地从中斡旋。”

En: That's wise, she nods. "Our goal is to *avert* catastrophe. Besides," she sighs, "You're likely to encounter them sooner or later."
Zh: “这样比较明智，”她点点头。“我们的目标是*避免*灾难。况且，”她叹了一口气。“你迟早会遇到他们的。”

En: That is a *bad* idea, one I won't be a party to. She shakes her head emphatically.
Zh: “这是个*坏*主意，我是不会参与其中的。”她毅然决然地摇摇头。

En: Somehow I doubt that lecturing them on the legitimate use of force will persuade them to stand down.
Zh: “不知怎么的，我怀疑合法使用武力的说教不能劝服他们停手。”

En: Of course -- excuse my hesitation before. She reaches over the guardwire and takes the photo; holds it in her hand...
Zh: “当然了——请原谅我之前的犹豫不决。”她跨过防护线，接过相片；然后把它握在手里……”

En: She reaches for the photo; takes it, then holds it in her hand...
Zh: 她朝相片伸出手；拿起它，然后握在手里……

En: She wears fingerless gloves, her fingernails are cut short and fractured. Like those of a working woman.
Zh: 她戴着一副无指手套，手指甲剪得很短，而且还断裂了。就像工人女性一样。

En: It was taken with a Trigat, not long ago. This is the man's upper body. There were no more markings on his hands or legs.
Zh: “这是用崔格特相机拍的，就在不久之前。这是男人的上半身。他的手或者胳膊上没有别的记号。”

En: Stay quiet. Observe the woman's expression.
Zh: 保持安静。观察女人的表情。

En: What do you think?
Zh: “你怎么看？”

En: Her mouth is relaxed, the accordion lines near her mouth vanish. The pearls of her eyes move slowly on the photo's surface.
Zh: 她的嘴唇很放松，嘴角附近的褶皱消失了。她的眼珠慢慢在相片表面移动。

En: She has no excess of emotions for this cadaver. Has she seen dead bodies before? It's likely.
Zh: 她对这具尸体没有表现出过度的情绪。她之前见过尸体吗？有这个可能。

En: Sorry, she breaks her concentration. "I was trying to see if I can read the web of interdependencies between these points -- the stars." She points to one on the photo paper.
Zh: “抱歉，”她分散了自己的注意力。“我想试着看能不能阅读这些点之间的相互依赖的网格——就是这些星星。”她指着相片上的其中一颗。

En: I can't. But that's how you read this story. The points themselves don't have letters, numbers, anything. Their size, location on the body and distance from each other tells you what they represent.
Zh: “我不能。不过阅读故事就是这么回事。这些点本身没有字母，数字或者任何东西。它们的大小，在身体上的位置，还有彼此之间的距离，都能讲述它们代表的信息。”

En: Close, she nods. "Port cities. This is an Oranjese Map of the Waterways -- a sailor's tattoo worn by wayfarers of the Dolorian century, over 300 years ago. The sailors would mark their bodies to map their travels."
Zh: “接近，”她点点头。“港口城市。这是奥兰治的水路地图——那是300多年以前的德洛莉丝世纪，旅人身上的水手纹身。水手会用他们的身体来绘制旅程的地图。”

En:
What travels did the dead man make?	“那死者经历过什么旅行？”
0x01000045000089FB	Dialogue Text	PIER / JOYCE LYNCHING AND TATTOOS	Joyce Messier	Quite a few. Vredefort -- the Oranjese capital -- traditionally stands on the right shoulder." She points to it on the photo. "He started somewhere near here, I think."
Zh: “挺多的。弗里德堡——奥兰治首都——习惯放在右肩。”她指着相片说到。“我想，他是从那附近的某个地方开始的。”

En: Revachol, she says. "Those are the two constants: Vredefort on the shoulder and Revachol in the heart. They started the tradition of these maps right after the discovery of Insulinde, at the dawn of the Interisolary Age."
Zh: “瑞瓦肖，”她说到。“这里有两个恒定点：肩膀上的弗里德堡，还有心脏上的瑞瓦肖。就在发现伊苏林迪之后，他们就开创了这种地图传统，就在跨洲时代初期。”

En: You said you can't read it.
Zh: “你说你看不懂。”

En: Until the executions start? Truthfully -- I don't know. It depends on their progress identifying the members of the lynch mob. And their impatience.
Zh: “直到处刑开始？说真的——我不知道。这取决于他们辨认私刑成员的进度如何。还有他们的耐性。”

En: They don't report their progress to you?
Zh: “他们没有跟你汇报进度吗？”

En: Not on this matter. I'm afraid they consider this a personal initiative. There is a brief silence. Seagulls squawk over the bay...
Zh: “这件事没有。恐怕他们觉得这是私人事情。”一阵短暂的沉默。海鸥在海湾尖叫着……

En: What? You mean you don't know what's going on?
Zh: “什么？你的意思是你不知道发生了什么事？”

En: Like stars in the sky?
Zh: “就像天空中的星星吗？”

En: Places? Like stars in the sky?
Zh: “地点？就像天空中的星星吗？”

En: Like vias on a microcircuit?
Zh: “就像微型电路上的小孔吗？”

En: Places? Like vias on a microcircuit?
Zh: “地点？就像微型电路上的小孔吗？”

En: Like blooms in a pattern?
Zh: “就像花朵盛开的图案吗？”

En: Places? Like blooms in a pattern?
Zh: “地点？就像花朵盛开的图案吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Port cities. On the oceans. This is an Oranjese Map of the Waterways -- a sailor's tattoo worn by wayfarers of the Dolorian century -- as early as 300 years ago. The sailors would mark their bodies to map their travels.
Zh: “港口城市。在大海上。这是奥兰治的水路地图——德洛莉丝世纪旅人身上的水手纹身——那是早在300多年以前的事了。水手会用他们的身体来绘制旅程的地图。”

En: Somewhere in an office lit by a single green desk lamp captain Ptolemaios Pryce -- 58, bald and bespectacled -- is writing in a ledger on his desk. Rows and rows of days and weeks, laconic remarks in a single column: *patrol*, *case*, *vacation*, *injured*...
Zh: 在某处的一间点着一盏绿色台灯的办公室里，警长托勒密·普莱斯——58岁，秃头，戴着一副眼镜——正在书桌前记录手册。一排排的是日期和星期，还有一列简短的备注：*巡逻*，*查案*，*休假*，*受伤*……

En: Vredefort? Vredefort has a military academy. The Semenese islands saw a recent conflict. In Revachol he was executed.
Zh: 弗里德堡？弗里德堡有一所军事学院。塞美尼岛最近发生了冲突。而他在瑞瓦肖被处刑了。

En: I can't. This man was no sailor -- and these are no ports. I can understand geographic fragments, but not their meaning.
Zh: “我不懂。这男人不是个水手——这些也不是港口。我能理解地理上的碎片，但是不知道它们的含义。”

En: What is the use of this map?
Zh: “这张地图有什么用？”

En: Who could tell me more?
Zh: “谁能告诉我更多信息？”

En: That's all for the tattoos. Thank you for your help.
Zh: “纹身的事就到这里吧。感谢你的帮助。”

En: The sailor's soul would use it to fly back home if they should die abroad. This is a sort of... contraption. To be reeled back in by. The *silver cord*, they would call it.
Zh: “如果水手死在国外的话，他们的灵魂就会用它飞回到自己的家乡。这是某种……很精妙的设计。地图之后将被回收，他们把它叫做*脐带*。”

En: This one is going nowhere but the morgue.
Zh: “这人除了停尸房，哪里都去不了。”

En: I've spoken to him. For now the soul is fastened inside his corpse.
Zh: “我跟他说过话了。目前他的灵魂还被困在自己的尸体里。”

En: This one has flown quite far by now.
Zh: “这家伙现在已经飞得很远了。”

En: Nowhere. There is no soul.
Zh: 任何地方都没有。根本就没有灵魂。

En: The times have changed.
Zh: “时代已经变了。”

En: She remains unfazed. "That is precisely what the sailors feared when they drew these maps. A fear of drowning -- within one's own corpse."
Zh: 她还是镇定自如。“水手绘制这些地图的时候，害怕的就是这个。害怕沉溺——在自己的尸体里。”

En: I would say he's near the Arcade Islands by now, ready to exit the Insulindian and enter the pale. If I've read his home address correctly. She smiles.
Zh: “我会说现在他已经接近阿卡德岛了，准备离开伊苏林迪，进入灰域。如果我没把他的家乡地址弄错的话。”她笑了。

En: The Old Old World passing by and the New New World already here.
Zh: 旧旧世界已经过去，新新世界已经到来。

En: You said you can't read it -- seems like you can.
Zh: “你说你看不懂——似乎你可以啊。”

En: Where is he now?
Zh: 他现在在哪？

En: An empire without an emperor.
Zh: 一个没有皇帝的帝国。

En: ...for about half a minute -- in silence.
Zh: ……大约过去了半分钟——沉默无语。

En: It's a matter of days, not weeks.
Zh: “几天的事情，而不是几星期。”

En: Whatever I can help you with. We need to contain the situation.
Zh: “我会尽我所能地帮你。我们必须控制这种局势。”

En: Then he made his way to the Preto Grande, through what I think must be Staadskanaal -- an artificial channel through the Occident. From the Preto he sailed to the Insulindic ocean. First the Semenese islands, then this... She points to his heart.
Zh: “然后他去了帕累托格兰德，穿过了——我觉得应该是斯塔德卡纳尔——跨越奥西登国家的一条人工运河。从帕累托，他驶向了伊苏林迪大洋。首先是塞美尼岛，然后是这里……”她指向他的心脏。

En: What next?
Zh: “接下来呢？”

En: Then what?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: What is that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: *IN MARTINAISE, LOOKING INTO KRENEL*, he writes in one. Then the man puts down his pen and rubs his temples with both hands. Outside there is a siren, distant gunshots on the streets of the Jamrock Quarter.
Zh: *在马丁内斯，调查科奈尔*，他在其中一排写到。接着，男人放下笔，用两只手按揉太阳穴。外面警报响起，加姆洛克区的街道上传来遥远的枪声。

En: This man is no brother of mine, but this is his service history.
Zh: “这个男人不是我的兄弟，不过这是他的服役历史。”

En: Maybe it's a record of his military career?
Zh: “也许是他从军的记录？”

En: Leave it be.
Zh: 不聊了。

En: Is that all? She puts down the photo.
Zh: “就这些吗？”她放下相片。

En: That makes sense to me. We have no more use for a Map of the Waterways. Just like we don't need sailors the way we used to. This is what the custom would morph into on the Occident -- mercenary tattoos.
Zh: “有道理。水路地图对于我们来说不再有用了。就像我们不再像以前那样需要水手了。这就是这种习俗在奥西登国家的变体——雇佣兵纹身。”

En: For all the boys looking for adventure -- a blood spatter on the seas.
Zh: 对于所有那些寻求冒险的男孩子来说——这就是飞溅在大海上的鲜血。

En: His platoon members? The other contractors -- though I do *not* suggest you go and show them that picture. This man was their friend and comrade.
Zh: “他的野战排成员？其他雇佣兵——我*不*建议你去找他们，把相片拿给他们看。这个男人是他们的朋友和战友。”

En: I need the information. (Mark it down: ask mercs about tattoos.)
Zh: “我需要信息。”（记录下来：询问雇佣兵纹身的事。）

En: You're right. Not a good idea. (Leave it off the schedule.)
Zh: “你说得对，这不是个好主意。”（不要把它列入计划。）

En: It could go this or the other way. Maybe if you're tactful, it could be beneficial?
Zh: 反正不是这样就是那样吧。也许你可以圆滑一些，可能会有益处呢？

En: Do what you have to do, detective Du Bois. *I* don't think deciphering that tattoo should come before public security. But if you should wade into the mob to find out -- I couldn't stop you.
Zh: “做你该做的吧，杜博阿警探。*我*不觉得破译那个纹身会比公共安全的优先级更高。不过如果你死命冲进暴徒中间想要找个答案——我也不能阻止你。”

En: Do what you have to do, detective. I don't think deciphering that tattoo should come before public security. But if you should wade into the mob to find out -- I couldn't stop you.
Zh: “做你该做的吧，警探。我不觉得破译那个纹身会比公共安全的优先级更高。不过如果你死命冲进暴徒中间想要找个答案——我也不能阻止你。”

En: I am relieved you think so. I don't think deciphering that tattoo should come before public security.
Zh: “你能这么想让我很宽慰。我不觉得破译那个纹身会比公共安全的优先级更高。”

En: We will be careful, ma'am.
Zh: “我们会小心的，夫人。”

En: She hands you back the photo. "Is there anything else I can help you with?"
Zh: 她把相片交还给你。“还有别的能帮你的吗？”

En: *Krenel* -- an Oranjese military company. As far as I know three arrived in Martinaise. They report to me sporadically, but they do not answer to me. To be frank, our relationship is deteriorating.
Zh: “科奈尔——奥兰治的一家军事公司。据我所知，有三个人来了马丁内斯。他们偶尔会向我汇报，不过不会听我的话。老实说，我们的关系在恶化。”

En: My bad, I thought you said *mud* bath...
Zh: “我的错，我把血浴听成了*泥浴*……”

En: You're right...
Zh: “你说得对……”

En: The International Collaboration Police (ICP) is charged with coordinating law enforcement activities in complex, multi-national investigations. Their database is the single largest source of information in the world.
Zh: 国际联合刑警(ICP)负责在复杂的跨国调查活动中协调执法行动。他们的数据库是世界上最大的信息来源地。

En: It's not the first I've heard of this. Where did this assault take place? And when?
Zh: “我不是第一次听说这个了。这个侵犯发生在什么地方？什么时候？”

En: The lieutenant consults his notebook, his eyebrows knitted in concentration...
Zh: 警督翻阅了一下笔记本，眉头拧成了一个结……

En: Odd... we haven't heard any reports about an *assault* in connection with the lynching. Where did it take place? And when?
Zh: “奇怪……我们没有收到任何跟私刑有关的*侵犯*报告。这件事发生在哪里？什么时候？”

En: Did you miss the part about *ceramic armour* and *automatic weapons*, detective?
Zh: “你错过了*陶瓷盔甲*和*自动武器*那部分吗，警探？”

En: That's less than reassuring.
Zh: “这一点也不能让人放心。”

En: I'm not trying to reassure you, officer. Quite the opposite, in fact. There is a brief silence. Seagulls squawk over the bay...
Zh: “我不是想让你放心，警官。事实上，正好相反。”一阵短暂的沉默。海鸥在海湾尖叫着……

En: Good. Then you've made progress. It's imperative that you move fast. The *tribunal* will not be patient.
Zh: “很好。那你取得了一定进展。你的行动有必要再快一点。*审判*可没什么耐性。”

En: Where *are* these mercenaries?
Zh: 这些雇佣兵*在哪里*？

En: Sent by the Pines.
Zh: 野松公司派来的。

En: Scab Leader murder bad reply.
Zh: 工贼头领对于谋杀的糟糕回复。

En: Clothes didn't fit right.
Zh: 衣服不合身。

En: Scab Leader built like brick.
Zh: 工贼头领体格健壮。

En: Scab Leader bad acting.
Zh: 工贼头领的糟糕演技。

En: Didn't pass background check.
Zh: 没有通过背景调查。

En: Someone you haven't met.
Zh: 有个你没见过的人。

En: Dresscode: Ceramic knight.
Zh: 着装要求：陶瓷骑士。

En: One is obviously the *Scab Leader* -- at the harbour gates. The one chanting the idiotic slogans. He's barely maintaining his disguise.
Zh: 其中一个显然是*工贼领导*——就在港口大门附近。就是念叨着愚蠢口号的那个。他几乎掩藏不了自己的伪装。

En: The other has a vantage point in a building South of the roundabout -- they were keeping tabs on you while you were canvassing the lorry drivers.
Zh: 另一个在环岛南部某个建筑里有利位置——在你游说那些货車司机的时候，他们一直在密切关注你。

En: No idea. Maybe she's right -- it's a better idea not to confront them? Also, there's something wrong with your brain. This was so easy, how could you fail it?
Zh: 不知道。也许她是对的——最好不要直接跟他们对峙？还有，你的脑袋出了点问题。要是真这么容易的话，你又怎么会失败呢？

En: One must be at the harbour gates, causing trouble. Directing strikebreakers perhaps? You can hear the sounds of the ruckus coming from there all the time.
Zh: 一个肯定在港口大门制造麻烦。可能在指挥那些破坏罢工者？你一直都能听见那边传来的喧闹声。

En: This is so embarrassing. You have no idea. There's something *wrong* with your brain. Luckily the lieutenant still has his.
Zh: 这也太尴尬了。你不知道。你的脑袋有点*问题*。幸好警督的没问题。

En: And the other one, de Paule, must be in one of the four-story buildings overlooking the roundabout. She was reporting back to you while we were canvassing the lorry drivers.
Zh: “还有另外一个，波勒，肯定在一栋四层建筑里，俯瞰着整个环岛。我们在游说货車司机的时候，她一定在向你汇报。”

En: And the other must be in one of the four-story buildings overlooking the roundabout. He or she was reporting back to you while we were canvassing the lorry drivers.
Zh: “另外一个肯定在一栋四层建筑里，俯瞰着整个环岛。我们在游说货車司机的时候，他或者她一定在向你汇报。”

En: That *would* afford a good vantage point. She nods, poised and unperturbed. "*Still* -- I hope you heed my advice. There's no need to kick the hornet's nest."
Zh: “那*的确*是一个绝佳的有利位置。”她点点头，镇定而平静。“*不过*——我希望你留心我的建议。没必要去捅马蜂窝的。”

En: Ma'am, with all due respect -- I've been around Martinaise and there's a giant hulk of a man in ill-fitting clothes at the harbour gates. Mr. 'Right to Work.' His heart *really* isn't in it.
Zh: “夫人，如我直言——我在马丁内斯附近转了一圈，港口大门那边有个衣服非常不合身的大块头男人。那位‘工作权先生’。他的心思*真的*完全不在里面。”

En: For all your talk of averting this catastrophe, the situation at the gates is a powder keg. Does this not bother you?
Zh: “你一直都在说要避免这场灾难，但是港口大门的局势就是个炸药桶。难道你不会感到不安吗？”

En: I'm thinking the company put one leading strikebreakers at the gates. What do *you* think?
Zh: “我在想公司派了一个工贼领袖在港口大门闹事。*你*怎么看呢？”

En: One must be the goon in ill-fitting work clothes by the harbour gates. The scab leader.
Zh: “其中一个肯定是港口大门那个穿着不合身工作服的怪人。工贼头领。”

En: One is probably in a building overlooking the roundabout.
Zh: “有一个可能在一栋能够俯瞰环岛的建筑里。”

En: I had another question for you.
Zh: “我还有其他问题要问你。”

En: Your concern may be appearances. Ours is keeping the peace.
Zh: “你关心的也许只是外在。我们关心的维护和平。”

En: That may be so. She is poised and unperturbed. "I still hope you heed my advice -- there's no need to kick the hornet's nest."
Zh: “也许是这样吧。”她镇定而平静。“我还是希望你留心我的建议——没必要去捅马蜂窝的。”

En: I hope I can answer it better.
Zh: “我希望我能给出更好的答案。”

En: That *would* afford a good vantage point, she says. "In any case, it's practically inaccessible."
Zh: “那*的确*会提供一个绝佳的有利位置。”她说到。“不管怎么说，它实际上是很难接近的。”

En: Heavens no, I'm not an undercover agent. There's a shortwave at the ship's wheel, she nods toward the sloop's cabin.
Zh: “我的天，当然没有，我不是卧底。帆船的方向盘上有个短波电台，”她朝着船舱点了点头。

En: Where is your radio, for contacting them -- if I may ask? Do you have an ear piece?
Zh: “你联系他们的无线电在哪——如果你不介意我这么问的话？你有耳机吗？”

En: Of course it bothers me, lieutenant, but my hands are tied. How would my employer react if it appeared I were intervening on behalf of the *Union*?
Zh: “当然会让我不安了，警督，不过我有诸多不便。如果我代表*工会*介入的话，我的雇主会有什么样的反应？”

En: Surely there are other people to ask about the tattoo. This is not necessary to complete the task, officer -- it's a dangerous side-task. Search elsewhere.
Zh: 肯定还能找其他人问纹身的事。没必要完成这个任务，警官——这是个很危险的支线任务。再去别处找找吧。

En: Challenge accepted! I say we do it.
Zh: 接受挑战！要我说，我们就接了吧。

En: And here I thought my modest payment of 70 reál would stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “我还以为我那微薄的70雷亚尔的报酬能够坚持下去……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: Side-shifting? Of course, *that's* why my payment of 70 reál didn't stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “转换立场？当然，*这就是*我那70雷亚尔的报酬无法坚持下去的原因……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: We're pulling the plug on this. Nothing else to be done. Good luck, everyone.
Zh: 我们打算拔掉插头了。别无选择了。祝好运，各位。

En: She looks beyond you, despondent.
Zh: 她沮丧地望着你身后。

En: Here comes the dark. The plug will be pulled... now.
Zh: 黑暗要来了。准备拔出插手……现在。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: She enjoys a small sip.
Zh: 她美美地抿了一小口茶水。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: She reconsiders that sip.
Zh: 她在重新考虑要不要喝口茶水。

En: Could it be all my fellows have failed? Alack and alas, sire! Forgive me! I shall do my best to end this pantomime...
Zh: 难道吾辈所有的伙伴全都失败了？呜呼哀哉，大人！请原谅吾辈！吾将拼尽全力终结这场哑剧……

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Sir...
Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Oh, she raises her brow as to say -- *and*?
Zh: “哦，”她扬起眉毛，似乎在说——*所以*呢？

En: You have? She smiles, carefully. "And how did you like Mr. Claire?"
Zh: “是吗？”她谨慎地笑了。“你觉得克莱尔先生怎么样？”

En: I didn't.
Zh: “我不喜欢他。”

En: He's a beautiful man. Beautiful and *just*.
Zh: “他是个出色的男人。又出色，又*正直*。”

En: Evrart Claire is a hero of the worker's movement. He is the champion I've sworn fealty to.
Zh: “艾弗拉特·克莱尔是工人运动的英雄。他是我宣誓效忠的勇士。”

En: He's a bloated rainbow-socialist.
Zh: “他是个臃肿的彩虹工人主义者。”

En: I can do business with him. For a socialist he's reasonable.
Zh: “我可以跟他做交易。对于一个工人主义者来说，他很通情达理。”

En: He is not the champion I have chosen -- I wish to swear fealty to you and the Cause of Capital. I too am an Ultra.
Zh: “他不是我选择的勇士——我愿意发誓效忠你和资本的事业。我也是一个极端主义者。”

En: He is not the champion I have chosen -- I wish to swear fealty to you and the Cause of Capital.
Zh: “他不是我选择的勇士——我愿意发誓效忠你和资本的事业。”

En: I have the feeling the international community does not approve of him.
Zh: “我感觉国际社会不会赞同他的。”

En: It's not important if I liked him. I was doing my job.
Zh: “我喜不喜欢他不重要。我只是在做自己的工作。”

En: Oh, come on now! He has his uses -- how else would he have stayed in power all these years? Or wait -- actually... She answers her own question:
Zh: “哦，拜托！他也有他的可取之处——不然这些年他是怎么掌权的呢？或者，等等——事实上……”她回答了自己的问题：

En: Corruption! That's how he's done it. Fantastic, würm-like corruption reaching into the bowels of the Earth. She looks at the ground and nods.
Zh: “腐败！他就是这么做的。太棒了，像蠕虫一般的腐败，深入地心。”她看着地面，点了点头。

En: The position of my *unusual* colleague does not reflect official policy. I hope you understand -- the RCM does not pick sides.
Zh: “我这位*不寻常*同事的立场不代表官方政策。我希望你能理解——RCM不会选择站在哪一边的。”

En: The RCM does not pick sides in this, madam. I hope it doesn't come off any other way.
Zh: “RCM不会选择站边的，夫人。我希望这件事不会以任何其他方式实现。”

En: Of course, he must have his practical uses -- how else could he have kept his position all these years... Or wait, actually...
Zh: “当然，他肯定有自己的实际用途——不然这么些年他是怎么能保住自己的位置……或者，等等，事实上……”

En: He *could* have kept it with corruption! Fantastic, würm-like corruption that reaches into the bowels of the Earth. She looks at the ground and nods in agreement with herself.
Zh: “他*可能*是通过腐败保住地位的！太棒了，像蠕虫一般的腐败，深入地心。”她看着地面，点头对自己的想法表示赞同。

En: Of course -- and I don't expect you to share anything he told you with me. I am not a *corrupt würm* myself. She turns to you: "However, if you felt like *discussing* something..."
Zh: “当然——我也不指望你分享任何他告诉你的事。我自己可不是*腐败的蠕虫。”她转向你：“不过，如果你想要*讨论*什么事情的话……”

En: Of course I don't expect you to share anything he told you with me. I'm not a *würm* myself. There's a pause. "But -- if you felt like passing *some* information..."
Zh: “当然，我也不指望你分享任何他告诉你的事。我自己可不是*腐败的蠕虫。”她转向你：“不过，如果你想要传递*某些*信息的话……”

En: What?! She laughs. "I am afraid you have mis-read the situation, detective. This is not some feudal conflict, it is a negotiation."
Zh: “什么？！”她笑了。“恐怕你把情况理解错了，警探。这不是什么封建冲突，这是一场谈判。”

En: And if it *were* a conflict, the RCM would not pick sides. My colleague's *unusual* approach to police work does not represent the organization at this time.
Zh: “而且，如果这*是*一场冲突的话，RCM也不会选择站边。在这个时候，我这位同事对待警察工作*不寻常*的方法，并不能代表整个组织。”

En: I've seen him and believe me -- it's a conflict. I've picked my side in the coming storm and it's you.
Zh: “我见过他了，相信我——这就是一场冲突。在即将到来的风暴中，我已经选好了要站在哪边——你这边。”

En: Okay, yes, perhaps I misread the situation as a bit more *Man From Hjelmdall* than it is.
Zh: “好吧，是的，也许我搞情况理解错了，以为它比*来自赫姆达尔的男人*还要更严重一些。”

En: Okay, yes, perhaps I misread the situation. And the era -- it's not feudal.
Zh: “好吧，是的，也许我搞情况理解错了。而且这个时代——也不是封建时代。”

En: Of course. And I don't expect you to share anything he told you with me. I'm not a *corrupt würm* myself. There's a pause. "However, if you felt like passing *some* information..."
Zh: “当然。而且我也不指望你分享任何他告诉你的事。我自己可不是*腐败的蠕虫。”她停顿了一下。“不过，如果你想要传递*某些*信息的话……”

En: Well, I can't stop you, can I? Just understand that I don't *expect* you to share anything he told you with me. I'm not a *corrupt würm* myself. There's a pause. "However, if you felt like passing *some* information..."
Zh: “呃，我也阻止不了你，不是吗？你要明白，我没有*指望*你分享任何他告诉你的事。我自己可不是*腐败的蠕虫。”她停顿了一下。“不过，如果你想要传递*某些*信息的话……”

En: Of course, detective. Excuse me for implying otherwise.
Zh: “当然，警探。请原谅我的暗示。”

En: The RCM does not pick sides in this. I hope it doesn't come off any other way.
Zh: “RCM不会选择站边的。我希望这件事不会以任何其他方式实现。”

En: I don't expect you to share anything he told you with me. I'm not a *corrupt würm* myself. There's a pause. "However, if you felt like passing *some* information..."
Zh: “我不指望你分享任何他告诉你的事。我自己可不是*腐败的蠕虫。”她停顿了一下。“不过，如果你想要传递*某些*信息的话……”

En: Just? Hmm... in what way?
Zh: “正直？嗯……以怎样的方式呢？”

En: In a funky socialist way. He looks out for the people.
Zh: “以一种放克风的工人主义的方式。他很照顾大家。”

En: In a nice, crunchy, white working class way of course.
Zh: “当然了，是以一种美好松脆的、白人工人阶级的方式。”

En: Yeah, he's not actually just, is he? He's useless.
Zh: “是啊，其实他一点也不正直，不是吗？他一无是处。”

En: Of course. *Some* of them, at least. The ones who work for him -- how else would he have stayed in power all these years... no wait, actually...
Zh: “当然。至少*有部分人*是。那些为他做事的人。——不然这些年他是怎么能掌控权利的……不对，等等，其实……”

En: Of course. How else would he have stayed in power all these years if it were not for *ethnic nationalism*... or wait...
Zh: “当然。如果不是*族群民族主义*，这些年他是怎么能掌控权利的……不对，等等……”

En: Huh! She nods, understandingly.
Zh: “哈！”她点点头，似有所悟。

En: There may still be room for another bigwig in my life -- in the future. Be on the lookout for a side-shifting class knight.
Zh: “在我的生命里——未来的生命里——也许还有留给另一位大人物的空间。要留心一位转换立场的阶级骑士。”

En: Yes -- I have *chosen* his side in the class war. My loyalty does not have a price and is non-negotiable. Sorry.
Zh: “是的——在这场阶级战争中，我已经*选择*站在他那边。我的忠诚是无价的，而且毫无商量余地。很抱歉。”

En: And here I thought my modest payment of 30 reál would stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “我还以为我那微薄的30雷亚尔的报酬能够坚持下去……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: And here I thought my modest payment of 100 reál would stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “我还以为我那微薄的100雷亚尔的报酬能够坚持下去……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: And here I thought my modest payment of 40 reál would stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “我还以为我那微薄的40雷亚尔的报酬能够坚持下去……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: And here I thought my modest payment of 130 reál would stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “我还以为我那微薄的130雷亚尔的报酬能够坚持下去……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: Side-shifting? Of course, *that's* why my payment of 30 reál didn't stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “转换立场？当然，*这就是*我那30雷亚尔的报酬无法坚持下去的原因……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: Side-shifting? Of course, *that's* why my payment of 100 reál didn't stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “转换立场？当然，*这就是*我那100雷亚尔的报酬无法坚持下去的原因……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: Side-shifting? Of course, *that's* why my payment of 40 reál didn't stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “转换立场？当然，*这就是*我那40雷亚尔的报酬无法坚持下去的原因……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: Side-shifting? Of course, *that's* why my payment of 130 reál didn't stick... She shakes her head in mock regret.
Zh: “转换立场？当然，*这就是*我那130雷亚尔的报酬无法坚持下去的原因……”她假装遗憾地摇了摇头。

En: Of course, she curtsies slightly. "You are an honourable man -- *way* above the money I could offer, so I won't even try. And of course..."
Zh: “当然，”她微微行了一个礼。“你是一个正直的人——我无论出*多少*钱也打动不了你，所以我根本都不会去尝试。当然了……”

En: I will. I've *always* wanted a side-shifting class knight on my side, sir, she curtsies slightly. "Now, of course..."
Zh: “我会的。我*一直*都希望我这边能有一位转换立场的阶级骑士，先生，”她微微行了一个礼。“现在，当然……”

En: In all good humour -- that was a donation. It has been registered and it will yield you no favours. He nods. "Another thing --"
Zh: “玩笑归玩笑——那是一笔捐款。它已经登记在册，不会给你带来任何好处。”他点点头。“还有一件事——”

En: I do not *expect* you to share anything Evrart told you with me -- not being a corrupt *würm* myself. She pauses. "However, if you felt like passing *some* information..."
Zh: “我没有*指望*你分享任何艾弗拉特告诉你的事——我自己可不是*腐败的蠕虫。”她停顿了一下。“不过，如果你想要传递*某些*信息的话……”

En: ...how could I stop you? Are we not human? Are we not *curious* to hear another person's take? It's only natural. We would only be... she smiles, "gossiping."
Zh: “……我又能怎么阻止你呢？我们都不是人类吗？我们都不*好奇*听听别人的想法吗？这只是本性而已。我们只不过是……”她笑了。“在八卦而已。”

En: Don't fall for it, it's a ploy to get you talking. The honey-mouthed syllables, the smiling, the conspiratorial jokes, all of it!
Zh: 别上当了，这是一种让你开口的策略。那甜言蜜语的音节，微笑，有阴谋的玩笑，全部都是！

En: Intellectually speaking... it would be quite *interesting* to hear what she has to say about these things...
Zh: 理智地讲……听听她对这些事情有什么看法其实挺*有趣*的……

En: Incredible, she shakes her head. "Simply incredible -- and how is it going? Has this *detecting* produced a gun?"
Zh: “难以置信，”她摇摇头。“简直是不可思议——那事情进展地怎么样了？那这次*调查*找到枪了吗？”

En: I'm not going to flash it around -- so let's just say I'm glad I found it. (Pat your gun.)
Zh: “我不会到处显摆它的——所以这么说吧，我很高兴能找到它。”（拍拍你的枪）

En: No need to flash my gun around. Let's just say I have it now. Thank god. (Pat your gun.)
Zh: “没必要到处显摆我的枪。这么说吧，现在它已经在我手上了。谢天谢地。”（拍拍你的枪）

En: Thank god -- and indeed *Mr. Evrart* as well. In this investigation one may have *use* for a firearm -- you wouldn't want to get caught with your pants down...
Zh: “感谢神明——而且的确还要感谢*艾弗拉特先生*。在这次调查中，有人也许会*用到*枪支——你可不想被逮个正着吧……”

En: Mr. Evrart helped me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生帮我找到枪了。”

En: Oh! Her eyes become large and round. "That's so *helpful* of him..."
Zh: “哦！”她的眼睛瞪得又大又圆。“他还真是*乐于助人*呢……”

En: I have a *bad* feeling about this -- please just say you have it. No loops, darling.
Zh: “我对此有种*不好*的感觉——请告诉我你找到了。不要再循环了，亲爱的。”

En: Stupendous! She nods, more than a bit surprised. "I didn't think he'd pull through."
Zh: “真是惊人！”她点点头，惊讶程度远不止一点点。“我没想过他能克服难关。”

En: Hmm... perhaps he is?
Zh: 嗯……也许他是呢？

En: *Beyond* curious. I will *choose* to interpret that as you turning the alcohol in the strike brew *down* -- for the sake of our professional relationship.
Zh: “*远不止*好奇。当你把罢工特酿里的酒精*减少*的时候——为了我们之间工作方面的关系，我会*选择*去解释它。”

En: Actually, I turned the knob like this. (Turn the heat *down*.)
Zh: “事实上，我是这样转动按钮的。”（把热度调*低*）

En: And because I don't like the idea of them any more drunk than they already are. She nods. "What else?"
Zh: “因为我不喜欢他们比现在喝的更醉。”她点点头。“还有别的吗？”

En: I actually *lied* that I opened the door. I didn't really do it.
Zh: “我说开过那扇门，其实我*撒谎*了。我并没有这么做。”

En: Stupendous. She takes a little bow. "Pulling one on the old würm. Commendably done. And... did this trick  yield anything interesting? I assume it was necessary -- for the case..."
Zh: “真是惊人！”她向你欠身致礼。“给那个老滑头添点堵。干得非常漂亮。而且……那这个小戏法有没有产生什么有趣的结果？我猜这是必要的——为了案子好……”

En: Detective, I advise you to be *very* selective with what information you choose to share. This may have consequences beyond our line-of-sight.
Zh: “警探，我建议你在分享信息的时候，一定要*非常*有选择性。这也许会产生超出我们视线范围之外的后果。”

En: The man whose door I opened planned to write an article into a local paper.
Zh: “被我打开的那扇门背后住着一个男人，他打算给当地报纸写一篇文章。”

En: About what?
Zh: “关于什么的？”

En: The strike being funded by a protection racket -- that has driven local shops out of business.
Zh: “这次罢工是由勒索保护费的组织资助的——这就导致了本地商铺破产。”

En: The strike being funded by a protection racket.
Zh: “这次罢工是由勒索保护费的组织资助的。”

En: Precisely, she smiles. "Don't worry about that article -- regional papers are useless anyway. You probably spared the man his kneecaps. And *I* can be sure that the local shops have a bone to pick with Evrart." She smiles.
Zh: “的确如此，”她笑了。“别为那篇文章担心——反正地区性的报纸也没什么用。那个男人的膝盖骨也许是保住了。而*我*可以肯定本地商铺对艾弗拉特是很不满的。”她笑了。

En: One that has apparently driven local shops out of business.
Zh: “显然就是因为这样导致了本地商铺破产。”

En: That would explain why the Whirling-In-Rags -- where the Union congregates -- is the only bar left in Martinaise. Well that and Frittte, but Frittte has an army of security guards... She thinks.
Zh: “那就解释了为什么褴褛飞旋——工会的聚集地——是马丁内斯*唯一*还在营业的酒吧。至于弗利多，他们有自己的警卫部队……”她想到。

En: That would explain why the Whirling-in-Rags -- where the Union congregates -- is the *only* bar left open in Martinaise. Well that and Frittte because...
Zh: “那就解释了为什么褴褛飞旋——工会的聚集地——是马丁内斯*唯一*还在营业的酒吧。不过那家弗利多，是因为……”

En: Nobody fucks with Frittte, ma'am.
Zh: “没人敢惹弗利多麻烦的，夫人。”

En: Don't worry about that one article, dear -- regional papers are useless anyway. You probably spared the man his kneecaps. And *I* can be sure that the local shops have a bone to pick with Evrart. She smiles.
Zh: “别担心那篇文章，亲爱的——反正地区性的报纸也没什么用。你也许是放过了男人的膝盖骨。而*我*可以肯定本地商铺对艾弗拉特是很不满的。”她笑了。

En: A flash of satisfaction. She can use this, in the future. To turn the tide against Evrart on the streets.
Zh: 一丝满足的情绪。未来，她可以利用这一点。扭转街区对艾弗拉特的态度和形势。

En: This is good. It pleases her.
Zh: 这是好事，让她很开心。

En: I told him about the mercenary tribunal -- he didn't seem too worried.
Zh: “我跟他说过雇佣兵审判的事——他似乎并不担心。”

En: Brinkmanship -- or sabre-rattling... Was he surrounded by *Union men* he wanted to impress?
Zh: “边缘政策——或者武力恫吓……他周围是不是有一群他想要打动的*工人成员*？”

En: I told him a drug trader might be somehow involved in the murder. He denied participating in the trade.
Zh: “我告诉他贩毒的事可能牵扯到凶杀案了，他否认参与贩毒。”

En: Well, of course he would -- that still doesn't mean he's not benefiting from the arrangement -- the lorries, the harbour, all of it. It just means he doesn't outright admit to it.
Zh: “好吧，他当然会了——不过这并不意味着他没有从这种安排中受益——货車，港口，全都是。这只是意味着他不会直接承认。”

En: That is very, very likely yes.
Zh: 很可能，非常有可能就是这样的。

En: Well, of course he would -- I really hope you and I get to talk about this drug trade matter in the future. The company has been suspecting foul play for years. Anyway...
Zh: “好吧，他当然会了——我真心希望未来你和我可以聊聊贩毒这件事。公司已经怀疑这种不正当行为好几年了。总之……”

En: Sounds like she'll solicit your help with something -- when she gets the chance.
Zh: 听起来她会在某件事情上寻求你的帮助——当她找到机会的时候。

En: We were alone. And he seems very sure of his ability to keep things in control.
Zh: “我们没有帮手。而他似乎很相信自己把控一切的能力。”

En: Or he wants you and me to *believe* he's calm and in control. He's a politician.
Zh: “或者他想要你和我*相信*他很冷静，一切尽在掌握。他是个政治家。”

En: Of course. Politics is showmanship. He is constantly performing for us... and lying too. I am sorry I don't catch more of his falsehoods, there are just so *many*...
Zh: 当然。政治是一种表演技巧。他不断地在吾辈面前表演……还有撒谎。很抱歉，吾辈没能抓住他的更多谎言，实在是*太多*了……

En: No. It was more like he wanted things to get *worse* -- in secret of course.
Zh: 不。更像是他想要事情变得*更糟糕*——出于某种秘密的动机。

En: Nowhere. Let's change the topic.
Zh: “没什么地方。我们换个话题。”

En: Of course -- to *other* matters you've discussed perhaps?
Zh: “当然——也许你还想讨论一些*别的*事情？”

En: I'm over-radiated, Harry, she sprouts. "I do silly things sometimes -- out of pale-related illness. Like take this job. The moral of the story is..." she lists:
Zh: “我被过度辐射了，哈里，”她吐露了心声。“有时候我也做些傻事——受跟灰域有关的疾病影响。就像这面船帆。这个故事的寓意是……”她一一列举：

En: I'm over-radiated, detective, she spurts out. "I do silly things sometimes -- out of pale-related sickness. Like take this job. The moral of the story is..."
Zh: “我被过度辐射了，警探，”她吐露了心声。“有时候我也做些傻事——受跟灰域有关的疾病影响。就像这面船帆。这个故事的寓意是……”

En: So it was a factor in her coming to Martinaise in the first place? Interesting.
Zh: 所以这是她一开始来到马丁内斯的一个因素？有意思。

En: Like what?
Zh: “比如说？”

En: Of course, detective, she simmers down. "You can always drop by later, should something come up -- now, what else can I do you for?"
Zh: “当然，警探，”她冷静下来。“之后如果发生什么事的话，你可以随时来找我——现在，我还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: *Now* say it: "Ma'am, this whole thing is a takeover."
Zh: *现在*，说吧：“夫人，这整件事就是一场接管行动。”

En: For once, I don't seem to know what the right thing to do is.
Zh: 仅此一次，我好像不知道什么是应该做的正确的事。

En: Good. They will start asking for more... And more...
Zh: “很好。他们会要求更多……还有更多……”

En: ...is what you're about to say when a cold streak runs through your heart. Once said, there's no going back, Harry.
Zh: ……你正打算这么说，突然一股寒意穿透你的心脏。一旦说出来，就没有回头路了，哈里。

En: Yes. What if something happens to her and you've left *one* question un-asked. Sneaky little question escaped your eye.
Zh: 是的。如果她出了什么事，而你还剩下*一个*没有问的问题。有个狡猾的小问题从你眼皮子底下溜走了。

En: Yes, there is still reality-based knowledge I can glean from her.
Zh: 是的，我还能从她那里学到跟现实有关的知识。

En: Right. She hasn't even told me what she knows about the lynching.
Zh: 没错。她甚至还没告诉我她对私刑了解多少。

En: (No. Say it.) "Ma'am, this is a takeover."
Zh: （不。说吧。）“夫人，这是一次接管行动。”

En: Almost gave you a heart attack, that one...
Zh: 几乎让你快要心脏病发作了，那一个……

En: Yes... come back to this later. This information could be explosive.
Zh: 是的……之后再回到这个话题吧。这条信息可是爆炸性的。

En: Actually... this is not Evrart-related, but I tracked down a suspect.
Zh: “事实上……这跟艾弗拉特没有关系，不过我追踪到一个嫌疑人。”

En: The lieutenant narrows his eyes.
Zh: 警督眯起双眼。

En: I do *not* think that's a good idea.
Zh: 我*不*觉得这是个好主意。

En: She looks positively surprised. "That's great. For the investigation, I mean."
Zh: 她看起来很惊喜。“那太好了。我的意思是，对于调查很好啊。”

En: It went really badly. She killed herself.
Zh: “情况变得非常糟糕。她自杀了。”

En: It didn't go too well. She got away -- *and* it doesn't look like she did it.
Zh: “情况不是很好。她逃跑了——*而且*看起来不像是她做的。”

En: That's really all I can say about that.
Zh: “我真的只能说这么多了。”

En: A *she...* The negotiator does not seem to be shocked at first.
Zh: “一个*她*……”交涉人一开始似乎并没有感到惊讶。

En: Not even a flinch. That must take willpower. Yet below it, some little crack starts running in the foundation.
Zh: 甚至连退缩都没有。那样肯定需要意志力。不过在那下面，基础上已经开始出现一些小裂痕。

En: I hope she wasn't a serious suspect then -- as to the suicide -- that must be *difficult*. Yet, in your line of work... She tilts her head.
Zh: “希望她不是重要嫌疑人——至于自杀——肯定很*困难*。不过，在你的工作岗位上……”她歪起头。

En: Not be too rare an occurrence.
Zh: 不是太罕见的事情。

En: A she... She raises a brow. "I hope she wasn't too serious a suspect. If she got away..."
Zh: “一个她……”她扬起眉头。“希望她不是特别重要的嫌疑人。如果她逃跑的话……”

En: We're not at liberty to discuss that ma'am.
Zh: “我们不方便讨论这个，夫人。”

En: Naturally. I completely understand. But... you mentioned something about Mr. Claire? Or something in conjunction with the Union even? I might have gotten the wrong idea, but...
Zh: “这是自然。我完全理解。不过……你提到关于克莱尔先生的一些事？或者甚至是跟工会有关的事？我可能是想错了，不过……”

En: But -- I won't pry further about the case. You mentioned something about Mr. Claire, I believe? Or something in conjunction with the Union? I might have gotten the wrong idea, but...
Zh: “不过——我不会进一步刺探案子的事了。我相信，你好像提到关于克莱尔先生的一些事？或者甚至是跟工会有关的事？我可能是想错了，不过……”

En: Curiosity for the Union burns brighter in her than curiosity for the case...
Zh: 她对工会的好奇心比她对案子的好奇心更重……

En: She didn't do it. She was framed.
Zh: “不是她做的。她是被陷害的。”

En: It's... not good. I did not do good there.
Zh: “情况……不太好。我表现地不太好。”

En: I don't even know why I told you. This isn't about Evrart...
Zh: “我甚至都不知道为什么告诉你。这跟艾弗拉特也没有关系……”

En: Oh, we humans just like to discuss things -- it's absolutely normal. I won't pry further. If anything regarding the Union has come up... She raises a brow. "They won't talk to *me*."
Zh: “哦，我们人类就是喜欢讨论各种事情——这是非常正常的。我不会进一步刺探了。如果有什么跟工会有关的事……”她扬起眉头。“他们是不会告诉*我*的。”

En: Unfortunate. She shakes her head. "I won't pry further into the details of your investigation, but let me just say -- I have *full* trust in your competence."
Zh: “很不幸。”她摇摇头。“我不会进一步刺探你们的调查细节了，不过我想说的是——我*完全*相信你的能力。”

En: This thing is what you call a... she pronounces the word with sudden carelessness: "*clusterfuck*. I am saddened to hear the news, of course. But we must continue. Lest the situation worsens."
Zh: “这种事情就叫做……”她突然漫不经心地说出了这个词：“*倒血霉*。当然，听到这个消息我很难过。不过我们必须继续。以免局势恶化。”

En: There's turmoil in there now -- but still she keeps it in check.
Zh: 现在那里有些骚动——不过她依然控制住了。

En: And you too, lieutenant, she turns to him and stresses: "The situation is perilous, but I am *sure* you're dedicated to avoiding further loss of life. Unlike the Union, who won't even let me talk to them."
Zh: “你也一样，警督，”她转向金，强调到：“局势非常危险，不过我*肯定*你们一直致力于避免更多的人员伤亡。不像工会，甚至都不让我跟他们谈谈……”

En: A barely perceptible nod.
Zh: 很难觉察到的一个点头动作。

En: Finally -- time to choose SIDES.
Zh: 总算是到了——是时候选择站在哪一边了。

En: Tell her, she'll *like* you for it.
Zh: 告诉她，她会因此*喜欢*你的。

En: Yes! Your disgusting tie agrees completely. Let's *gossip*.
Zh: 是的！你那条恶心的领带完全同意。让我们尽情*八卦*吧。

En: That's wrong! You don't get into loops!
Zh: 这样不对！你不会陷入循环的！

En: Totally slanderous. Maybe you've gotten into *one* tiny loop once.
Zh: 完全是诽谤。也许你曾经陷入过*一次*小循环。

En: The lieutenant will be more lenient toward sharing those, hopefully.
Zh: 警督会对分享信息的行为更宽容一些，希望如此。

En: The company did not 'send mercenaries after the Union'. I told you, in great detail, about the mistakes my side made. And of course I accept them completely.
Zh: “公司没有‘派雇佣兵去追杀工会’。我告诉过你，极其详尽地跟你说了我们这边犯下的错误。当然，我也全盘照收了。”

En: I just hope you don't share Evrart's view on things. After all, as he said: one's *dead* now. The Pines certainly hasn't killed any of theirs.
Zh: “我只是希望你不要跟艾弗拉特抱有相同的观点。毕竟，就像他说的：现在有个人*死了*。野松公司肯定不会杀自己人。”

En: Let's keep it that way -- she means to add, but then doesn't.
Zh: 让我们保持这个现状——她想要加上一句，不过放弃了。

En: Look who's defensive suddenly.
Zh: 看看谁突然变得防备起来了。

En: Hurry up on that probe. The moment you tell me you're finished at the traffic jam, I will *gladly* tell you the company's side of the story.
Zh: “尽快展开调查。等到你告诉我堵車那边的事处理完毕之后，我会很*乐意*告诉你公司这边的情况。”

En: She must really want you to look into the drugs -- otherwise she'd tell you.
Zh: 她一定是真的很想让你调查贩毒的事——否则她会告诉你的。

En: She was *worried* for a moment. That she's overplayed her hand by not sharing info sooner. Then she settles down, curious to hear more.
Zh: 她有那么一刻*担心*。担心没有早点分享信息是不是做的太过火了。然后她又镇定下来，好奇想要听到更多消息。

En: This gave her pause. She's pensive, suddenly. Hard to say about what...
Zh: 这让她停顿了一下。突然间，她陷入深思。很难说是关于什么的……

En: Have I underestimated the Union's ferocity? She *has* to ask herself by now..
Zh: 我是不是低估了工会的残忍？现在她*必须*问问自己了……

En: The prints are *hers*.
Zh: 指纹是*她的*。

En: She's sentimental all right. You don't know enough about her to find out why exactly. She will keep her secrets for now.
Zh: 她的确很多愁善感。你对她的了解不够，不知道到底是因为什么。她会暂时保守自己的秘密。

En: She nods. "It may be the only *break* we've ever caught as a species. The last one for four hundred years."
Zh: 她点点头。“这可能是我们这个物种能捕捉到的唯一*喘息*。四百年来的最后一次。”

En: After life, death. After death, life again. After the isola, the pale. After the pale, isola again.
Zh: 生后的世界，死亡；死后的世界，又是新生。洲之后，灰域；灰域之后，还是洲。

En: She would die to return to it. The pale. The past. Anything one can return to.
Zh: 她宁死也要回到那里：灰域，过去，人的任何归宿。

En: You could use a little of it yourself.
Zh: 你自己也可以来上一点。

En: With worry in his eyes. He does not know if it was the right thing to do. But he doesn't say anything.
Zh: 他的眼里有些忧虑。他不知道这样做到底对不对。但他什么也没说。

En: You wonder what Evrart has to say about this...
Zh: 你想知道艾弗拉特会怎么说……

En: Slowly, the sails turn a grey blue as more oxygen gets between you.
Zh: 渐渐地，你们之间的氧气越来越浓厚，船帆变成了一抹灰蓝。

En: The lieutenant watches her boat grow smaller on the bay, its white sails fluttering.
Zh: 警督看着她的船在海湾里越变越小，白色的船帆随风飘荡。

En: Another woman gone.
Zh: 又一个女人走了。

En: When I said *be wacky* I didn't mean *wildly, grossly irresponsible and damaging to the RCM*.
Zh: 我说*古怪一点*的时候，并不是指*鲁莽，极度不负责，还有损害RCM的名声。*

En: The pale was thought to be impregnable, perpetual. She points northwest. "Irene La Navigateur, the Queen of Suresne, sent *eight* expeditions, one after the other, into the mass at the edge of the world. Five of the crews did not return. Two did, but had lost their minds."
Zh: “灰域曾经被认为是难以改变的，永恒的。”她指向西北方向。“领航者艾琳，苏雷恩的女皇，派出了*8*支探险队伍，一个接着一个，深入世界的边缘。其中5支队伍没有归来。还有2支回来了，但却发了疯。”

En: Until after *years* of trial and error -- and the development of a *strict* psychological regime imitating the creation process of poetry...
Zh: “直到*数年*的反复试错后——开发出一套模仿诗歌创作过程的*严格*心理机制……”

En: The eighth expedition returned, sane and intact. They told of a new continent of matter. They told the queen and her councillor, Dolores Dei, that the pale had begun to condense, day after day, hour after hour, minute after minute.
Zh: “第8支探险队伍归来了，神智健全，完好无损。他们讲述了一个全新的物质大陆。他们告诉女皇和她的顾问，德洛莉丝·黛，灰域开始凝结，日复一日，每时每刻，每分每秒。”

En: The nations who colonized this isola called theirs *Mundi*. The World. It was all they knew, all they thought would be. That there would be something more was a gamble. Akin to another world -- or life after death.
Zh: “殖民这个洲的国家们将它们的故土称为*蒙迪*”“还会有别的什么东西存在的想法更像是一场赌博。就好像相信存在另一个世界——或者是死后的世界一样。”

En: Called Volta do Mar, or Return from the Sea.
Zh: 叫做环海，或者海上归来。

En: But they could not. They were sane and conscious, as islands began to appear on the horizon... There are 78,000 uninhabited islands in the Insulindian archipelago, officer. The freckled face of god, she smiles.
Zh: “但是他们做不到。他们神智健全，意识清醒，那些岛屿开始逐渐出现在地平线上……伊苏林迪群岛有78,000个无人居住的岛屿，警官。就像神明那张布满雀斑的脸，”她笑了。

En: She nods. "The phenomenon has never again been encountered. For a time the crew thought they were experiencing a hallucination. The mast-hand proclaimed 'L´Insulinde! L´Insulinde!' -- the signal to wake up."
Zh: 她点点头。“这种现象再也没有发生。有那么一段时间，队员们以为遇到了幻觉。瞭望员宣告‘群岛！群岛！’——那是清醒的信号。”

En: This ocean?
Zh: “这片大海？”

En: You've thought it a million times.
Zh: 你已经想过一百万次了。

En: On the second day, a great skua was shot down above the flagship Lysergique. The bird was preserved and brought back. Along with pollen. She looks to the sky, then back at you.
Zh: “第二天，一只飞在舰旗麦角之上的大贼鸥被打了下来。那只鸟被保存并带了回来。跟花粉一起。”她望了望天空，然后回望向你。

En: There was a sting in your heart at the mention... before. When she said its name.
Zh: 一提到……从前，你的心就会感到刺痛。当她说出那个名字的时候。

En: Four years later the queen’s councillor was proclaimed Her Innocence Dolores Dei, the elected world spirit. The age of humanism, internationalism, and parliamentary rule followed. We were *high*...
Zh: “四年之后，女皇的顾问被宣告成为无罪女王德洛莉丝·黛，被推选的世界之灵。随之而来的，就是人道主义、国际主义以及议会制度统治的时代。我们都*兴奋*了……”

En: Hmh, she nods with well-contained curiosity.
Zh: “嗯，”她点点头，带着几分适度的好奇心。

En: That money you gave me -- would that make things... *weird*? If I shared information, I mean?
Zh: “你给我的那些钱——会让事情变得……*古怪*吗？我的意思是，如果我跟你分享信息的话？”

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: He asked me to open a door.
Zh: “他让我打开一扇门。”

En: He asked me to deliver an envelope.
Zh: “他让我送一个信封。”

En: Which isn't to suggest our talks constitute *corporate espionage*. Even *if* they did -- it would be fine. But they don't, since you logged the money as a donation and this is *clearly* just gossip between friends.
Zh: “这并非意味着我们的对话构成了*商业间谍*行为。*就算*是这样——其实也没什么。不过并不会，毕竟你会把钱登记为捐款，而且，这*明显*只是朋友之间的闲聊而已。”

En: She's not even asking you anything. It's so easy to just say...
Zh: 她甚至什么都没要求你。很容易说……

En: The lieutenant might have -- but I don't remember you logging anything as anything, Harry.
Zh: 警督也许会——不过我不记得你登记过什么东西，哈里。

En: I helped him turn up the heat on the borscht.
Zh: “我帮他的罗宋汤增加了热度。”

En: Ma'am, this whole thing is a takeover.
Zh: “夫人，这整件事就是个接管计划。”

En: I'd rather talk about something else for now, if you don't mind. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我还是宁愿先聊点别的，如果你不介意的话。”（总结。）

En: Of course, detective, she simmers down. "Should something come up, later down the road -- don't be afraid to drop by for a chat."
Zh: “当然可以，警探，”她冷静下来。“未来如果发生什么事情——不要害怕，随时过来跟我聊聊吧。”

En: Oh! Her eyes become large and round. "That's so *helpful* of him..."
Zh: “哦！”她的眼睛瞪得又大又圆。“他还真是*乐于助人*呢……”

En: The lieutenant looks at you and you can swear his jaw muscle is trembling.
Zh: 警督看着你，你可以发誓他的下颌肌肉在颤抖。

En: Unconventional police officers sometimes *lose* their guns. They then go around and tell people about this -- to gauge their reactions. It's all part of *detecting*.
Zh: “非常规的警官有时会*弄丢*他们的枪。然后就会到处走动，把这个消息告诉别人——以此观察他们的反应。这都是*侦查*的一部分。”

En: So very, very helpful, she repeats. "And how is that going? Do you have any *leads* on that gun?"
Zh: “真是非常，非常乐于助人呢，”她重复到。“那事情进行地怎么样了？有没有关于枪的*线索*？”

En: Mr. Evrart says it's *almost* ready to be found soon.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生说*基本上*很快就会找到。”

En: Check it out! (Pull out your gun.)
Zh: “你看！”（掏出你的枪。）

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Ah yes. As you said. She looks confused for a moment.
Zh: “啊，是的，就像你说的那样。”看起来她似乎困惑了一下。

En: Please don't get him in a loop. If he gets in a loop it will last *forever*. Ask him to say something else please.
Zh: “请不要把消息同步给他。一旦这样就会*永远*持续下去。请让他说点别的吧。”

En: Of course. Thank you for the advice -- I'm glad you were here to assist. She turns to you: "Your *other* dealings with Evrart are still of considerable interest to me..."
Zh: “当然了。感谢你的建议——很高兴能有你在这里协助。”她转向你：“你跟艾弗拉特之间的*其他*交易，我还是相当感兴趣的……”

En: Your gun, yes -- do you want to talk about something else now?
Zh: “你的枪，没错——现在你想聊的别的吗？”

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: He seems to be in some sort of *loop*.
Zh: 他似乎陷入了某种*循环*。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Executive function is completely shot. I keep pushing buttons and nothing is happening.
Zh: 执行功能完全崩溃了。我一直在按按钮，但是什么也没发生。

En: What is happening?
Zh: “出什么事了？”

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Well, *that's* not good. Looks fine over here. You sure it's not some sort of *glandular* problem?
Zh: 呃，*那*可不太妙啊。我这里看起来还好。你确定这不是某种*腺体*问题吗？

En: No, it's not that -- it's... I have no idea. This is bad.
Zh: 不，不是的——这是……我不知道。这下糟糕了。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Sweetheart, please stop repeating that now. Her eyes look worried, frightened even.
Zh: “甜心，请不要再重复这句话了。”她的眼里满是担忧，甚至有些害怕。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Please... She looks around.
Zh: “拜托……”她看了看周围。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Yes, yes, Mr. Evrart... this gossiping idea was terrible on my part, I take it back.
Zh: “是的，是的，艾弗拉特先生……对我来说，这个闲聊的主意实在是太可怕了，我收回。”

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: How do we get out of here?
Zh: 我们怎么才能解决这个问题？

En: What makes you think we *will*?
Zh: 是什么让你觉得我们*会*这样做呢？

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: The woman scans her surroundings as though a neurologist might magically appear.
Zh: 女人环顾四周，好像某个神经学家会像变魔法一样突然出现。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Fear seeps into you as you say it...
Zh: 当你说出这句话的时候，恐惧侵入了你的身体……

En: ...your skin crawls...
Zh: ……沿着你的皮肤缓慢爬行……

En: MR. EVRART IS HELPING HIM FIND HIS GUN.
Zh: 艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Do you want me to take you to a hospital, sir?
Zh: “要不要我送你去医院，先生？

En: Things were going so well before. There's no other option. The process must be interrupted.
Zh: 之前一切都还挺顺利的。没有其他选项了。必须中断这个进程。

En: You think we should... turn him off and back on again?
Zh: 你觉得我们是不是应该……把他关掉，然后再重启？

En: Officer? She waves her hand in front of your eyes.
Zh: “警官？”她在你眼前挥挥手。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: Good God...
Zh: “我的天啦……”

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: She clasps her thermal cup, uncertain.
Zh: 她茫然地握紧了保温杯。

En: She considers taking a sip. She doesn't.
Zh: 她想喝了一口茶水。不过没有这么做。

En: Mr. Evrart is helping me find my gun.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生正在帮我找枪。”

En: That's beautiful... She inspects it.
Zh: “很漂亮……”她端详着它。

En: Keeping her calm, perfect posture. Her eyes move across the muzzle keenly.
Zh: 保持着她那镇定而完美的姿态。她的眼睛敏锐地扫过枪口。

En: A Villiers 9mm, front loaded? Two or three barrelled...
Zh: “维利耶9mm，前膛枪？双管或者三管……”

En: Three. It was not easy to get this gun back, I'll tell you that.
Zh: “三管。我可以告诉你，把它找回来可不容易啊。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Villiers & La Salle was one of the original 14 indotribes of Revachol, did you know? Artillery and ballistics. Before the Revolution of course, they belong to some Gottwalders now.
Zh: “维利耶&拉萨尔是瑞瓦肖原来的14寡头之一，你知道吗？专攻火炮和弹道学。当然了，是在大革命之前，现在他们属于某些戈特伍德人。”

En: Mhm, she nods. "Villiers & La Salle was one of the original 14 indotribes of Revachol, did you know? Artillery and ballistics. Before the Revolution of course, they belong to some Gottwalders now."
Zh: “嗯，”她点点头。“维利耶&拉萨尔是瑞瓦肖原来的14寡头之一，你知道吗？专攻火炮和弹道学。当然了，是在大革命之前，现在它们属于某些戈特伍德人。”

En: She phrases it as if it's a pity. She *also* phrases it as a transition out of you waving your gun around.
Zh: 她说的好像这件事挺遗憾的事。她说得*还像*你挥舞手枪的一个过渡。

En: What else? It helps to sometimes hold inventory of the things you've achieved, you know. A smile. "Clears the soul."
Zh: “还有什么？你知道吗，有时候保存你已经达成的事件清单是很有帮助的。”她笑了。“能洗涤你的灵魂。”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: Well, maybe he's not as *helpful* as you thought then? Is there anything else?
Zh: “呃，那也许他不像你想的那么*乐于助人*？还有别的吗？”

En: Did he now? Her eyes narrow. "Well, then it should be any day now. Unless, of course, he's *lying* to you. Anyway, was there anything else you've heard?"
Zh: “他有吗？”她眯起眼睛。“呃，那从现在开始，任何一天都有可能找到。当然了，除非，他在*骗*你。不管怎么说，还有什么你想知道的吗？”

En: Did you now? She's intrigued, if a little confused. "What sort of borscht is he making?"
Zh: “是吗？”她很好奇，还有一点困惑。“他在做什么样的罗宋汤？”

En: Unimportant.
Zh: “这不重要。”

En: The cook makes it to keep the strikers drunk. Helps them strike.
Zh: “厨房调制的汤能让罢工者保持醉酒状态。帮他们罢工。”

En: You're right detective -- that whole undertaking was *very* unimportant. Why did we do it?
Zh: “你说得对，警探——这整件事都*非常*不重要。为什么我们要这么做呢？”

En: The strike brew, she nods. "That's a classic. And by *turn up the heat* I presume you mean *put more alcohol in it*?"
Zh: “罢工特酿，”她点点头。“很经典。那你说的*增加了热度*，我推测你的意思就是*往里面多加点酒精*咯？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Why, if I may ask? Why make them *more* drunk? Aren't they corked enough already?
Zh: “如果可以的话，我能问为什么吗？为什么要让他们*更*醉一些呢？他们醉的还不够吗？”

En: Yes, detective -- what *were* you hoping to accomplish with this strange thing?
Zh: “是的，警探——你*希望*用这件奇怪的事达成什么目的呢？”

En: An act born of sympathy for the working man. I set fire to the fumes of struggle.
Zh: “我这种行为是出于对工人的同情。我点燃了斗争的火焰。”

En: I worship Al Gul in many ways.
Zh: “我用许多方法来崇拜阿尔古。”

En: I did it out of sympathy for alcoholism. It's a cause dear to me.
Zh: “我这么做是出于对酒精中毒的同情。这是我很珍视的一个事业。”

En: It's uninteresting, I thought it would make the broth taste better.
Zh: “它太乏味，我觉得这样会让肉汤的味道变得更好。”

En: Very curious. She blinks both eyes. "A very curious thing to do."
Zh: “非常奇怪。”她眨巴着双眼。“这是件很奇怪的事。”

En: Truly, but that's how he operates. He just does things, ma'am. And then talks about them -- even if it's inappropriate.
Zh: “的确如此，不过他就是这么做的。这就是他处事的方式，夫人。然后还会谈论起它们——就算这样真的很不合适。”

En: A strange equanimity has overtaken the lieutenant. He's just going with the flow now. Easier that way.
Zh: 警督被一种奇怪的镇定包围了。现在他只打算顺其自然。这样会比较简单。

En: What else?
Zh: “还有别的吗？”

En: A referral, you mean? I take it this was for someone in the RCM... She waves her hand. "Don't answer that."
Zh: “你是说，引荐吗？我想是给RCM的某个人的……”她挥挥手。“别回答了。”

En: Yes, a referral.
Zh: “是的，引荐。”

En: No, I mean like a real door. To someone's house.
Zh: “不，我的意思是一扇真正的门。别人家的门。”

En: My liege, this woman has half-convinced herself already. All it would take is a little nudge...
Zh: 大人，这个女人已经半信半疑了。只需要轻轻推一把……

En: She nods, carefully, as though handling a match near a powder keg. "Such referrals may sometimes get you information from a man like Evrart," she says, raising an eyebrow. "Did it?"
Zh: 她小心地点点头，仿佛正捏着一只炸药桶附近的火柴。“这样的引荐有时候能让你从艾弗拉特那样的人那里得到信息，”她扬起一边的眉毛，说到。“是吗？”

En: Oh my -- a real door?
Zh: “天啦——一扇真正的门吗？”

En: The lieutenant does not say anything, but you hear the nylon of his jacket rustle as he looks at you.
Zh: 警督什么也没说，不过当他看着你的时候，你听见他的尼龙夹克发出沙沙的声响。

En: I helped scare someone for Mr. Claire.
Zh: “我帮克莱尔先生恐吓别人了。”

En: I scared this Gary for Mr. Claire. He got pretty scared.
Zh: “我帮克莱尔先生恐吓盖理。他吓坏了。”

En: It was nothing.
Zh: “这不算什么。”

En: It had to be done -- I got information. It was useful to the case.
Zh: “必须这样做——我也换来了信息。对这起案件很有帮助。”

En: Of course. The RCM has the right to inspect any premises. She nods: "How else would you catch the bad guys? The information was useful I hope?"
Zh: “当然。RCM有权搜查任何房屋。”她点点头。“不然你们怎么抓住坏人呢？我希望你得到的信息能派上用场？”

En: That's good to hear, detective. Police work is rarely pretty, but it sounds like you did what you had to do.
Zh: “听到这个我很高兴，警探。警察工作一般都不太好看，不过听起来你做了自己该做的事。”

En: My-my! What I'm hearing is... She says, slow and teacher-like: "You inspected a premises because you suspected that it was involved in the narcotics trade..."
Zh: “天啦！我听到的是……”她说话的方式很慢，有点像老实：“你搜查了一栋房屋，因为怀疑那里可能跟毒品交易有关……”

En: Her bartering mind cannot fathom that you didn't ask anything in return.
Zh: 她那种物物交换的思想没法理解你不要求任何回报的行为。

En: Frankly, what was done was not pretty at all -- but neither was it illegal. And it was not for nothing. He turns to you...
Zh: “说实在的，那件事做的一点也不漂亮——不过也没有犯法。而且这也不是毫无缘由的。”他转向你……

En: He seemed disappointed when I told him I suspect his men did *not* do it. Like he wanted them to.
Zh: “当我告诉他——我怀疑他的人*没有*做的时候，他似乎有些失望。好像他反而希望是他们做的。”

En: He didn't seem at all worried about the whole conflict that's developing.
Zh: “似乎他一点也不担心正在发展的整个冲突。”

En: The Union's militant wing organized the lynching.
Zh: “工会里的激进分子组织了这次私刑。”

En: Evrart asked the Union's militant wing to fully cooperate with the investigation.
Zh: “艾弗拉特让工会的激进分子必须完全配合调查。”

En: This militant wing has singled out a suspect for me.
Zh: “这些激进分子帮我挑出了一个嫌疑人。”

En: I advise you to be *very* selective with what information you choose to share. This may have consequences beyond our line-of-sight.
Zh: “我建议你在分享信息的时候，一定要*非常*有选择性。这也许会产生超出我们视线范围之外的后果。”

En: This seems serious...
Zh: 好像挺严重的……

En: He means beyond the coming days and weeks -- or the grasp of newspaper and radio reports that may trickle to your desk if ever you return to Precinct 41. In the far future.
Zh: 他的意思是未来几天和几个星期——或者是当你回到41分局的时候，源源不断地流向你办公室的报纸，还有电视台的报道。在遥远的未来。

En: Evrart says the Wild Pines sent mercenaries after the Union -- and now one's dead.
Zh: “艾弗拉特说野松公司派雇佣兵追杀工会——现在有一个人死了。”

En: Misinformation... She shakes her head vigorously. "This is all because we haven't shared information on the lynching yet. See, already the adversary uses it to their advantage. Don't let him."
Zh: “误报……”她用力地摇摇头。“这都是因为我们还没有分享关于私刑的信息。看见没，对手已经在利用这一点了。别让他得逞。”

En: I wouldn't have taken Evrart for a sabre-rattler. Was he surrounded by *Union men* he wanted to impress?
Zh: “我并不会认为艾弗拉特是一个黩武主义者。他周围不都是一群他想要打动的*工会成员*吗？”

En: Yes, she leans slightly closer: "That's the talk about town. The *Hardie boys* they're called..." A crooked smile returns to her face. "I find the name rather amusing, honestly."
Zh: “是的，”她稍微靠近了一些：“城里都是这么传的。他们好像叫做*哈迪兄弟*……”一个不自在的笑容又回到了她的脸上。“老实说，我觉得这个名字真的很好笑。”

En: He wasn't trying to impress anyone. I think he *wants* things to escalate.
Zh: “他没想打动任何人。我觉得他*想要*事件升级。”

En: You're right. He was probably just showing off to me.
Zh: “你说得对。他也许只是想向我炫耀。”

En: Or he wants you and me to *believe* he wants to go to war.
Zh: “或者他想要你和我*相信*他想要战争。”

En: Of course. Brinkmanship takes showmanship. We should have seen it before, in his office. He might well have been performing for us.
Zh: 当然。边缘政策是需要表演技巧的。我们之前应该已经见识过了，在他的办公室。他可能一直在跟我们演戏。

En: There's always that risk, ma'am. But if I may offer my opinion...
Zh: “风险总是存在的，夫人。不过如果可以的话，我想提出我的意见……”

En: He means it.
Zh: “他是认真的。”

En: Don't be. He was not performing. He was already picturing a war. This woman underestimates Evrart.
Zh: 不对。他并不是在演戏。他已经在构想一场战争了。这个女人低估艾弗拉特了。

En: I think he may have just been messing with us.
Zh: “我觉得他也许只是在干扰我们。”

En: Maybe he was manipulating me.
Zh: “也许他是在操纵我。”

En: Kim's right. I'm pretty sure he meant it.
Zh: “金是对的。我很肯定他是认真的。”

En: No, I'm pretty sure I'm reading it correctly.
Zh: “不，我很肯定我阅读的信息没有错。”

En: I'll think it over, detective. Thank you for relaying this information to me.
Zh: “我会仔细考虑的，警探。感谢你把这个消息告诉我。”

En: She tries to hide it, but some *great doubt* is spreading within her. There is a crown slowly cracking above her head.
Zh: 她试着隐藏，不过某些*巨大的怀疑*正在她内心深处蔓延。有一顶王冠正在她头顶慢慢破裂。

En: Ma'am? Is everything alright?
Zh: “夫人？你没事吧？”

En: What's this *great doubt* you're talking about?
Zh: 你说的*巨大的怀疑*是指什么？

En: You can't say. Only that she's hiding the magnitude of it from you.
Zh: 你说不出来。只是觉得她向你隐瞒了事情的严重性。

En: What? Yes, of course. Everything's fine. Perhaps there's more gossip you'd like to share?
Zh: “什么？哦，当然了。我没事。也许你可以多分享一下小道消息？”

En: How benevolent, she thinks for a second. "Hopefully they'll help you sort this whole business out... if they haven't already?"
Zh: “真是太仁慈了，”她想了一会儿。“希望他们能帮你查清整件事情……如果还没有的话？”

En: While we appreciate your assistance, ma'am, I'm afraid we can't discuss the specifics of an ongoing investigation with you.
Zh: “我们很感谢你的协助，夫人。调查正在进行中，恐怕我们不能跟你讨论其中的细节。”

En: We cannot discuss the specifics of an ongoing investigation, ma'am. The lieutenant adjusts his glasses. "Besides, you haven't exactly been forthcoming yourself..."
Zh: “调查正在进行中，我们不能跟你讨论其中的细节，夫人。”警督调了调眼镜。“况且，你自己也没有提供太多信息……”

En: That's all I've got to say.
Zh: “我只能说这么多。”

En: What you've said is quite enough. You have given me a lot to consider -- and may have helped me prevent this conflict from escalating.
Zh: “你说的已经足够了。你给了我很多需要考虑的信息——甚至帮我阻止了一场冲突的升级。”

En: She nods. "That is only fair."
Zh: 她点点头。“这样才公平。”

En: I have reason to believe the lynching was a cover-up.
Zh: “我有理由相信私刑只是一种掩饰。”

En: It looks like you may untie this knot yet! Her eyes smile and a tingle runs down your spine.
Zh: “看起来也许你可以解开这个结！”她笑弯了眼，一阵刺痛感沿着你的脊椎向下窜。

En: There's something in you that really likes the way she's looking at you just now.
Zh: 你心里有某种东西——真的很喜欢她刚才看着你的样子。

En: Oh my... *very* interesting. So the militant wing is protecting one of their own...
Zh: “天啦……*非常*有意思。所以说激进分子在保护某个自己人……”

En: Her mind races to the conclusion that most benefits her interests. That doesn't mean she's wrong, though.
Zh: 她的大脑急不可耐地得出了一个对她最有利的结论。不过，那也并不意味着她错了。

En: I've said all I can.
Zh: “我能说的都说了。”

En: Well, I hope it yields something. Otherwise you'll have opened that door for naught. She smiles. "Anything else?"
Zh: “呃，我希望这样能有所收获。不然你就白白打开了那扇门。”她笑了。“还有别的吗？”

En: Naturally, detective. There are lighter topics to gossip about.
Zh: “这很自然，警探。还有更轻松的话题可以八卦的。”

En: Hear that, hero? Feels good, doesn't it? You should relay confidential information more often.
Zh: 听见了吗，英雄？感觉很好，不是吗？你应该再多传递一些机密信息。

En: Sounds like he has you running errands, detective. A well-established dominance ritual... She thinks. "Where did he have you deliver it?"
Zh: “听起来他在让你跑腿，警探。一种享有盛誉的支配仪式……”她想到。“他让你把信送去哪里？”

En: Here.
Zh: “这里。”

En: To a nameless settlement, down the coast. Nearby.
Zh: “给一个无名的聚居地，就在海岸下面。附近。”

En: Ah yes, I've been meaning to go there... She looks over the bay.
Zh: “啊，是的，我一直想去那儿……”她看向海湾。

En: Here? She looks around. "Oh no... what does that bloated hellbat want with my little cinder block town?"
Zh: “这里？”她看了看周围。“哦，不……那个肥蝙蝠想对我的小煤渣城做什么？”

En: It's clear the village has already grown dear to her... strangely so. Why *did* she come here?
Zh: 显然这个村子对她而言变得越来越珍贵了……有点奇怪。她*为什么*会来这里？

En: No, don't tell me. I don't want to know what he has in store for this place... Probably a statue. It's a statue, right?
Zh: “不，别告诉我。我不想知道他为这个地方准备了什么……可能是一座雕像。是一座雕像，对吧？”

En: A giant statue of him or, better yet, his twin brother. Practically the same thing, but makes him seem less like a psychopath.
Zh: “他的一座巨型雕像，或者，更好的是，他的双胞胎兄弟。实际上是一样的，不过让他看起来没那么像个精神病患者。”

En: With longing... clear and simple longing. Strange. Why does she want to go there?
Zh: 带着渴望……简单明了的渴望。奇怪。她为什么想去那里？

En: He wants to build a youth centre here. For the children of Martinaise.
Zh: “他想在这里建一座青少年活动中心。为了马丁内斯的孩子。”

En: What size boots do you wear, madam?
Zh: “你穿多大的靴子，夫人？”

En: A youth centre with Edgar Claire's statue on top of it! She looks down the jetty, remorsefully.
Zh: “一座顶上有艾弗拉特雕像的青少年活动中心！”她低头看着码头，极其懊恼。

En: Go ahead, help him. Make it so. I have no power to stop him.
Zh: “去吧，帮他。就这么做吧。我没有阻止他的权利。”

En: A youth centre with Edgar Claire's statue on top of it... Her eyes run across the water, remorsefully.
Zh: “一座顶上有艾弗拉特雕像的青少年活动中心……”她的眼睛掠过水面，样子极其懊恼。

En: The humidity crawls under your skin. Your eyes track her gaze: past the net picker... toward the concrete square of the nameless village off the coast of Martinaise.
Zh: 湿气在你的皮肤下面蔓延。你的眼睛追逐着她的目光：经过拾网人……前往马丁内斯海岸那个无名村庄的混凝土广场。

En: The concrete plates cross to meet in an abstract shape, in them: small footprints -- impressions left by rubber boots. Small rubber boots.
Zh: 混凝土板以抽象的形状纵横交错，在那上面：一串小小的脚印——橡胶靴留下的印记。小橡胶靴。

En: Close your eyes.
Zh: 闭上眼睛。

En: Keep them open.
Zh: 睁开眼睛。

En: What? She looks at the soles of her tiny green rubber boots: "Thirty-five, I think. Why? I have mutant's feet. Very small."
Zh: “什么？”她看着脚上那双小绿色橡胶靴的鞋底：“我想，是35码吧。为什么这么问？我的脚基因突变了。非常小。”

En: Yes. I'm sad I will never have the time, detective -- I've always wanted a dilapidating fishing village.
Zh: “是的。很遗憾，我永远也不会有那个时间的，警探——我一直很想要一座破败的渔村。”

En: Numb 12.
She smells the cold air, then withdraws her hand slowly.
Zh: 她嗅了嗅冰冷的空间，然后慢慢收回她的手。

En: You're quite fond of this village, aren't you?
Zh: “你很喜欢这个村子，不是吗？”

En: When you were a teen? *Slumming* it, like you told me before?
Zh: “那是你十几岁的时候吗？就像你之前跟我说的，去*体验贫穷生活*？”

En: You've been to Martinaise before?
Zh: “你之前来过马丁内斯？”

En: Yes, I was *slumming* it with some girl-friends of mine. We had boats and... She smiles. "Don't hold it against me -- my *paramour* certainly did not."
Zh: “是的，我跟一群女友来体验*贫穷生活*。我们有船，而且……”她笑了。“别跟我过不去——小伙子们肯定不会的。”

En: Hah! I think I was in my twenties actually. I'm ashamed to say I wasn't a teen anymore. It must have been my early twenties, because I remember a particularly *vile* disco track.
Zh: “哈！我想其实应该是20多岁。我不好意思说自己不是青少年了。肯定是我20出头的时候，因为我记得一首特别*猥琐*的迪斯科曲子。”

En: I'm over-radiated, Harry, she sprouts. "I do silly things sometimes -- out of pale-related illness. Like sail over here. The moral of the story is..." she lists:
Zh: “我被过度辐射了，哈里，”她吐露了心声。“有时候我也做些傻事——受跟灰域有关的疾病影响。就像这面船帆。这个故事的寓意是……”她一一列举：

En: I'm over-radiated, detective, she spurts out. "I do silly things sometimes -- out of pale-related sickness. Like sail over here. The moral of the story is..."
Zh: “我被过度辐射了，警探，”她吐露了心声。“有时候我也做些傻事——受跟灰域有关的疾病影响。就像这面船帆。这个故事的寓意是……”

En: I'm glad to see your *short-term* memory appears intact. In any case, I wasn't a *teen* anymore, I would have been in my early twenties. I remember a distinctly *vile* disco track...
Zh: “很高兴看到你的*短期*记忆还完好无损。不管怎么说，我已经不再是一个*青少年*了，应该是20出头的时候。我记得一首特别*猥琐*的迪斯科曲子。”

En: Not overly so. It's not like this was the only place we visited -- me and my girlfriends from Ozonne with our shiny boats, like reavers...
Zh: “没那么夸张。又不是说那里是我们唯一去过的地方——我还有我的那群欧佐纳尔女友，乘着我们闪闪发亮的船，像派对动物一样……”

En: Sounds like you miss those times.
Zh: “听起来你很想念那些时光。”

En: Oh no -- she's sentimental all right.
Zh: 哦不——她的确很多愁善感。

En: And something more. Something deeper rooted, like a sickness.
Zh: 还有别的什么。某种更根深蒂固的东西，就像一种疾病。

En: Oh no -- she's sentimental all right. Why would she come here otherwise?
Zh: 哦不——她的确很多愁善感。否则她为什么会来这里？

En: If you say so.
Zh: “如果你这么说的话。”

En: Why did you come here then? To this jetty?
Zh: “那你为什么要来这里？来到这个码头？”

En: She is more defensive about it than usual.
Zh: 她比平常更防备一些。

En: An imperceptible droplet lingers. Sweet like the scent of chewing gum on some letter, long ago...
Zh: 一滴极其细微的液滴依然残留着。甜蜜的有如信件上口香糖的香气，那是很久很久以前的事了……

En: He's probably dead by now -- even his shack is long gone... not that it matters. These buildings are all carbon copies of one another.
Zh: “现在他可能已经死了——甚至他的小屋也早已不再……并不是说这很重要。这些建筑全都只是一个又一个的复制品。”

En: Do not spend 22 days a year in pale transit, don't waste your twenties slumming it with your stupid friends, and don't deliver Evrart Claire's mail. Her bony finger is pointed like an arrow at your chest...
Zh: “不要每年花22天时间在灰域中穿梭，不要在20出头的时候跟愚蠢的朋友一起体验贫穷生活，还有，不要投递艾弗拉特·克莱尔的邮件。”她瘦骨嶙峋的手指头像一支利箭，瞄准你的胸口……”

En: She's pointing it *quite* hard in there. Feels like a knife.
Zh: 她瞄准地*非常*用力。感觉就像一把尖刀。

En: Are you satisfied, detective? What else can you tell me about your *mail delivery quest* for Evrart? Do you think it will *improve* the place?
Zh: “你满意了吗，警探？关于艾弗拉特交给你的*邮递任务*，你还有什么要告诉我的吗？你觉得这样能*改善*这里的环境吗？”

En: Full of ghosts and ancient memories. She smiles. "Has this errand yielded you any... information?"
Zh: “满是幽灵和远古的记忆。”她笑了。“这个偏题的信息有没有给你提供任何……信息呢？”

En: Until then -- is there anything *I* can help *you* with?
Zh: “在那之前——*我*还有什么能帮*你*的吗？”

En: The temperature between her skin and her hydrophobic raincoat rises, warming the Numb 12 perfume she's imbibed herself with. You have no other option but to say...
Zh: 她的皮肤和防水雨衣之间的温度升高了，温暖着她身上的麻木12号香水。你别无选择，只能说……

En: Below her hydrophobic raincoat, in the humidity between her skin and the neoprene. And on the silk scarf on her neck, a perfume. You have no other option but to say its name...
Zh: 在她的防水雨衣下面，在她的皮肤和氯丁橡胶之间的湿气中间。她脖子上的丝巾，散发着一股香水味。你别无选择，只能说出它的名字……

En: Disco isn't vile.
Zh: “迪斯科不猥琐。”

En: Disco *is* vile.
Zh: “迪斯科*的确*很猥琐。”

En: Not as vile as me. She looks at the eternite and cinder block soaking, the overgrown carcasse of some motor carriage...
Zh: “不像我这么猥琐。”她看着浸泡在水中的亘石和煤渣砖，还有汽車里杂草丛生的残躯……

En: Not as vile as me... She looks over the bay, her green eyes shining.
Zh: “不像我这么猥琐……”她看着海湾，绿色的眼睛在闪烁。

En: It is -- but not as vile as me. She looks at the eternite and cinder block soaking, the overgrown carcasse of some motor carriage...
Zh: “它就是——但是没有我这么猥琐……”她看着浸泡在水中的亘石和煤渣砖，还有汽車里杂草丛生的残躯……

En: It is -- but not as vile as me... She looks over the bay, her green eyes shining.
Zh: “它就是——但是没有我这么猥琐……”她看着海湾，绿色的眼睛在闪烁。

En: A takeover?
Zh: “接管计划？”

En: Yes. It's not a strike. You aren't being let in because there's nothing to negotiate. The Union is taking the terminal.
Zh: “是的。这不是一次罢工。不让你进去是因为没什么好谈判的。工会要接管中转站。”

En: If they're *taking* it... She look toward the colourful mountain of crates, like toy blocks rising above Martinaise.
Zh: “如果他们要*接管*它……”她望向那些色彩斑斓、堆得像山一样高的集装箱，就像玩具积木高耸在马丁内斯上方。

En: He's ready for a war. They all are.
Zh: “他已经准备好开战了。他们都准备好了。”

En: They most certainly are not. Her eyes return to you. "Krenel has a thousand men on their payroll. The next batch will be a platoon of twenty men and a gunship."
Zh: “他们肯定没有。”她的目光转回到你身上。“科奈尔的工资单上有几千号人。下一批将会是一个20人的排，还有一台武装直升机。”

En: He has told me so in his own words -- he's taking it. He'll also use the drug trade to finance the harbour.
Zh: “他用自己的方式告诉我的——他要接管它。他会用贩毒为港口提供资金。”

En: Green livery changing into red, blot by blot.
Zh: 绿色的外漆变成了红色，一个接着一个。

En: Like a cancer of the blood, metastasising...
Zh: 就像一种血癌，正在转移……

En: *Taking* it... She looks toward the colourful mountain of crates, like toy blocks rising above Martinaise.
Zh: “*接管*它……”她望向那些色彩斑斓、堆得像山一样高的集装箱，就像玩具积木高耸在马丁内斯上方。

En: Then we're talking about a *war*. Evrart needs to let me into the harbour at once. We need to talk about this.
Zh: “那我们说的就是一场*战争*了。艾弗拉特必须立即放我进入港口。我们需要好好谈谈。”

En: An insane goal. Her eyes return to you. "Krenel has a thousand men on their payroll. The next batch will be a platoon of twenty men and a gunship. You can't simply *trick* them."
Zh: “一个疯狂的目标。”她的目光转回到你身上。“科奈尔的工资单上有几千号人。下一批将会是一个20人的排，还有一台武装直升机。你不能只是简单地*欺骗*他们。”

En: War is their goal. They think they can win before you even know it's started.
Zh: “战争就是他们的目标。他们觉得自己可以获胜，在你甚至还不知道战争已经打响之前。”

En: An insane goal. Her eyes return to you. "Krenel has a thousand men on their payroll. The next batch will be a platoon of twenty men and a gunship, the one after that, a hundred."
Zh: “一个疯狂的目标。”她的目光转回到你身上。“科奈尔的工资单上有几千号人。下一批将会是一个20人的团，还有一台武装直升机，在那之后，是100人。”

En: The war *is* their goal. I told you before -- Evrart has always wanted this to escalate.
Zh: “战争*就是*他们的目标。我之前跟你说过了——艾弗拉特一直都想要局势升级。”

En: No, her eyes return to you. "It's naive of me. He sent you -- this *is* the negotiation now."
Zh: “不，”她的目光转回到你身上。“我太天真了。他派你过来——现在*就是*在谈判。”

En: I can ask him to let you in.
Zh: “我可以让他放你进去。”

En: And if he does not avert his course it will be a war negotiation, detectives.
Zh: “如果他不改变方向的话，那这将会变成一场战争谈判，警探。”

En: And if he does not change course it will be a war negotiation, detective.
Zh: “如果他不改变方向的话，那这将会变成一场战争谈判，警探。”

En: I've seen the Union's forces. They're better organized than these mercenaries. They also have the support of the people of Revachol West.
Zh: “我见过工会的武装力量。他们的组织性要强过这些雇佣兵。他们还有瑞瓦肖西部人民的支持。”

En: It will take more than Krenel to wipe them out. Wild Pines will need to send more and better-equipped men. Make no mistake, ma'am...
Zh: “要把他们除掉的话，光靠科奈尔是不够的。野松公司需要派出更多、装备更精良的人。别搞错了，夫人……”

En: I am sure you have the money. The question is how many years and how many lives are you willing to sacrifice?
Zh: “我肯定你很有钱。问题是愿意牺牲多少年，还有多少条人命。”

En: I have seen them. The Union is well prepared to take on Krenel.
Zh: “我见过他们了。工会已经做好了迎战科奈尔的准备。”

En: It will be a massacre, the Union is massively out-gunned.
Zh: “这将会是一场大屠杀，工会在火力方面差的太多了。”

En: It will take a long time to kill them all. A lot of lives will be lost.
Zh: “杀光所有人需要很长的时间。会有很多人失去性命。”

En: You think Krenel is the only contractor Wild Pines employs? They'll only be the start. More will come. The question is how many years and lives will it take? She falls silent.
Zh: “你以为野松公司只雇佣了科奈尔这一个契约人吗？他们只不过是开始而已。后面还会有更多。问题是需要耗费多少年，需要掠夺多少条性命？”她陷入沉默。

En: What do you suggest I do?
Zh: “你建议我怎么做？”

En: They have the support of Revachol West. They have the position... this will be a prolonged and bloody conflict. The only question is how long -- and how bloody. She falls silent.
Zh: “他们有瑞瓦肖西部的支持。他们有地位……这将会是一场旷日持久的血腥冲突。唯一的问题是多久——还有多血腥。”她陷入沉默。

En: Does it matter? Her mind's made up. It has been for some time. The choice was whether to *tell* her this. And you've already made it.
Zh: 这有关系吗？她已经下定决心了。而且已经有一段时间了。选择就是要不要*告诉*她的。而且，你已经做出选择了。

En: Does it matter? Or has her mind been made up for quite a while now?
Zh: 这有关系吗？或者她早些时候就已经下定决心了？

En: It's not the RCM's job to make these decisions.
Zh: “做这些决定不是RCM的工作。”

En: All that remains is the denouement. Let them have the harbour.
Zh: “唯一剩下的只有结局了。把港口给他们吧。”

En: The workers should have the harbour.
Zh: “工人应该拥有港口。”

En: Cut off the snake's head. Evrart's pushing all this.
Zh: “擒贼先擒王。艾弗拉特是一切的幕后推手。”

En: You've already made up your mind, ma'am. What is it?
Zh: “你已经下定决心了，夫人。是什么呢？？

En: First, will this affect your decision making process?
Zh: “首先，这会影响你的决策过程吗？”

En: Everything affects the decision making process, detective.
Zh: “每一件事都会影响决策过程，警探。”

En: Officer... The look on the lieutenant's face conveys uncertainty. He doesn't even sound angry.
Zh: “警官……”金脸上的表情有些犹豫不决。他甚至一点都不生气。

En: A constrictor knot of contrary motives: greed, fear. There may be a way to affect her through these microscopics...
Zh: 一个相反动机的蟒蛇结：贪婪，恐惧。也许能通过这些微观信息想办法影响她的决定……

En: It's a bluff. Call it. Have them open fire and see how long they last.
Zh: “这就是在虚张声势。那就来吧。让他们开火，看看能坚持多久。”

En: (Look at your wrist.) "It's apocalypse o' clock. Time to commence the Gloaming."
Zh: （看着你的手腕。）“末日的时间到了。是时候着手迎接黄昏。”

En: I wish there was a *disco* option.
Zh: “我希望会有一个比较*迪斯科*的选项。”

En: I'm afraid we won't disco our way out of this one... she says, with considerable regret.
Zh: “恐怕迪斯科没办法帮我们解决这个问题……”她带着相当大的遗憾如是说到。

En: Like the previous leaders of the Union were assassinated? I suspect by Evrart and Edgar themselves... She considers it.
Zh: “就像工会的前任领导被暗杀一样？艾弗拉特和埃德加会怀疑我的……”她在考虑。

En: I admit there's some *poetry* in the idea. And like poetry, it's also useless. There would still be Edgar. They've factored it in. The Union is them and they are Martinaise.
Zh: “我承认这个主意里有一些*诗意*的成分。而且它也像诗歌一样，毫无用场。而且还有埃德加。他们已经考虑过了。工会是他们的，而他们是马丁内斯的。”

En: But it is not a bluff. I assumed so before. That you're *part* of his ploy even, telling me this... to keep assuming would be wishful thinking at this point.
Zh: “但这并不是在虚张声势。我之前就假设过了。甚至告诉我这些信息的你，也是他策略的*一部分*……在此时此刻，让我继续维持这个所谓如意算盘的假设。”

En: They will last as long as the drug trade in Jamrock provides them with support. With all due respect, that will be a long and bloody time.
Zh: “只要加姆洛克的毒品贸易能给他们提供支持，他们就能永远坚持下去。恕我直言，这将会是一段漫长而又血腥的时光。”

En: One day you will realize you can't simply go around saying these things about the citizens we're here to protect.
Zh: “总有一天你会意识到：你不能这样随意地谈论我们所保护的公民。”

En: Of course. Your job is to clean up after them. And it looks like there will be a *lot* of cleaning to be done in the near future.
Zh: “当然。你的工作就是清理他们之后的烂摊子。看来在不久的将来，会出现*很多*清理工作。”

En: Do you think it's within my authority to give it to them? Do you think I can control the board and the shell-shocked mercenaries they've sent?
Zh: “你以为我有权把它交给他们吗？你以为我能控制董事会，还有他们派来的这群患有战斗疲劳症的雇佣兵吗？”

En: There will be no *gloaming*. There will be slow, simmering, hideous civil war. The beginnings of a failed state. No rapture, just ugliness.
Zh: “不会有什么*黄昏*的。即将到来的是漫长的而又可怕的内战。一个失败国家的开始。没有狂喜，只会弄得很难看。”

En: So, what are you going to do?
Zh: “所以，你打算怎么做？”

En: The lieutenant looks at his own watch and sighs. "Maybe so, detective, but that doesn't mean we're not obliged to try and stop it."
Zh: 警督看着自己的手表，叹了口气。“也许是吧，警探，不过这并不意味着我们没有尝试阻止它的义务。”

En: He's right. There will be slow, simmering, hideous civil war. The beginnings of a failed state. No apocalyptic rapture, just ugliness.
Zh: “他说得对。即将到来的是漫长的而又可怕的内战。一个失败国家的开始。没有末日的狂喜，只会弄得很难看。”

En: It is. It is well within her authority. It's been clear for some time that this woman is more than she lets on.
Zh: 是的。这完全就在她的职权范围之内。经过这段时间，有一点也明确：这个女人远不止她表现出来的这幅模样。

En: All that's left is the denouement.
Zh: 现在唯一剩下的只有结局了。

En: Bring in everything you have and wipe them from the face of the earth.
Zh: “动用你们拥有的一切，把他们从地球表面彻底抹杀掉。”

En: Such a thing isn't in my character.
Zh: “我的性格做不出这种事。”

En: It is. It is well within her character.
Zh: 就是的。她就是这种性格。

En: I will take your advice, lieutenant-yefreitor. I will surrender Terminal B to the Union.
Zh: “我会接受你的建议，荣誉警督。我会把B中转站让给工会。”

En: I will take your advice, detective. I will surrender Terminal B to the Union.
Zh: “我会接受你的建议，警探。我会把B中转站让给工会。”

En: I will surrender Terminal B to the Union.
Zh: “我会把B中转站让给工会。”

En: I thought it was not within your means.
Zh: “我以为这不在你的职权范围之内。”

En: Is that within your means?
Zh: “这包含在你的职权范围之内吗？”

En: She puts her hand in the rain. She's silent for a second.
Zh: 她把手伸进雨中，沉默了几秒。

En: It is. It is well within her authority. It's been clear for some time that this woman is more than she lets on.
Zh: 是的。这完全就在她的职权范围之内。经过这段时间，有一点也明确：这个女人远不止她表现出来的这幅模样。

En: Ma'am... This may well unravel property law this side of the river. If that occurs, we may never see the end of this kind of confrontation. The next time there will be two strikes... He looks toward the harbour. "Then four, then a hundred."
Zh: “夫人……这样可能会瓦解河流这一侧的物权法。如果这种事情发生，我们也许永远也看不到这种对垒的终结。下次会发生两场罢工……”他望向港口。“然后是4场，之后是100场。”

En: What happens will happen. She takes the end in her rain-slick hand and starts untying the knot.
Zh: “该发生的总会发生。”她伸出被雨水淋湿的手，抓住绳子的一段，开始解开绳结。

En: And what about the next time?
Zh: 那下一次呢？

En: This won't be the last *strike*. There'll be another, then another, until the rule of law unravels.
Zh: “这不会是最后一次*罢工*的。还会有下一次，又一次，直到法治瓦解。”

En: Look to the water falling, without saying a single thing.
Zh: 看着滴落的雨水，一言不发。

En: Is this... safe though? I mean... Won't they start doing it all the time?
Zh: “不过，这个……安全吗？我的意思是……他们不会一直这样做吗？”

En: What will I do... she says slowly looking around.
Zh: “我会怎么做……”她环顾四周，悠悠地开了口。

En: The wind blows, waves crash in the distance. She crosses her arms and asks: "Did I ever tell you how they discovered this isola? During our... reality low-down?"
Zh: 海风吹起，远处的海浪在翻腾。她抱起双臂，问到：“我有没有告诉过你，他们是怎么发现这个洲的？在我们聊起……现实真相的时候？”

En: The wind blows, waves crash in the distance. She crosses her arms and asks: "Did I ever tell you how they discovered this isola? During our talks?"
Zh: 海风吹起，远处的海浪在翻腾。她抱起双臂，问到：“我有没有告诉过你，他们是怎么发现这个洲的？在我们之前谈话的时候？”

En: Her arms fall to her sides, her spine relaxes.
Zh: 她的双臂垂放在两侧，脊椎放松了下来。

En: It may be the only *break* we've ever caught as a species. The last one for four hundred years.
Zh: “这可能是作为一个物种的我们，唯一的一次*突破*。四百年来的最后一次。”

En: No, ma'am.
Zh: “没有，夫人。”

En: You said you would. One day.
Zh: “你说你会讲的。某一天。”

En: You said I'm uneducated, remember?
Zh: “你说我很无知，还记得吗？”

En: Typically arrogant of me.... Absent-minded as well. Interisolary travel has left holes in my consciousness, I'm afraid.
Zh: “我这是典型的傲慢自大……还很健忘。恐怕，跨洲旅行在我的意识里留下了破洞。”

En: Tell me.
Zh: “告诉我吧。”

En: Do we have the time?
Zh: “我们还有时间吗？”

En: We do for this, lieutenant-yefreitor.
Zh: “我们这样做就是为了这个，荣誉警督。”

En: We do for this, officer.
Zh: “我们这样做就是为了这个，警官。”

En: His arm rises for a second, as if he's about to put it on your shoulder. He doesn't, but the intention was there...
Zh: 他的手臂抬高了几秒，好像打算把手搭在你的肩膀上。他没有这么做，不过意图是有的……

En: We do for this, she nods.
Zh: “我们这样做就是为了这个，”她点点头。

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: It was anything but *normal*. There was no precedent, people thought she was punishing the admirals, or had gone mad, or both...
Zh: “一点也不*正常*。没有先例，大家觉得她是在惩罚海军上将，或者是疯了，或者两者都有……”

En: Each of those expeditions would have been led by an admiral. Sounds like a purge. Like she was purging her political rivals.
Zh: 每一次探险都会由一名海军上将带领。听起来像是清洗运动。就像她是在清除自己的政敌。

En: Eight? Is that a normal number?
Zh: “8支？这个数字正常吗？”

En: Slowly raining down until it formed a vast ocean.
Zh: “慢慢滴落，直到最终汇成一片浩瀚的海洋。”

En: There are rain circles on water all around, humidity crawls up your back like a piano trill.
Zh: 雨水在周围的水面上环绕，湿气就像钢琴的颤音爬上了你的后背。

En: Put your hand in the rain.
Zh: 把手伸进雨中。

En: The droplets feel warm. Like spring rain.
Zh: 雨滴感觉很温暖，就像春雨一样。

En: So does the lieutenant. His mouth is slightly open as he looks to the sky.
Zh: 警督也是。他看向天空，嘴巴微张。

En: The air is cold and scented with petrichor.
Zh: 空气很冷，带着一种下雨时才有的尘土味道。

En: 'After life, death -- after death, life again. After the world, the pale -- after the pale, the world again.'
Zh: “‘生后的世界——死亡；死后的世界——又是新生。世界之后——灰域；灰域之后——又是新世界。’”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: A total shift, she nods. "In human comprehension of reality. On the second day, a great skua was shot down, above the flagship Lysergique. The bird was preserved and brought back. Along with pollen."
Zh: “一次彻底的转变，”她点点头。“人类对现实理解的彻底转变。第二天，一只大贼鸥在飞跃旗舰*麦角号*上空的时候被击落。那只鸟被保存并带了回来。跟花粉一起。”

En: Sounds cruel.
Zh: “听起来很残酷。”

En: Sounds *political*.
Zh: “听起来很*政治*。”

En: She nods. "There was no precedent for such an undertaking. People thought she was punishing the admirals, or had gone mad, or both..."
Zh: 她点点头。“之前没有过这样的先例，大家觉得她是在惩罚海军上将，或者是疯了，或者两者都有……”

En: Leaving us *here*.
Zh: “把我们留在了*这里*。”

En: The great skua was the first bird they saw?
