Zh: “大贼鸥是他们看见的第一只鸟？”

En: And this was the last break we got?
Zh: “这是我们最后的喘息？”

En: It's raining.
Zh: “下雨了。”

En: What will you do? (Proceed.)
Zh: “你会怎么做？”（继续。）

En: On Caillou... She studies your reaction. "The Pebble, the largest of the fertile, uninhabited islands of the northeast Insulindian archipelago. Four centuries and two revolutions later."
Zh: “在卡尤……”她研究着你的反应。“又名鹅卵石，伊苏林迪群岛东北部最丰饶的无人岛。后来经历了四个世纪和两场革命。”

En: The first living, autonomous organism. Proof of reality. It's the symbol of Insulinde, detective, the coat-of-arms of the Suzerain and the wings on the crest of the Commune.
Zh: “第一个有生命的、自主的生物体。现实的证明。它是伊苏林迪的象征，警探，宗主国的纹章，公社顶饰上的翅膀。”

En: I seem to have it in for it. Or it for me. I broke one.
Zh: “我好像总是跟它过不去。或者是它跟我过不去。我弄坏过一只。”

En: In your defence -- it is a nasty creature, who plucks food from the throats of lesser birds. Yet much like Revachol, it is also magnificent. And rare. Imagine the suzerain of seagulls.
Zh: “为了帮你辩解的话——它是一种恶心的生物，会从小鸟的喉咙里抢夺食物。不过它也像瑞瓦肖一样，宏伟而壮丽。而且很珍稀。想象一下海鸥的领主。”

En: The nations of Mundi proceeded to discover five more isolae -- or they discovered us -- all in the rush of the great interisolary reconnection...
Zh: “蒙迪接着又发现了5个洲——或者是它们发现了我们，都是在各洲间重联热潮中发现的……”

En: But these others weren't uninhabited. We had to kill people there, wipe out indigenous populations, gunboat economies. Or they came to do the same for us. Or had done to each other. But here... she spreads her arms.
Zh: “但是其他的并不是无人居住。我们必须杀掉那里的人，彻底消灭土著人口，有炮舰为经济开路。不然他们也会对我们做同样的事情。或者对彼此做同样的事情。不过这里……”她张开双臂。

En: There was no one but the skua. The Liliacea. And the blood beech and the River Esperance. It was the New New World. The Mondials used it to amass the greatest concentration of wealth mankind has ever seen -- Revachol the Suzerain.
Zh: “除了贼鸥什么都没有。百合。血榉木和埃斯佩兰斯河。它是新新世界。蒙迪人用它累积了人类历史上前所未有的巨额财富——瑞瓦肖宗主国。”

En: Revolution, poverty, and the mercurial rise of capitalism, she nods.
Zh: “大革命，贫穷，还有随之而来的资本主义崛起，”她点点头。

En: What happened? (Look around.)
Zh: “发生什么事了？”（环顾四周。）

En: Well done, Suzerain! (Nod.)
Zh: “干得漂亮，宗主国！”（点头。）

En: But what was done exactly? Revolution, the rise and crash of capital, international intervention...
Zh: “但是到底做了什么呢？大革命，资本的兴起和崩溃，国际干预……”

En: It is. She pulls the hood over her head. "Soon it will be spring and everything will blossom."
Zh: “是的。”她拉起兜帽，盖在头上。“春天很快就要来临，一切都会重焕生机。”

En: The gangs will run wild. The lieutenant keeps his hand in the rain. "Jacking motor carriages, ferrying amphetamines through Coal City. Spring is tough in Revachol."
Zh: “黑帮会失去控制的。”警督的手依然放在雨中。“打劫汽車，从煤城运送安非他命。瑞瓦肖会度过一个艰难的春季。”

En: We will see.
Zh: “我们走着瞧吧。”

En: The age of capital has only begun. I will talk to my employers in person. We will amputate and cauterize Martinaise -- if you handle the situation on the ground.
Zh: “资本的时代才刚刚开始。我会私下跟我的雇主聊聊。我们会给马丁内斯*截肢*，再灼烧消毒——如果你能处理好眼下的局势。”

En: *We*? She generally avoids that term with her employers.
Zh: *我们*？提到她的雇主，她通常会避免这种用法。

En: More than she seems.
Zh: 远不止这么简单

En: You're going *now*?
Zh: “你*现在*就要走了吗？”

En: Yes. Mr. Claire has a two-month head start. I can't let it grow any bigger. And I've exhausted all my options from here.
Zh: “是的。克莱尔先生提前抢占了2个月的先机。我不能让它继续发展下去。我在这里已经别无选择了。”

En: Understood. (Let her go.)
Zh: “理解。“（让她走。）

En: There are no employers. She's a member of the board. Probably a partner.
Zh: 没有什么雇主。她是董事会的成员。也许是合伙人。

En: Forget about that -- what was that about the Age of Capital only beginning? You need to talk politics with this lady.
Zh: 忘了吧——资本的时代刚刚开始是什么意思？你需要跟这位女士聊聊政治。

En: You know what this lady needs? Some political advice. Even though she's a high-powered corporate executive and you're an amnesiac degenerate, you're pretty sure she needs to hear your opinion on this.
Zh: 你知道这位女士需要什么吗？一些政治上的建议。尽管她是个位高权重的企业高管，而你只是一个失忆的败类，但你十分肯定她需要听听你的建议。

En: You *are* the Wild Pines. There are no employers.
Zh: “你*就是*野松公司。没有什么雇主。”

En: You *are* the Citizens Militia. There are no superiors. She turns to you, rope in hand.
Zh: 她转向你，手里还握着绳子：“你*就是*公民武装。没有什么上级。”

En: What? Of course I have superiors.
Zh: “什么？我当然有上级啊。”

En: I am. (Nod.)
Zh: “我是的。”（点点头。）

En: That's right, detective. And next time you should confer with them before you go setting events in motion.
Zh: “没错，警探。下次行动之前，你应该先跟他们协商一下。”

En: Despite his words he's not really sure whether to be annoyed with you or not.
Zh: 尽管说了那些话，他并不是很确定是否应该生你的气。

En: Events are already in motion. Whether your actions accelerated or momentarily retarded their progress, even the lieutenant cannot really say.
Zh: 事情已经开始了。你的行为到底是加速或是暂时延缓了它们的进程，就连警督也说不太清。

En: That's right. We're all answerable to someone.
Zh: “没错。我们都要对某人负责。”

En: I answer for my own actions. I don't hide behind some faceless organization.
Zh: “我会为自己的行为负责。我不会隐藏在某个匿名的组织背后。”

En: Even after all this time I still don't really understand who you are or what your angle is.
Zh: “即使过了这么久，我还是没有真正理解你是谁，或者你的立场是什么。”

En: Wait -- that must mean you're *really* rich.
Zh: “等等——那肯定意味着你*真的*很有钱。”

En: I am. She tugs on the rope.
Zh: “是的。”她拉紧绳子。

En: Here I am. I am the face and I'm here to answer -- Rejoyce Leyton.
Zh: “我就在这里。我就是外在的那张脸，我来这里就是为了回答问题——雷乔伊斯·莱顿。”

En: I have not deceived you. I showed you who I was -- Rejoyce Leyton.
Zh: “我没有欺骗你。我很明确地告诉过你我是谁——雷乔伊斯·莱顿。”

En: I have not deceived you. I told you who exactly who I was -- Rejoyce Leyton.
Zh: “我没有欺骗你。我很明确地告诉过你我是谁——雷乔伊斯·莱顿。”

En: How far along are they? The tribunal?
Zh: “他们进行到什么地步了？审判？”

En: Don't forget to shoot the bourgeoisie in the head.
Zh: “别忘了朝资产阶级的脑袋开枪。”

En: Don't forget to purge the world of degenerates.
Zh: “别忘了净化这个堕落的世界。”

En: What further plans do you have for the age of capital, ma'am?
Zh: “你对于资本时代还有什么更进一步的计划吗，夫人？”

En: Use vague rhetoric obsessed with its own legitimacy -- like I do.
Zh: “使用那些沉迷于自身合理性的暧昧言辞——就像我一样。”

En: I will try to do so, Harry. She smiles. "If not for you, then for Revachol."
Zh: “我会试试的，哈里。”她笑了。“如果不是为了你，那就是为了瑞瓦肖。”

En: I will try to do so, detective. If not for you, then for Revachol.
Zh: “我会试试的，警探。如果不是为了你，那就是为了瑞瓦肖。”

En: In short, Harry, we will revoke the pigment standard. Your money will no longer need to be black like chitin and there will be *so* much more of it.
Zh: “简而言之，哈里。我们会废除色素标准。你的钱不再需要像甲壳素一样的黑——而且，还有会*更多*的钱。”

En: In short, detective, we will revoke the pigment standard. Your money will no longer need to be black like chitin -- and there will be *so* much more of it.
Zh: “简而言之，警探。我们会废除色素标准。你的钱不再需要像甲壳素一样的黑——而且，还有会*更多*的钱。”

En: Wow.
Zh: “哇哦。”

En: I *know*. She tugs on the rope.
Zh: “我*知道*的。”她拉紧了绳子。

En: I don't want to do that, Harry.
Zh: “我不想这么做，哈里。”

En: I don't want to do that, detective.
Zh: “我不想这么做，警探。”

En: I do not want to shoot myself in the head. She tugs on the rope.
Zh: “我可不想让自己的脑袋挨枪子。”她拉紧了绳子。

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不呢？”

En: Good.
Zh: “很好。”

En: Very good.
Zh: “非常好。”

En: Harry, I will try keep the best interests of the Moralist International in mind during our talks.
Zh: “哈里，在我们谈话的时候，我会尽量把国际道德伦理委员会的利益记在心里，警探。”

En: I will try keep the interests of the Moralist International in mind during our talks, detective.
Zh: “在我们谈话的时候，我会尽量把国际道德伦理委员会的利益记在心里，警探。”

En: Is it? She tugs on the rope.
Zh: “是吗？”她拉紧了绳子。

En: Keep the peace -- and I will keep my end of the bargain.
Zh: “你负责维持治安——我会信守我的诺言。”

En: One last thing, Lieutenant Du Bois. She starts the engine. "I've given the matter much thought and come to this conclusion: You're not an amnesiac. You're *insane*."
Zh: “最后一件事，杜博阿警督。”她发动了引擎。“我仔细考虑了这件事之后，得出了一个结论：你不是失忆。你是*疯了*。”

En: I know -- because I, too, am insane. I just hide my illness better. And I'm rich.
Zh: “我知道——因为我，跟你一样，也疯了。我只是把自己的病症隐藏地更好一些。而且，我很有钱。”

En: You're over-radiated?
Zh: “你被过度辐射了？”

En: What do you have?
Zh: “你得了什么病？”

En: I'm over-exposed, baby. My travels take me through the pale dozens of times a year. I've got the longing -- and I've got it *bad*. She points to her heart.
Zh: “我过度暴露了，亲爱的。每年我需要来回在灰域中穿梭十几次。我染上了那种渴望——而且我的情况很*糟糕*。”她指向自己的心脏。

En: The same strict psychological regimen the eighth admiral developed when he crossed the pale and discovered this isola -- the *Volta do Mar*. It's used by interisolary travellers and other troubled souls even to this day.
Zh: “第8个海军将军穿越灰域，发现这个洲——*沃尔塔玛*的时候，他开发出了一套同样严格的心理养生法。跨洲旅行者和其他不安的灵魂都在使用这种方法，直至今日。”

En: How do you keep your shit together?
Zh: “你是怎么保持冷静的？”

En: How do you keep it together?
Zh: “你是怎么保持冷静的？”

En: Goodbye, Rejoyce Leyton. (Conclude.)
Zh: “再见，雷乔伊斯·莱顿。”（总结。）

En: Watch out for yourselves. They will strike soon.
Zh: “你们自己小心。他们很快会动手的。”

En: She smiles. "Watch out for yourselves. They will strike soon."
Zh: 她笑了。“你们自己小心。他们很快会动手的。”

En: Goodbye, nether creature of the forbidden swamp. (Conclude.)
Zh: “再见，禁忌沼泽的下界生物。”

En: Watch out for yourself. They will strike soon.
Zh: “你们自己小心。他们很快会动手的。”

En: She smiles. "Watch out for yourself. They will strike soon."
Zh: 她笑了。“你们自己小心。他们很快会动手的。”

En: No, detective -- no one's as insane as you.
Zh: “不，警探——没人像你一样疯狂。”

En: No, detective -- no one's as insane as you.
Zh: “不，警探——没人像你一样疯狂。”

En: Isn't *everybody* a little insane?
Zh: “*每个人*不都有点疯狂吗？”

En: Don't worry madam. I am very sane.
Zh: “别担心，夫人。我非常理智。”

En: How far along is Krenel's *investigation*?
Zh: “科奈尔的*调查*进行多久了？”

En: A confrontation is imminent. They have followed in your footsteps...
Zh: “一场对峙即将降临。他们一直在跟随你们的脚步……”

En: As your investigation reaches a climax, so does theirs. They are your shadow. Arm yourselves. Armour yourselves...
Zh: “当你的调查达到高潮的时候，他们也一样。他们是你的影子。备好武器。穿好盔甲……”

En: Protect their targets. Violence may be unavoidable, but we can limit the casualties.
Zh: “保护他们的目标。暴力也许是不可避免的，不过我们可以限制伤亡人数。”

En: When will they make their move? Where?
Zh: “他们会在什么时候行动？在哪里？”

En: Soon. I do not know precisely. They have cut off all communication, you see. They know I've been feeding you information.
Zh: “很快。我也不知道具体细节。你也看见了，他们切断了所有联系。他们知道我在给你提供信息。”

En: Goodbye, Joyce L. Messier. (Conclude.)
Zh: “再见了，乔伊斯·L·梅西耶。”（总结。）

En: One last thing, nameless detective. She starts the engine. "I've given the matter much thought and come to this conclusion: You're not an amnesiac. You're *insane*."
Zh: “最后一件事，无名警探。”她发动了引擎。“我仔细考虑了这件事之后，得出了一个结论：你不是失忆。你是*疯了*。”

En: '*Après la vie, la mort*,' they summarized it for the queen and Dolores Dei; '*Après la mort, la vie encore! Après le monde, le gris. Après le gris, le monde encore!*'
Zh: *“生后的世界，死亡”*他们为女皇和德洛莉丝·黛概括了一下：“死后的世界——又是新生！世界之后——灰域；灰域之后——又是新世界！*”

En: Your stomach gurgles... something tells you that your chance to track down the reality rhizome has left. Better return to Measurehead, see what he has to say.
Zh: 你的肚子咕咕叫着……某种东西告诉你，追踪现实根茎的机会已经离你而去。最好回到测颅先生那里，看看他有什么要说的。

En: You would prefer something else? Not a youth centre?
Zh: “你比较喜欢别的吗？不要青少年活动中心？”

En: First, there won't *be* a youth centre, whatever he's told you or the residents. It'll be something horrific. Perhaps even *worse* than a statue -- so yes, I do.
Zh: “首先，不会*有*什么青少年活动中，不管他到底是跟你或者其他居民怎么说的。它会是一个很可怕的东西。甚至会比一座雕像更加*糟糕*——所以没错，我是这么想的。”

En: A *fishery*. I've been speaking with Lilienne here. She gave me the idea. The infrastructure is all here, and with my connections...
Zh: “一个*渔场*。我跟莉莉恩聊过了。她给了我灵感。基础设施已经齐全，再加上我的关系……”

En: Sadly, it's just one of the million things I'll never get around to. I just have to accept that I'll never be the rich candy-girl who goes around solving people's problems with money.
Zh: “很可惜，这也是我永远抽不出时间去做的几百万件事情的其中之一。我必须接受一个事实——我永远也不会变成那种拿着钱到处帮人解决问题的富有甜心女孩。”

En: Yeah, we need to pull the plug, there's nothing else to do.
Zh: 是啊，我们需要拔掉插头，别无选择。

En: Are you not afraid it'll kill him?
Zh: 你不怕这样会杀了他吗？

En: *Physically* he should be fine. Or at least no worse than usual...
Zh: *从身体上*看，他会没事的。或者至少不会比平常糟糕……

En: He's able to contain the anger and surprise.
Zh: 他能够控制住愤怒和惊讶。

En: Of all the things to be stuck looping this has got to be one of the most witless.
Zh: 在所有那些陷入困境的事情来说，这也肯定会是最愚蠢的那一件。

En: Okay, I'm gonna do it. Hold on...
Zh: 好吧，我会这么做的。等一下……

En: I disagree. It sounds like a strike brew -- *and* you made them *more* drunk? Aren't they corked enough already?
Zh: “我不同意。听起来像是一种罢工特酿——*而且*你还要让他们变得*更醉*？他们醉的还不够吗？”

En: Weird? Oh no. One of the positive things to come from the Revolution is the *unhindered* exchange of information, you see, even when it comes to *trade secrets*...
Zh: “古怪？当然没有。大革命带来的积极影响中，有一件就是*畅通无阻*的信息交流，你也看见了，即使涉及到*商业机密*也一样……”

En: This is going to be hard to recover from.
Zh: 这一定会很难恢复的。

En: What if he forgets everything *again*?
Zh: 如果他*又*把一切都忘了呢？

En: Lieutenant Du Bois? HARRY! She waves her hand in front of your eyes.
Zh: “杜博阿警督？哈里！”她在你眼前挥了挥手。

En: Harry! Are you alright?
Zh: “哈里！你还好吧？”

En: Detective? Is everything alright in there?
Zh: “警探？你没事吧？”

En: What happened?
Zh: “出什么事了？”

En: Judging from your tone of voice, probably not.
Zh: “从你的预期判断，也许不是。”

En: I'm *great*. Are *you* alright?
Zh: “我*非常好*啊。*你*没事吧？”

En: I'm much better now that you're back. You seemed to be caught in some sort of *loop*.
Zh: “你回来了，我现在感觉好多了。你似乎陷入了某种*循环*。”

En: I'm not sure, exactly, but you seemed to be caught in some sort of *loop*.
Zh: “我也不太肯定，不过你似乎陷入了某种*循环*。”

En: No, not at all, I'm afraid. You seemed to be caught in some sort of *loop*.
Zh: “不，恐怕一点也没有。你似乎陷入了某种*循环*。”

En: Never mind the details, we've wasted enough time as it is. What matters is that you *seem* to have got out of it...
Zh: “不要在意细节了，我们浪费的时间已经够多了。重要的事你*似乎*已经走出来了……”

En: Loop?
Zh: “循环？”

En: That sounds like me.
Zh: “听起来很像我。”

En: I have no idea what you're talking about.
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么。”

En: Now please, ask me something else before it happens *again*. Something that *preferably* doesn't include the words *mister* or *Evrart*.
Zh: “现在，在它*再次*发生之前，问我点别的吧。*最好*是不包含*先生*或者*艾弗拉特*之类的事。”

En: Nope -- you misunderstand. I turned the knob like this. (Turn the heat *down*)
Zh: “不——你误解了。我是这样转动按钮的。”（把热度调*低*）

En: *And* in the process you turned up some information relevant to your investigation. Only from Evrart, surprisingly!
Zh: “*而且*在那个过程当中，你发现了一些跟调查有关的信息。只是从艾弗拉特那里得来的，很让人惊讶！”

En: Yes. Evrart is a showman at heart.
Zh: “是的。艾弗拉特内心深处是一个表演者。”

En: I should be. She nods -- there's that crooked smile again. "In my youth I had a brief dalliance here in Martinaise. He was an older man, with impossibly broad shoulders..."
Zh: “我理应如此。”她点点头——又是那个不自然的笑容。“我年轻的时候，在马丁内斯有过一段短暂的罗曼史。他的年纪比较大，肩膀宽阔的让人难以置信……”

En: We told ourselves we were the worst thing to happen to the coast since the Coalition landed in '08. Imagine! She tosses her head.
Zh: “我们告诉自己，自从联盟军在08年登陆海岸以来，我们将会遭遇这里最大的惨剧。想象一下！”她摇摇头。

En: You're wrong, detective. And next time you should confer with those superiors before you go setting events in motion.
Zh: “你弄错了，警探。下次行动之前，你应该先跟那些上级协商一下。”

En: You misunderstand, detective. We're all answerable to someone.
Zh: “你误解了，警探。我们都要对某人负责。”

En: On the contrary, detective. She tugs on the rope. "It's a real *drag*."
Zh: “正好相反，警探。”她拉紧绳索。“这是个真正的*累赘*。”

En: So you're sad you can't buy the place? (Conclude.)
Zh: “所以你因为不能买下这个地方感到伤心吗？”（总结。）

En: Good. The lieutenant returns. "He has not passed out from it. Perhaps I worried for nothing... Are we still on the reality low-down -- or should we do actual policework now?"
Zh: “很好。”警督回来了。“你没有因此晕过去。也许我的担忧是毫无道理的……我们还在说现实内幕吗——或者现在应该做点实际的警察工作？”

En: Good. The lieutenant returns. "He has not passed out from it. Perhaps I worried for nothing... Are we still on the reality low-down -- or should we do actual policework now?"
Zh: “很好。”警督回来了。“你没有因此晕过去。也许我的担忧是毫无道理的……我们还在说现实内幕吗——或者现在应该做点实际的警察工作？”

En: Well then. Reality *is* a strange place -- getting stranger by the day. Ask away, officer. I'll help however I can.
Zh: “那好吧。现实是个奇怪的地方——每天都在变得更奇怪。问吧，警官。我会尽我所能地帮你。”

En: The *Great* kind.
Zh: “很*伟大*的那种。”

En: As if it's self-explanatory, beyond patriotism. A fact.
Zh: 似乎这是一件不言而喻的事情，超越了爱国主义。是一个事实。

En: What makes Revachol great?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖为什么伟大？”

En: History, detective. They built this city to *resolve* History -- our part in it at least, our centuries.
Zh: “历史，警探。他们建造了这座城市来*决定*历史——至少是我们在历史中那部分，我们的世纪。”

En: Okay. *Who* built this city?
Zh: “好吧。*谁*建造了这座城市？”

En: The nations of the Occident. Or migrant workers from Semenine and Iilmaraa, depending on your creed.
Zh: “奥西登国家。或者是来自塞美尼和伊尔玛的移民工人，这取决于你的信仰。”

En: *When* was Revachol built?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖是什么*时候*建成的？”

En: In the Dolorian century, 380 years ago.
Zh: “在德洛莉丝世纪，380年以前。”

En: We are standing on a fertile, self-sufficient island able to sustain up to 200 million people; in the middle of the Insulindic ocean -- the world's connective tissue... She smiles. "It's where the money is."
Zh: “我们站在一个富饶、自给自足的岛屿，它能够养活2亿多人；在伊苏林迪大洋中央——世界的连接组织……”她笑了。“这里就是金钱所在的地方。”

En: And why will it *resolve* History? (Continue.)
Zh: “为什么它能*决定*历史呢？”（继续。）

En: By that I mean conflicts. Ideological conflicts. The stuff of men.
Zh: “我指的是冲突。意识形态的冲突。人类的东西。”

En: Why here?
Zh: “为什么会在这里？”

En: They say it's where the *terrible* questions of our time will be answered -- the tensions are highest, the faultlines deepest.
Zh: “他们说，对于我们这个时代那些*可怕的*问题，能在这里找到答案——这里的局势是最紧张的，断层也是最深的。”

En: Seriously, I want to know what *you* are.
Zh: “说真的，我想知道*你*是什么。”

En: I want to know what *you* are.
Zh: “我想知道*你*是什么。”

En: What is... (Think of something close to you.)
Zh: 那个……（想起跟你很亲近的东西）

En: RCM can access ICP database.
Zh: RCM可以访问ICP数据库。

En: RCM authority comes from Coalition.
Zh: RCM的权利是联盟授予的。

En: RCM Décomptage known.
Zh: 了解RCM的倒装结构。

En: RCM uses unjacketed ammo.
Zh: RCM使用无壳子弹。

En: RCM Kim debrief done.
Zh: RCM金的汇报完毕。

En: Belongs to cop infraculture!
Zh: 属于警察亚文化！

En: The price of a 1.5 litre bottle of Commodore Red? The first line of the Firefighter's Code? Square root of three? I don't know what you mean by *something close to you*. Ask a normal question.
Zh: 一瓶1.5公升装海军准将红朗姆的价格？消防员手册第一条的内容？3的平方根？我不知道你说的*离你很近的东西*是什么意思。挑个普通的问题吧。

En: It remains a mystery what you mean by this *something close*. This isn't about you. It's about *reality*.
Zh: 你说*离你很近的东西*的意思依然是个谜。这跟你无关。这跟*现实*有关。

En: Six kilometres southwest -- in the Valley of Dogs -- junior officer Chad Tillbrook takes aim at a rabid, black dog licking its wounds in the grass.
Zh: 西南方6公里外的地方——在野狗山谷——初级警员查德·提尔布鲁克瞄准了一只狂暴的黑狗，它正在草丛里舔舐伤口。

En: To his left, his partner Émile Mollins whispers: "You heard what happened to Tequila Sunset? In Martinaise?"
Zh: 左侧，他的搭档埃米尔·莫林小声说到：“你听说龙舌兰日落的事了吗？在马丁内斯？”

En: Yes, he lost his mind, Tillbrook answers, finger on the trigger. "Don't worry, Émile..." He pulls on it slowly. Slowly now... "He'll find it again."
Zh: “是的，他疯了，”提尔布鲁克回答到，他的手指还扣在扳机上。“别担心，埃米尔……”他慢慢扣动手指。慢慢地……“他会清醒过来的。”

En: Are you sure he didn't die of auto-erotic asphyxiation?
Zh: “你确定他不是因为性窒息死去的吗？”

En: You can't get a venereal disease from that, can you?
Zh: “你不会因为那个患上性病的，对吧？”

En: What is... (Reach for something fundamental.)
Zh: 那个……（想想某些基础的东西）

En: Jamais vu thought complete.
Zh: 旧事如新完成。

En: Dolores Dei.
Zh: 德洛莉丝·黛。

En: A strange coldness comes over you, as you look at the world. The waves sway the sloop, slowly. That's all.
Zh: 你看着这个世界，突然被一种奇怪的寒意包围。海浪轻轻摇晃着单桅帆船。仅此而已。

En: It's on the tip of your tongue -- a *doubt* of sorts, already in your head -- but it's not fully formed yet. You have to wait and return to this later, once the *Jamais Vu* thought is complete.
Zh: 它就在你的舌尖——已经存在于你脑海中的某种*怀疑*——只是目前还没完全成形。你必须等待，之后再回来，等到*旧事如新*思维完成。

En: The doubt is fully formed in your head -- the alien feeling persists -- you just can't *word* the question yet. You have to complete the *Jamais Vu* thought first.
Zh: 怀疑在你的脑海完整成形——那种奇异的感觉依然留存着——你还不能把这个问题*表达出来*。你必须先完成*旧事如新*。

En: What is... all of this? The scent, the sound, the air?
Zh: 这一切……是什么？味道，声音，空气？

En: OF REVACHOL.
Zh: 是瑞瓦肖。

En: Indeed. There is a term of endearment they coined for it. In the Dolorian century, when humanity was *high* on this world, discovering more and more of it -- these archipelagos included...
Zh: “的确。他们为此发明了一个爱称。在德洛莉丝世代，那时整个世界正处于人道的*高度*，发现了越来越多的岛屿——这些列岛包括……”

En: The Confederate Republic of Mesque -- the world's largest state by territory -- has fallen into an especially nihilistic strain of nationalism lately.
Zh: 梅斯克联邦共和国——世界上领土面积最大的国家——最近已经陷入了一种特别虚无主义的民族主义。

En: Ma'am. He turns to her again. "This philosophical police detective is very *sensitive* and *impressionable* because of his recent medical episode."
Zh: “夫人，”他再次转向她，“因为最近的那个医疗插曲，这位哲学警探非常*敏感*，而且*很容易被影响*。”

En: Now that we're alone -- what *is* pale?
Zh: “现在只有我们俩——灰域*是*什么？”

En: What is pale?
Zh: “灰域是什么？”

En: Got it. Tell me something else then.
Zh: “明白了。那跟我说点别的吧。”

En: Of course. She seems slightly relieved.
Zh: “当然可以。”她似乎稍微轻松了一些。

En: Achromatic, odourless, featureless. The pale is the enemy of matter and life. It is not *like* any other -- or *any* thing in the world. It is the transition state of being into nothingness.
Zh: “无色，无味，毫无特征。灰域是物质和生命的敌人。它*不像*其他的东西——或者世界上的*任何*东西。它是存在到虚无状态的过渡。”

En: The negation of being.
Zh: 存在的对立面。

En: The negation of...
Zh: “对立面……”

En: That's right -- the negation of being. She tightens her hood around her neck, it's cold outside.
Zh: “没错——存在的对立面。”外面很冷，她拉紧了脖子周围的兜帽。

En: Is it... here? (Look around.)
Zh: “它……在这里？”（环顾四周。）

En: What are its physical qualities?
Zh: “它有什么物理特征？”

En: Is it *the pale* or *pale*?
Zh: “那是*一片灰域*，还是*灰域*？”

En: If we're surrounded by pale, how do you get from isola to isola?
Zh: “如果我们被灰域包围，你又是怎么在一个个洲之间往来的呢？”

En: The pale can damage the mind?
Zh: “灰域会损坏人的精神吗？”

En: What is *entroponetic*?
Zh: “*灰域学说*是什么？”

En: How much pale is there compared to the world?
Zh: “跟世界相比，到底有多少灰域？”

En: Let's return to reality, please. (Conclude.)
Zh: “请让我们回到现实吧。”（总结。）

En: No, detective, we're safe. She points to the sea. "It begins there, 6,000 kilometres to the north, and even more to the south, east, and west. You are in the middle of the isola."
Zh: “不，警探，我们很安全。”她指向大海。“它是从那里开始的，北部6000公里以外的地方，南部、东部和西部甚至更远。你身处一个洲的中央。”

En: As your gaze instinctively turns north, a small black pit opens up in your stomach.
Zh: 你本能地将目光转向北方。感觉自己的胃里打开了一个小黑洞。

En: You hear your pulse rise, the air feels caustic and cold suddenly.
Zh: 你听见脉搏剧烈跳动的声音，空气突然变得刺痛和冰冷起来。

En: And... (point north) ...there?
Zh: “还有……（指向北边）……那边？”

En: Six thousand kilometres from the end of the world?
Zh: “距离世界尽头6000公里？”

En: Yes -- that is enough. Many cities are built *much* closer.
Zh: “是的——那就够了。很多城市建造的要*更近*一些。”

En: An uproar of matter, darling, *rising* into the pale. Rolling. Evaporating even, a great vision. The area of transition between the world and the pale is called *porch collapse*.
Zh: “一场物质的骚动，亲爱的，*升入*灰域。翻滚。甚至会蒸发，这是一种很壮观的景象。世界和灰域之间的过渡区被称为*门廊崩溃区*。”

En: Imagine a grey coronal mist, cold vapour, marked by spores of an opportunistic microorganism -- a mould that's adapted to grow at the edge of the unrest. It's...
Zh: “想象一团灰色的冕，冷蒸汽，特征是含有机会性致病菌的孢子——适合生长在动荡边缘的一种霉菌。它……”

En: It's difficult to describe -- or even measure -- something whose fundamental property is the suspension of properties: physical, epistemological, linguistic...
Zh: “很难描述——或者甚至是测量——某种基本特性是悬停特性之存在的东西：物理学上的，认识论上的，语言学上的……”

En: The further into pale you travel, the steeper the degree of suspension. Right down to the mathematical -- *numbers* stop working. No one has yet passed the number barrier. It may be impossible.
Zh: “越深入灰域，悬停的程度就越陡峭。甚至直到数学——*数字*也停止了作用。还没人能穿越数字的阻碍，但它兴许可能。”

En: There's a slippery article in there...
Zh: 这里面有一个很狡猾的冠词……

En: Pale when it's particular -- this territory of pale -- *the* pale when it's pale in its entirety. Once you've seen it... She tosses her head. "Language balks at the difference."
Zh: “特指的时候是灰域——灰色的领域——作为一个整体的时候，是*一片*灰域。等你见到它的时候……”她摇摇头。“语言回避了这种差异。”

En: Oh, it is... Her lungs deflate, her words sound like a sigh, "...*so* difficult for us."
Zh: “哦，是的……”她的肺部泄了气，话语听起来像是一声叹息，“……对我们来说真是*太*困难了。”

En: A squall of birds, hardware operating in the harbour. Firm, self-evident.
Zh: 鸟儿在尖叫，港口的硬件在运作。牢固，不言而喻。

En: It is possible to force dimensions on the pale -- in modern times we can even compress its latitude, bouncing radio waves from one end to the other. Shortening the path.
Zh: “给灰域强加维度是有可能的——在现代，我们甚至可以压缩它的界限，将无线电波从一端传送到另一端。缩短路径。”

En: But it is still hard for humans to navigate the pale without getting lost. Or having our minds damaged.
Zh: “不过人类还是很难在不迷路的情况下穿越灰域。或者是不让我们的精神受损。”

En: She closes her eyes and breathes out heavily: "... the most *disco* thing you will ever see."
Zh: 她闭上眼睛，重重地呼出一口气：“……会是你所见过的，最*迪斯科*的东西。”

En: Extensively.
Zh: “影响很广泛。”

En: How?
Zh: “怎么会？”

En: Some say the damage stems from extreme sensory deprivation. Others argue that pale somehow *consists* of past information, that's degrading. That it's rarefied past, not rarefied matter.
Zh: “有人说损伤来源于极端的感官剥夺，其他人反驳说灰域是由衰变中的过往之信息*组成*的，他们认为它是稀薄的过去，而不是稀薄的物质。”

En: They call it *the blend-over of the self*. The pale does not only suspend the laws of physics, but also the laws of psychology, maybe History, even... The human mind becomes over-radiated by past.
Zh: “他们把它叫做*自我的融合*。灰域不仅暂停了物理学法则，还有心理学法则，甚至可能还有历史……人的思想变得被过去过度辐射。”

En: Who says and who argues?
Zh: 这是谁说的，又是谁反驳的？

En: Who says and who argues?
Zh: “这是谁说的，又是谁反驳的？”

En: The logical positivists say -- the dialectical materialists argue.
Zh: “逻辑实证主义者说的——辩证唯物主义者反驳的。”

En: What does this over-radiation feel like?
Zh: “这种过度辐射的感觉是怎么样的？”

En: It feels terrible. Absolutely terrible. International standards strictly limit civilian travellers to six days of pale exposure per year...
Zh: “感觉很可怕。绝对的可怕。国际标准严令禁止普通旅客每年在灰域中的暴露时间超过6天……”

En: It's more for her -- way more.
Zh: 对于她来说更多——远远不止。

En: Up to my gills, officer.
Zh: “深入肺腑，警官。”

En: *You're* not a civilian passenger?
Zh: “*你*不是普通旅客吗？”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: No, nameless detective of the Citizens Militia. I am a member of the *Entroponetic Business Class*. I'm cleared and trained for 22 days of pale transit annually.
Zh: “不是，无名的公民武装警探。我是*灰域商务舱*的成员。我能保持清醒，每年都要接受22天的灰域旅行训练。”

En: Perhaps that explains her strange pining after the Revolution? Some degraded early memories...
Zh: 也许这就解释了她为什么会在大革命之后离奇憔悴？某种退化的早期记忆……

En: Are *you* over-radiated?
Zh: “*你*被过度辐射过了吗？”

En: *You're* not a civilian passenger?
Zh: “*你*不是普通旅客吗？”

En: She's looking out toward the sea, what at, it's hard to say.
Zh: 她远眺大海，在看什么，很难言说。

En: She's over-radiated. And then some.
Zh: 她被过度辐射了。而且还远不止此。

En: No, Lieutenant Du Bois. I'm 'Entroponetic Business Class.' I'm cleared -- and trained -- for 22 days of pale transit annually.
Zh: “不是，杜博阿警督。我是‘灰域商务舱’成员。我能保持清醒——而且受过训练——每年22天的灰域旅行训练。”

En: *Entroponetics*, she corrects, "is the scientific study of the pale. Or a recent iteration of it, by way of Graad. The study of the pale reaches back 6,000 years -- the Perikarnassians called it the Western Plain."
Zh: “*灰域学说*，”她纠正到，“是对灰域的科学研究。或者它最近的迭代，由格拉德提出的。针对灰域的研究可以追溯到6,000年前——佩里卡纳西斯人将它称为西部平原。”

En: They had not travelled the entire circumference of the Perikarnassian super-isola. It was not merely in the West -- it was everywhere. Even then; surrounding them...
Zh: 他们还没有完全环绕佩里卡纳西斯超级大洲整整一周。它不仅仅是在西边——它无处不在。即便在那时；都环绕在他们周围……

En: Did they... cross it? The Western Plain?
Zh: “他们……穿越它了吗？西部平原？”

En: There *are* signs of pretermodern crossings. Successful navigation of the pale relies not just on technical know-how, but intensive psychological preparation. Some of these tactics have been known for thousands of years.
Zh: “超现代穿越的迹象是*存在*的。要想成功穿越灰域，依靠的不仅仅是技术知识，还需要强化的精神准备。其中有些策略已经流传了千年已久。”

En: What has entroponetics changed then?
Zh: “那灰域学说改变了什么？”

En: Nothing. We remain powerless before the pale. The only real advance in pale transit is the speed with which an aerostatic craft can pierce it. Less exposure leads to less... *effects* later.
Zh: “什么也没有。在灰域面前，我们还是有心无力。在穿越灰域方面唯一真正的进步，就是静风舰的速度可以将它穿透。这样就能减少暴露时间……之后的*影响*就越小。”

En: Aerostatic craft?
Zh: “静风舰？”

En: Hybrid airships, detective. Conventional rotors or jet engines no longer add velocity after the point of reference for motion is suspended -- once you've crossed from near pale to far pale...
Zh: “混合动力飞艇，警探。在运动的参考点悬停之后，一旦你从附近的灰域穿越到遥远灰域的时候，传统的转子或者喷射引擎就不再增加速度……”

En: In essence, we throw them in and they come out the other end -- if we throw them precisely.
Zh: “大体来说，我们把它们扔进去，然后它们从另一端出来——如果我们扔的足够精确。”

En: If we do not?
Zh: “如果不能呢？”

En: Then they don't.
Zh: “那它们就出不来。”

En: The pale outweighs reality two to one -- there is more pale than there is matter. And the ratio is slipping.
Zh: “灰域跟现实的比重是二比一——灰域要比物质更多。而且这个比例正在下滑。”

En: Slipping how? To our detriment or...
Zh: “怎么下滑？对我们有危害，还是……”

En: What do you think, detective? She looks you in the eye.
Zh: “你怎么看，警探？”她直视你的双眼。

En: It's shrinking. There is more and more of the world.
Zh: “它在萎缩。世界变得越来越大了。”

En: It's growing. There is more and more of the pale.
Zh: “它在生长。灰域变得越来越多了。”

En: Oh no, detective -- it's not. One of the few measurable effects of the pale is that it is expanding at an unknown rate.
Zh: “哦，不，警探——不是的。关于灰域为数不多的可测量效果显示，它正在以一种未知的速率扩张。”

En: Precisely. One of the few measurable effects of the pale is that it is expanding at an unknown rate.
Zh: “没错。关于灰域为数不多的可测量效果显示，它正在以一种未知的速率扩张。”

En: An intuitive conclusion of that development is that one day the pale will cover everything -- but this sort of talk is mostly left to extremists.
Zh: “那种发展的其中一个直观结论就是：灰域会覆盖一切——不过这类言论大多是极端主义者发表的。”

En: It's not extreme at all. I know it's going to happen.
Zh: “这一点也不极端。它知道一定会发生的。”

En: It's not extreme at all. It's going to happen.
Zh: “这一点也不极端。它一定会发生的。”

En: Cover *everything*? That can't be. Where would we go...
Zh: “覆盖*一切*？不可能。那我们能去哪里……”

En: Most people -- and indeed most private and government sector organizations; entire civilizations and religions even -- find handy ways to ignore, or downplay that knowledge.
Zh: “大部分人——以及大多数私人和政府部门组织；整个人类文明，甚至是宗教——都在寻找有效的方式来忽略，或者是低调处理这种知识。”

En: I suggest you do the same.
Zh: “我建议你也应该这么做。”

En: Yes, sweet reality, she stomps her foot. "But before we do, tell me detective..."
Zh: “是的，甜美的现实，”她跺跺脚。“不过开始之前，告诉我，警探……”

En: Is this the first time you're hearing this? Do you really not remember anything?
Zh: “你是第一次听到这些吗？你真的什么都不记得了？”

En: This isn't really an inquiry, is it? You can't remember a single thing -- this is the first time you're hearing about any of this.
Zh: “这并不是一次询问，不是吗？你什么都记不起来——这是你第一次听到所有这些内容。”

En: You're right. This is all new to me.
Zh: “你说得对。这些对我来说都是全新的。”

En: No. Nothing like that.
Zh: “不。不是这样的。”

En: I'd like to discuss something else, if you don't mind.
Zh: “如果你不介意的话，我想讨论点别的。”

En: Of course, how *silly* of me. Another basic term perhaps? She stands attentively.
Zh: “当然了，我真是*太愚蠢*了。也许再说一个基础现实？”她站在原地，精神高度集中。

En: There is no way in *any* reality that she's still buying your *shtick*. Better drop it.
Zh: 不管在*什么样*的现实中，她都不会再相信你的那些*鬼话*。最好还是放弃吧。

En: Of course, pardon my prying -- another basic term perhaps, or... She stands attentively.
Zh: “当然了，请原谅我这么爱打听——也许再说一个基础现实，或者……”她站在原地，精神高度集中。

En: Nothing. Just alcohol fumes and blackout.
Zh: “不记得。只有酒精的烟雾和眩晕。”

En: I sometimes sense vague shadows of a past, and it's not good.
Zh: “有时候我会感觉到一个过去的模糊阴影，而且并不太好。”

En: I'm getting a sense of who I am, but no, I didn't know this.
Zh: “我开始有些了解自己是谁了。但是，不，我不知道这个。”

En: Then tell me -- what do you *think* of the pale?
Zh: “告诉我——你怎么*看待*灰域？”

En: Beyond curious... Tell me -- what do you *think* of the pale?
Zh: “出于好奇……告诉我——你怎么*看待*灰域？”

En: It's terrifying.
Zh: “它很可怕。”

En: You were right. It's disco.
Zh: “你说得对。它很迪斯科。”

En: It will end all life.
Zh: “它会终结所有生命。”

En: Its advance can only be stopped with immediate, total, ruthless Communism.
Zh: “只有即刻的、完全的、无情的康米主义才能阻止它的发展。”

En: A powerful anti-communist force. Perhaps *too* powerful... but still. They have it coming.
Zh: “一种强大的反康米主义力量。也许*过于*强大了……不过至少。他们要遭殃了。”

En: I'm worried. I have to say, it doesn't sound very *liberal* to me.
Zh: “我很担心。不得不说，在我听起来可不是很*自由主义*。”

En: I don't know how you've all survived with it -- it's a huge accomplishment.
Zh: “我不知道你是怎么幸存下来的——这是一项伟大的成就。”

En: Who am I to think *anything* of it?
Zh: “我有什么资格去想*任何事*呢？”

En: Hmhm... Her eyes tense. Crow's feet radiate from them. She observes you: your bloodshot eyes and swollen face.
Zh: “嗯……”她的眼睛紧绷。鱼尾纹蔓延开来。她在观察你：你那双充血的眼睛，还有肿胀的面孔。

En: Your ailing constitution, sweaty forehead, the beads there and your heavy breathing.
Zh: 你那病态的身体，汗津津的额头，那把大胡子，还有沉重的呼吸。

En: You really didn't know. This does *not* spell good for the investigation, detective. If you don't know even this, then... She stops mid-sentence.
Zh: “你真的不知道。这对调查来说*不是*什么好事，警探。如果你连这个都不知道……”话说了一半，她就停住了。

En: I'll handle it. It's under control.
Zh: “我会处理好的。一切尽在掌握。”

En: Yes. I'm in serious trouble here.
Zh: “是的。我这里遇到大麻烦了。”

En: This investigation will be my masterpiece. The one they remember me by. I promise.
Zh: “这次的调查将会成为我的杰作。他们会因此而记住我的。我保证。”

En: It'll be a fucking disaster, I know it. All those people will die.
Zh: “那会是一场操蛋的大灾难，我知道的。那些人都会死。”

En: Your colleague, Lieutenant Kitsuragi, is competent. I checked up on him. And you have me -- I will try to assist you in any way I can.
Zh: “你的同事，曷城警督，是个很能干的人。我已经调查过他了。而且你还有我——我会尽我所能的协助你。”

En: I hope so, I truly do. If I may suggest -- hold on to your colleague Kitsuragi. I ran a check on him and he is very competent. In the meanwhile...
Zh: “希望如此，真心的。如果我可以提个建议的话——坚持跟你的同事曷城一起行动。我调查过他，他是个很能干的人。与此同时……”

En: Some of that assurance is meant for herself, as much as it's meant for you. She must have *a lot* on the line here.
Zh: 有些保证是说给她自己听的，跟说给你听的一样。她一定有*很多*话要说。

En: Some of that assurance is meant for herself, as much as it's meant for you. She must have *a lot* on the line here.
Zh: 有些保证是说给她自己听的，跟说给你听的一样。她一定有*很多*话要说。

En: ...you have me -- I will assist you in any way I can. Even if we have to do it one basic term at a time. She gives you a slight bow.
Zh: “……你还有我——我会尽我所能的协助你。虽然我们一次只能了解一个基本现实。”她向你欠身致礼。

En: Even if we have to do it one basic term at a time. She gives you a slight bow.
Zh: “虽然我们一次只能了解一个基本现实。”她朝你微微点点头。

En: Someone *else* you've met may have been *exposed* as well. The strange grey-haired woman in her lorry.
Zh: 你之前见过*其他*在灰域中*暴露*过的人。货車里那个灰头发的奇怪女人。

En: Do *lorry drivers* pass the pale?
Zh: “*货車司机*会穿越灰域吗？”

En: Yes. Carried in the hulls of airships, she nods. "It's a horrific job. Automation will abolish it, soon."
Zh: “是的。搭乘在飞艇内部，”她点点头。“这是个可怕的工作。自动化会废除掉它的，很快。”

En: An acidic smile on her lips. It's getting worse every year.
Zh: 她的嘴角露出一个酸涩的笑容。一年比一年糟糕了。

En: Gone -- like a skipping stone beneath the surface.
Zh: 消失了——就像表面之下掠过的一块石头。

En: One and all, she closes her eyes. "They say pale is death, but for the Universe."
Zh: “所有人，”她闭上眼睛。“他们说灰域就是死亡，不过是对于宇宙而言的。”

En: Off we go into the wild pale yonder.
Zh: “我们即将进入远方荒芜的灰域。”

En: Stay silent.
Zh: 保持沉默。

En: *Off we go...* you see the hanged man's mouth open.
Zh: *我们出发……*你看见被吊起来的男人嘴巴大张。

En: Gulp.
Zh: 吞咽。

En: It doesn't look like you're *doing* the same. The suggestion was meant in earnest, detective. Live life.
Zh: “看起来你不像是*在做*同样的事。我的建议是很真诚的，警探。好好活着。”

En: Why should we just leave and leave -- and the world get left behind?
Zh: 为什么我们要不断离开——把世界抛在脑后？

En: That we continue to persist at all is a testament to our faith in one another.
Zh: “我们还能继续坚持，本身就是我们对彼此信任的证明。”

En: Ask for Kim to step away while you discuss the pale.
Zh: 在你谈论灰域的时候，让金回避一下。

En: Kim trusts you.
Zh: 金相信你。

En: Kim distrusts you.
Zh: 金不相信你。

En: Good idea. Just ask him. He won't make a scene.
Zh: 好主意。问问他吧。他不会当众大吵大闹的。

En: Yeah, you still aren't over the fear that something bad might happen. He expressly said you've had enough excitement for today.
Zh: 是啊，你还是无法克服害怕某些不好的事会发生的恐惧。他明确说过，你今天受到的刺激已经够多了。

En: ...is what you want to do. But should you? He expressly stated you shouldn't hear about it. What if it renders you mad? Or catatonic? *Or* makes you lose your memory again?
Zh: ……就是你想要做的。但你应该这样吗？金明确说过你不应该了解这件事。如果它会让你发疯呢？或者是精神紧张？*或者*让你再次失去记忆？

En: Yes? She's waiting for another term.
Zh: “你说？”她在等待另一个术语。

En: Kim, can I talk to the ma'am alone for a second?
Zh: “金，我能跟这位夫人单独聊几分钟吗？”

En: Fine. He steps away with his notes.
Zh: “好吧，”他收起笔记本走开了。

En: Fine. He steps away with his notes.
Zh: “好吧，”他收起笔记本走开了。

En: Now -- what is the pale?
Zh: “现在——灰域是什么？”

En: Good. The lieutenant returns. "You have not passed out from it. Perhaps I worried for nothing... Are we still on the reality low-down -- or should we do actual policework now?"
Zh: “很好。”警督回来了。“你没有因此晕过去。也许我的担忧是毫无道理的……我们还在说现实内幕吗——或者现在应该做点实际的警察工作？”

En: Good. The lieutenant returns. "You have not passed out from it. Perhaps I worried for nothing... Are we still on the reality low-down -- or should we do actual policework now?"
Zh: “很好。”警督回来了。“你没有因此晕过去。也许我的担忧是毫无道理的……我们还在说现实内幕吗——或者现在应该做点实际的警察工作？”

En: No, but... please. A *little* context. We're not in such a hurry.
Zh: “不行，不过……请吧。稍微*来点*承前启后吧。我们也没那么急。”

En: We have time for you to tell me why you need a *reality lowdown*.
Zh: 我们有时间可以给你——告诉我你为什么需要*现实真相*。

En: We are where we are. I have no truer answer to give, unfortunately. She watches you closely as you scan the horizon.
Zh: “我们就在我们所在的地方。很可惜，我给不出更确实的答案了。”你的目光扫视着地平线，她密切地关注着你。

En: Hah -- and what *is* this Revolution I keep hearing about?
Zh: “哈——我一直听到的这个大革命*是*什么？”

En: It's quite easy: every hundred years or so our species gets together to decide what's next: who gets shot in the head and who gets the mineral rights -- it's a real *kerfuffle*.
Zh: “很简单：每隔100年左右，我们的物种就会聚在一起，决定接下来会发生的事情：谁的脑袋会挨枪子，谁会拿到矿产权——它是一场真正的*动乱*。”

En: This is not going to happen with the lieutenant present -- unless you can convince him to step aside.
Zh: 警督在场的情况下是不行的——除非你能说服他回避一下。

En: See? What can he do. You're a grown man.
Zh: 看见没？他又能做什么。你是个成年人了。

En: You needn't have worried.
Zh: “你没必要担心的。”

En: That was fucking insane. Kim.
Zh: “真他妈的太疯狂了，金。”

En: Right. Let's get to work lieutenant.
Zh: “没错。我们继续工作吧，警督。”

En: Some more reality lowdown before we go.
Zh: “离开之前，再多了解一下现实的真相。”

En: I am happy to oblige. She awaits your question.
Zh: “我很乐意效劳。”她在等待你的问题。

En: Good luck, detectives. She nods you good-bye.
Zh: “祝好运，警探们。”她点头向你道别。

En: But I did. Anyway... He looks around, a little uncomfortable now.
Zh: “但我还是担心了。总之……”他看了看周围，有些不太自在。

En: Dammit, you're a grown man, the lieutenant thinks. Shouldn't have gotten *protective* here.
Zh: 该死，你是个成年人了，警督想到。不应该*保护欲*过剩的。

En: Yes, well... he thinks. "The rest of us have dealt with it for thousands of years, so..." He looks around, a little uncomfortable now.
Zh: “是的，好吧……”他想到。“我们其他人几千年都应付过来了，所以……”他看了看周围，有些不太自在。

En: You will have to deal with it too, the lieutenant thinks. This is why I didn't want you to know.
Zh: 所以你也要面对它，警督想到。这就是我为什么不想让你知道。

En: What was the RCM again?
Zh: “再说一次什么是RCM？”

En: He's being sarcastic.
Zh: 他这是在讽刺。

En: Yes, lieutenant. Permit me to conclude with this -- *who* you are -- to me -- is the police. The only legitimate law enforcement authority in Revachol.
Zh: “是的，警督。请允许我总结一下——你是*谁*——在我看来——你是个警察。瑞瓦肖唯一合法的执法机构的成员。”

En: So, permit me to conclude with this -- *who* you are -- to me -- is the police. The only legitimate law enforcement authority in Revachol.
Zh: “所以，请允许我总结一下——你是*谁*——在我看来——你是个警察。瑞瓦肖唯一合法的执法机构的成员。”

En: Steel yourself, officer, she says in a reassuring manner. "Your colleague, Lieutenant Kitsuragi -- he is competent. I checked up on him. And you also have me -- I will try to assist you in any way I can."
Zh: “坚强起来，警官。”她以一种鼓舞的口吻说到。“你的同事，曷城警督——他很能干。我已经调查过他了。而且你还有我——我会尽我所能的协助你。”

En: All right. We're in. I know these *all* look good, but begin with the first, okay.
Zh: 好吧。我们开始吧。我知道*一切*看起来都很好，不过先从第一个开始，好吧。

En: All right. You're in. Begin with the first one -- you'll appear *worldly*.
Zh: 好吧。你准备好。从第一个开始——你会表现出*不谙世事*的样子。

En: This has been informative, thank you ma'am. I'm sure my memory-impaired partner has *many* more questions to ask... He turns to you. "...about even more fundamental aspects of reality..."
Zh: “这是很有教育意义的，谢谢你，夫人。我很肯定，我那位记忆受损的搭档还有*很多*问题要问……”他转向你。“……关于现实的一切更为基础的方面……”

En: Might I suggest *not* asking them all right now? Ma'am Messier will be here later too. And tomorrow. Isn't that true, ma'am?
Zh: “我能不能建议*不要*现在全问出来？梅西耶夫人晚上也会在这里。明天也是。没错吧，夫人？”

En: Absolutely -- my commitment here is long term.
Zh: “当然——我的承诺是长期有效的。”

En: But won't I be *lazy* if I don't do it all now?
Zh: “不过，如果我不一口气问完的话，不会太*懒散*了吗？”

En: You're right lieutenant. I'll continue later.
Zh: “你说得对，警督。我稍后再继续。”

En: But I want to know everything now.
Zh: “但是我现在就想知道一切。”

En: Of course not, He shakes his head. "You're *already* diligent for getting this far -- and diligent boys remember where they left off."
Zh: “当然不会，”他摇摇头。“你*已经*很努力地走到这一步了——勤勉的小伙子会记得他们之前中断在什么地方。”

En: He nods. "It's better not to eat all your candy at once."
Zh: 他点点头。“最好不要一次性把糖果全部吃完。”

En: That is understandable -- but *perhaps* it's better not to eat all your candy at once?
Zh: “这是无可厚非的——不过*也许*不要一口气把糖果全部吃完？”

En: Indeed. I am always at your service.
Zh: “的确。我随时可以为你效劳。”

En: Everyone wants to talk about themselves. She will, in time.
Zh: 每个人都想谈论自己的事。她也会，迟早的事。

En: Vast, lukewarm and unknowable. Flowing in and out of sight.
Zh: 广阔，冷淡，不可知。在你的视线里来来去去。

En: This is one thought you *need* to complete. Where are you?
Zh: 这是你*必须*完成的一种思维。你在哪？

En: Yes, and perform auto-erotic asphyxiation on yourself with your funny neck tie. Out in the open somewhere!
Zh: 是的，用你那条可笑的领带玩点性窒息。就在某个露天的地方！

En: We always do.
Zh: “我们总是这样。”

En: And if those authorities drink so hard they need help recalling the basic terms of reality -- well, I am here to help. She bows and smiles.
Zh: “而且，如果那些官方人士喝得太凶，需要别人帮忙回想起基本现实的时候——我乐意效劳。”她鞠了一躬，笑了。

En: ...and if the law officials of our nation need help with the basic terms of reality -- well, then I am here to help. She bows.
Zh: “……而且，如果我们国家的执法人员需要基本现实方面的帮助——那我乐意效劳。”她鞠了一躬。

En: Dark orange flames reflect in her green eyes. An oil fire on the ocean.
Zh: 深橙色的火焰倒映在她绿色的眼睛里。那是海洋上的石油火焰。

En: Wonderful.
Zh: “太棒了。”

En: ORG: Occident-Revachol-Graad.
Zh: ORG：奥西登-瑞瓦肖-格拉德。

En: ORG: Occident-Revachol-Graad.
Zh: ORG：奥西登-瑞瓦肖-格拉德。

En: You should ask the Paledriver about this. See what she says.
Zh: 你应该问问灰域驾驶员。看看她有什么说法。

En: That poor woman must have stories to tell like you wouldn't imagine...
Zh: 那个可怜女人要告诉你的故事，肯定是你想象不到的……

En: The doubt is fully formed in your head -- the alien feeling persists -- yet something's still missing. You have finished mulling over the *Jamais Vu* thought, but your Conceptualization skills are not nuanced enough to make sense of it...
Zh: 怀疑在你的脑海完整成形——那种奇异的感觉依然留存着——不过还是缺了点什么。你已经仔细考虑过*旧事如新*的思维，但是你的概念化技能还不够细致，无法理解它……

En: Actually, yes. The thought did cross my mind when Mademoiselle Lilienne earlier told me about the fresh produce these women sell to restaurants on the Delta.
Zh: “事实上，有的。当那边的莉莉恩小姐告诉我这些女人把新鲜农产品卖给德尔塔的餐馆之后，我的脑子里确实出现过这种想法。”

En: This... *reality*? She pulls her hood closer around her neck.
Zh: “这个……*现实*？”她拉紧了脖子周围的兜帽。

En: Yes, reality is your side-case.
Zh: 是的，现实是你的支线任务。

En: An *experimental* side-case.
Zh: 一个*实验性*的支线任务。

En: The aim is to gain a deeper understanding.
Zh: 目标是获得更深入的理解。

En: I am conducting a personal investigation into the world I find myself in.
Zh: “我正在对现在身处的这个世界进行一个私人调查。”

En: By learning about the world I hope to learn more about myself.
Zh: “通过了解这个世界，希望我能更了解自己一些。”

En: I got so drunk I forgot literally everything.
Zh: “我喝得太凶，把一切都忘光了，毫不夸张。”

En: My theory is that this inquiry into the nature of reality will ultimately converge with our murder investigation. They are really one and the same.
Zh: “我有一个理论：对现实本质的调查最终能与我们的凶杀案调查接轨。它们其实同属于一个案子。”

En: I hope my inquiry into the nature of reality will ultimately converge with our murder investigation. They are two halves of the same case.
Zh: “我希望能通过对现实本质的调查，最终能与我们的凶杀案调查接轨。它们就像是同一个案子的两个部分。”

En: How *truly* curious -- a sort of philosopher-detective!
Zh: “还*真是*求知欲爆棚呢——就像某种哲学警探！”

En: Aren't all...
Zh: 难道不都是这样吗……

En: Aren't all philosophers detectives?
Zh: “哲学家不都是警探吗？”

En: And aren't all detectives philosophers?
Zh: “而且警探不都是哲学家吗？”

En: *"What?"* She chortles, nearly spilling her tea.
Zh: *“什么？”*她咯咯笑了，差点把茶水喷了出来。

En: I went on a three-day bender and now need you to explain the world to me.
Zh: “我饮酒作乐了整整三天，现在我需要你来帮我解释这个世界。”

En: I woke up in a trashed hotel room, superstar-style. Before that everything's a blank.
Zh: “我从一个乱七八糟的旅社房间里醒来，特别有超级明星那种范儿。在那之前，一切都是空白。”

En: A supra-natural event took place. (Point to your head.) "Within my head -- on alcohol."
Zh: “发生了一起超自然事件。”（指着你的脑袋。）“在我的脑袋里——因为酗酒。”

En: This person is not easily disoriented, but you've done it.
Zh: 这人不容易感到迷惑，不过你做到了。

En: Why not? She tosses her head. "I've heard the analogy made by a blindingly modern school of philosophy called *karperie*. It may not be as far fetched as it sounds."
Zh: “为什么不呢？”她点了点头。“我听说一个特别现代化的哲学学校——名叫*卡佩里*——做过这种类比。可能并没有听起来的那么吸引人。”

En: Yes, *all* of them.
Zh: “是的，*全部*都是。”

En: Except the ones who are philosophers.
Zh: “除了那些哲学家。”

En: Actually, she turns to the lieutenant, "I've heard the analogy made by a blindingly modern school of philosophy called *karperie*. It may not be as far fetched as it sounds."
Zh: “事实上，”她转向警督，“我听说一个特别现代化的哲学学校——名叫*卡佩里*——做过这种类比。可能并没有听起来的那么吸引人。”

En: Except the ones who are detectives.
Zh: “除了那些警探。”

En: Naturally, he nods.
Zh: “理所当然，”他点点头。

En: There may be *some* detectives who are not philosophers.
Zh: “可能有*一些*警探不是哲学家。”

En: A deep synthesis, she nods. "I'll assist you however I can then. Go ahead. Ask."
Zh: “一种深度的综合，”她点点头。“我会尽我所能协助你的。来吧。问吧。”

En: No time to explain -- just give me the low-down.
Zh: “没时间解释了——赶紧把内幕告诉我。”

En: It's related to that *medical episode* -- I have trouble remembering even the most basic terms of reality.
Zh: “这跟那个*医学插曲*有关——我甚至连最基础的现实都记不起来了。”

En: Please don't make me say it again.
Zh: “请别让我再说一次。”

En: Ah, yes -- the *episode*. Sounds like an acute case of encephalopathy now that I think of it... She puts down her thermal cup and looks at you.
Zh: “啊，是的——*插曲*。现在我这么一想，很像是一起急性脑病的案例……”她放下保温杯，看着你。

En: She's trying to see whether you're bluffing -- it's easy not to fidget. You're *not*.
Zh: 她尝试判断你是否在蒙骗她——不烦不燥是很容易的。你*没有*。

En: Don't be fazed, madam. He functions perfectly well. He only needs a... *lowdown* on all of reality.
Zh: “不要焦虑，夫人。他的功能还是很完美的。他只需要一点……关于所有现实的*内幕*。”

En: But then, I'm no doctor. She takes a long sip of tea. "Ask away, officer -- I'll help however I can."
Zh: “不过，我也不是医生。”她喝了一大口茶水。“问吧，警官——我会尽我所能地帮你。”

En: Trying to gauge by your expression and demeanour whether you're joking. She concludes you're not.
Zh: 尝试通过你的表情和行为来判断你是不是在开玩笑。她的总结是没有。

En: Such an acute encephalopathy... just doesn't seem possible. She puts her beverage down and stares at you. "Surely you're joking..."
Zh: “这么急性的脑病……似乎是不太可能的。”她放下饮品，凝视着你。“你一定是在开玩笑吧……”

En: Don't be fazed, madam. He functions perfectly well. He only needs a... *lowdown* on all of reality.
Zh: “不要焦虑，夫人。他的功能还是很完美的。他只需要一点……关于所有现实的*内幕*。”

En: We may be here awhile, then. She takes a long sip of tea. "Ask away, officer -- I'll help however I can."
Zh: “那么，我们也许要在这里待一阵子了。”她喝了一大口茶水。“问吧，警官——我会尽我所能地帮你。”

En: Actually.... that's not why I need you to give me the low-down.
Zh: “其实……这不是我需要你的内幕消息的原因。”

En: Oh? You've really piqued her curiosity now. "Then why?"
Zh: “哦？”现在你真的激起她的好奇心了。“那是为什么？”

En: A wink. There is a mischievous streak to him egging you on. This might go beyond mere strategy.
Zh: 一个眼神。他怂恿你的行为有种恶作剧的倾向。这可能不仅仅是战略问题了。

En: Yes. The lieutenant takes a step closer to you. "Why don't you tell Mrs. Messier what you told me, hmh?"
Zh: “是的，”警督向你走近一步。“为什么不把你跟我说的那些话告诉梅西耶女士呢？”

En: Yes! The lieutenant takes a step closer to you, rubbing his hands together. "There is a *stranger* reason, isn't there?"
Zh: “是的！”警督向你走近一步，摩擦着双手。“有一个*陌生*的理由，不是吗？”

En: A sort of... spiritual quest? Something of ancient Iilmaraan origin? There's more than a trace of irony in her voice.
Zh: “或者……精神追求？是不是起源于古伊尔玛的东西？”她的声音里不止只有一丝讽刺的痕迹。

En: Yes, a spirit quest sounds like the thing I'm on.
Zh: “是的，精神追求听起来像是我正在做的事。”

En: None of that foreign wisdom. I only seek deep, ancient PATRIOTIC truths.
Zh: “没有那些什么异国智慧。我只是在探寻深入的、古老的爱国真理。”

En: Forget the past, my quest concerns the coming apocalypse.
Zh: “忘掉过去，我的追求跟即将到来的末日有关。”

En: Actually... that is not why I need a low-down.
Zh: “其实……这不是我需要内幕消息的原因。”

En: She nods, attentively.
Zh: 她聚精会神地点点头。

En: Yes! The lieutenant takes a step closer to you, rubbing his hands together. "There is a *stranger* reason, isn't there?"
Zh: “是的！”警督向你走近一步，摩擦着双手。“有一个*陌生*的理由，不是吗？”

En: Yes. The lieutenant takes a step closer to you. "Why don't you tell Mrs. Messier what you told me, hm?"
Zh: “是的，”警督向你走近一步。“为什么不把你跟我说的那些话告诉梅西耶女士呢？”

En: He gives you a slight nudge on your side, apparently enjoying himself.
Zh: 他轻轻推了你一下，显然很享受。

En: You will find that *most* things concern the coming apocalypse. It's part of his *condition*.
Zh: “你会发现*大部分*事情都跟即将到来的末日有关。这是他当前*状况*的一部分。”

En: I've never heard of an *apocalyptic* medical condition, but then reality is a strange place. Ask away, officer -- I'll help however I can.
Zh: “我从没听过跟*末日*有关的身体状况，不过现实是个奇怪的地方。问吧，警官——我会尽我所能地帮你。”

En: I've never heard of a *nationalistic* medical condition, but then reality is a strange place. Ask away, officer -- I'll help however I can.
Zh: “我从没听过*民族主义的*身体状况，不过现实是个奇怪的地方。问吧，警官——我会尽我所能地帮你。”

En: You will find he doesn't approve of foreign wisdom. It's part of his *condition*.
Zh: “你会发现他不赞成异国智慧。这是他当前*状况*的一部分。”

En: You will find him a big fan of the *ancient ways* -- it's part of his *condition*.
Zh: “你会发现他是*古老方式*的忠实粉丝。这是他当前*状况*的一部分。”

En: Nothing wrong with ancient ways, she says. "Our *modern* ones aren't necessarily better. Ask away, officer -- I'll help however I can."
Zh: “古老方式并没有错，”她说到。“我们*现代*的方式也不一定更好。问吧，警官——我会尽我所能地帮你。”

En: Actually -- now is not a good time for a reality low-down. Maybe later.
Zh: “其实——现在不是了解现实内幕的好时机。也许之后再说吧。”

En: Where are we?
Zh: “我们在哪？”

En: Tell me what times are these again?
Zh: “再跟我说说现在是什么时候？”

En: What times are these?
Zh: “现在是什么时候？”

En: What is this *acute encephalopathy*?
Zh: “*急性脑病*是什么？”

En: What am *I*?
Zh: “*我*是什么？”

En: Seriously now... who *are* you?
Zh: “说真的……你*是*谁？”

En: What are *you*?
Zh: “*你*是什么？”

En: Tell me about this world again?
Zh: “再跟我说说这个世界？”

En: What *world* is this?
Zh: “这个*世界*是什么？”

En: What is this? (Spread your hands.)
Zh: “这是什么？”（摊开你的手。）

En: I want to know what the pale is.
Zh: “我想知道灰域是什么。”

En: You said *pale*. What is pale?
Zh: “你提到了*灰域*。灰域是什么？”

En: That's all for now. (Conclude for now.)
Zh: “暂时先这些。”（暂时结束。）

En: A bird? She tilts her head. "A *sphenicid* -- a flightless bird of the polar regions?"
Zh: “一只鸟？”她歪起脑袋。“一只*企鹅*——极地地区一种不会飞的鸟类？”

En: Keep guessing.
Zh: “继续猜。”

En: I meant what is this *place* here?
Zh: “我是说现在这个*地方*是哪里？”

En: Some sort of... krill hunter? No, wait... Joyce raises her eyebrows. "You're an ancient ruin, a symbol of hubris and decay, half-submerged in some salty sea."
Zh: “某种……磷虾猎人？不对，等等……”乔伊斯扬起眉毛。“你是远古的废墟，是傲慢和衰败的象征，半浸在咸咸的海水里。”

En: What -- really?
Zh: “什么——真的吗？”

En: Ah! She spreads her arms almost as wide. "*This* is the pier of Rue de Saint-Ghislaine 33A, where the tenants have been kind enough to rent me a slot..."
Zh: “啊！”她大大地张开双臂。“*这里*是圣吉莱纳路33A的码头，这里的居民很友好，租给我一个船位……”

En: Of course you're not, my dear. I'm just terrible at guessing games.
Zh: “你当然不是了，亲爱的。我只是很不擅长猜谜游戏。”

En: Let me ask you something else.
Zh: “让我问你点别的。”

En: Of course, she tosses her permanent-waved hair. "Ask me anything."
Zh: “当然可以，”她甩了甩波浪卷的头发。“问什么都行。”

En: What is Rue de Saint-Ghislaine 33A?
Zh: “圣吉莱纳路33A是什么？”

En: ...or two. She looks around.
Zh: “……或者是两个。”她看了看周围。

En: A pre-revolutionary tenement. Old buildings are called *tenements*, you see, and new buildings *batiments*, after *les batiments noveau*. But 33A and 33B are not *noveau*, they're old. She looks up at the crumbling facade...
Zh: “一栋大革命前的房屋。你看，旧建筑叫做*房屋*，新建筑叫*大楼*。不过33A和33B并不*新*，它们很旧。”她抬头看着那破败的外观……

En: This one used to be eight to ten stories tall -- a real high rise by the standards of the last century. Built to mirror the skyscrapers across the bay in the Delta. That was before the war, of course.
Zh: “这一栋之前有8-10层楼高——按照上世纪的标准，是一栋真正的高层建筑。建起来是为了跟海湾对岸德尔塔的摩天大楼遥相呼应的。当然，那是战前的事情了。”

En: Who lived in them?
Zh: “谁住在里面？”

En: Mostly the urban middle class, I believe. This was once *primo* real estate. Before the cannons lopped four or five stories off...
Zh: “我相信，大部分都是城市的资产阶级。这里曾经是*优质*房地产。在大炮削掉了4层或者5层楼之前……”

En: So, we're on the periphery? Basically nobodies?
Zh: “所以，我们在边缘了？基本上没人？”

En: Are we near the ocean?
Zh: “我们靠近大海吗？”

En: What kind of city is Revachol?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖是一个什么样的城市？”

En: We're in Martinaise, baby.
Zh: “我们在马丁内斯，亲爱的。”

En: Mhm. And what is *Martinaise*?
Zh: “嗯。那*马丁内斯*是什么？”

En: Yes. We are on an island in an ocean. The world's largest body of water -- the Insulindic.
Zh: “是的。我们在一座海中小岛。世界上最大面积的水域——伊苏林迪。”

En: Martinaise is a district of Revachol. She looks around, her green raincoat flapping in the wind. "A very small district tucked away near the Industrial Harbour. North of the 8/81 and Jamrock."
Zh: “马丁内斯是瑞瓦肖的一个城区。”她看了看周围，绿色雨衣在风中飞舞。“一个藏匿于工业港口附近的小小片区，坐落在8/81和加姆洛克以北。”

En: You would be excused for not knowing about it. Unimportant, they say. Forgotten even -- shelled to smithereens during the Revolution. She shrugs. "It has its charms, just not this time of year."
Zh: “不知道这个地方是可以被原谅的。就像他们说的，这地方并不重要，甚至已经被遗忘——在革命中被炮火轰成了碎片。”她耸耸肩。“它有自己的魅力，只是现在已经不是它的时代了。”

En: Oh, we're quite a way off. About... She points across the water, where the skyscrapers rise...
Zh: “哦，我们还差得远呢。关于……”她指向对岸，摩天大楼耸立的地方……

En: So, we're in an unimportant part of an important place?
Zh: “所以，我们在一个重要地方的不重要地区？”

En: It's not really a sea. It's the Bay of Revachol. And the bay feeds into the ocean.
Zh: “其实并不算是真正的大海。这里是瑞瓦肖海湾。海湾本身是汇入大海的。”

En: So, we're pretty much at the centre of the world?
Zh: “所以，我们基本上是处于整个世界的中心了？”

En: Okay, that's Martinaise. What's *Revachol*? (Continue.)
Zh: “好吧，那是马丁内斯。那*瑞瓦肖*是什么？”（继续。）

En: Revachol -- Revachol is what you call a *city*.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖——瑞瓦肖就是我们说的一座*城市*。”

En: Tell me more about Martinaise.
Zh: “跟我多说说马丁内斯。”

En: I think it's fair to say so. Martinaise is about... She points across the water, where the skyscrapers rise...
Zh: “我觉得这么说很公平。马丁内斯……”她指向对岸，摩天大楼耸立的地方……

En: I'm not a good ambassador. I've only been here once before, as a teenager. Not a lot has changed... She closes her eyes. "There are ruins, a terminal, fishing boats, reeds. Boys with boxy shoulders."
Zh: “我不是个好大使。我只在十几岁的时候来过这里一次，这儿和那时的改变不大……”她闭上双眼。“那里有废墟，中转站，渔船，芦苇丛。还有肩膀四四方方的男孩。”

En: You mentioned a sea. What sea is this?
Zh: “你提到了大海。这是什么海？”

En: *Baby?*
A casual term of endearment popular among the 50+ crowd. It's a disco holdover, pay it no heed.
Zh: “50多岁人群中很流行的一种非正式亲昵用语。这是迪斯科的后遗症，不要太在意。”

En: What's the name of this island?
Zh: “这座岛叫什么名字？”

En: Caillou, as you already know. She looks to the waters. "Imagine a pebble, a smoothed over pebble amidst a great blue sea. Mis-shapen, cracked. The cracks are the River Esperance. We're in the delta of this river, on the sixth branch -- the Martinaise distributary."
Zh: “你应该已经知道了，卡尤。”她看着水面。“想想一个鹅卵石，一个平滑的鹅卵石，矗立于一片蓝色大海的中央。残缺，破碎。那些裂纹就是埃斯佩兰斯河。我们就在这条河的三角洲，第六支流——马丁内斯支流。”

En: Caillou. She looks to the waters. "Imagine a pebble, a smoothed over pebble amidst a great blue sea. Mis-shapen, cracked. The cracks are the River Esperance. We're in the delta of this river, on the sixth branch -- the Martinaise distributary."
Zh: “卡尤。”她看着水面。“想想一个鹅卵石，一个平滑的鹅卵石，矗立一片蓝色大海的中央。残缺，破碎。那些裂纹就是埃斯佩兰斯河。我们就在这条河的三角洲，第六支流——马丁内斯支流。”

En: It is clear this *pebble* is of enormous value to her...
Zh: 显然这个*鹅卵石*对她来说有着重大的价值……

En: We may be here awhile, then. She takes a long sip to clear her throat. "Ask away, officer -- I'll help however I can."
Zh: “那么，我们也许要在这里待一阵子了。”她喝了一大口茶水，润润嗓子。“问吧，警官——我会尽我所能地帮你。”

En: No. You're not joking at all, are you? She sighs. "Well, ask away, officer -- I'll help however I can."
Zh: “不。你根本不是在开玩笑，对吧？”她叹了口气。“好吧，问吧，警官——我会尽我所能地帮你。”

En: I'm a disco holdover myself.
Zh: “我本人就有迪斯科后遗症。”

En: Ha! She smiles. "Aren't we all?"
Zh: “哈！”她笑了。“我们不都是吗？”

En: She refers to your corresponding ages.
Zh: 她指的是跟你相当的年纪。

En: She opens them: "As I told you: Martinaise used to be a province, a workers' resort before the city swallowed it and the artillery did its part. Now the reeds are the real star of the show here. The further down the coast, the wilder it gets."
Zh: 她打开了话匣子：“这里曾经是一个大行政区，一个工人的度假场，直到瑞瓦肖城将它吞噬，大炮将它摧毁。现在芦苇丛才是这幕历史剧的主角，沿着海岸线走得越远，景象就越为荒凉。”

En: She opens them: "This place used to be a province, a workers' resort before the city swallowed it and the artillery did its work. The reeds are the real star of the show here now. The further down the coast, the wilder it gets."
Zh: 她打开了话匣子：“这里曾经是一个省，一个工人的度假胜地，直到这座城市将它吞没，大炮将它摧毁。现在芦苇丛才是这场演出真正的明星。沿着海岸线走得越远，就越荒凉。”

En: A brief spike of longing in her upright body. The dots under her eyelids shake.
Zh: 她笔直的身体中生出一阵短暂而激烈的渴望，她眼睑下瞳孔的小点在摇晃。

En: You were here as a teenager?
Zh: “你十几岁的时候来过这里？”

En: Yes, I was *slumming* it with some girlfriends of mine. We had boats and... She smiles. "Don't hold it against me -- the boys certainly did not."
Zh: “是的，我跟一群女友来体验*贫穷生活*。我们有船，而且……”她笑了。“别跟我过不去——小伙子们肯定不会的。”

En: There is a spark in the air, a live wire hanging in the wind -- in your hung over neocortex. Then the sea air cools it down.
Zh: 空中有一个火花，一条通电电线在风中飞舞——在你那宿醉未醒的大脑皮层里。然后，海风让它冷静了下来。

En: Known to the early Revacholians as *les Immensités Bleues* -- The Blue Immensities.
Zh: 就是早期的瑞瓦肖人熟知的*无垠之蓝*——无边无际的蓝色。

En: Tell me about another, perhaps even *more* fundamental aspect of reality.
Zh: “再跟我说点别的，也许来点现实*更加*基础的方面。”

En: I would be happy to. What is a preposterously expensive education for if not sharing.
Zh: “我很乐意。如果不分享的话，这种贵得离谱的教育还有什么意义。”

En: You're a smart woman.
Zh: “你是个很聪明的女人。”

En: Perhaps. Her earrings chime as she nods. "My intellectual vanity will be my undoing."
Zh: “也许吧。”她点点头，耳环也随之发出一阵声响。“我对知识的虚荣心，将会成为我的祸根。”

En: Ah, yes -- back to the *low-down*.
Zh: “啊，是的——说回*内幕*。”

En: You're no dummy yourself.
Zh: 你自己也不傻啊。

En: So am I.
Zh: “我也是。”

En: No -- or intellectual vanity will be *my* undoing!
Zh: 不——或者知识的虚荣心将会成为*我*的祸根！

En: And the crown on your head as you lay in the casket.
Zh: 还有你躺进棺材时头上戴的那顶皇冠。

En: A smart boy getting smarter, one basic term of reality at a time...
Zh: “聪明的男孩会变得更聪明，一次只说一个基本的现实……”

En: ...and to Humanity at large.
Zh: ……以及整个人类。

En: Speak for yourself, officer. I'm well known in certain parts. As to the place, you're right. We are... She points across the water, where the skyscrapers rise...
Zh: “这是你自己的想法，警官。我在某些部分是很出名的。至于这个地方，你说得对。我们的确……”她指向对岸，摩天大楼耸立的地方……

En: What is that? (Point to Cindy the SKULL.)
Zh: “那是什么？”（指向骷髅头辛迪。）

En: The girl in the old-lady rags?
Zh: “穿着老太太一样破旧衣服的那个女孩？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: No, no, I meant something basic about reality.
Zh: “不不，我的意思是一些基本现实。”

En: Ah, yes. There's something very satisfying about discussing the fundamental facts of reality. Go ahead.
Zh: “啊，是的。讨论基本现实能让人产生一种满足感。说吧。”

En: Looks like a sullen and rebellious member of a teen *infraculture*.
Zh: “看起来像是一个沉闷又反叛的青少年*亚文化*团体成员。”

En: Infra... culture?
Zh: “亚文化？”

En: Yes -- you and I belong to the supraculture. We're common, the herd. The music on the radio, the food in the chain restaurant -- those are all too *popular* for the girl in the old-lady rags.
Zh: “是的——你和我属于主流文化。我们很普罗大众。收音机里的音乐，连锁餐厅的食物——对于那些穿着老太太破烂衣服的女孩来说，有些太*流行*了。”

En: She prefers a fantasy world -- an *infraculture* with its own dress code and vernacular. It is an illusion, I'm afraid. There is no refuge from the supraculture.
Zh: “她更喜欢一个虚幻的世界——拥有特殊着装要求和方言的一种*亚文化*。恐怕它是一种幻觉。没有人能逃避主流文化的。”

En: Suddenly you're not so sure *you're* part of the supraculture...
Zh: 突然间，你不确定*自己*到底是不是主流文化的一员……

En: Okay, now explain the same thing -- but to a child.
Zh: “好吧，现在重新解释一遍——不过要按照给小孩解释的方式。”

En: I understand everything. Make it more complicated somehow.
Zh: “我都能理解。不知怎么的，好像变得更复杂了。”

En: Young people who dye their hair funny colours and wear old people's clothes are stupid and their little rebellion is self-defeating...
Zh: “那些把头发染成奇怪颜色，穿着老年人衣服的年轻人比较愚蠢，他们的那点小叛逆不过是弄巧成拙罢了……”

En: I can't. That's how simple it is. One may dye their hair green and wear their grandma's coat all they want. Capital has the ability to subsume all critiques into itself. Even those who would *critique* capital end up *reinforcing* it instead...
Zh: “我不能。就是这么简单。有人也许可以把头发染成绿色，就想穿着奶奶辈的衣服。资本拥有将所有批评纳入其中的能力。就连那些*批判*资本的人，最后反而让它*强化*了……”

En: I think *I* may be part of an infraculture.
Zh: “我想*我*可能是亚文化的一份子。”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: Of course -- no time for silly infracultures. There are more pressing, reality-centric questions to be asked.
Zh: 当然——没时间搭理什么愚蠢的亚文化了。还有更紧迫、跟现实更密切相关的问题需要问。

En: Her eyes go round with surprise. "And what would *that* be?"
Zh: 她惊讶地睁大双眼。“*那*会是什么？”

En: Cop.
Zh: “警察。”

En: Disco.
Zh: “迪斯科。”

En: She lets out an unrestrained laugh, the crows feet stretching onto her cheeks...
Zh: 她放声大笑，连鱼尾纹都延伸到脸颊上了……

En: My reality-impaired colleague is not so far off... The lieutenant wanders back into the conversation -- he's been sorting his notes.
Zh: “我这位现实障碍的同事并不是很离经叛道……”警督又回到了对话中——他一直在整理自己的笔记。

En: Many of the RCM's fashions, even weapon preferences, borrow heavily from classic Vespertine cop shows. My precinct alone has three officers who go by the name *Ace*...
Zh: “很多RCM的流行服饰，甚至是武器偏好，都严重借鉴了经典的维斯珀警察秀。单说我们一个分局，就有三个叫*埃斯*的警官……”

En: Ha! I can see that, yes. I dabbled in those dark arts myself -- not so long ago. She smiles enigmatically. "I assure you, it was a thoroughly *supracultural* phenomenon. All-permeating. Downright mandatory."
Zh: “哈！我能看出来，没错。我自己也涉猎过那种黑暗艺术——就在不久之前。”她露出一个神秘兮兮的微笑。“我向你保证，那是一种彻头彻尾的*主流文化*现象。全面渗透。完完全全的强制性。”

En: Nothing *infra* about disco, baby.
Zh: “迪斯科一点也不*亚文化*，亲爱的。”

En: But please -- do continue this reality-exploration. I was just remarking.
Zh: “不过——请继续这次的现实探索吧。我只是随口说说而已。”

En: Yes, let's... She settles down -- still a quivering smile on her face.
Zh: “是的，我们继续……”她冷静下来——脸上仍然挂了一丝颤抖的微笑。

En: You're disco?
Zh: “你喜欢迪斯科？”

En: We got this covered -- about another basic reality term.
Zh: “我们学到了——另一个关于基本现实的术语。”

En: Those days are long behind me... She looks inland with sarcastic wistfulness. "And even in the early Thirties I would say I was more of a *New* girl. Disco was a minor, but still enjoyable facet of the whole thing for me."
Zh: “那些日子早已经离我远去了……”她看向内陆，带着一丝苦涩的哀伤。“甚至在30年代初期，我会说自己是个更*新时代*的姑娘。迪斯科并不是最重要的，不过对我来说仍然有愉悦的一面。”

En: Be my guest. She takes a sip from her thermal cup.
Zh: “别客气。”她从保温杯里喝了一口茶水。

En: The *New*?
The New, a cultural era -- and the name of the decade it ravished, the Thirties. It came out of post-revolutionary Revachol. It was... ultraliberal.
Zh: “新时代，一个文化时代——它沉迷的那个十年的名字，叫三十年代。它起源于大革命之后的瑞瓦肖。它是……极端自由主义的。”

En: It involved *lots* of partying, as you might imagine. And champagne coloured *everything*. By the looks of it -- that would have been when you came of age. Now... She raises her eyebrow at you.
Zh: “你可以想象得到，它包含了*大量*的派对。而且*一切*都是香槟色的。从外表看来——应该就是你刚成年的时候。现在……”她看着你，扬起了眉头。

En: Sounds fun. I can't remember a single thing.
Zh: “听起来很有意思。不过我什么都想不起来了。”

En: Champagne? That doesn't sound like anything *I'd* be into. I'm straight edge.
Zh: “香槟色？听起来绝对不是*我*的菜。我是个钢铁直男。”

En: Got it -- let's take it back to reality central for the next one.
Zh: “明白了——我们回到现实中心，继续下一个话题。”

En: Of course. You're much more *grounded*, a serious detective on a case called *reality*. Speaking of -- is there anything else?
Zh: “当然。你会更*理性*一些，你是一个严肃的警探，正在调查一起名为*现实*的案件。说到这里——还有别的吗？”

En: Wasn't it Guillaume le Million who said: *If you can remember the New, you weren't there*?
Zh: “纪尧姆·列米利翁不是说过：*如果你能记起新时代，那你根本没经过新时代？*”

En: Maximum brio, officer.
Zh: “你真是生机勃勃啊，警官。”

En: Wait -- Guillaume le Million? I was just thinking about that guy! Whatever happened to him?
Zh: “等等——纪尧姆·列米利翁？我刚还在想这家伙呢！他到底怎么了？”

En: Ah, yes... She looks hesitant. "This is not very *central* to reality, is it?"
Zh: “啊，是的……”她看起来有些犹豫。“这并不是现实的*中心*，不是吗？”

En: I still want to know.
Zh: “我还是想知道。”

En: I can't be one hundred percent sure, but I believe he died due to complications from venereal disease.
Zh: “我不能100%的肯定，不过我相信他是因为性病引起的并发症去世的。”

En: So you're saying he *didn't* vanish in a  puff of stardust? (Conclude.)
Zh: “所以你是说他*没有*消失在一阵星尘中？”（总结。）

En: I'm afraid no, but then again -- one can't be entirely sure. It's all part of his *mystique*. Ask someone else and you may get a more champagne-coloured take...
Zh: “恐怕没有，不过话说回来——谁都不能完全肯定。这也是他*奥秘*的一部分。找别人问问，也许你能知道一种更香槟色的说法……”

En: Which one?
Zh: “哪一种？”

En: *All* of them.
You? You are an officer of the RCM, she says energetically.
Zh: “你？你是一名RCM警官，”她精力充沛地说到。

En: For some reason your mind is a veritable repository of Guillaume le Million lyrics, trivia, and b-sides. But the time to explore this is when you're alone, facing a mirror perhaps? Right now it would derail the reality low-down.
Zh: 出于某种原因，你的脑子里是一个名副其实的*仓库*，里面塞满了纪尧姆·列米利翁的歌词，琐事和杂闻。不过还是等你一个人的时候再探索吧，也许可以在镜子前面？现在讨论会偏离现实内幕的。

En: Anyway, she concludes. "What other basic facets of reality should we discuss?"
Zh: “总之，”她总结到。“我们还可以讨论什么其他的基本现实呢？”

En: Good. And what is the Revachol Citizens Militia?
Zh: “很好。那瑞瓦肖公民武装是什么？”

En: Nothing more nor less than the de facto law enforcement body of post-revolutionary Revachol, detective.
Zh: “大革命之后瑞瓦肖事实上的执法机构，仅此而已，警探。”

En: The Revachol Citizens Militia?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖公民武装？”

En: *Precisomundo*!
Zh: “*完全正确*！”

En: This abbreviation -- what does it stand for?
Zh: “这个缩写词——代表什么意思？”

En: It stands for Revachol Citizens Militia.
Zh: “代表瑞瓦肖公民武装。”

En: Yes, the lieutenant steps in to make a gesture encompassing you both: "*We* are the Revachol Citizens Militia."
Zh: “是的，”警督向你走近一步，比了个手势，把你们俩都包围其中。“*我们*就是瑞瓦肖公民武装。”

En: Yes. That means: not de jure. The RCM acts in what is poetically called the *twilight of international law*; both at the behest of the Coalition Government -- and to its chagrin.
Zh: “是的。那就意味着：法律不承认。RCM的行动被赋予了一个诗意的名称——*国际法的暮光*；他们不仅需要服从联盟政府的命令——还要受它的委屈。”

En: You said *de facto*?
Zh: “你说*事实上*是什么意思？”

En: The RCM's responsibilities are defined by the Emergency, Wayfarer, and Aliments Acts -- three pieces of legislation keeping the city in a -- let's be honest -- laissez-faire stasis to the benefit of foreign capital.
Zh: “RCM的职责是由紧急法案、旅人法案和食品法案界定的——这三项法律让这座城市保持着一种——我们就实话实说了吧——自由放任的状态，这样外国资本就能从中受益。”

En: Are we?
Zh: “我们是这样的吗？”

En: We *are*.
So I'm basically one of the good guys?
Zh: “所以基本上可以说我是好人了？”

En: So I'm basically a lackey of capital?
Zh: “所以我基本上就是资本的狗腿子了？”

En: So I'm basically a thrall to foreign interests?
Zh: “所以我基本上就是外国利益的奴隶了？”

En: So I'm basically going to avoid this subject and ask the next question in this line of inquiry.
Zh: “所以我基本上打算避开这个话题，继续问调查过程中的下一个问题。”

En: Truly moralist of you. The Coalition Government would applaud you for staying out of politics. And yet...
Zh: “你是个货真价实的道德卫士。联盟政府会为你不插手政治的态度而喝彩的。不过……”

En: There's nothing *basic* about your role, detective. It's true that the RCM keeps everything the way our seemingly *permanent* provisional rulers like it... She leans in.
Zh: “你的角色一点也不*基本*，警探。RCM把一切都保持在这个表面上的*永久*临时统治者喜欢的样子，这是一个不争的事实……”她靠近了一些。

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: That's it for the *who I am* part then.
Zh: “那这就是*我是谁*部分的答案了。”

En: All three are good to know, the lieutenant looks up from his notes, "when we're out *policing*."
Zh: “了解那三个法案是很好的，”警督抬起埋在笔记本里的头，“特别是我们在外面*维持治安*的时候。”

En: Permit me to conclude with this: *who* you are -- to me -- is the police. The only legitimate law enforcement authority in Revachol...
Zh: “请允许我总结一下：你是*谁*——在我看来——你是个警察。瑞瓦肖唯一合法的执法机构的成员……”

En: And if those authorities drink so hard they need help recalling the basic terms of reality -- well, I am here to help. She bows and smiles.
Zh: “而且，如果那些官方人士喝得太凶，需要别人帮忙回想起基本现实的时候——我乐意效劳。”她鞠了一躬，笑了。

En: ...and if the law officials of our nation need help with the basic terms of reality -- well, then I am here to help. She bows.
Zh: “……而且，如果我们国家的执法人员需要基本现实方面的帮助——那我乐意效劳。”她鞠了一躬。

En: Thank you, ma'am.
Zh: “谢谢你，夫人。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Back to those basic terms of reality then.
Zh: “那就说回那些基本现实吧。”

En: Glad to have been of assistance -- the little that I know.... anything else?
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你——用我仅有的一点知识……还有别的吗？”

En: Glad to have been of assistance -- the little that I know. Anything else I can do?
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你——用我仅有的一点知识。我还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Yet on the other hand -- I know these people. I deal with them daily. Let me tell you, dear, they are not fans of you.
Zh: “不过，另一方面——我了解这些人。我每天都跟他们打交道。我可以告诉你，亲爱的，他们可不是你们的粉丝。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: The post-revolutionary decade was a disaster for the Coalition Government. Revachol in the Twenties was hell, especially on the west side of the river: gang warfare, a botched privatisation scheme, a nuclear pile meltdown...
Zh: “大革命后的十年对于联盟政府来说是一个灾难。20年代的瑞瓦肖宛如地狱一般，特别是河流西部：帮派冲突，乱七八糟的私有化方案，核反应堆熔毁……”

En: They called it the *International Zone* -- because no nation wanted to claim responsibility. The RCM restored peace where the Coalition failed. A true-blue citizens' initiative, she smiles. "They will never forgive you."
Zh: “他们把它叫做*国际区*——因为没有国家想声称对此负责。联盟失败的地方，RCM让它恢复了和平。一个不妥协气馁的公民主动权案例，”她笑了。“他们永远也不会原谅你们的。”

En: That's *somewhat* of an exaggeration, the lieutenant interjects. "In reality, ours is a mutually beneficial arrangement."
Zh: “那稍微*有点*夸大其词了，”警督插了一句。“事实上，这是一种互惠互利的安排。”

En: Revacholians get to keep the peace in Revachol, and the Coalition doesn't have to worry about it... He coughs. "Anyway, sorry to intrude. Please continue."
Zh: “瑞瓦肖人可以守护瑞瓦肖的和平，而联盟也不必担心瑞瓦肖再起波澜……”他咳嗽了几声。“不管怎么说，很抱歉打扰了你们。请继续吧。”

En: These are... unimportant times, detective. She puts her finger to her lips, then points at you: "You and I were born after the dust had settled, a thousandth of a second too late."
Zh: “现在是……不重要的时候，警探。”她把手指放在嘴唇上，然后指向了你：“你和我出生在尘埃落定之后的时代，晚了千分之一秒。”

En: Too late for what?
Zh: “什么晚了？”

En: Got it. Another question, then.
Zh: “明白了。那就再问个问题。”

En: For the *Big Time*. Her eyes light up. There's a flash of teeth.
Zh: “错过了*大时代*。”她的眼睛亮了起来。牙齿白的发光。

En: The Revolution, she says.
Zh: “大革命，”她说到。

En: The smile of a predator. No doubt what she's got in mind.
Zh: 掠夺者的笑容。毫无疑问，她脑子就在想这个。

En: It's a neurological disorder, caused by a lack of vitamin B in the brain. Symptoms include retrograde amnesia. It's... quite serious -- you should get yourself checked out.
Zh: “那是一种神经障碍，是因为大脑缺乏维他命B导致的。症状就包括逆行性遗忘。它还……挺严重的——你应该好好检查一下。”

En: She conveys it in short, cold bursts, trying not to invest too deeply in the condition of this doomed detective.
Zh: 她用简短、冷静的语言表达了自己的想法，尽量不让自己过于深入地了解这个倒霉警探的状况。

En: What causes encephalopathy?
Zh: “是什么导致脑病的？”

En: Got it. Let me ask something else.
Zh: “明白了。让我来问点别的吧。”

En: Well, it's either bariatric surgery -- or long term alcohol use.
Zh: “要么是减肥手术——要么就是长期饮酒。”

En: Oh, it's probably from surgery then.
Zh: “哦，那可能是因为手术了。”

En: It's definitely the drinking.
Zh: “绝对是因为喝酒。”

En: She nods, slowly.
Zh: 她慢慢地点点头。

En: That seems... unlikely.
Zh: “那好像……不太可能。”

En: In case he goes down later, souring the entire portfolio.
Zh: 以防他之后垮台，毁掉整个投资组合。

En: I know you've got a lot riding on me. I won't let you down.
Zh: “我知道你对我抱有极大的兴趣。我不会让你失望的。”

En: It's not just me, detective. All of Martinaise has placed its chips on your success. I pray we've all bet wisely.
Zh: “不仅仅是我，警探。整个马丁内斯都把筹码押在了你的成功上。我只能祈祷我们全都赌对了。”

En: What this boils down to is this *reality* thing is stupid. Blow this joint, grab a bottle and drain that shit right down your throat.
Zh: 归结起来就是这个叫*现实*的东西很愚蠢。离开这个地方，抓起一瓶酒，把这些破烂事都灌到喉咙里去吧。

En: Wait, wait, this *reality thing* is the only game in town. It's probably in your interest not to blow it.
Zh: 等等，等一下，这个*现实*是城里唯一的游戏了。为了你的利益，最好别把它搞砸了。

En: I have, uh, somewhere to party. Right now. [Leave.]
Zh: “我有，呃，可以派对的地方。现在。”[离开。]

En: Wipe that urge from your mind and get back to reality.
Zh: 把那种冲动从你脑海里抹去，回到现实中去。

En: Yes! And *lie* about it too! To her!
Zh: 是的！而且还要*撒谎*！对她撒谎！

En: No time for more questions now -- the art world calls. A performative experiment. You wouldn't understand. [Leave.]
Zh: “现在没时间问更多问题了——艺术世界在向我召唤。一场表述行为的试验。你不会明白的。”[离开。]

En: Of course, she says, apparently unsurprised. "Well, I'll be here if you need me."
Zh: “当然，”她说到，显然一点也不惊讶。“如果你需要的话，我会一直在这里。”

En: What's the Big Time?
Zh: “什么是大时代？”

En: Unimportant, as I said. The mineral rights were all distributed during the Revolution -- as were the bullets to the heads.
Zh: “就像我说过的，这不重要。大革命期间，矿产权已经全部被分散出去了——就像散入脑袋里面的子弹。”

En: You've got a predatory streak.
Zh: “你有掠夺成性的倾向。”

En: All men are predators, dear. Nothing much to be done about *that*. It's all a matter of where you get to file your teeth...
Zh: “所有人都是掠夺者，亲爱的。对于*那一点*，没什么可以做的。这完全不过是你要去哪里磨牙的问题……”

En: She won't manoeuvre her way out of this one.
Zh: 她是没办法通过耍花招摆脱这个的。

En: Hmh... She hums.
Zh: “嗯……”她低吟到。

En: She raises the corner of her mouth smirking, revealing a canine.
Zh: 她翘起嘴角笑了，暴露出一颗犬牙。

En: Wait, what's an ultra?
Zh: “等等，极端主义者是什么？”

En: An ultraliberal, it's a type of liberal. From the Revolution. It's... not the moderate kind.
Zh: “极端自由主义，是一种自由主义的形式。起源于大革命。它……不是比较温和的那种。”

En: What I am *not* is a basic term of reality, she smiles.
Zh: “我*并不是*一个基本现实，”她笑了。

En: There may be a way to get her speaking about herself -- at some later juncture.
Zh: 也许有办法能让她谈论自己的事——在之后的某个特定时刻。

En: I am an ultra.
Zh: “我就是一个极端主义者。”

En: Stop dodging -- what are you?
Zh: “别再回避了——你是什么？”

En: What are you?
Zh: “你是什么？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: I am the vilest of the vile, she says with a sudden flash of teeth. "A traitor, a devourer of nations and infants..."
Zh: “我是最卑鄙无耻的那一个，”她开口说到，牙齿白的发光。“一个叛徒，一个国家和婴孩的吞噬者……”

En: It's sharp.
Zh: 很尖锐。

En: Dios mío! (Draw a cross.) "A LIBERAL!"
Zh: “我的天啦！”（画一个十字。）“一个自由主义者！”

En: Cool. I *liberate* pretty hard myself.
Zh: “厉害。我本人也挺*自由*的。”

En: I don't understand -- what's so vile about that?
Zh: “我不明白——这有什么可耻的？”

En: Yes, she nods slowly. "I am the nether creature of the forbidden swamp. I pushed the king under a *shitwagon* and betrayed the Revolution. My kind surrendered the nation to financial colonists..."
Zh: “是的，”她慢慢点点头。“我是禁忌沼泽的下界生物。我把国王推到一辆*粪車*下面，背叛了大革命。我这类人把国家拱手献给了金融殖民者……”

En: No, she shakes her head, slowly. "Not like me. I am the nether creature of the forbidden swamp, who pushed the king under a *shitwagon* and betrayed the Revolution..."
Zh: “不，”她慢慢地摇了摇头。“不像我。我是禁忌沼泽的下界生物。我把国王推到一辆*粪車*下面，背叛了大革命。我这类人把国家拱手献给了金融殖民者……”

En: Haven't you heard? She nods, pedagogically: "I am a nether creature of the forbidden swamp, one of those who pushed the king under a shitwagon and betrayed the Revolution..."
Zh: “你没听见吗？”她摆出一副教育的姿势，点了点头：“我是禁忌沼泽的下界生物。我把国王推到一辆*粪車*下面，背叛了大革命。我这类人把国家拱手献给了金融殖民者……”

En: I can see you thought we'd gone extinct. After all, no sane person identifies as an *ultraliberal* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks into your eye. "You're a man of the left, no? Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- are you repulsed?"
Zh: “我看得出来，你以为我们已经灭绝了。毕竟，没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“你是左翼分子，对吧？告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: I can see you thought we'd gone extinct. After all, no sane person identifies as an *ultraliberal* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks into your eye. "You've got Revachol in your blood, a real nationalist, no? Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- are you repulsed?"
Zh: “我看得出来，你以为我们已经灭绝了。毕竟，没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“你的身体里流淌着瑞瓦肖的血液，你是一个真正的民族主义者，对吗？告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: I can see you thought our kind were all extinct. After all, no sane person identifies as *this* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks into your eye. "I can see we're not so different, you know... Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- do you find me frightening?"
Zh: “我看得出来，你以为我们已经灭绝了。毕竟，没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“我能看出我们没什么不同，你懂得……告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: I can see you thought we'd gone extinct. After all, no sane person identifies as an *ultraliberal* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks into your eye. "You're a centrist at heart, a real moralist, no? Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- do you find me frightening?"
Zh: “我看得出来，你以为我们已经灭绝了。毕竟，没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“你内心里是一个中间派，一个真正的道德家，不是吗？告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: Perhaps you thought we'd gone extinct. After all, no sane person identifies as an *ultra* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks into your eye. "Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- are you repulsed?"
Zh: “也许你觉得我们已经灭绝了。毕竟，没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: Are you *liberated* enough to offer up your home on a plate for financial colonists? She looks you in the eye. "No, I think not. Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- do you find me frightening?"
Zh: “你能*自由*到把自己的家园拱手送到金融殖民者的嘴边吗？”她看着你的眼睛。“不，我不这么觉得。告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: In her green eyes you see a mixture of truth and self-satire. Decades of guilt *and* pride.
Zh: 在她的那双绿眼睛里，你看见真相和自我讽刺的混合物。数十年的愧疚*和*自豪。

En: We surrendered the nation to the financier pirates. No sane person identifies as an *ultraliberal* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks you in the eye. "You're a man of the left, a true communard. Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- are you repulsed?"
Zh: “我们把国家拱手送给了金融强盗。没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“你是左翼分子，对吧？告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: We surrendered the nation to the financier pirates, no sane person identifies as an *ultraliberal* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks you in the eye. "You've got Revachol in your blood, a real nationalist. Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- are you repulsed?"
Zh: “我们把国家拱手送给了金融强盗。没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“你的身体里流淌着瑞瓦肖的血液，你是一个真正的民族主义者。告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: We surrendered the nation to financier pirates, no sane person identifies as *ultraliberal* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks into your eye. "I can see we're not so different, you know. Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- do you find me frightening?"
Zh: “我们把国家拱手送给了金融强盗。没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“我能看出我们没什么不同，你懂得……告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: I can see you thought I'd gone extinct. No sane person identifies as an *ultraliberal* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks you in the eye. "You're a centrist at heart, a real moralist. Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- do you find me frightening?"
Zh: “我看得出来，你以为我们已经灭绝了。毕竟，没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“你内心里是一个中间派，一个真正的道德家，不是吗？告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: We surrendered the nation to financial colonists. No sane person identifies as an *ultra* anymore. Not in broad daylight. She looks you in the eye. "Tell me -- now that I've *uncoiled* myself -- are you repulsed?"
Zh: “我们把国家拱手送给了金融殖民者。没有哪个神智健全的人会给自己贴上*极端自由主义者*的标签。在光天化日之下不会。”她看着你的眼睛。“告诉我——既然我已经*暴露*了自己——你会感到憎恶吗？”

En: You're a monster.
Zh: “你是个怪物。”

En: I forgive you.
Zh: “我原谅你。”

En: I forgive you -- but only because you're charming.
Zh: “我原谅你——不过只是因为你很有魅力。”

En: Forgive her.
Zh: 原谅她。

En: But only because you like pearlescent teeth and those light green eyes...
Zh: 不过只是因为你喜欢她那如珍珠一般光芒四射的牙齿，还有那双浅绿色的眼睛……

En: *A Deuill*, she pronounces: "*Who being of great Charme and Guille, sneaketh into the homes of the Godlie*."
Zh: “*哀悼*，”她断言到：“*即便是伟大的查尔梅与吉莱，也会悄悄混入歌德莱之家。*。”

En: That's a pity, she bows. "I hope we can remain collegial despite the fact that I'm a high priestess of capital. Don't take it personally -- it was only business, as they say."
Zh: “很遗憾，”她鞠了一躬。“尽管我是资本的女祭司，还是希望我们可以维持合作关系。我不会针对你——就像他们说的，生意就是生意。”

En: That's a pity, she bows. "I hope we can remain collegial despite my affection for regicide and multiculturalism. We didn't *mean* to kill the king, I assure you."
Zh: “很遗憾，”她鞠了一躬。“尽管我喜爱弑君和多元文化论，还是希望我们可以维持合作关系。我可以向你保证，我们不是*故意*要杀害国王的。”

En: That's a pity, she bows. "I do hope we can remain collegial despite it all. Tell me, when you look in the mirror, do you not see a monster staring back at you?"
Zh: “很遗憾，”她鞠了一躬。“尽管种种这般，还是希望我们可以维持合作关系。告诉我，当你照镜子的时候，难道你没看见有一头野兽也在回望着你吗？”

En: That's a pity, she bows. "I hope we can remain collegial despite it. Remember, we were partners, whether you like to admit it or not. There's blood on many hands, I assure you."
Zh: “很遗憾，”她鞠了一躬。“尽管如此，还是希望我们可以维持合作关系。记住，不管你承不承认，我们都是伙伴。我可以向你保证，很多人手上都沾满鲜血。”

En: That's a pity, she bows. "I hope we can remain collegial despite my monstrousness. The ultras didn't have anything against the king *or* the Commune, you see. It was just business."
Zh: “很遗憾，”她鞠了一躬。“尽管我暴行累累，还是希望我们可以维持合作关系。你看，极端主义者并不会反对国王*或者*是公社。这只不过是生意而已。”

En: Honestly... she pauses. "I may have even *preferred* it, had the communards won. Who knows? They might really have built something better. But they didn't, because they lost."
Zh: “老实说……”她停顿了一下。“我甚至会更*倾向于*这样，让公社赢得胜利。谁知道呢？他们也许真能建设出更好的东西。不过他们没有，因为他们失败了。”

En: When the dust settled, the liberals were the only ones left to clean up the mess. By virtue of their survival they were handed enormous power to shape the future... She turns her gaze to the delta. "This was all our last generation managed."
Zh: “当一切尘埃落定，唯一只剩下自由主义者在收拾残局。由于得以幸存，他们被赋予了巨大的权利去塑造未来……”她的目光飘向了德尔塔。“这就是我们的上一代达成的目标。”
